## HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

### 2016-07-20 | SB 3.25.5 | HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### Vedic system, Household life, Detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7H5hZviN3FA?t=30
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That is the Vedic system of household life."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the Vedic system of household life, emphasizing the duties of the son and husband regarding family responsibilities and detachment.

In the absence of the father, it is the duty of the grown son to take charge of his mother and serve her to the best of his ability so that she will not feel separation from her husband. And it is the duty of the husband to leave home as soon as there is a grown son to take charge of his wife and family affairs. That is the Vedic system of household life. One should not remain continually implicated in household affairs after the time of death. He must leave. Family affairs and wife may be taken charge of by a grown son.

#### Technology, Health, Computer Vision Syndrome
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7H5hZviN3FA?t=76
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Anybody who looks at the laptop screen continually for three to four hours every day, they develop a disease called computer syndrome disease."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the negative health effects of prolonged laptop and mobile screen use, including computer vision syndrome, and mentions that millions of people are under threat.

So, of late we are hearing many things about mobiles and laptops and computers. Every other day we see that a new mobile is released and a new laptop is released. It's like that. So, one of the newspapers have reported that anybody who looks at the laptop screen continually for three to four hours every day, they develop a disease called computer syndrome disease. New words and new diseases. And one of the things that happens is because you are staring at the screen, people are not blinking their eyes. They are not blinking. They continuously watch. So, because of that, there is little water which is there in the eye. You know, eyes, generally, they are a little watery. That's like a lubricant. That water is getting dried up, and there is an itchiness and some kind of a pain. And slowly the symptoms are increasing and their vision is getting blurred. And there is a pain if you look at the screen, or if you look at anything also, it's paining. The eyes are paining. And so they are saying in the whole world, millions of people are under threat now because everybody obviously has to work on the laptops and computers, and they are continuously looking at the screens without blinking. There is only one personality who can see without blinking: Paramatma. It's an image. It's an imitation. So anyway, so they are looking like that. They are saying immediately there are 70 million people are under threat. Immediately. Later on we don't know how many will come under this. So, that's one news article we read.

So, 20-20-20 formula, they are saying. When laptop came, people thought that that's it, we got the breakthrough, we can do so many things. Actually we can do so many things. #### Technology, Vulnerability, Contradiction, Maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7H5hZviN3FA?t=535
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "We are in pursuit of technology to make ourselves very powerful, but what actually happening is we are becoming vulnerable."
**Summary:** The speaker argues that the pursuit of technology for power leads to vulnerability and new diseases, highlighting the contradictory nature of Maya.

But what they don't realize is anything that gives you pleasure or anything that gives you little comfort will always come with a price. It will always come with a price. You have to pay the price. Laptop has made you powerful, but be ready for computer vision syndrome. And mobile has made you very powerful, be ready for sleepless nights and temporary blindness. And if you continue to work on mobile like that, you may get permanent blindness also. Temporary will become permanent. So that's the... So people are wanting to be powerful, and they are chasing the technology to gain power. But what actually happening is they are becoming more and more vulnerable to diseases, new new diseases and new new symptoms. These were not existing one decade back, we didn't had any issues like this. But now there are so many issues like that. And this is what some research they have done and somebody has published. And that is... It is not been given a big propaganda to all this. It's all simply some way they mention something and pass it on. What and all waiting by these laptops and mobiles, what and all diseases and what and all will come on to us nobody knows. Nobody knows. If we start discussing about what will happen if you keep the mobile and talk, that's one big paper you can write. But these things are not available. They will not, they will hide it off because there are industries built on this technology. So the point is we are in pursuit of technology to make ourselves very powerful, but what actually happening is we are becoming vulnerable. Actually, we are becoming weak. So this is called contradiction. We see these contradictions everywhere in this material world. You're looking for power, but actually, you are becoming weaker.

#### Maya, Contradiction, Happiness, Pain
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7H5hZviN3FA?t=682
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "If you're looking at happiness, it comes with a pain. It's a package."
**Summary:** The speaker uses Prabhupada's example of a cigarette advertisement to illustrate the contradictory nature of Maya, where the pursuit of happiness always comes with pain.

Like Prabhupada's times in the 60s, Prabhupada used to go on walking and then once he saw an advertisement, he says, become cool. And it's about an advertisement of a cigarette. And then Prabhupada says, see, he's smoking fire and he wants to become cool. And he said, this is contradiction. See, maya is like that. It is contradictory. So coming back to this principle that if you're looking at happiness, it comes with a pain. It's a package. It's like a coin. On the coin, there is a head and there is a tail. You're getting excited by the head. Wow. So nice. Wonderful. But behind the head, there is a tail also, right? So similarly, in this world, if there is a promise of happiness, that's only a head. And if you chase that head, there is a tail also. It'll come slowly. The tails will be visible. Initially, they are not visible. So this is the situation. We are living in a contradictory kind of a scenario. We are hoping to achieve something, but we are getting something else. This is maya. Right?

#### Austerity, Power, Detachment, Enjoyment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7H5hZviN3FA?t=940
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "bhoga or attachment to enjoyment will make you weaker and weaker and weaker, susceptible to diseases and weaknesses and everything."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the material world is designed such that enjoyment leads to weakness, while austerity leads to power, referencing Krishna and Brahma creating the world through tapas.

This world is designed in such a way. bhoga or attachment to enjoyment will make you weaker and weaker and weaker, susceptible to diseases and weaknesses and everything. And anybody who practices tapas, austerities, he'll become more and more, more and more powerful. This is the principle. In second canto, Krishna says, I created this world by my tapas, by my power of tapas. And how did Brahma create this world? By tapas. The whole world works on this principle. This is the creation, the principle behind this material world. So if we want to remain powerful in our endeavors, if we want to remain powerful in our life, if we want to remain powerful in our pursuits of advancement of Krishna consciousness, we have to practice tapas meticulously. Forget about Krishna consciousness. Anything that you want to achieve in this world, you need tapas. Only that will be rewarded. That's the only power. Right? So look at these examples that we have discussed. They're running behind enjoyment, but they're becoming weaker, weaker and weaker, and they're getting into contradictions, and they don't have any personal power. But here, kardhamamuni. But then, if kardhamamuni is so powerful, then pitari prastite aranyam. If he's so powerful, then why did he go to aranyam? Right? Why he has gone to aranyam? Very powerful, no? So he can do so many things. He created one city, a floating city, and he can do so many things also. But instead of doing all that, why did he go to aranyam? pitari prastite aranyam mātuhu prīci kṛśaya. So why he has went means, in order to increase his austerities, and be away from the promise of enjoyment in household life. Be away from all this. For small, small pleasure, imagine how much payment we are making. Little mobile, little message, little this symbol, little this symbol. All those symbols are there, no? For those little, little pleasures, imagine how much we are paying.

Highest pleasure in this world is sexual life. Now imagine how much we have to pay. The Vedic civilization knows this, the payment that we have to make. They know this. And that's why they say pitari prastite aranyam. He decided, no, I have to go. Vedic civilization means after 50, be in household life for 25 years, and then after 50, you have to reduce your attachment. And the final reduction, you know, say after 70, take sannyas. Completely aloof from all these things. Become eligible. Purify yourself and go back to Krishna. The life of freedom. That life of freedom, if you want here, then you'll only see contradictions. Now if you want to be healthy, you have to follow 20-20-20 some rule, right? You want freedom, but Maya is imposing more and more restrictions here. So this is Vedic civilization. #### Varnashrama, Detachment, Social Interactions, Krishna Consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7H5hZviN3FA?t=1426
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Varnashrama is simply a social tool to make our interactions very smooth, to make our material life stable."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Shastra advises detachment after 50 to avoid competition with one's son, and that Varnashrama is designed to smooth social interactions and allow time for Krishna consciousness.

That's why Shastra is saying after 50 years, leave it. This race you leave it. Go, go away. It is advising, because otherwise you will become competitor to the son. Son is setting up his house, and this fellow will become a competitor. Leave it. Go, go away. 50 years you can go away, along with the wife, and go for tithya yathras, Vanaprastha. Do tithya yathras for 25 years, and develop detachment with your family members. And once you are ready, leave the wife with the grown-up son. By then, you know, you would have settled a little bit. And then you hand over, and you go take sannyas. Go away. Otherwise, there is no meaning to this life. Simply competing with your own sons and grandchildren in enjoyment. So, very scientifically, the whole Varnashrama is designed in such a way that your social interactions will become very smooth. Varnashrama is simply a social tool to make our interactions very smooth, to make our material life stable, to allow us to have some time in order to spend our time in Krishna consciousness. This is the concept behind Varnashrama. How to make materially a pleasant situation, so that we can focus on higher goals of life. Right?

Nowadays, you go and ask anybody, we don't have time, they'll say, right? Everybody is busy. It's an ever-increasing work outside. In ever-increasing race, people are just going on and on and on. #### Maya, Choices, Happiness, Distraction, Attention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/7H5hZviN3FA?t=1716
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "If somebody has more choices to make, his happiness is less it seems."
**Summary:** The speaker shares an experiment's conclusion that having more choices leads to less happiness due to anxiety and dissatisfaction, highlighting the paradox of Maya and the importance of attention.

Recently, they have done one experiment. You know, and based on the experiment, the conclusion is very simple. If somebody has more choices to make, his happiness is less it seems. His happiness is coming down. If he has got so many choices to make, then they said, how is that? Because if you have so many choices, you are first of all in anxiety what to choose, because there are 108 channels or some 50, 60 groups. First is anxiety. And secondly, after choosing, he's not satisfied because he's thinking, maybe something I'm missing. He's not satisfied. So actually, happiness has come down. Look at this paradox of Maya. You want happiness and you created choice. Why people create variegatedness? Variety is the mother of enjoyment. So you created varieties for enjoyment. Now you ended up in less happiness. That's the research. Nobody is having any happiness in this world. They have too many choices, no happiness. This is the paradox. So devotees should be very careful. This is the distracting energy of the Supreme Lord. We should be very careful not to get distracted. That's why attention, there's so much, like Madhupananda Prabhu always talks about that, right? Why that has been emphasized so much? Because that is what is missing in this world today. Everybody is distracted. Everybody is unhappy.

**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### devotional service, association, personalism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DL2jxxsV-FA?t=30
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Association with devotees means association with the Lord."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of associating with Krishna conscious devotees for spiritual advancement and understanding the personal nature of the Absolute Truth.

The process of advancing in Krishna consciousness and devotional service is described here. The first point is that one must seek the association of persons who are Krishna conscious and who engage in devotional service. Without such association, one cannot make advancement. Simply by theoretical knowledge, our study, one cannot make any appreciable advancement. One must give up the association of materialistic persons and seek the association of people. Without the association of devotees, one cannot understand the activities of the Lord. Generally, people are convinced of the impersonal feature of the Absolute Truth because they do not associate with devotees. They cannot understand that the Absolute Truth can be a person and have personal activities. This is a very difficult subject matter, and unless one has personal understanding of the Absolute Truth, there is no meaning to devotion. In the same way, service or devotion cannot be offered to anything impersonal. Service must be offered to a person. Non-devotees cannot appreciate Krishna consciousness by reading the Srimad Bhagavatam or any other Vedic literature wherein the activities of the Lord are described. They think that these activities are fictional, manufactured stories because spiritual life is not explained to them in the proper mood. To understand the personal activities of the Lord, one has to seek the association of devotees, and by such association, when one contemplates and tries to understand the transcendental activities of the Lord, the path to liberation is open and is freed. One who has firm faith in the Supreme Personality of Godhead becomes fixed, and his attraction for association with the Lord and the devotees increases. Association with devotees means association with the Lord. The devotee who makes this association develops the consciousness for rendering service to the Lord, and then being situated in the transcendental position of devotional service, he gradually becomes perfect. It's a very crucial verse.

#### consciousness, materialism, detachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DL2jxxsV-FA?t=181
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker uses the recent demonetization event in India as an analogy to illustrate how people's consciousness is easily absorbed in material topics, and how this tendency can be redirected towards Krishna consciousness.

Prabhupada quotes this many times in his lectures and conversations. And this is part of that shloka book also, the selected verses that the devotees have come up with. I think after last November 8th, the subject matter in this country has changed. For the last more than a month or so, everybody is only dreaming about two notes, 500 rupees and 1000 rupees. So after the demonetization, anywhere you touch anybody, they are all talking about this only. And in fact, some people are dreaming also. They are standing in front of ATM counters, and there is a big queue, and then somehow they reach the machine. And there is money. Generally, you don't get money, but in the dream, they are getting money. So, you know, like that, the whole consciousness of the country has changed. Everybody is talking about money. So if you want to meet anybody and strike a relationship, it's very easy to start with this topic, demonetization. And immediately they'll catch onto it and will say yes, yes and like that. And then they start to see the reality that is life itself. So, we're And in one or two minutes you will understand whether it is favorable or unfavorable. Based on that, you know, you can proceed further. So everybody is talking about it.

#### Krishna consciousness, cultivation, research
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DL2jxxsV-FA?t=297
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So if we can use the same phenomena of researching and getting convinced about what we are doing, and convinced about Krishna consciousness, we become stronger."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the demonetization analogy, highlighting how people research and become convinced of their opinions. He suggests applying the same dedication to researching and understanding Krishna consciousness.

Many people are glorifying the prime minister. And of course some people are criticizing. And everybody has their own opinion. Some people are saying it will benefit. Some people say it may not benefit. Like that, the various, you know, opinions are coming out. And if I am supportive of the prime minister, then I will always look for the topics which are in line with my understanding. There is an article on how demonetization has affected the country positively, 15 ways. And immediately I am interested, what are these 15 ways? And if somebody says, no, how it has spoiled the whole economy, then I don't want to read that. I keep it aside. So anyway, like that, I go more and more deeply into this subject, justifying how my stand is correct, right? And if you do a little bit of research in the internet, you will see innumerable articles which will justify your stand, either positive or negative. So using your research, you become more and more aware of the fact that you are in a position to justify your stand. You become more and more convinced about your standpoint. So that's what people are doing now. They are all becoming, some group is positive, some group is negative. So if we can use the same phenomena of researching and getting convinced about what we are doing, and convinced about Krishna consciousness, we become stronger. So that's what we have to do. So we have to do it. So these are first ten ways of what we are doing. The third way is called the... ''Tad-yosana'' Jyoshana means cultivation. Cultivation means a systematic process. So if we can cultivate and research in order to justify our stand, that Krishna is the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and service to Him is the highest goal of our time, I think that we are at the right place. vedic literature.

#### liberation, devotion, association
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DL2jxxsV-FA?t=438
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A devotee is not praying to come out of the bad habit. That is only a byproduct. He is praying for shraddharati bhaktir anukramishyati."
**Summary:** This segment explains that liberation is a byproduct of increasing attachment to Krishna, not the primary goal. It emphasizes the importance of positive discussions about Krishna consciousness with devotees.

Then this verse is promising that apavarga vartmani will become freed. First of all this is what happens in our lives. We become freed. A devotee is not praying for becoming freed. Let us say we are struggling with a bad habit. A devotee is not praying to come out of the bad habit. That is only a byproduct. He is praying for shraddharati bhaktir anukramishyati. Whether he is harassed by a habit, harassed by somebody XYZ, or some material world, some things are disturbing him, he is not saying please remove all these disturbances from my life. He is saying please increase my attachment to the Lord. So anyway, so apavarga vartmani. So if you can do research by discussing with the devotees Vaishnavas, because they're all Krishna group, like you know the positive demonetization, positive group he said and they're all discussing how it is positive. Their whole discussion points is about positive. So similarly when the devotees they discuss, they positively discuss about the aspects of Krishna consciousness and then they become more convinced about the whole subject. Satam prasangam mama viryasamvido. So prasangat means association. Through the association of satam, pure devotees, we discuss about the potency of the Supreme Lord and then we become freed. All those habits or whatever things which are harassing us slowly we will be freed from that. Krishna will release us from those habits. But then, what is our endeavour? Tad-josanāt. We should endeavour to discuss about Krishna.

#### Krishna, pastimes, cultivation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DL2jxxsV-FA?t=570
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, can we get that much of pleasure while discussing about Krishna's past times?"
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes discussing Krishna's wonderful activities and pastimes, comparing the pleasure derived from them to a pleasing preparation. He stresses the systematic cultivation of this knowledge.

What is that we have to discuss about Krishna? Specifically it is mentioned here, vīrya, that is wonderful activities, saṁvidā. So, in the translation, Prabhupāda is saying discussion of the past times and activities of the Supreme Lord. There is a specific emphasis given on the past times of the Lord. So, if we can discuss those. It is saying, hṛtkarṇa rasāyana. These stories are rasāyana, very pleasing. Like on Ekadeshi days, they make one preparation. The nice banana. They call that as rasāyana, very pleasing. So, can we get that much of pleasure while discussing about Krishna's past times? So, this verse is assuring that if you can discuss with, associate with satāṁ prasaṅgān, associate with pure devotees, then those discussions will be very pleasing to the heart. So, here the many important points are there. Because we are sādhakās, we need to develop this in a systematic way. Tad-joṣānāt. We have to cultivate. Cultivation means, as I said, it is a systematic process. For example, a farmer is cultivating a piece of land. And he cannot just do whatever he wants. And at the end of three months, or four months, or one year, he expects a nice output from the field. It is not possible. He has to follow a systematic process.

#### transcendental knowledge, assimilation, hunger
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DL2jxxsV-FA?t=687
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If you are not having hunger, that eagerness to learn more and more, that means somewhere there is some problem."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of food digestion to explain the process of assimilating transcendental knowledge. He emphasizes that a sign of proper assimilation is a growing hunger for more knowledge.

First he has to put seeds, he has to grow them, he has to safeguard them. And then, so by doing all these things systematically, then he can expect, okay, there is a nice crop coming up. So similarly, when we hear this transcendental knowledge, we have to cultivate this knowledge. In one place, Prabhupāda translates this Tad-joṣānāt as assimilation. See, this transcendental knowledge, when you hear, the next step is you have to assimilate. When you go, after this class, we will all be going to Prasādam Hall, and we have our nice breakfast. After the breakfast, the next step, although we may not know, the next step is to assimilate the breakfast, right? You may not consciously doing it, the body does it. End result you will know, you are energetic. And after four years, four hours, three and a half to four hours, again you start feeling hungry. If you are healthy, and if you are working on it, if you are working, you are physically active or mentally active, after three and a half, four hours, then again you become hungry, ready to eat again, right? So, if you hear this transcendental knowledge, and the next step is you assimilate it, I will come back to that, what is that assimilation. After the assimilation, you work on it, you try to act on it, then the next result will be, you will be hungry for more knowledge. After a day or after two days or whatever, you become more hungry about this transcendental knowledge. If you are not having hunger, that eagerness to learn more and more, that means somewhere there is some problem.

#### digestion, social media, overload
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DL2jxxsV-FA?t=798
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Now, the messages have become too many. Digestion is not happening."
**Summary:** The speaker extends the digestion analogy to the consumption of information on social media, warning against the dangers of information overload and the lack of assimilation.

Sometimes, you know, if you have digestive problems, you will not get hungry after three and a half, four hours. You know, you eat in the morning only, but in the evening also you become hungry. So, similarly, this, you know, transcendental knowledge you take, but you don't digest, then there is a possibility. See, nowadays, it's all about social media, right? We have so many channels of communication. The Facebook is there, WhatsApp is there, and so many other channels are there. And as devotees, we have dovetailed these channels, and we communicate Krishna Katha, right? We put pictures of Krishna, we put quotes, and we send articles. And so that people can read, people can get aware of what is our philosophy. And we also keep getting, throughout the day, so many messages, so many articles, so many videos, so many pictures. Now, there is one danger here. I have seen, sometimes, when we get a good article, we read the headline, some nice headline will be there. Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavad Gita and Demonetization, something. Very nice. And then, immediately we press forward, and we have a group, send it off. And you have not read, and it will go to your hundred people who are there in the group, and they all forward another hundred, and they have not read. It goes on like this. Some people will read, of course. Now, the messages have become too many. Digestion is not happening.

#### overeating, social media, assimilation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DL2jxxsV-FA?t=939
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Forwarding is a Shudra job."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to critique the unthinking forwarding of messages on social media, comparing it to a 'Shudra job' and emphasizing the importance of digesting and implementing the knowledge.

Overeating. You know, you see this picture, you see that picture, you see this. And sometimes, when I open my own WhatsApp account, I see, you know, that number will be there, 40 messages, 50 messages. I become panicky. Oh my God, so many messages. And then, you know, then you see, quickly, quickly. You want to see, because you don't want to suddenly go into the end. You know, you want to see. But you see very quickly. 50 messages over, and you are ready for the next 50. Why you cleared it up? Because if some messages come, I should not, I should not miss it. So, like that. Now, what happened to these 50 messages? So nicely, so much of endeavor, somebody has put in, and then they have sent you, you are not reading. Maybe you are forwarding. That's okay. Forwarding is a Shudra job. Cut and paste and forwarding is a Shudra job. It's a laborer job, you know, skill, control C, control V, control C, control. It's a Shudra job. But writing an article and posting, it's a Brahmanical job. So somebody has done, somebody has done that Brahmanical job. That's why we are able to see some content on the, some content on the social media. But we should not become, you know, Shudras, sending, sending to hundreds of people. So this is, this is the anomaly of our age now, that there is a content overload. There is an information overload on everybody's head. Not only in our own channels, Krishna conscious channels, anywhere you take, there's a huge overload of information. To digest all that, and to able to come up with a, an action plan, what to do next, that is becoming a very intense job. So that's why devotees, they should be very careful about this, whatever they're getting this information.

#### digestion, preaching, implementation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DL2jxxsV-FA?t=1074
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you can preach that subject, in our own words, to whatever level, whatever capacity we have, let us say you're able to preach to somebody, that means you have digested it."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that while reading Krishna conscious content is beneficial, the benefit is minimal without digestion and implementation. He suggests preaching or writing as ways to demonstrate and deepen understanding.

For sure, they're all connected to Krishna. There's no doubt. And when you read, when you read, you're going to get a benefit, no doubt about it. But that benefit will be very minimal, very minimal. If you don't digest and try to implement that, then it is very, very minimal. Let us say you read that article, and then you preach to somebody, that means you have digested it. If you can preach that subject, in our own words, to whatever level, whatever capacity we have, let us say you're able to preach to somebody, that means you have digested it. So sometimes you may ask this question, how do I know that I digest it? But this is one indication, if you're able to represent those thought processes, that ideas, and then you preach to somebody, then you may say, no, I don't have opportunity to preach, then it doesn't matter. You read this, can you represent those thoughts, represent those thoughts, in writing, without saying that, just write down, some 5-10 minutes can I write down, some 1 or 2 sentences, in my own words, if you can repeat that in writing, that also means that you have digested, you have understood. See what happens, if you get anything easily, you lose easily. And if you read something, and it appears to be very easy, digestible very nicely, I can assure you, you will lose it very easily. Because you have not done any tapasya, to digest it, to understand it. In this world, tapasya is very important.

#### tapasya, cultivation, purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DL2jxxsV-FA?t=1183
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So when you do like that, you spend time with that, a sentence, or you know, a paragraph, then, you are digesting it nicely."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of tapasya (austerity) in digesting transcendental knowledge. He suggests using logic, questioning, and intelligence to analyze and implement the knowledge for purification.

So, so as students, of transcendental knowledge, this step has to be given, a lot of importance, tajyoshana, the cultivation. You have to, let us say, make a statement, you use your logic, you use some questioning, you use your intelligence, and see, how, why, can I, how can I implement, how can I explain, this is all for our purification. So when you do like that, you spend time with that, a sentence, or you know, a paragraph, then, you are digesting it nicely. Next day also, you will be able to recollect, for example, taking notes, very good, but you have to go back, and read those again, and see if you can write down, without seeing these notes. So like that, some of these steps, if we don't do, then, you know, it appears to be very easy, in our minds, but we will not be able, to present our thoughts, we will not be able, to put down in writing, you know, we will, we will see stars, instead of seeing Krishna, right? So, this is very, very important. So, if you can do that, spend time, nicely analyze, assimilate, and understand this knowledge, the next one will, we know this, will be freed, from, you know, this, entangling, entangling thoughts, entangling actions, entangling habits, whatever, will be freed from all that.

#### attachment, mind, knot
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/DL2jxxsV-FA?t=1285
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This attraction is called, knot, this is the knot in the heart, a knot, heart, you know, knot in the, you know, your, in your mind, so that is the knot, you know, you cannot go, little bit, you can go here and there, but again, you will come back to the same thought, right?"
**Summary:** The speaker describes the nature of attachment as a 'knot' in the heart or mind, which pulls one back to unwanted thoughts. He introduces two methods for dealing with this knot: cutting it off with transcendental knowledge or opening it up through understanding.

Generally, our attachment is, in the mind, you know, there is a pull, mind or heart, they are connected, these two, so there is a pull, you know, it is, it is pulling you. You want, we want to go away, from that thought, but, you know, there is a pull, there is an attraction. Let us say, some thought is coming up, in the mind, you know, it is not good for me, you want to run away, there is some place, something in you, which is saying, let me go away from this thought, but there is a pull also, it is pulling you, there is an attraction, it is there, right? So, this attraction is called, knot, this is the knot in the heart, a knot, heart, you know, knot in the, you know, your, in your mind, so that is the knot, you know, you cannot go, little bit, you can go here and there, but again, you will come back to the same thought, right? So, if there is a rope, which is holding you, a knot, which is holding you, one thing is, you can take a sword, and you can cut it off, that is what Krishna says, in the fourth chapter, this transcendental knowledge, is compared to a sword, and you can cut it off, if that rope is there, talk, it cuts off, that is why Prabhupada, some of the words of Sri Prabhupada, when he says, on such and such, some topic, that is it, cut off, better cut off, otherwise, fool, rascal, so many, so, immediately cut off, so, but some things, we won't be able to, you know, it requires, repeated hearing, and, so, when a rope is there, a knot is there, one is, you can cut it off, one more is, you can open it up, that opening, is this, you have to understand, to open, you have to understand, where the knot is, hold it, you know, like that, then you can come out of that, particular thing, that is pulling you back, you can open it up, and then you are freed, see.

**Key Quote:** "This is the greatest opportunity Srila Prabhupada has given in Kali Yuga."

That's what this verse is, satam prasangam, somehow, stick on, to this, association, of devotees, whatever, may happen, anything, may happen, simply, hold on, to this association, then, one day, apavarga vartman, we will be freed, then, we develop, attraction, to the lord, so, we have to put, all our endeavours, on to this association, if you leave this, then, we will fall into, that hubbububai, and, we don't know, where we will go, we may land up, in this also, you know, anything, we don't know, so, that's why, we have to be very careful, this is the greatest opportunity, Srila Prabhupada has given, in Kali Yuga, he has created, these wonderful pockets, of association, called, ISKCON, Hare Krishna Movement, and, where, we can get access to, transcendental association, so, if you can take full advantage, then, we will become perfect, in this life, any questions?

### 2017-04-09 | SB 1.8.46 | HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### Kurukshetra, Justice, Politics
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CS-j9pPzSo4?t=30
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The whole clique of politics centered around this point and all the kings and residents of the whole world became involved in this fight between the rival brothers."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the political context of the Battle of Kurukshetra, focusing on Yudhishthira's claim to the throne and the widespread involvement in the conflict.

The Pious King Yudhishthira was mortified because of the mass massacre of human beings in the Battle of Kurukshetra, especially on his account. Duryodhana was there on the throne and he was doing well in his administration. And in one sense there was no need of fighting. But on the principle of justice, Yudhishthira was to replace him. The whole clique of politics centered around this point and all the kings and residents of the whole world became involved in this fight between the rival brothers.

---
#### Krishna's Inconceivability, Yudhishthira's Mortification, Superhuman Actions
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CS-j9pPzSo4?t=69
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As super soul of King Yudhishthira, he did not allow the King to be convinced by the words of Vyasa and others, including himself."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Yudhishthira's distress over the Kurukshetra deaths and his inability to be consoled, even by Krishna and Vyasa, explaining it as Krishna's desire to orchestrate a meeting between Yudhishthira and Bhishmadeva.

Lord Krishna was also there on the side of King Yudhishthira. It is said in the Mahabharata, Adiparva, that 640 million men, that means 64 crore, were killed in the 18 days of Battle of Kurukshetra. And some hundreds and thousands were missing. Practically this was the greatest battle in the world within 5000 years. This mass killing simply to enthrone Maharaja Yudhishthira was too mortifying. So he tried to be convinced with evidences from histories, by great power, by great sages like Vyasa and the Lord himself that the fight was just because the cause was just. But Maharaja Yudhishthira would not be satisfied even though he was instructed by the greatest personalities of the time. Krishna is designated herein as the performer of superhuman actions. But in this particular instance neither he nor Vyasa could convince King Yudhishthira. Does it mean that he failed to be a superhuman actor? No, certainly not. The interpretation is that the Lord as Ishwara, or the super soul in the hearts of both King Yudhishthira and Vyasa, performed still more superhuman action because the Lord desired it. As super soul of King Yudhishthira, he did not allow the King to be convinced by the words of Vyasa and others, including himself.

---
#### Krishna's Plan, Bhishmadeva's Glory, Divine Will
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CS-j9pPzSo4?t=179
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Lord wanted that King Yudhishthira be pacified by the words of Bhishma Deva, so that the world could see that Bhishma Deva excelled all in knowledge, including the Lord Himself."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining Krishna's plan, emphasizing the Lord's desire for Bhishmadeva to see the Pandavas enthroned and to be glorified for his knowledge, even above Krishna himself.

The Lord wanted that at the last stage of his material existence the great warrior Bhishmadeva see him personally and see his beloved grandchildren King Yudhishthira etc. now situated on the throne and thus pass away very peacefully. Bhishmadeva was not at all satisfied to fight against the pressure of Western Hillary and media hereafter, fight against the Pandavas, who were his beloved fatherless grandchildren. But the Kshatriyas are also very stern people, and therefore he was obliged to take the side of Duryodhana, because he was maintained at the expense of Duryodhana. Besides this, the Lord also desired that King Yudhishthira be pacified by the words of Bhishma Deva, so that the world could see that Bhishma Deva excelled all in knowledge, including the Lord Himself.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CS-j9pPzSo4?t=374
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** The speaker addresses the common misconception of comparing Krishna to ordinary humans, emphasizing Krishna's superhuman nature (Adbhuta Karmana) and using the example of lifting Govardhana Hill to illustrate his unique abilities.

You know, he has given a title like that. Basically, eve teasing by men in the public places. Yes. The squads will go around and then see if there are any, a young man or a young girl is, you know, they are there. And they want to see whether there is any harassment there. So, one more politician, you know, somehow he didn't like the name, anti-Romeo. Then he made a comment that Krishna was also used to tease young girls. So why don't you name this as anti-Krishna squads? Why are you making it as anti-Romeo? You know, that is his point. Of course, everybody got on his case. And then he apologized and went back. Many people think Krishna, you know, compare Krishna with ourselves. And that's how they make such comments. Here, in one sentence, one single word, Suta Goswami is telling about Krishna's special position. In one simple, one single word. He says, The Lord's activities are Adbhuta means the superhuman activities. So, these people, they take Krishna loosely and then say, Krishna danced with many girls. Krishna is also, you know, played with many gopis. So, why not me? This is a common question many people ask about Krishna. Why Krishna did this? Answer is, Krishna is Adbhuta Karmana.

#### Krishna's Potency, Inconceivable Nature, Limitations of Human Logic
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CS-j9pPzSo4?t=502
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna lifted Govardhana hill. If you can also lift Govardhana, then yes, you can go and do whatever Krishna has done."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to emphasize Krishna's superhuman abilities, using the example of lifting Govardhana Hill. He contrasts conceivable and inconceivable actions, highlighting the limitations of human logic in understanding Krishna.

Krishna lifted Govardhana hill. When he is seven years old, just seven years old, on his little finger, he held a huge mountain called Govardhana. And he kept it like that for seven continuous days. Krishna lifted Govardhana hill. That is Adbhuta Karmana. If you can also lift Govardhana, then yes, you can go and do whatever Krishna has done. Forget about lifting Govardhana. You keep the little finger like this for one hour. Then you will decide. Then we can decide what you can do. We cannot even. After one hour, it will start showering. So, this is our level. But the people, they have no idea. They equate the Lord with our own situation. This is a common psychology or the common mentality of every one of us. But then we try to assess others according to our own situation. We don't realize that the other person can be different. The other person can have different capacities. We don't understand all this. So, we include Krishna also like that in our calculations. And we think Krishna is an ordinary human being, simply because he is having two hands and two legs, and he appears like that, right? But here it is mentioned. Krishna is Adbhuta Karmana. He is a superhuman. He can do superhuman activities. If we want to advance in Krishna consciousness, now, this is the first point that we have to understand. Krishna is different from me. And Krishna is so different from me that he is superhuman. He can do superhuman activities. He can do inconceivable activities. There are two words are there. Conceivable. Inconceivable. Conceivable means you can understand using your intelligence, your reasoning power, your logic. You can understand that. Let us say I say.

---
#### Conceivable vs. Inconceivable, Limitations of Logic, Accepting Krishna's Specialness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CS-j9pPzSo4?t=669
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If we want to understand Krishna, the first thing that we have to accept is Krishna is very special and he is inconceivable in his activities."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of carrying heavy bags to illustrate the difference between conceivable and inconceivable actions, emphasizing that our limited logic cannot comprehend Krishna's unlimited potencies and that we must accept Krishna's specialness to understand Him.

Somebody is carrying a rice bag on his back. We all say yes, possible. Why? It is conceivable. Using our logic, using our logic we can understand. It is okay. 50 kg is okay. If I say somebody is carrying a sugar bag, 100 kg, still we will say possible. You know at least in my childhood days, I used to see people carrying sugar bags. Some people even, you know one and half, you know they used to manage like that, up to 150 kg. Now a days I don't know. So the, so we can still say yes it is possible. But moment we say, yes it is possible, sometimes people even, say the way they said it, they are telling you, yes it is possible. It is not possible. You can never say yes. You can never say it. They won't tell you. They will say yes it is possible. Some people don't even know it. We just can't understand it. So they are saying that, it is possible. I have heard some people say, you get the same one. But it is possible. Someone is carrying a sugar bag. No. Hey. You are having to do it. So. Some people are saying this. That is a. Some people have put, you know, the sugar bag. Why? It is inconceivable to your logic, right? That is your logic. That is your limitation. So your limitation doesn't mean that such a personality doesn't exist. So this is an important point to understand. If we want to understand Krishna, the first thing that we have to accept is Krishna is very special and he is inconceivable in his activities.

#### Complete Spirit vs. Matter, Krishna's Spiritual Body, Bhagavad Gita's Teachings
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/CS-j9pPzSo4?t=896
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is complete spirit. Krishna is body and is inside. Everything is complete spirit."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Krishna's complete spiritual nature with the material nature of our bodies, emphasizing that Krishna's body is not subject to material limitations and referencing the Bhagavad Gita's teachings on the nature of spirit.

Right? So we should know that Krishna is complete spirit. Krishna is body and is inside. Everything is complete spirit. Whereas we, our body is material. But inside, we are all spirit. We are spirit souls, but covered by body. So it's like a combination. You know, we have matter and inside there is a spirit. So there is a weakness. Matter is weak. If this table is there, it is complete matter. So you can see there is a spectrum of substances. There is a complete matter. There is we. There is a matter. And inside actually we are spirit. Krishna is complete spirit, inside and outside. Now spirit means what? That is explained in Bhagavad Gita, the preliminary scripture, preliminary to Srimad Bhagavatam. Now what is that? Bhagavad Gita says, Spirit cannot be cut. Spirit cannot be burnt. These are all very important characteristics. Krishna first time in the history of this mankind is revealing two kinds of substances. He is saying there is one thing called matter. We have very much experience of it. But he is revealing there is one more substance, a separate category of substance, and that is called spirit. Then you may ask, What is that spirit? What are its characteristics? That Krishna is explaining in the second chapter of Bhagavad Gita. He is saying, You cannot cut the spirit he is saying. You cannot burn spirit. Let us say you throw spirit into the fire. What is our understanding? If you throw anything into the fire, What will happen? Very big fire. Huge fire. What will happen? Completely burnt and it will become ashes, right? That is our experience of matter. But Krishna is now talking about one more different substance. And he is saying, If you throw that into the fire, Spirit is thrown into the fire, nothing will happen he is saying. See? He is talking about a different kind. Now Krishna's body is completely spirit. He is completely spiritual body.

That means, His body is not affected by fire. They all used to go, Graze in the, Vrindavan forest. And you know, By evening they all used to come back. When, One day, When Krishna was actually, You know, Going along with the cows, And they are all moving, Strolling across the forest, And all that, Suddenly there was a forest fire. You know, Forest fire means, It doesn't come, With a notice. It suddenly happens. Samsara, Dhava, Nalali, The Loka. Forest fire is sudden. You know, Two branches will rub each other, There is a fire. So suddenly there was a fire. And all the, Gopas and cows, They are calling out to the Lord, For protection. Krishna please save us. And immediately Krishna comes to the scene. Now what did Krishna do, To, Save the, The friends from this forest fire? He opened his mouth, See it is very unusual you know, Inconceivable, The point is, Krishna's actions are inconceivable. It is beyond our logic, your tiny logic. So he opened his mouth, And then he swallowed, The whole fire into himself. Now Krishna's body is, Spirit. Now if, Even if the spirit is in the fire, Nothing will, Happen.

#### consciousness, purity, perception

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In pure consciousness, one can perceive the existence of the consciousness. One can perceive a slight reflection of the Supreme Personality of Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker describes pure consciousness as the original, unpolluted state where one can perceive the self and a reflection of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

The pure status of consciousness, or Krishna consciousness, exists in the beginning. Just after creation, consciousness is not polluted. The more one becomes materially contaminated, however, the more consciousness becomes obscured. In pure consciousness, one can perceive the existence of the consciousness. One can perceive a slight reflection of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the clarity and transparency of pure consciousness, linking it to seeing reality and one's own existence, and emphasizes its spiritual nature.

As in clear, unagitated water, free from impurities, one can see everything clearly. So in pure consciousness, or Krishna consciousness, one can see things as they are. One can see the reflection of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and one can see his own existence as well. This state of consciousness is very pleasing, transparent. In the beginning, consciousness is pure. We are discussing from the chapter titled, Fundamental Principles of Material Nature. It is interesting to see, in the Fundamental Principles of Material Nature, the verse is talking about consciousness. We all know consciousness is spiritual, indestructible, all pervading in the body, and doesn't end when the body ends.

#### consciousness, purity, distraction

**Summary:** The speaker explains the characteristics of pure consciousness: clarity, freedom from change/distraction, and peace, using the example of WhatsApp as a source of distraction.

And here, the vrutibhir lakshanam, the characteristic traits of pure consciousness, are explained. So, when the consciousness is pure, it is evident in three characteristics. One is, svacchatvam, it is pure, it is clear, clarity, transparent. And then, avikaritvam. Avikaritvam means, there is no change. Prabhupada translates this as, free from distractions. If you want to understand distraction, then keep a mobile with WhatsApp. Then, you will clearly understand what distraction means. So, if you can be free from distraction, you have attained a pure consciousness. So, you can see, it's a long journey to really come to the stage of freedom from distractions. It's a long journey. I'll come to that.

#### consciousness, peace, Bhagavad Gita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu7eHNYYyHE?t=194
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Many people say, I don't have peace of mind, but actually, peace is connected to consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker continues describing the characteristics of pure consciousness, focusing on peace (shantatvam) and then transitions to discussing the Bhagavad Gita and the concept of Kshetra (field of activities).

Avikaritvam, and then, shantatvam. Shantatvam means, peace, serenity. Many people say, I don't have peace of mind, but actually, peace is connected to consciousness. So, these are the three things: clarity, clear, transparent; then, no change, or freedom from distraction; and, peaceful. These three are the vrittibhir lakshanam proktam. The Shastra is declaring that these are the three characteristics of pure consciousness. Chetasah. Chetah is pure consciousness. So, if you look at Bhagavad Gita, in the 13th chapter, if you see, verses 6 to 7, Krishna explains, He defines, what is field of activities. He says, Kshetra. Kshetra, Kshetrajnayor, we know. That is called Jnanam. That is knowledge.

#### Kshetra, avyaktam, consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu7eHNYYyHE?t=257
**Verse References:** BG 13.6-7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, in the beginning, the mahātattvā, that is pure consciousness, is clear. It is pure, doesn't have any contamination."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Krishna's definition of Kshetra, including the unmanifested (avyaktam), and connects it to pradhāna and mahātattvā, stating that mahātattvā is pure consciousness in the beginning.

So, what is Kshetra? So, Krishna is saying, mahābhūtāni ahankāro buddhir avyaktam eva ca. He uses the word avyaktā. So, it's very interesting, that the field of activities includes avyaktam, unmanifested. So, in our body, our body is also field of activity for us. Field of activity can also refer to the world, that we see all around us. So, so, our body has a portion of avyaktam. An unmanifested little portion is there. Little portion is there. Now, what is this unmanifested portion that Prabhupāda is explaining in the purport? The unmanifested three modes of material nature. The unmanifested three modes of material nature is called pradhāna. So, we all have in our bodies, there is a little pradhāna is there. There is a little bit of mahātattvā is there. And that, Prabhupāda is saying, in the beginning, the mahātattvā, that is pure consciousness, is clear. It is pure, doesn't have any contamination.

But as soon as the modes appear, and consciousness divides into these three modes, and we will see further creation happens. So, in the beginning, the consciousness is pure, but it becomes, starts contaminated. Contaminations will creep in. What is contamination? Contamination is nothing but the three modes. These are the contaminations in this world. So, interesting to know, that there is some avyaktam, is inside, is within the field of activities. That avyaktam is pradhāna, or mahātattvā. That's also called consciousness. In one place, it is termed as contaminated consciousness.

It is a shadow of the Supreme Lord's consciousness. Shadow means, although the mahātattvā is connected to Kṛṣṇa, still it appears to be matter. So, that is interesting. Mahātattvā is appearing, manifested as matter. And a portion of that mahātattvā comes into us. After all, this body, is a sample universe. Universe has a virāt body, and we have a sample body. So, whatever we perceive in the virātrupa, or the universal body, you can perceive them, in our body also. The structure is same. We are very tiny, but the Lord's body, the Lord's form, is mighty, gigantic.

#### material imitation, purification, characteristics

**Key Quote:** "So this is actually affecting that, that contaminated consciousness. It is purifying the consciousness element."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how material nature imitates spiritual elements like mind and intelligence, and how chanting Hare Krishna purifies the contaminated consciousness. He notes the difficulty in distinguishing between mind, intelligence, ego, and contaminated consciousness, emphasizing the importance of recognizing them by their characteristics.

So, like spiritual mind is there, material nature imitates that and makes a material mind. Similarly, there is intelligence and material nature imitates that and stitches one material intelligence and ego. So similarly, there is this avyaktam, this is also present in our bodies. So, when we chant, Hare Kṛṣṇa, Hare Kṛṣṇa, Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa, Hare Hare, Hare Rāma, Hare Rāma, Rāma Rāma, Hare Hare, so this is actually affecting that, that contaminated consciousness. It is purifying the consciousness element. cetaha ceto dharpanam ājana But actually, it's not easy to discriminate all this mind, intelligence, ego and this contaminated consciousness. Not easy, because they all work like one unit and it is not easy. That's why Bhagavatam is saying, how you can recognize? It is saying, vṛttibhir lakṣanam By characteristics, you can recognize that yes, this is actually belonging to consciousness, belongs to consciousness.

#### purity, Krishna consciousness, three modes
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu7eHNYYyHE?t=617
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The mission of human life is to purify our existence. Now, what is this purification? The purification is purifying the consciousness from the contamination of these three modes."
**Summary:** The speaker reiterates that purity comes through chanting and spiritual practices connected to Krishna, summarizing Krishna consciousness as the topmost process for attaining purity. He defines purification as freeing consciousness from the contamination of the three modes of nature.

So, svacchattvam, the purity or the clarity comes by chanting or any spiritual method connected to Kṛṣṇa. Basically, to summarize, Kṛṣṇa conscious process, what Śrīla Prabhupāda has given, that's the topmost process to attain purity. Of course, we all know, from the beginning, the śāstra's advisors or the guru, guru, śāstra, sādhus, everybody says, the mission of human life is to purify our existence. Now, what is this purification? The purification is purifying the consciousness from the contamination of these three modes. That is called purification. The three modes obscure the consciousness. It is, Prabhupāda gives example of, like a, of course, like a glasses, having some color, colored glasses. It obscures your vision. It doesn't allow you to see the reality. It doesn't allow you to see the things as they are in this world. It doesn't allow. It obscures. What is that which is obscuring? This, sattva-raja-sthamo-guṇa. These are the contaminations.

#### three modes, forgetfulness of Krishna, remedy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu7eHNYYyHE?t=835
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains that the three modes indicate forgetfulness of Krishna, and the degree of forgetfulness determines which mode one is influenced by. He states that the solution to remove this contamination is to remember Krishna.

Let us go to little fundamentals. What is these three modes? So, we know the three modes are, the three modes indicate forgetfulness of Krishna. That means, Krishna, and the moment you forget, then, there is a modal experience. There is a three modes come into force. The, to the extent, to the degree to which you forgot, you will be pushed into that kind of a mode. Ignorance is the most drawn away from Krishna. Passion, you have little chance because there is little fire. Satva guna, you have more chance. You are little closer. Of course, from Satva guna also, you can become a demon. But, you have a, you have a great chance, because, you know, Satva guna is good, swacha, already you are in a pure state, at least within this material world. So, if you see, forgetfulness of Krishna means modes. That's the contamination. And if you want to remove that contamination, you have to add Krishna. You have to add remembrance of Krishna. This is the simplest solution. If you want to remove forgetfulness, you have to add remembrance. That's all. Very simple equation. Krishna consciousness is a simple equation.

The deepest obscured consciousness, you can see in the trees. And the highest you can see in a pure devotee. So there are a lot of, a range of consciousnesses you will perceive. The varieties you perceive, they are all in terms of the consciousness. The 84 lakh species are actually divided according to these conscious levels. So, so when a, when somebody gets attracted to very unconscious activity, or very, doesn't have the sensitivity of behaving like a gentleman, if he becomes unconscious about all these things, then he'll be, he will be cursed to become a tree like Nalakuvara and Manigriva. #### systematic study, mind, understanding
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu7eHNYYyHE?t=1305
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

If you see the dictionary definition of study, it says, by exercising the mind, you should improve your understanding. So, this is called study. There should be an exercise in the mind. If there is no exercise, then, you know, the result is very clear. We will sleep off. You know, your mind has to be active. Actually, reading is not passive. Never passive. But then, if you are not careful, then it can, you know, you can get into this kind of a, you know, you will get sleepy. So, study means you have to exercise your mind and elevate your understanding. Let us say, before reading Bhagavad Gita, your understanding is 10. After reading Bhagavad Gita, your understanding should be 11, 12, 13, 14. The more you exercise your mind, you can take your understanding to say, 14, 15, 20, whatever. You are utilizing your time very efficiently. But, you should become more and more systematic in the further study. Otherwise, we are not taking advantage of these wonderful principles that Srila Prabhupada has given us. So, don't divert, distract your attention.

#### distraction, pure consciousness, intelligence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Fu7eHNYYyHE?t=1390
**Verse References:** BG 2.41
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker connects distraction to the contamination of consciousness, defining pure consciousness as being undistracted from Krishna. He quotes Bhagavad Gita 2.41 to illustrate the difference between focused and unfocused intelligence.

And that is called avikaritvam. The property of consciousness, pure consciousness is that it is undistracted from Krishna. It is free from external distractions. But, as soon as the contamination is present, then it starts distracting. So, that distraction is explained here in the Bhagavad Gita. In the 2.41, it is said, vyavasayatmika buddhi ekeha kuru nandana. Now, pure consciousness means his buddhi, his intelligence is vyavasayatmika, fixed in only one, that is Krishna. And what is the other consciousness? The consciousness which has got a dust or contamination, bahushakahi anandhascha buddayo avyavasayana. Avyavasayana means unfocused, distracted. And what will be his life? His bahushaka, in his life there are many, many shakas of information, knowledge, many, many things are there. Actually, if you look at the material world, the activities you see, they have hundreds and thousands of bahushakahi, anandhascha, unlimited shakas of fields of expertise are there.

### 2018 - 10 -11 | SB 3. 32. 16 | HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### material attachment, fruitive activities, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3AYeWK0rQWE?t=70
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Srimad Bhagavatam criticizes persons who are too materially attached."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the topic of material attachment and how it hinders liberation, as discussed in the Srimad Bhagavatam.

In this and the following six verses, Srimad Bhagavatam criticizes persons who are too materially attached. It is enjoined in the Vedic scriptures that those who are attached to the enjoyment of material facilities have to sacrifice and undergo certain ritualistic performances. They have to observe certain rules and regulations in their daily lives to be elevated to the heavenly planets. It is stated in this verse that such persons cannot be liberated at any time. Those who worship demigods with the consciousness that each and every demigod is a separate god cannot be elevated to the spiritual world, what to speak of persons who are simply attached to duties for the upliftment of their material condition. So the chapter is titled 'Entanglement in Fruitive Activities,' and then we see how that entanglement happens in these six verses.

---
#### karma, intelligence, cause and effect
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3AYeWK0rQWE?t=138
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Somebody who knows that this world runs on the principle of cause and effect, effect is the fruit, and there must be a cause behind the fruit, is an intelligent person."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the principle of cause and effect, emphasizing that understanding this principle is a sign of intelligence, while attributing everything to chance is considered less intelligent and even demoniac.

Here it is mentioned, Prabhupada starts his purport saying that Srimad Bhagavatam criticizes the persons who are too materially attached. Such people who are attached to the material world, they do their prescribed duties very nicely and with great faith, and they daily perform all such prescribed duties, but with attachment to the fruit to result. In this world, we cannot avoid fruit. This world is designed in such a way that fruit is always there. Anybody who knows this, that means there are activities and there are fruits, actually he is an intelligent person. Somebody who knows that this world runs on the principle of cause and effect, effect is the fruit, and there must be fruit, and there must be a cause behind the fruit, is an intelligent person. People who think, he may be a great scientist, but who think that everything is happening by chance, they are actually less intelligent and in fact demoniac. So people who understand that there are, there is a cause and there is an effect, they are intelligent persons.

#### maya, illusion, mental concoction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3AYeWK0rQWE?t=675
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The whole enjoyment that everybody is running after is just a rosy picture created in the mind, that's all."
**Summary:** This segment explains how Maya creates a rosy picture of enjoyment in the mind, which is ultimately an illusion and doesn't exist in reality.

We may be living in this world, but everybody lives in their own world, Asakta Manasaha. They are fully absorbed in their world and in their world they see a rosy picture of how they can enjoy this world. The whole enjoyment that everybody is running after is just a rosy picture created in the mind, that's all. It actually doesn't exist on the floor, doesn't exist on the ground level. It's all the dreamy, rosy picture in the mind. So that's Maya. It shows you that one carrot, rosy picture in the mind is like a carrot, ajao. Take one more step, take one more step, you will be happy. You can achieve that enjoyment, whatever you are visualizing. Particularly in colorful ways, the mind visualizes the enjoyment between a male and female. Now this is the rosy picture in the mind. The rosy picture in the mind is like a carrot, ajao. Take one more step. This is the, the biggest visualization process that goes on in the mind, asatthamana saha. But actually that doesn't exist in the, the real world. What we imagine and what we get is a huge difference. That's the cheating that goes on in this world by Maya. Maya's cheating is, it is showing awareness. This is the biggest visualization process that goes on in the mind.

#### maya, illusion, disappointment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3AYeWK0rQWE?t=777
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "It is promising one crore, but it will give you one paisa, or even less than that."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a circus company to illustrate how Maya creates grand expectations that ultimately lead to disappointment, comparing it to promising a fortune but delivering almost nothing.

This is the biggest visualization process that goes on in the mind. Asatthamana saha. This is the very nice picture of the world and the enjoyment. But what actually you get it, is much less. It is promising one crore, but it will give you one paisa, or even less than that. It is like that. Of course, Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur, he gives that example, that famous example of one circus company coming to a village. And the circus company is there. And they are setting up the whole circus. Months together is going on and there's a curiosity is building up in the villages. You know, mind game. Mind is visualizing, wow, it's going to be very nice. Such a big setup. It must be very nice. Everybody has made up their minds. And then on the date of announcement, there's a huge queue. Everybody wants to enjoy. Everybody wants to experience what it is, that facilities and then enjoyment that is this promise. And then on the date of announcement, there's a huge queue. Everybody wants to enjoy. Everybody wants to experience what it is, that facilities and then enjoyment that is this promises. And then, and then that, the ticket value is very less. One paisa. And that fellow goes, the first person goes inside. There's a huge built bank guy with a big stick. So nicely beats him up. One paisa you want such big facility? Go away. So, so he comes out. And then, here is the story. Then here is the story, once he comes out, the other person is asking, hey, how is it inside? He said, I cannot describe. You have to go. You have to go inside. So like that, Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur says, how the whole village got beaten up.

#### maya, mental concoction, disappointment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3AYeWK0rQWE?t=895
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We should never believe our mind."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how the mind creates mental concoctions and rosy pictures that don't exist in reality, using the example of high divorce rates in metropolitan cities to illustrate the disappointment that follows believing in these illusions.

So material world, this Maya is like that. It creates visualizations, something is here. It mixes up. Mind is a creative element. It mixes up. You see something, you see something else. It combines both and comes up with something nice, very interesting. Actually Bhagavad Gita language for that phenomena is called concoction, mental concoction. What sort of people, they say, you are very creative, you know, you are creating nicely, but it is actually concoction. You know that decoction, there is a, you know, they mix, there is some little water, there is some powder, they do, they mix up some things and make a final decoction. So concoction is a similar derived word, which is like that. It mixes up some few things you saw here, here, here, here, and then it creates a rosy picture. That is the material world, which doesn't exist. It cannot exist in this world, because Krishna has completely certified that it is Dukkhalaya. Any promise of that kind of a rosiness or enjoyment or happiness, if you believe, then you are called Asakta Manasaha. You are now getting addicted to some picture in your mind, which doesn't exist. It is impossible that it exists. In metropolitan cities, particularly Hyderabad, there is a place where many software companies are there. And very surprisingly, they are very young, very surprisingly, most of the, many couples, they have filed divorce cases. And not only filing divorce cases, it is very, what do you say, the wife also files criminal cases on the husband and then, because, you know, they fought so bitterly. It has gone to such a very unfortunate situation, but that is the thing. So what happened to all those rosy world? What happened? What happened to all those wonderful pictures that you saw only in your mind, but, but finally you got only one paisa, and then you decided to part ways, what happened to all that?

#### mind control, devotion, loyalty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3AYeWK0rQWE?t=1065
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We should never believe our mind, Acharya says that we should never ever believe our minds and its promises."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of not trusting the mind until one reaches love of Godhead, comparing the mind to a prostitute that is never loyal and constantly shifts its focus.

So we should never believe our mind. Never believe our mind, Acharya says that we should never ever believe our minds and its promises. And to what extent, what level we should not believe our mind, till you reach love of Godhead, you cannot believe your mind. That's all. No, I am, I am some 10 years old or 20 years old, 30 years old, so now I can, little bit, I can believe word of the mind, there is no, there is no chance, till we reach love of Godhead, we can never believe our minds. So, in your mind, your, mind is compared to a vabhicharani, a prostitute. So, it will never be loyal to you. One second, it will be loyal to you. Next second, it will go and start focusing on one more object. One more object. So there is other way it jumps, you know. It is never loyal to you. One moment, you know, you may feel nice Krishna conscious thoughts are there. Yes. conscious thoughts are there and you are very happy and already some feeling is coming that yes my mind is very nice and I can depend and immediately when you shift your eyesight to something else in the temple hall as soon as the mind is capable of taking you to some other object that means it is not loyal so we should not believe our mind rather we should force our mind to always focus on mayya sattva mana pardha yogam yajna madhashaya we should only force the mind always towards Krishna mayya sattva mana so otherwise yes you see

So, the way addiction is a process, coming out of the addiction is also a process. The process will take time. Sometimes, we all coming from various backgrounds, having many habits.
