**Summary:** The speaker answers a question about dealing with anarthas (unwanted habits), explaining that the Krishna consciousness process addresses the root cause, which is the desire to enjoy and exploit the material world, rather than just treating symptoms.

And everything else will slowly fall apart. So, as you said, the intervention is very simple. It is very common for all the anarthas. It is very common for all the habits which we want to get rid of. So, should I be hankering for some process which is pinpointing to that particular anartha or habit? Or I should just depend on the common process which is being followed? The habits that we developed, previous to our coming to Krishna consciousness, they are very complex and interlinked with each other. And the foundations for all those sinful habits, Prabhupada already told, they are the four pillars. Right? That intoxication. Illicit sex. Meat eating. All those things. They are interconnected and related. And so, the habits that each one of us, we have developed previously, is not that you attack one symptom and it will all be okay. That symptom will go away. No. Because of many other interconnected other habits. Again. This will come up. For few days or few months, apparently there is a pacification. But again, there is a possibility of relapse. It will come back. So, our treatment is not symptomatic. That okay, you try to address. Put some quick fix. A little like English medicine. Something is there, just pop in one pill and then it is all over. No. So, we, the process that Krishna Consciousness, process that Shilpa Prabhupada has given, it starts working from rectifying the root. And the root cause is very simple. The root cause is our desire, like in this verse, our desire to enjoy this world. This is the root cause. Our desire to exploit this world. This is the root cause. So, once you address that, any amounts of methodologies to rectify the habit will not be successful. But then, since we are all addressing that, we are also, we are developing servitude. We are trying to serve rather than exploit. We are trying to respect rather than seeking respect. And we are trying to do paropakar rather than. Again, exploit others. You know, we are already, the medicine is working strongly.

#### identification, material nature, forgetfulness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mx_j6WbDJ7c?t=0
**Verse References:** SB 3.26.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because of his forgetfulness, the transcendental living entity accepts the influence of material energy as his field of activities, and thus actuated, he wrongly applies the activities to himself."
**Summary:** The speaker recites the verse Srimad Bhagavatam 3.26.6, its word-for-word meaning, and translation, emphasizing the living entity's forgetfulness and misidentification with material energy.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Narayanam Namaskrutya, Naranjaya Vanarottamam, Devim Sarasvatim Vyasam, Tatho Jaya Mudhiraye. Nashtapraye Shabadreshu, Nityam Bhagavata Sevaya, Bhagavati Uttama Shlokhe, Bhakti Bhavati Naishtiki. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 3, Chapter 26, Text 6: Evam Parabhidhyanena, Katrutvam Prakrutehe Pumaan, Karmasu Kriyamaneshu, Gunaihi Atmani Manyate. In this way, Para, Ada, Abhidhyanena, by identification, Kadrutvam, the performance of activities, Prakrutih, of the material nature, Puman, the living entity, Karma Shukriya Maa Nesho, while the activities are being performed, Gunaihi, by the three modes, Atmani, to himself, Manyate, he considers. Translation, purported by His Divine Grace, A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swamishloprapada. Translation, because of his forgetfulness, the transcendental living entity accepts the influence of material energy as his field of activities, and thus actuated, he wrongly applies the activities to himself.

#### Krishna consciousness, purification, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mx_j6WbDJ7c?t=274
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One becomes liberated by accepting Krishna as the Supreme Lord, as Master, and by changing the mode of consciousness from material consciousness to Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker reads Srila Prabhupada's purport, explaining how the soul in its pure state should be in Krishna consciousness and how liberation is achieved by accepting Krishna as the Supreme Lord and shifting from material to Krishna consciousness.

Purport by Eshapropaar: The forgetful living entity can be compared to a man who is under the influence of disease and has become mad or to a man haunted by ghosts who acts without control and yet thinks himself to be in control. Under the influence of material nature, the conditioned soul becomes absorbed in material consciousness. In this consciousness, whatever is done under the influence of the material energy is accepted by the conditioned soul as self-actuated. Actually, the soul is in its pure state of existence, should be in Krishna consciousness. When a person is not acting in Krishna consciousness, he is understood to be acting in material consciousness. Consciousness cannot be killed, for the symptom of the living entity is consciousness. The material consciousness simply has to be purified. One becomes liberated by accepting Krishna as the Supreme Lord, as Master, and by changing the mode of consciousness from material consciousness to Krishna consciousness. The sense of the Bhaktivedanta Parvato Madhyana Timidana Dhasya Ananjana Shalakaya Satchuran Militam Ena Tasmayashri Gurave Namah. Namah Om Vishnupādaya Krishna-Preshnaya Bhūtale Shri Mathe Bhaktivedanta Swamini-Tināminé. Namaste Sarasvate Devē Gaudhavānīm Prakādiné Nirvisahishā Shūnyavādī Pāśyātya-Dēśatādiné. Jaya Śrī-Kṛṣṇa-Caitanya Prabhu-Nityānanda.

#### material nature, identification, knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mx_j6WbDJ7c?t=427
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A devotee of the Lord is keen to understand this body and mind and this material nature all around us in order to glorify Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how Kapila Muni describes the living entity being fooled by material nature, emphasizing the bodily concept of life as the root cause of problems and the importance for devotees to understand the body, mind, and material nature to better serve Krishna.

We are understanding the fundamental principles of material nature as explained by Lord Kapila to his mother Devahuti. In the last verse we have seen how the material nature creates the varieties of forms and the Jeeva becomes illusioned by seeing these forms by Jnana Guhaya, by the knowledge covering feature. So here Kapila Muni is further explained. Kapila Muni is further explaining how the living entity is fooled in this material nature. The living entity is the main disease. The root disease is identification with this body. This is called bodily concept. Concept means idea. So bodily concept means he thinks that he is this body. He thinks. So this is called concept or idea. In the thinking what will you think? You think about ideas. So when somebody thinks that he is this body, that is Parabhidhyanena, identification. I am this body is the root cause of all the maladies, all the problems here. Then what happens when he thinks that he is this body? Katrutvam prakrute puman. Material nature is actually doing all the activities of this body. But Karmasu kriyamaneshu, but the Jeeva thinks he is doing all those activities. Gunair atmani manyate. He thinks himself as the doer. So the Parvapati is explaining in the translation, because of his forgetfulness, the transcendental living entity accepts the influence of material energy as his field of activities, and thus actuated he wrongly applies the activities. So we have to understand this fundamental principles of material nature. What is our existence? We are not this body. But we should know this body. We cannot be in ignorance of what is this body and mind. And we cannot be in ignorance of how this material nature works. A devotee of Krishna cannot be in ignorance, particularly Krishna's creation. This is all Krishna's creation. We are all his devotees. And we are in certain bodies. We are in this world. So a devotee of the Lord is keen to understand this body and mind and this material nature all around us in order to glorify Krishna. If we want to do more and more service to the Lord, then we have to understand this fundamental principles of material nature.

#### material nature, control, Kshetra
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mx_j6WbDJ7c?t=670
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Everything is done by the nature, but there are certain elements on which we can gain control."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on how material nature performs all bodily and mental activities, using the example of digestion and hunger, and addresses the question of responsibility for actions, clarifying that while nature does everything, we have some control and are not helplessly pushed.

So what is happening here? The first point is everything is done by material nature. Prakruti, Kriyamanani, Gunai, Karmaani, Sarvasaha. All the activities of the body, the body and mind are done by the material nature only. Each and every activity. Now I am saying like this. Now this is, I am thinking, I am doing this, but actually this is done by the material nature. In this body and mind, there are many things we don't even know how they are going on. For example, in the morning we had a little bit of a sweet rice after Mangala Aarti. And then, so we feel nice satisfaction. And after some time again we start feeling hungry. That means the sweet rice is digested. And then we are waiting for breakfast. As soon as we say breakfast, in the mind you will see steaming idlis and Sambha. Immediately there is a water and then there is a hunger, right? Happening. So you have no control on how the food is digested, how the hunger is generated, and you know, the how the colorful pictures appear in your mind. You have no idea. All these things are happening, done by the material nature. So for a conditioned soul who identifies with his body, everything is done by the material nature. If everything is done by material nature, let us say somebody commits a murder, if everything is done by material nature, then why he is arrested? And why he is given punishment? You can say I have not done, you know, only the material nature has done. The point is in this mind-body system, in this, this is called Kshetra. Here it is said, his field of activities. Kshetra means field of activities. So everything is done by the nature, but there are certain elements on which we can gain control. It is not that we are helplessly pushed towards some activities and we have really absolutely there is no control. No. And on the other hand, I have full control on my mind and body and everything, and that is also not a fact. No control or I have absolute control, that, these two are extremes and it's not, it's not there. That is not true. There are certain things that are given which can be controlled by us.

#### self-actuation, enjoyment, Deha and Dehi
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Mx_j6WbDJ7c?t=1185
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Activities are done by the nature, but he thinks I am seeing I'm enjoying."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how we accept material energy as our field of activity, thinking we have control and can enjoy, and how self-actuation occurs when we agree with the mind's suggestions, leading us to wrongly apply activities to ourselves, emphasizing the distinction between Deha (body) and Dehi (soul).

And then how are you accepting as your field of activities? It is part of my activity. It is a field on which I have control. It is a field on which I can enjoy. I am the Lord of all I survey within that field. This is how the soul thinks. So you are accepting the influence and you are thinking, this is my field of activities. I have control. I can enjoy. And Prabhupada is saying here, thus actuated, self-actuation. Now this sentence we have to understand. Self-actuation means what? So when the mind is suggesting, look, you are actuated. That means you accept that and then you look. So this is called self-actuation. The matter is controlling the soul. The matter is pushing the soul. You see like this, you think like this, you eat like this, you do like this. Everything it is pushing. So when it pushes, you are actuated. Yes, yes, yes. So at some level, soul is accepting. You accept the influence and say yes. As soon as the soul says yes, then automatically the eyes move and you see the object and you think, yes, I am the enjoyer. I am the controller. I know all those feelings will come. So anybody who is, so Prabhupada is saying thus actuated, he wrongly applies the activities to himself. Activities are done by the nature, but he thinks I am seeing I'm enjoying. I am eating actually, I am means the soul. Soul is not eating. The machine, the body is eating. the soul is thinking, yes I am eating. So all those activities are wrongly applied to himself. So you have to clearly see there are two things here, Deha and Dehi. They have distinct activities. Deha is fully under the control of material energy, material nature, and this Deha is controlled by it, and there is a Dehi inside, that is soul. And soul is only a knower, it knows.

So as you go on accepting the influence of the mind and the energy on us, we remain Godasa. That means whatever the mind says, whatever the senses says, we try to follow. Such people, why they are doing like that? As if they have no control. They have identified completely that I am this body, I am this mind. Whatever mind says, I have to listen to it. I am helpless. Whatever mind suggests, I have to go by it. I am helpless. So now this is not a proper thinking. This is identification with mind-body. So a devotee, once he comes into Krishna Consciousness, the first thing that he observes is, he sees his mind separate. He is able to see that, oh my mind is trying to pull me, maya, maya is trying to pull me away from Krishna. So he observes that, starts observing, right? And he comes to know that some of the, in fact in the beginning days, he is not aware of the mind, all the suggestions of the mind are completely maya. **Summary:** The speaker shares anecdotes, including one about an aviation minister who couldn't resist smoking, to illustrate the extraordinary pressure of Maya and how we become activated against our own interests by giving consent, emphasizing that we are responsible for our choices.

Third minute passed by, fourth minute passed by, he is very uncomfortable. We are thinking what happened? On one side he is nicely listening to whatever we are saying but somehow he is a little uncomfortable. And in the fifth minute he suddenly took out one cigarette and then he started smoking in front of us. That is an embarrassment that he had. That's why secretary told after two minutes you should go away because he cannot hold beyond that. He is a chain smoker. When he is travelling in the flight, he is a minister. In the flight they will tell you should not smoke in the toilet or washroom. And there are smoke detectors are there and there only he goes, the minister. He is an aviation minister. He goes into the... This thing and then he will be smoking. Why? Because this pressure of this matter on us, this maya is not ordinary. Extraordinary. Goes on. You become activated. Against your own interest you get activated and you agree to do. You give your consent. Then material nature will do everything. Once you accept and say yes, then nature will do everything. But who is responsible? You, because you gave the consent. When maya came and suggested, kavo, already you ate a few dozen rasgullas. Now seventh one is asking kavo. And let us say you say yes and automatically everything goes up and... Now who is responsible afterwards if you get vomiting or some diarrhea? You lose motion or whatever. Who is responsible? You, because you gave the permission.

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of proactively rejecting Maya's proposals, comparing Maya to someone changing outfits to tempt us, and explains that changing consciousness is the key to regaining control, and that Krishna shows special favor to sincere devotees struggling with attachments.

So a living entity has to be proactive. He cannot say, no, mera haath mein kuch bhi nahi hai. No, you have to decide what to reject. If the mind is saying, you know, you do this, you do this, you do this, you do this, you do this, you do this, you do this, you do this, you do this, you do this, you do this, you do this, you do this, you do this, you do this, you do this, you do this. A devotee, a practicing devotee should say, no I don't like this proposal, get lost. Then it will go back and colorfully, it changes itself. Maya is very tricky. For example, it comes with a saree then say, no, reject. Then it goes back and puts one more dress, and comes back and say, how is this now? You don't like it. No, get lost. Look at entrails, your roots will change. That goes on and on. How is it now? Now this looks okay. Then immediately it catches you. You want this? Okay. I will do this for you. You accepted, no? Now everything will happen. Then everything it will do. You cannot say in the end, no, no, no, actually I don't want it. You cannot say because you already gave consent to the body. Of course, sometimes the pressure of Maya is so high, the person doesn't feel that he has any freedom. You know, he is completely free. Parabhidhyana, identification is so strong, it is just drag. Animals are like that. They are completely under the control of the material nature. They have no other, they have no freedom like that. Completely. Nature says go like this. That's all. But a human being has an opportunity of this control. How? By changing his consciousness. Only by changing his consciousness, he can regain his control. Since devotee has developed Krishna consciousness, he is able to see his mind, he is able to see the proposals of the mind and he is able to say no. For example, on Ekadashi day, many devotees, they fast at least up to afternoon. Now, why he is able to fast? Now, already, it's 8.45, we are already feeling hungry, we are already looking for breakfast and this and that. On that day, Ekadashi day, 8.45 what happens? Again, we feel hungry. But, what do you do? We say no. No to that urge. Mind is proposing, kao. Then, you are saying, no, today I am fasting. This is called tapas. Power of tapas a living entity can transcend the influence of this metal energy by the Krishna conscious tapas you can say no I will not but let us say despite knowing this philosophy despite knowing you know what is good for him a devotee is not able to give up these attachments then Krishna is watching he is seeing that because of this parabhidhyanena this boy this living entity is not able to give up the attachment then it is said that if that devotee is sincere then what Krishna does is he shows special favor in order to take him out of the that attachment because on his own he is not able to because of the parabhidhyanena identification is very strong he is not able to come out so at that time Krishna shows a very special favor.

#### conditional life, freedom, material consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4gqMho82fRQ?t=647
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is the freedom that the spirit soul has, not only to Narada Muniji, this freedom is possible to each one of us."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the difference between conditional life, where one is forced to follow many conditions, and the freedom of exalted devotees like Narada Muni, who can travel anywhere freely, emphasizing that this freedom is potentially available to everyone.

That he is a product of this world, he is connected to this world in any way, and then that life he goes through is called samsruti, that means conditional life. What is the meaning of conditional life? That means he will be forced to follow a lot of conditions. This is conditional life. For example, Narada Muni, an exalted devotee, parampara acharya, if he decides to go to moon planet, he can very easily go there. He wants to travel to any other planet or any other universe, in and out of the spiritual world, anywhere freely can move. This is the freedom that the spirit soul has, not only to Narada Muniji, this freedom is possible to each one of us. It is a desire of the living entity to see the creation of God because we are all part and parcels of Krishna. There is a natural tendency that we want to see the various aspects of the creation of Krishna. That is there. We want to go here, there, here, there, but that travel is very easy and full of freedom for the devotees like Narada Muni because they are free from this material consciousness. They are free from any of the false identification with this world. This is the freedom of the living entity.

#### conditional life, false identification, suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4gqMho82fRQ?t=756
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The deepest root cause for all our suffering is to identify with this body, to think that I am this body."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the freedom of devotees with the restrictions imposed on those attached to the material world, explaining that the root cause of suffering is identifying with the body and its connections, leading to limited conceptions and misery.

This is the situation of such exalted devotees, but if we want to go to any other planet, then there will be a lot of conditions imposed on us. You know, for example, Narada Muni, he can decide and then he goes into the sky and then reaches the moon planet. And if you want to go like that, and three feet you will go and then you'll break your back. Next two, three years you have to recover from that. So this is called condition. So you cannot just, you know, decide and then go to any planet or wherever you want to go. We should know the difference. Internally, the soul feels that it is free and decide anything, wants to fly, wants to go, jump, dance. At the heart, the Jiva, here it is mentioned, is actually such a Dhananda, is full of bliss, always wants to jump, dance, roll, fly, move, you know, wherever it wants to go, you know, there is no limitation. But then because of the material consciousness, how this material consciousness begins? It begins by thinking that I am this body. The deepest root cause for all our suffering is to identify with this body, to think that I am this body. Everything connected to this body is mine, my family, my parents, my society, my caste, my state, my country, or you may say my human society, why not animal society? So all these conceptions are partial, they are limited, and any limited conception will bring misery.

**Summary:** The speaker contrasts limited material conceptions with the complete picture of Krishna as the maintainer of all, explaining that identifying as a servant of Krishna frees one from illusion. He then describes how conditioned souls build 'castles in the sky' with material desires, illustrating the nature of illusion.

One should have the complete picture. Partial picture, limited conception will bring misery. One should have the complete picture leads to misery reaction. What is the complete picture? Krishna is the leader and maintainer of all living entities. He is the, I belong to Krishna. I doesn't belong to this world in any way. Actually, by nature, I am supposed to be completely free. So a living entity when he starts identifying himself as servant of Krishna then he becomes free from all the Maya and the illusions which are harassing him slowly gets cleared. So that's why, so but otherwise a living entity goes on dreaming in this world. I am going to be like this. I am going to be like that. This this is called Gandharva Pura, that means he builds castles in the sky. There is a theory that you imagine whatever you want and you will become that. So the Jeeva, they tries to imagine that he he has a big castle and wonderful first-class interiors and white milky, I can see the furniture and wonderful spacious bed and all kinds of facilities, latest technology, my own home theater. I can see any movie that I want and has all kinds of sound systems. And when I'm watching, there are so many servants, they come and give me that popcorn so I can go on eating and watching all those, my own. I don't want to go to outside theater and all that. The theater should be inside my house. Going outside the theater, that's it. And then all those hundreds and thousands of employees should be there. And I am the employer. I am the employer. I'll maintain all those families. Then I should have many dogs, cats, some, what to say, some animals. And so then, obviously, cars, if possible, my own jet. This is the casual he builds.

#### Krishna Leela, false philosophy, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4gqMho82fRQ?t=1250
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Nobody can become God. Nobody can become Krishna, Narayana."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies the true meaning of 'Leela' as engagement in Krishna's activities, contrasting it with the dangerous interpretation that suffering is Leela. He refutes the Mayavadi philosophy that one can become God, emphasizing the importance of recognizing Krishna's transcendental nature.

So, actually Leela means, Prabhupada is saying, our employment in the activities of the Lord. This is called Leela. If we are becoming part of Krishna's activities, that is Krishna Leela. You are also becoming part of that. Not this Prabhupada is saying that stool eating the hog. A living entity can become, and then if you say he is enjoying his pastimes, a most dangerous interpretation. See, generally when there is a lot of suffering, the living entities, they question, what is this? Is there any solution to this? There is some chance that it may come to Krishna consciousness. But if you give the philosophy, that no, no, no, this suffering and all is not there. It's just your Leela, that's all. It's a pastime. And as soon as you become as, you know, God, then, you know, all this suffering, everything is removed. It will go away. So, your goal is to become God. This is what they encourage. But actually, that is the most dangerous interpretation. Nobody can become God. Nobody can become Krishna, Narayana. But these Mayavadis, what they do, they try to bring down the activities of Krishna to their level.

#### offenses, Krishna Leela, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4gqMho82fRQ?t=1380
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Applying our own material concepts onto Guru and Krishna is the biggest offense."
**Summary:** The speaker warns against applying material concepts to Guru and Krishna, emphasizing that it is a grave offense that hinders spiritual progress. He stresses the importance of seeing the spiritual master as non-different from Krishna and offering appropriate respect.

Applying our own material concepts onto Guru and Krishna is the biggest offense. A living entity, it is continuously suffering with his material concepts. The prominent being, I am this body. And if we extrapolate, we apply all these concepts to Guru and Krishna, then we'll be misled. If the understanding is that Prabhupada, the spiritual master, is non-different from Krishna. He is as good as Krishna. One should offer respect in that level. Treating Prabhupada as good as Krishna in terms of the respect. But the moment you think that Guru is an ordinary person, you will not be able to give such respect. That's why it becomes an offense in chanting Hare Krishna. Anybody who thinks that Guru is an ordinary person, it becomes an offense. His chanting will go. So that's why one should be very careful not to extend material concepts to Guru and Krishna. One has to see them, that they are transcendental. They are beyond all these activities that I said. You can see all around, day after day. So there is a Prabhupada saying here, the past tense of the Lord cannot be dragged to the level of the conditioned activities of the living entity. You cannot drag it to our level. So either Krishna is eating, stealing butter, eating butter, and running in fear. All these activities may look like an ordinary child's activity of this world. But you cannot drag them to our level. In our consciousness, we should never ever apply our own concepts to Krishna Leela or Prabhupada's Leela.

We cannot understand the spiritual master. For example, in the final manifest days of Prabhupada, Prabhupada's body was completely emaciated. You know, he became kind of a bony. The skin is touching the bones. It's a very painful situation. And in that situation, according to our calculation, you know, he, people cannot function properly. According to our calculation. But Prabhupada, as an Acharya, he knows our calculations. And then he teaches. Even at that time. Just one day before also, he's dictating Bhagavatam purports. And he's explaining what is Krishna Consciousness. He says everything is moving. Everything is working. Everything is maintained by the supreme will of Krishna. This is Krishna Consciousness. #### Krishna consciousness, materialism, supreme controller
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/4gqMho82fRQ?t=1616
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If somebody can see that Krishna is the supreme controller, every smallest thing is also happening by his will only. This is called Krishna Consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of moving a table to explain that matter cannot move on its own, implying a supreme controller. He quotes Krishna from Bhagavad Gita to support the idea that Krishna controls both moving and non-moving entities, emphasizing that seeing Krishna as the supreme controller is Krishna consciousness.

This matter cannot move on its own. For example, this table has come here. This seat has come here because somebody has shifted from that corner to here. And after the class is over again, this table has to be shifted again. So the living entity is needed to move the matter. Now, there's such a big matter is there. Whole universe is. That's all matter only. Now, are you moving? Mayavadi will say yes. But actually a sane person, I'm talking about normal man, not mad person. All Mayavadis are mad people. They say I am moving the sun. I am moving the star. I am moving the moon and all that. Are we moving? We're not moving. But then which living entity is moving this such a big matter? Logically you should analyze also. Then you will realize there should be a a personality. And that personality is the supreme personality of God. And Krishna says, mayadhyakshena prakruti suyatesa chara chara chara and achara moving and non-moving living entities which are produced by this material nature is controlled by me, the supreme controller. So Prabhupada says, if somebody can see that Krishna is the supreme controller, every smallest thing is also happening by his will only. This is called Krishna Consciousness. But then people, if some suffering comes to them, they become focused on the suffering and reliving that suffering. But behind that they don't see there is Krishna. There is a loving Krishna. Then they can see that no one is playing the role of Krishna. No one can see that Krishna is the supreme controller. No one can see that Krishna is the supreme controller. But only a small amount of Krishna can see that Krishna is the supreme controller.

He emphasizes that a pure devotee sees only Krishna because they understand that nothing happens without the Lord's will, removing all illusion.

These three gunas and who is that he is Krishna. ### Can Vedic Knowledge be Proven by Modern Science? | Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### Vedic knowledge, proof, authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/sarYJxz5hv0?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "When any Vedic statement is made, that itself is an evidence. There is no need of any proof."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that the modern mind is diseased because it seeks scientific proof for Vedic statements, which is not the proper way to understand Vedic knowledge, as it comes directly from Vyasadeva and is self-evident.

The modern mind is so diseased. When we hear something, we immediately try to find a proof for it from the scientific field or some other field. We try to bring some arguments to prove this Vedic statement. No, that is not the proper way of understanding this Vedic knowledge. So, Vyasadeva, who is an incarnation of Krishna, he himself has given this knowledge. I came across one theorem in mathematics. It is called Godel's theorem of incompleteness. One mathematician proved that mathematics is not complete and consistent. So, mathematicians themselves proved that mathematics is incomplete. So, by holding on to the incomplete field, how can you learn conclusive truths? That's why such fields of approach to knowledge are compared to holding on to the dog's tail to cross the ocean of science, whereas Prabhupada's books and knowledge are complete and consistent. Now, you can see the value. Prabhupada says, "These are not my books. These are written by Vyasadeva. This knowledge is coming from Vyasadeva." When any Vedic statement is made, that itself is an evidence. There is no need of any proof.

### Cittam to "Sense Control" | SB 3.26.12 | HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### subtle senses, consciousness, false ego
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SNa3AJfvJX4?t=388
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Consciousness is the function of the soul, and therefore behind consciousness there is soul."
**Summary:** This segment explains the four internal subtle senses (mind, intelligence, ego, and contaminated consciousness), their characteristics, and how material contamination pollutes pure consciousness leading to false ego.

The internal subtle senses are experienced as having four aspects in the shape of mind, intelligence, ego, and contaminated consciousness. Distinctions between them can be made only by different functions since they represent different characteristics. The four internal senses or subtle senses described herein are defined by different characteristics. When pure consciousness is polluted by material contamination, and when identification with the body becomes prominent, one is said to be situated under false ego. Consciousness is the function of the soul, and therefore behind consciousness there is soul. Consciousness pervades, polluted by material contamination, is called ahankara.

---
#### elements of matter, senses, subtle body
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SNa3AJfvJX4?t=516
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The living entity thinks that these senses are mine, these are my senses, but actually these senses are all matter and inside this body there is the spirit soul which is the living entity."
**Summary:** This segment describes the 24 elements of matter (five gross elements, five subtle elements, ten senses, and four internal senses), emphasizing that the senses are material while the soul has its own spiritual senses covered by matter.

Original substance, original matter is in the form of pradhana and in the pradhana there are 24 elements that are there but they are dormant, they are not visible. And what are those 24? That enumeration is given in these three verses. The first verse is talking about there are five gross elements and they are known as bhū, āpa, agni, maruta, nabha. These are the names of those gross elements and our body is made up of these mahābhūtāni, these five gross elements. But there are subtle elements also, they are known as tanmātra, tanmātrāni. There are also five, smell, taste, color, touch and sound. These are also matter only but they are subtle. And these tanmātrāni are recognized as the subtle elements. These are known as tanmātra, tanmātrāni. There are also five, smell, taste, color, touch and sound. These are also matter only but they are recognized by or measured by indriyāṇī daśastrotrā. There are five indriyas for knowledge acquisition. Here it is said indriyā means sense. Here it is mentioned the sense of seeing. We have a sense of seeing, sense of smell and there is a sense of, it's called text. A tactile sense, sense, we can recognize the touch and then there is a sense of taste and a sense of hearing. There are five senses which, which can measure these five subtle elements, tanmātrāni. And then also there are five working senses. A argan for speaking, hmm, so, And then there are argan for working. Then traveling, generating and evacuating. These are the five senses. So, total there's ten senses, five knowledge-acquiring senses and five working senses. All are matter. Hmm. But the living entity, he thinks that these senses are mine, these are my senses, but actually these senses are all matter and inside this body there is the spirit soul which is the living entity and inside that living entity that soul has its own spiritual senses. Soul is not Narakaar or Jyothi, it has its senses, soul has, soul is experiencing, it has its own senses, but those senses are covered by this matter senses, senses made up of matter, so this is twenty, five gross elements, five tanmatrani, ten, then ten senses, twenty, then comes four, mano buddhi rahankara chittam, there are four internal senses, they are known as mind, intelligence, ego and then chittam. Chittam, here it is translated as mind.

#### false ego, sense gratification, love, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SNa3AJfvJX4?t=1771
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If that senses can be engaged for the satisfaction of Krishna then that is called love and only through that love this hunger can be handled."
**Summary:** This segment explains how the soul's faculties are covered by material counterparts, leading to false ego and a race for sense gratification, but that engaging the senses in Krishna's service transforms this into love, satisfying the senses' hunger.

#### material energy, conditioned souls, modes of nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2dxdhsZPvsg?t=274
**Verse References:** BG 7.12
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Material energy has the power to cover knowledge, but this covering cannot be applied to the Supreme Personality of Godhead."
**Summary:** This segment explains how material energy covers knowledge, affecting conditioned souls but not the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and discusses the influence of the modes of nature.

PURPORT BY ASHRAPRAVAPA. Material energy has the power to cover knowledge, but this covering cannot be applied to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. It is applicable only to the Praja or those who are born with material bodies, the conditioned souls. The different kinds of living entities vary according to the modes of material nature, as explained in Bhagavad Gita and other Vedic literature. In Bhagavad Gita 7.12 it is very nicely explained that although the modes of goodness, passion and ignorance are born of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, He is not subject to them. In other words, the energy emanating from the Supreme Personality of Godhead is not subject to them. the lord is the father of all living entities because he impregnates material energy with the conditioned souls therefore the conditioned souls get bodies created by the material energy whereas the father of the living entities is aloof from the three modes. It is stated in the previous verse that the material energy was accepted by the supreme personality of godhead in order that he might exhibit pastimes for the living entities who wanted to enjoy and lord it over the material energy.

---
#### material world, enjoyment, rebellion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2dxdhsZPvsg?t=377
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This world was created through the material energy of the lord for the so-called enjoyment of such living entities why this material world was created for the sufferings of the conditioned souls is a very intricate question."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the purpose of the material world, created for the enjoyment of conditioned souls, and the question of why suffering exists, linking it to the Lord's pastimes and the rebellious nature of some living entities.

This world was created through the material energy of the lord for the so-called enjoyment of such living entities why this material world was created for the sufferings of the conditioned souls is a very intricate question there is a hint in previous verse in the word li laya which means for the pastimes of the lord the lord wants to rectify the enjoying temperament of the conditioned souls. It is stated in bhagavad gita that no one is the enjoyer but the supreme personality of godhead this material energy is created therefore for anyone who pretends to enjoy. There is no necessity but at the same time there is a necessity similarly there was no necessity to create this material world for the sufferings of the conditioned souls but at the same time there are certain entities known as Nityabaddha were eternally conditioned. We say that they have been conditioned from time immemorial because no one can trace out when the living entity, the part and parcel of the Supreme Lord, became rebellious against the supremacy of the Lord.

---
#### obedience, atheism, conditioned souls, material nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2dxdhsZPvsg?t=471
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is a fact that there are two classes of men, those who are obedient to the laws of the Supreme Lord, and those who are atheists or agnostics who do not accept the existence of the God and who want to create their own laws."
**Summary:** The lecture contrasts those obedient to the Lord's laws with atheists, discusses the origin of conditioned souls, and explains how they are influenced by the three modes of material nature, leading to forgetfulness of their spiritual identity.

It is a fact that there are two classes of men, those who are obedient to the laws of the Supreme Lord, and those who are atheists or agnostics who do not accept the existence of the God and who want to create their own laws. They want to establish that everyone can create his own laws or his own religious path. Without tracing out the beginning of the existence of these two classes, we can take it for granted that some of the living entities revolted against the laws of the Lord. Such entities are not the same as the living entities. They are called conditioned souls, but they are conditioned by the three modes of material nature. Therefore, the words Unayarvichitra are used here. In this material world, there are 84 lakh species of life. As spirit souls, they are all transcendental to this material world. Why then do they exhibit themselves in different stages of life? The answer is given here. They are under the spell of the spirit. They are under the spell of the three modes of material nature. Because they were created by the material energy, their bodies are made of the material elements created by the material body. For recovered by the material body, the spiritual identity is lost and therefore the word Mumuhe is used here, indicating that they have forgotten their own spiritual identity. This forgetfulness of spiritual identity is not the same as the spiritual identity of the Jeevas or souls who are conditioned, being subject to be covered by the energy of the material nature. Jnana Guhaya is another word used. Guha means covering. Because the knowledge of the minute conditioned souls is covered, they are exhibited in so many species of life.

---
#### illusion, modes of nature, material body, Katha Upanishad
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2dxdhsZPvsg?t=585
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As they are forgetful of their spiritual identities, they think the material body is to be themselves to the conditioned soul, Mee means the material body."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on how living entities are illusioned by material energy, covered by different modes, and identify with their material bodies, referencing the Katha Upanishad to emphasize that the Supreme Personality of Godhead is unaffected by material nature.

It is said in the Srimad Bhagavatam, 7th chapter, 1st canto, the living entities are illusioned by the material energy. In the Vedas also. It is stated that eternal living entities are covered by different modes and they are called tricolored, red, white and blue living entities. Red is the representation of the mode of passion, white is the representation of mode of goodness and blue is the representation of mode of ignorance. These modes of material nature belong to the material energy and therefore the living entities under these different modes of material nature have different kinds of material bodies. As they are forgetful of their spiritual identities, they think the material body is to be themselves to the conditioned soul, Mee means the material body. This is called Moha or bewildered man. It is repeatedly said in the Katha Upanishad that the Supreme Personality of Godhead is never affected by the influence of material nature. It is rather the conditioned souls or the minute infinitesimal parts and parcels of the Supreme – who are affected by the influence of material nature that are known in the Supreme employee planes of material nature and who appear in different bodies under the different material modes. The Sanstha Bhaktivedanta Parvato Madhyana Timuranda Asya Gyananjana Shalakaya Yaksharun Militam Ena Tasmaya Shri Gurave Namaha. Namah Om Vishnupadhaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Shri Mate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamini. Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvisesha Shunyavadi Pashyatya Deshatarine. Jai Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Shri Advaita Gadhadhara Shiva Sadhi Gauravhaktavrunda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare.

#### liberation, material nature, three modes, variegatedness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2dxdhsZPvsg?t=712
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So what is binding us to this world is this Gunair. Vichitra. These are the three modes of material nature."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the topic of liberation from material nature, explaining that the three modes (Gunair Vichitra) are what bind us to this world and that understanding their influence is the first step to freedom.

Lord Kapila is explaining about the material nature, about the three modes to her mother Devahuti. The topic is about liberation. The mother asks about the liberation from this material world. So what is binding us to this world is this Gunair. Vichitra. These are the three modes of material nature. So if one wants to come out of the influence of these three modes, first of all, he has to understand how they are influencing us. So these three modes Gunair, Vichitra, Vichitra means variegated. So the energy which is working here in this world. This world is called material energy. But it is not working uniformly. It is working in three Vichitra, three types, three varieties. That means in Satva Guna, Rajya Guna and Dhamma Guna. Actually nobody will have one Guna like that. Purely it's generally a combination of all these three Gunas. So, under this influence of the three modes, Vichitra, there is a variegatedness. Srujateem, the creation is done in such a way that there is a variegatedness.

**Key Quote:** "There are two types one type is knowledge covering and one more is pulling it pulls you you may be thinking no no I will not fall for nano banana."
**Summary:** The speaker describes two features of illusion: knowledge covering and a 'pulling' feature that entices individuals with new trends and technologies, even when they are aware of the potential for time wasting.

There are two types one type is knowledge covering and one more is pulling it pulls you you may be thinking no no I will not fall for nano banana this will be I know this trends you decided you know what is wrong with it for hours will go five hours will go why I should waste my time on this because once you know your picture is like it and all those things are over that is over now nobody will see that picture so again you have to spend so much time again three four hours on it so somebody will realize that no no no it's a lot of time is getting wasted I will not fall for this but in the next instant I will there is a pulling feature of this illusion ok, no, Gilby is not attractive to you, fine here is nano banana and tomorrow nano banana will go away, some other banana will come and so continuously the living entity is enticed see look, here you have failed, don't worry here is a more powerful technology you are not looking so well here that's fine here you will look even more charming why don't you try this tool this is called that pulling it pulls you down and then after that covers your knowledge you know, it's like a hypnotic influence.

Shlokas also we have read if somebody becomes let us say comes in front of the Lord and takes Darshan And somehow by seeing the charming form of the Lord, he becomes absorbed in that darshan. He is seeing Krishna, he is seeing Radharani, he is seeing all the beautiful forms, the dress and all that. Actually the shloka says that living entity is liberated. Just by having some intense darshan, you are coming to a liberated blood. Can you believe this? Just by having darshan of the Lord, you are liberated. You are not under the influence of the three modes. Liberation means you have gone beyond the three modes. That's the stage. But because we are conditioned, we are still sadhakas. After seeing the deity, we are so happily mesmerized by all that. And again when you turn around, and you see somebody else, immediately we get charmed, attracted by that form. Even though he may be appearing as, you know, part of this material world. For example, deity is apparently made out of material elements. It is apparently part of this world. But actually, after the installation is done, the Lord is present. What we are seeing is directly the Supreme Lord. And the devotees can experience that liberation when they have darshan of a beautiful form of the Lord. And so, it is concluded here that Krishna cannot be under the modes. All the three modes are coming from Krishna's energy only, but Krishna is not under the influence of those three modes. This is very important. That means, as we go on thinking of Krishna, we can be experiencing a liberated life.

#### equality, Lord Shiva
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2VOyMSQ_Wgo?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Shiva has no equal in the material world in regard to equality towards everyone."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the concept of Aprati Purusham, meaning one who has no equal, and applies it to Lord Shiva's impartiality.

The word Aprati Purusham used in this verse means one who has no equal. Lord Shiva has no equal in the material world in regard to equality towards everyone.

---
#### desire, anger, suicide
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2VOyMSQ_Wgo?t=17
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This distressed her more than she could tolerate and she looked at her husband as if she were ready to blast him with her vision."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Sati's distress at being denied permission to visit her father's house, her anger towards Lord Shiva, and the implication that she contemplated suicide.

His wife Sati knew that her husband was equal towards everyone. So why in this case was he so unkind to his wife that he did not allow her to go to her father's house? This distressed her more than she could tolerate and she looked at her husband as if she were ready to blast him with her vision. In other words, since Lord Shiva is the Atma, Shiva also means Atma, it is indicated here that Sati was prepared to commit suicide.

---
#### persuasion, relationships, gender dynamics
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2VOyMSQ_Wgo?t=54
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Sati took shelter of a woman's last weapon, weeping, which forces a husband to agree to the proposal of his wife."
**Summary:** The speaker humorously describes Sati's use of weeping as a 'weapon' to persuade Lord Shiva, highlighting the dynamics in a husband-wife relationship.

Another meaning of the word Aprati Purusha is the personality who has no rival. Since Lord Shiva could not be persuaded to give her permission, Sati took shelter of a woman's last weapon, weeping, which forces a husband to agree to the proposal of his wife. That is the sense of the Bhaktivedanta Parva. So a man may invent a powerful weapon like this. This is a very powerful weapon. A nuclear weapon. But back at home, the wife is having a much more powerful weapon called weeping, which will force a husband to agree to the proposal. So here the whole issue is the proposal.

---
#### desire, anger, prevention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2VOyMSQ_Wgo?t=101
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So whenever anger comes, it means that some proposal or some desires of ours are Pratighatha, prevented."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that anger arises when one's desires or proposals are thwarted, using the Sanskrit term 'Pratighatha' to describe this prevention.

Sati has a proposal to her great husband, Aprati Purusham. And Lord Shiva has not accepted that proposal. Suhrudhrudruksha. She wants to see her relatives, that is the desire. But Pratighatha, she was prevented. Now once the desires are prevented, we all know the result will be Rusha, anger. So whenever anger comes, it means that some proposal or some desires of ours are Pratighatha, prevented.

---
#### introspection, desire, sorrow
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2VOyMSQ_Wgo?t=161
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Whenever we get angry, we should introspect and see what is that desire or proposal that I have made, which was prevented from fulfilling."
**Summary:** The speaker advises introspection when anger arises, to identify the unfulfilled desire or proposal that caused it, leading to feelings of sorrow or 'Durmana'.

So whenever we get angry, we should introspect and see what is that desire or proposal that I have made, which was prevented from fulfilling. So Pratighatha Durmana. So as soon as she was prevented from fulfilling her desire to see the relatives, then Durmana, she started feeling sorry in the mind. This is a little more detailed description of what happens when a desire is not fulfilled.

#### desire, identification, mind

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

So, everything starts with a desire. And her desire is to enjoy the company of the relatives and to see them in a certain function at the father's house. So as soon as that was forbidden, Pratighata, prevented, she became very angry. Actually, in Sadhana stage, we should not identify with the desires that crops up in the system, in the mind. So, it is important that we should understand how to manage our desires, how to manage our ideas and proposals. So,

### Disassocating from the Influence of Past Conditioning | Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa | SB 6.9.23-25
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### prayer, protection, divine intervention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lLyEqGSVdx4?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Thus, we also expect the Lord to protect us from this dangerous condition."
**Summary:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa begins by chanting and reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 6, Chapter 9, Texts 23-25, focusing on the Lord's protection in past situations and appealing for similar protection now.

OM NAMO BHAGAVATE VASUDEVAYA. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 6, Chapter 9, and Texts 23, 24 and 25. I will read the first two verses myself. Text 23: The Manu, named King Satyavrata, formally saved himself by tying the small boat of the entire world to the horn of the Masya Avatara, the Fish Incarnation. By the grace of the Masya Avatara, Manu saved himself from the great danger of the flood. May that same Fish Incarnation save us from the great danger. May that same Fish Incarnation save us from the great and fearful danger caused by the son of Tvasta. Text 24: In the beginning of creation, a tremendous wind caused fierce waves of inundating water. Text 25: In the beginning of creation, a tremendous wind caused fierce waves of inundating water. The great waves made such a horrible sound that Lord Brahma almost fell from his seat and the lotus into the water of devastation, but he was saved with the help of the Lord. Thus, we also expect the Lord to protect us from this dangerous condition.

---
#### illusion, perception, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lLyEqGSVdx4?t=127
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We are all conditioned to various degrees, but we think we are God. This is why we cannot understand who God is or see Him face to face."
**Summary:** The speaker recites Text 25 and its translation and purport by A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada, explaining why conditioned souls cannot see the Supreme Personality of Godhead, emphasizing the illusion of considering oneself God.

Text 25: Ya Eka Eesho Nijamaya Ya Naham Sasarjaya Nanu Shujama Vishvam Vayamnaya Syaapipurah Sameeha Taha Pashyamalingam Prudhagisham Anina. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swamishya Prabhupada. Translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead who created us by His external potency, and by whose mercy we expand the creation of the universe, is always situated before us as the Supersoul, but we cannot see His form. We are unable to see Him because all of us think that we are separate and independent gods. Purport by Shri Prabhupada: Here is an explanation of why the conditioned soul cannot see the Supreme Personality of Godhead, face to face. Even though the Lord appears before us as Lord Krishna or Lord Ramachandra and lives in human society as a leader or king, the conditioned soul cannot understand Him. Abhajananti Mamudha Manushim Tanumashritam. Rascals, mudhas, deride the Supreme Personality of Godhead, thinking Him an ordinary human being. However, insignificantly, the Lord appears before us as Lord Krishna, and we think that we are also God, that we can create a universe that we can create, or that we can create another God. This is why we cannot see or understand the Supreme Personality of Godhead. In this regard, Shri Madhvacharya says, Lingam eva pashyamaha kadachit abhimanastu. Devanam apisanyava prayaha kaleshu nastya va taratam yena sopitu. We are all conditioned to various degrees, but we think we are God. This is why we cannot understand who God is or see Him face to face. The sense of the Bhaktivedanta Prabhupada.

---
#### context, prayer, dependence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lLyEqGSVdx4?t=282
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Although the Lord is before us in the form of the Supersoul, we are not able to see Him because we think that we are also an independent controller."
**Summary:** The speaker provides context for the verses, explaining that the demigods are offering prayers to the Supreme Lord in a helpless situation, acknowledging that although the Lord is present as the Supersoul, they cannot perceive Him due to their own sense of independent control.

So we are reading from the chapter title, The Appearance of Godhead, The Appearance of the Demon Vrutrasura. And in this chapter, we have seen a few shlokas back that Demon Vrutrasura indeed makes His appearance. And so the demigods, looking at His power, they become bewildered, and in a helpless situation, they are offering their prayers to the Supreme Lord. So here, they are mentioning some examples of how Krishna or the Lord has protected His devotees previously. So they are appealing to the Lord to protect them similarly this time. And so, here, in the last shloka, they are mentioning, although the Lord is before us in the form of the Supersoul, we are not able to see Him. We are not able to understand or not able to see Him because pashyama lingam prudha-eeshamanina eeshamanina because we think that we are also an independent controller. So that is the context.

#### intelligence, universal intelligence, mystery
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lLyEqGSVdx4?t=366
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "How is it that the smallest, the living entity that we see, is exhibiting such a powerful intelligence, and skill? This is a big mystery for the scientists."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a detailed story about scientists researching ants and discovering their incredible intelligence, such as breaking coriander seeds into four parts to prevent germination and performing complex surgeries with a high success rate, highlighting the mystery of such advanced skills in tiny creatures.

Actually, nowadays, looks like there is a lot of research going on, about ants. You know, the scientists are interested to understand what the ants are doing. And, you know, generally, ants, I quoted this example in one of the classes also, the ants, they save the grains for the rainy day, you know, for this season. So, when the scientists are analyzing, they understood that whenever an ant gets a grain, it divides that into two and keeps them into their godown. Ants have godowns, storekeepers, everything is there. So then they were very curious, why is the ant breaking the grain into two? Then they figured out that if the grain is broken, then it will not, you know, germinate. It will not become a seedling, a small plant. So, that's why the ants break the seeds, so that it won't grow into some plant or something and they miss the food. So that's going on. And meanwhile, they saw there is one coriander seed. And this time, the ant has broken the coriander seed into four parts. And they were surprised. Why is this? Other seeds are only two. Now it is broken into four. So they started analyzing. They found that coriander seed, if you break them into two, still seedling is possible. It can germinate and become a small, small seedlings. So that's why the ant knows that this is a coriander seed. And if I break only into two, then, you know, it will not, this, it is not okay. So it breaks them into four. And then the story, in a rehearsal, the scientists are blown off. How is it that they are so intelligent? We also don't know that coriander seed. Ants have such an intelligence. Even scientists are bewildered. Then, the story goes one step further. Then they found, one day they saw one of the ants, fellow ants, got injured. In the day-to-day existence, they have very fragile legs. And, you know, whole body is very small. So then, one of the fellow ants came to this ant and found that, that fragile leg, something gone wrong. And then, in front of the eyes of the scientists, that ant has performed a surgery and amputated that leg of the ant, that fellow ant, which got injured. So now the scientists are blown off. They are thinking, only human society has surgeons. Here is a much more skilled surgeon. In fact, they found that the fellow ant doesn't do operations every time. First it analyzes whether it can be cured by some medicine. If not, then it does a skilled operation and removes that little portion. They found that 90% success rate in the surgery. Other cases, probably they wouldn't have seen. 100%. Nowadays, I mean, not all doctors, but some doctors, you go with any problem, they immediately say, surgery. Cut it. But ant is not saying, first it will analyze whether medicine is okay. And then, if not, then do the surgery. How is it that the smallest, the living entity that we see, is exhibiting such a powerful intelligence, and skill? This is a big mystery for the scientists.

#### universal intelligence, dependence, creation

Because, in human society, if somebody has to become a surgeon, first five years of MBBS, and before that you have to cross NEET, that examination, and then five years of MBBS, and then after that, there is a specialization. You know, you get, you become a surgeon. That is the, so, just to develop such a talent and skill, in the human society we see, it takes so many years, but here we are seeing, in the ant society, very, that kind of skilled operations they are able to do, and getting a 90% success rate. They were shocked. So, there is, this universal intelligence. They, they don't want to accept, but, that indicates to, the presence of, a universal intelligence. So, here, the demigods, are appearing, appealing to, that universal intelligence, Krishna, in the form of the, super soul. The Lord, who is giving the, intelligence to that ant, is the Lord in the heart, and, he is the super soul, and he knows, how to guide that living entity. So, that's why here, they are saying. Actually, is creating, is maintaining, and is destroying. We are also creating but, means, following, the direction of the, original creator, we are also creating, and expanding the creation, but, we are actually, completely dependent on, the supreme creator, that is, Krishna. So, one has to understand that there is an energy which is actually working in this world.

Similarly, once I was coming from Vijayawada to Hyderabad and then slowly I saw that the driver is doing like this, so I got scared. So I offered to him, you know, if you want you can take coffee tea or something, basically if he feels inhibited. So I offered to him, you know, if you want you can take coffee tea or something. He was inhibited, hesitant because Swamiji is seated behind and how can I ask coffee tea? No problem, you please ask, otherwise you will ask my life. Coffee tea is okay, much better. So he went there and then our car was parked next to some bushes and then it is just close to my window, so just I happened to see there is one interesting bird, not bird, there is a small animal, maybe squirrel type and you know it is moving around and it is doing something and at one point suddenly the squirrel became very, what do you say, alert, stunned and all that. I thought probably it has found me or something like that but actually one big bird just zoomed into the bush to catch this squirrel. Tuck! the squirrel disappeared. It just disappeared into the bushes and then there is some commotion and finally the bird went away, unsuccessful. How does the squirrel know that this bird is my enemy? Even sometimes we human beings don't know who is our enemy. How did this little squirrel know and that bird looked very unusual to me, not seen ordinarily. We see, you know, regular, the birds, you know. Peacocks, you know, some of those parrots and all those things. This looks a little medium sized, some colors, very unusual bird and when that came, it understood that yes, this is my enemy. Immediately defense mechanisms, protection, everything came into picture and then it has protected itself. One should see this tremendous intelligence which is all pervading in this universe.

**Summary:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa uses the example of ants performing surgery and a squirrel evading a predator to illustrate the presence of a universal intelligence guided by Krishna.

So we are reading from the chapter titled The Appearance of the Demon, Phritharasura. And in this chapter we have seen few shlokas back, that demon Vratharasura indeed makes his appearance and so the demigods looking at his power, they become bewildered and in a helpless situation they are offering their prayers to the Supreme Lord. So here they are mentioning some examples of how Krishna or the Lord has protected His devotees previously. So they are appealing to the Lord to protect them similarly this time. And so here in the last shloka, they are mentioning, although the Lord is before us in the form of the Supersoul, we are not able to understand or not able to see Him because Pashyama is a Lingam Pritha Eshamanina, Eshamanina, because we think that we are also an independent controller. So that is the context. Actually nowadays, looks like there is a lot of research going on about ants, you know, the scientists are interested to understand what the ants are doing and you know, generally ants, I quoted this example in one of the classes also, the ants, they save the grains for the rainy day, you know, for this season. So when the scientists are analyzing, they understood that whenever an ant gets a grain, it divides that into two and keeps them into their godown. Ants have godowns, storekeepers, everything is there. So then they were very curious, why is the ant breaking the grain into two? Then they figured out that if the grain is broken, then it will not, you know, germinate, it will not become a seedling, a small plant, it will not, you know. That's why the ants break the, you know, the seeds, so that it won't grow into some plant or something and they miss the food. So that's going on and meanwhile, they saw there is one coriander seed and this time the ant has broken the coriander seed into four parts and they were surprised, why is this? Other seeds are only two. Now it is broken into four. So they started analyzing. They found that coriander seed, if you break them into two, still seedling is possible. It can germinate and become a small, small seedlings. So that's why the ant knows that this is a coriander seed and if I break only into two, then you know, it will not, this, it is not okay. So it breaks them into four and then store it in the air salt. The scientists are blown off. How is it that they are so intelligent? We also don't know that coriander seed. Ants have such an intelligence. Even scientists are bewildered. Then the story goes one step further. Then they found, one day they saw one of the ants, fellow ants, got injured. Day to day existence. They have very fragile legs. And they know. Whole body is broken. Body is very small. So then one of the fellow ants came to this ant and found that, that fragile leg, something gone wrong. And then in front of the eyes of the scientists, that ant has performed a surgery and amputated that leg of the, that ant, that fellow ant, which. I don't know the evidence, but I think it is a one-time operation which got injured. So now the scientists are blown off. They are thinking only human society has surgeons. Here is a much more skilled explosion. In fact, they found that the fellow ant doesn't do operations every time. First it analyzes whether it can be cured by some medicine. If not, then it does a skilled operation and redoes the surgery after a day. But if it is not cured and removes that little portion they found that 90% success rate in the surgery other cases probably they won't have seen hundred percent nowadays I mean not all doctors but some doctors you go with any problem they immediately say surgery cut the rope but aunt is not saying first it'll unless their medicine is okay and then if not then do the surgery how is it that the smallest the living entity that we see is exhibiting such a powerful intelligence and skill this is a big mystery for the scientists because in here if we discuss the activity of a doctor or a Vet, if we talk about it in the In human society, if somebody has to become a surgeon, first five years of MBBS and before that you have to cross NEET, that examination and then five years of MBBS and then after that there is a specialization, you know, you get, you become a surgeon. That is the, so just to develop such a talent and skill, in the human society we say, we see it takes so many years, but here we are seeing in the ant society, very, that kind of a skilled operations they are able to do and getting a 90% success rate. They were shocked. So there is this universal intelligence. They don't want to accept, but that indicates too the presence of a universal intelligence. So here the demigods are appearing, appealing to that universal intelligence, Krishna, in the form of the super-soul. The Lord who is giving the intelligence to that ant is the Lord in the heart and he is the super-soul. And he knows how to guide that living entity. So that's why here they are saying, There is only one Isha, the supreme controller. By his energy, he is actually Sasarja, he is creating, he is maintaining and he is destroying. And what is Anusrujama Vishwam, we are also creating, but Anusrujama, Anu means following the direction of the original creator, we are also creating and expanding the creation, but we are actually completely dependent on the supreme creator.

#### material energy, seer, Kshetrajna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zVRzPvgNIYg?t=1991
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I'm simply sitting and I'm just coming to know what is happening in this field."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that our bodies are made of material energy, and the living entity within is the seer (Kshetrajna), simply observing the actions of the body, which are controlled by Krishna's external energy.

And inside this body, a living entity is situated as Kshetrajna, as a seer. So we see actually our own body also. We see the senses. And we also see many objects, you know, for our senses, we need some objects to be seen. Those objects are also there and they're also made up of this material energy. So one important point is that since we are seers only, that means we're only knowers of the field, how is it that the hands are moving, legs are moving, my eyes are working? I'm only a seer inside. I'm simply sitting and I'm just coming to know what is happening in this field. And you know, all those things are happening. It's exactly like you're sitting inside a car and tires are running and a break is happening and so many things are going on. You're just sitting as a passenger behind. How a seer can move something that he's seeing? Let us say I want to move this camera. I cannot move because I'm a seer and that is seen. So when the seer is able to move, say I'm moving my hands, how is this possible? This is possible only by that energy. There is a different energy which is controlling these hands and legs and all my senses and sense objects. It's not me. I'm only seeing. But the movements are happening by some other agency and that is Nizamahaya, Krishna's energy, external energy. And then this energy is not moving erratically or without any discipline, a facade way, no. We are seeing there is a global intelligence, there is a massive intelligence behind the actions of this. Jada energy, the energy which is otherwise inert, that is working wonderfully because of an intelligent personality. Actually, Shukadeva Goswami gives this logic in the second canto to prove that, you know, of course we are seer and the scene, the scene is animated, you know, we are seeing so many things. They're all getting animated. So that means there is an animating energy and that is the external energy. And the director for this animation is the Supreme Lord who is present as the super soul.
