#### detachment, Krishna's energy, fundamentals
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zVRzPvgNIYg?t=2195
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only because we identify with our body, with what is happening in this body, we unnecessarily assume that all these things are mine and then struggle and with emotions we make it complicated."
**Summary:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa explains that understanding that we are separate from our thoughts and emotions, and that they arise from previous actions, helps us detach and avoid unnecessary struggles. He emphasizes the importance of remaining fascinated by the fundamentals of Krishna consciousness.

So Nizamahaya Naha Sasarja, many, sometimes some devotees, they, they get worried about, you know. What's happening? You know how it's like, people feel emotional about some bad thoughts or some, envious thoughts or lusty thoughts in the mind. Our they may also become very emotional about some bad habits, which are pulling them so if you understand that all these things are I am able to see means I am separate from all this. Say an envious thought comes, you are able to see, you are a seer, and you are seeing an envious thought, that means you are separate from it. It is not mine, it is coming up in the field, why it is coming up in the field? Because previously I have put something there, it's only my own previous actions, but currently I am able to see that thought separate from me, it is actually not mine. Only because we identify with our body, with what is happening in this body, we unnecessarily assume that all these things are mine and then struggle and with emotions we make it complicated. So this knowledge that I am a knower, a Kshatriya, I am not this body, I am a spirit soul, it may appear to be very routine. And you know, we have heard about it. I have heard many times, like yesterday also I was mentioning to some devotees, if we want to progress in Krishna Consciousness, always remain fascinated by these fundamentals. You should remain fascinated. You see, Prabhupada, what is his position, a Mahabhagavata Acharya, and somebody analyzed what are the slokas that he quoted. Yeah, of course he is quoting for us, but he is ever fascinated by quoting those same slokas again and again. Dehino esminada dehe kaum. He tells this in Paris, then goes to London, Dehino esminada dehe kaum, Prabhupada is not tired. He is ever fascinated by those fundamentals and he wants to give that knowledge to all of us. So.

#### Krishna's direction, demigods, intelligence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zVRzPvgNIYg?t=2355
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every condition of the soul thinks that He is God."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that the systematic intelligence we see in the world comes from Krishna, and that demigods acknowledge Krishna as the supreme Isha. The problem arises when conditioned souls think they are God, leading to a disconnect from Krishna's direction.

These are the secrets, this energy, where we see a systematic tremendous intelligence and that is coming from Krishna. This energy cannot move, Jada, this energy, for example, this table cannot move on its own. These are all fundamentals. Somebody has to move it. Similarly, the entire external energy is a Jada, but how it is moving? That is because Krishna's direction is this. So the demigods, they are aware of this. They are saying, yeah, Ika Iso Nizamayaya Naha Sasarja. There is only one Isha. And demigods are also, they are also Ishas. But they are saying, you are the supreme Isha, see this knowledge demigods have. And they are saying, we are also creating, aena anusrujama vishvam, we are also creating many things in this world, we are expanding the creation, but that is Anusrujama. Only because you are giving intelligence, unless Krishna gives intelligence, you know, we cannot do anything. These hands cannot move, this tongue cannot work, the eyes cannot see. A living entity is completely dependent like that. But why is it that it is not able to see all this? Because, Eesha maanina. The whole problem starts with this manina. Your thinking. And what is that thinking? Every condition of the soul thinks that He is God. He may not really, some of the people, they might not really think that I am God. But but as soon as one identifies himself with the body and he thinks that some creation he makes and he thinks this is my creation, I did it, my creation, that means he is saying I am God. Only God is the only creator. Even demigods, they know that we are only secondary creators, that too following the direction of the Supreme Lord, we are able to create something. So, if the demigods are agreeing to Krishna's position as the sole doer of everything, now what about the conditioned souls? So, if we want to come out of the doership, then this is the secret. We should always recognize that Anusrujama Vishwam, my Lord, I am able to create this only because... you know, because of your direction or your intelligence.

#### devotional service, Sevonmukha, Ishamanina
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zVRzPvgNIYg?t=2535
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every conditioned soul thinks that the whole world, whole Vishwa is centered around Him."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that devotees depend completely on Krishna and execute their duties with His guidance, gaining insights that enhance their performance. He contrasts this with conditioned souls who think they are the doers, leading to an inability to see Krishna. The counteraction to this Ishamanina is to increase our service aptitude, becoming Sevonmukha.

So devotees, they understand this, they depend completely on Krishna. And as Krishna permits, as Krishna directs, the devotees, they keep executing their duties and while doing their duties, they get a lot of intelligence, insights. They get a lot of intelligence, insights. And those insights will enhance their performance and then devotees will become successful even with, you know, little endeavors. This is an experience of all of us, you know, we may be doing the smallest service or whatever, you are absorbed, you are dependent on Krishna, then you will see, you know, some insights, some little light, some flash, whatever you call it. And then suddenly our work gets completed quickly. Whereas outsiders, they take so much of time to do that. So, Pashyama Lingam Pradageshamana. Okay, now if somebody thinks that I am also a creator, I am also a doer, then what happens? Demigods are saying, Na Pashyama. We will not be able to see your form. My Lord. No, this is the danger. Although Krishna, Lord, is there in the heart and when He appears physically, He will be there, He may be there in front of us, but the conditioned soul will not be able to recognize Him because we are absorbed in ourselves as the Isha. Every conditioned soul thinks that the whole world, whole Vishwa is centered around Him. Yes. You know, when He gets into a community or gets into some kind of a group also, that self-prominence will be very strongly be present in the mind. Things should happen the way I want. Things should be for my comfort. Things should be, you know, centered around me. I should get the attention. This is the thinking of a conditioned soul. He wants to become the cineshore of the eyes of the whole community. As soon as he enters, everybody should see him. Everybody should. And if they don't see him, he becomes depressed. He becomes very neglected. He thinks, oh, you know, nobody's recognizing me and gets neglected. So that's because of his thinking, Ishamanina. So the only counteraction to this. This Ishamanina is one single word that is called Sevonmukha.

#### conditioning, Satvaguna, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zVRzPvgNIYg?t=2845
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Why misery comes? Because we break these conditions."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the concept of conditioning, explaining that everyone is subjected to various conditions in life. He highlights that those in Satvaguna experience fewer conditions and greater freedom. He emphasizes that misery arises from breaking these conditions and that devotees strive to rise to the level of Satvaguna through the guidance of a spiritual master and the practice of sadhana.

Now, in each of the living entities, there are various degrees of conditioning. But everybody is conditioned. What is this conditioning? We are subjected to follow innumerable conditions in our life. And each person has his own set of conditions to follow. Like we were discussing, I was discussing with one devotee, how, you know, when, for example, some devotees, when they eat, they have to wait for six to seven hours to again eat something. But some devotees, within three hours or three and a half hours, completely digested and hungry. Where is the next person? He is very hungry. The condition for him is three hours, three and a half hours. And condition for him is six hours or seven hours. But both of them are having conditions. One may feel like eating whole day. Go on eating. But actually, no, there is a condition. So like that, there are innumerable conditions. If somebody sits down and tries to list down the conditions that he has to follow, oh my God, so many conditions are there. But these conditions are not same. If somebody is in Satvaguna, his conditions are less. He feels more freedom, free from diseases, free from all these things. He can also see things as they are. His knowledge is bright. And so he doesn't get implicated in conditions, breaking conditions unnecessarily. He can see. Why misery comes? Because we break these conditions. We don't follow the conditions. As soon as we break, then the reaction is ready. So that's why Satvaguna means you should see, oh, these are the conditions. Let me adhere to it so that it's all clean. Now, devotees, after we became devotees, whatever condition we may be, we are trying to rise to the level of Satvaguna. When we take shelter of a bonafide spiritual master, Prabhupada, first of all, he gives the sadhana process, a regiment he gives, where you have to discipline your mind and body and everything, day after day, day after day, day after day, automatically the devotee comes to the sattvic platform. But this process is, coming to Satvaguna process is not easy. It is full of tapasya. Not easy. Generally not easy. Like Vishwamitra, to get rid of his passion and come to Satvaguna, what and all he had to do? But in our case, that process is being made joyful by Lord Chaitanya, by this process of Sanketan, Prasadam, you know, chanting and all those things. So devotees, if they can apply themselves to this process, which is given by Prabhupada, gradually they come to the Satvaguna. They can see how this world works. And so that, you know, we don't break any of the rules of this world also. And then there are rules, conditions of the, even the spiritual life.

#### discipline, Sattva Guna, consequences
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zVRzPvgNIYg?t=3079
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "More discipline, more happy. Why? Because I am saved from the future misery."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of discipline in spiritual life, highlighting that devotees should happily accept discipline to avoid future misery. He explains that offering services to Krishna in Sattva Guna means not hurting or disturbing others and avoiding cruelty or destructive thinking. He uses the example of Indra killing Vishwaroopa to illustrate the consequences of actions.

He slowly develops his knowledge and tries to discipline himself by those injunctions. Shastric injunctions are Guru's instructions. So this is the key point. If we want to become disciple, we have to be disciplined. We have to happily accept discipline. More discipline, more happy. Why? Because I am saved from the future misery. Because unknowingly if I break something, misery will come. And so that's why they want to be, they want to be very careful. Now this big misery has come to, you know, these demigods. Why? Because Indra has killed a brahmana priest, Vishwaroopa, out of anger, in a fit of anger. Just killed him off. Now he is facing the reality. What is the result? The consequence is that Vishwaroopa's father, Tvasta, he becomes angry. See, you have to see all this, consequences means. Now he does a Homa, a Yajna, and he says, he prays to the Yajna Purusha that please give me a son who can kill Indra. Then comes Uttarasura. And then to face Uttarasura, now again they are approaching the Supreme Lord. Let us say Indra did not do that, killing of Vishwaroopa, no problem, no. They won't be in this miserable condition. Right? Now this is one more important point. We should offer our services to Krishna in Sattva Guna. We offer our foodstuff in Sattva Guna, highest, best quality. Similarly, we should also offer our services in Sattva Guna. That means what? When I am doing my service, I am not hurting, disturbing any of the stakeholders. I am not breaking any rules. I am not being cruel or destructive in my thinking. I am not, you know, this is the kind of thinking. Dvesha is not there. This is the, Sattvic offering means, we cannot think, no, it is Krishna's service, I will do whatever way I want. And you know, because it is Krishna's service. Doesn't matter. No, yes, that is Krishna's service. But we have to offer the best quality service to Krishna, whatever is the best quality that we can offer. And that is the service in Sattva Guna.

**Summary:** The speaker advises caution and consultation before acting, especially when representing an organization, to avoid future bondage or negative consequences. He reiterates that the key to counteracting the false ego (Ishamanina) is to increase our service aptitude and desire to serve Krishna.

So here, although demigods are in Sattva Guna generally, they, you know, they committed some mistake and then we are seeing the consequences. And the consequences have to be addressed. Similarly, when we also do anything, particularly representing the organization, we should be very careful about the future bondage or consequences. And so do a lot of deliberation, do a lot of consultation before we do anything. So by consulting, by taking all the help, still something goes wrong, okay, we tried our best. It is the wish of the Supreme Lord. We can, you know, we can take it like that. So that's the, some of the points here. The crucial issue, crucial point is that the Ishamanina, thinking that I am the, I am God, can be counteracted only by increasing our service aptitude, wanting to do more and more, wanting to give more and more service to Krishna. That very attitude, whether we are able to really give or not. That's the best remedy to counteract this Ishamanina.

Things themselves are believed to be the doer of activities, which are actually carried out by nature. That and 5.8, both are related. Actually, although the soul by nature is a seer inside, but we feel that we are doing, and we also feel that we are knowing. I am the knower. I am the doer. That's because he identifies with the body. So that is called bewilderment at the level of ego. See one should know that he is the knower, he is the doer, he is the knower, he is the doer. You should know, mind is not all. Above mind there is intelligence. Above intelligence there is ego. And all these guys are bringing their own demands, bringing their own confusions into our system. One should know this. So, in the mind something comes. That's not the end of it. And don't identify with those thoughts and become very depressed. Oh, I am like this. You are not that. So, bewilderment comes only because you are the knower. Because when you think, I am this body, Eesha Manana, when you get that feeling, so the only counteraction is focus on this fundamental. What is that? I am not this body, I am a spirit soul, I am an eternal servant of Guru and Krishna. You go on repeating, I am eternal servant of Guru and Krishna. Try to act on that platform. ### Fearless and auspicious path | HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa | SB 6.1.17 | 05-12-2019
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### Bhakti, Fearlessness, Mahajanas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fpNgEkLCq0M?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One should fearlessly follow the twelve Mahājanas and their line of disciplic succession and thus be liberated from the clutches of Māyā."
**Summary:** The speaker reads and explains a verse and purport from the Srimad Bhagavatam, emphasizing the auspicious and fearless nature of the path of Bhakti, contrasting it with karmakāṇḍa and jñānakāṇḍa, and advocating following the Mahājanas.

Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 6, Chapter 1, and Text 17. sadrīcino hyayam loke vantākṣemo kutobayahā duṣīlā sādavo yatra nārāyaṇa-parāyaṇā. Translation, purported by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Śrī Prabhupāda. Translation, the path followed by pure devotees who are well-behaved and fully endowed with the best qualifications is certainly the most auspicious path in this material world. It is free from fear and it is authorized by the Shastras. Purported by Śrī Prabhupāda, one should not think that the person who takes to Bhakti is one who cannot perform the ritualistic ceremonies recommended in the scriptures. One should not take to Bhakti in the Karmakanda section of the Vedas or is not sufficiently educated to speculate on spiritual subjects. Māyāvādīs generally allege that the Bhakti path is for women and illiterates. This is a groundless accusation. The Bhakti path is followed by the most learned scholars such as the Gosvāmīs, Lord Caitanya Mahāprabhu and Rāmānujācārya. These are the actual followers of the Bhakti path. Whether or not one is educated or aristocratic, one must follow in their footsteps. Mahājano enagata sapanta. One must follow the path of the Mahājanas. The Mahājanas are those who are taken to the path of devotional service. Sūśīlā sādavo yatrā nārāyaṇa parāyaṇa. For these great personalities are the perfect persons. As stated in the Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam, yasyāsthi bhaktir bhagavati akiñcana, sarvair guṇaiṣṭatra samāsate surā. One who has unflinching devotion to the Personality of Godhead has all the good qualities of the demigods. That is the sense of the Bhaktivedanta purport.

#### Fear, Solutions, Technology
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fpNgEkLCq0M?t=180
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Akutobhaya, fearless path, nobody knows."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses a recent crime in Hyderabad and the public's reaction, including proposed solutions from politicians and the use of technology like safety apps, highlighting the underlying fear and the limitations of material solutions in achieving true fearlessness.

So in the last week, there was one incident in Hyderabad that shook up the entire country. There was a cruel murder, rape and murder of a woman by the four, so to say, miscreants, rowdies. So everybody in the city was disturbed by the cruelty involved in the incident. Unnaturally, many leaders across the country responded to it, condemning the whole incident. And the discussion came up in the Parliament also, where the entire country of leaders, they are present there. And naturally, they are all very angry at the accused. And in fact, some of them are suggesting that the way these people burnt that lady, similarly, these accused also should be burnt, burnt alive. Somebody suggested that they should be castrated. Somebody else suggested that they should be stoned to death. Like that, we hear the various, you know, it's natural because they could see the heinous cruelty involved in the whole incident. And it was such a disturbance that even in our youth programs, many youngsters were asking why this has happened and what's the solution. So although there was such an incident, I think some three, four years back in Delhi, and they have enacted one act called Nirbhaya, Nirbhaya Act. And of course, the trail is still going on, I think. So this time, some of the politicians, they suggested, leaders, they suggested that we should make this act even more strict and ensure that there are courts which can process or complete this case quickly. You know, like that, we hear many people suggesting. But although there are strict laws and their emotions and their solutions can be understandable, but still, the fear is still there. A woman has to go and work in the outside society, still there is a fear. And there is a fear in the parents that whether all these formulas will work or not. When the incident happened, the local home minister said, no, that woman should have pressed 100, one, zero, zero. Immediately, we all responded, why she has not pressed wrong? And then somebody else suggested, now we have a special app. You can download that app. And then whenever you are in trouble, you can just press one button only. You don't need to press three buttons. You know, 100 could be just one button you press and it will all be over. So still, and then as soon as the incident happened, there are one lakh downloads of that app. Everybody is, you know, they want to be this. But still, everybody knows, somewhere something is missing. Why? Because, Akutobhaya, fearless path, nobody knows. Everybody is thinking in a certain way. But everybody knows that, you know, for example, one woman, let us say, she downloads an app. Can she walk free at 11 o'clock or 3 o'clock? 12 o'clock in the midnight? Of course, there is no need to walk at that time. But anyway, we can't say anything nowadays.

So, how is it they can actually know what is good and what is not good and then how can they actually perfect their lives? You know, she is concerned. And so, you can see it has touched the conscience of millions of people in the country. Everybody is not comfortable. But unfortunately, they don't know whom to approach. They have to approach Shushila Sadavo. They have to approach, well known, well behaved, saintly persons. Let us say you are going to, you know, some pilgrimage. Sometimes, you know, you have to ask some people on the roadside to get the right direction. Sometimes, we followed the Google, we reached right on the banks of a river. Where will you go? So, it's not always, you know, reliable. It's not akutobhaya. Google is also fearful. So, we immediately, we have to ask. Now, let us say when you are going, there are two people are there. There is one drunkard is there. And next to it, there is a well-dressed person is there. Now, whom will you ask for direction? Obviously, you will not ask the drunkard. You will ask the next person who is apparently little well-dressed and apparently well behaved. It's natural, actually. You will not ask the ruffians and rowdies for direction. Those should be shushila, well-behaved people you would like to go and ask. Well-behaved and sadhavaha. They should be saintly because you may say, oh, even mayavadhis are also well-behaved. They are also, you know, they behave very nicely. So, why not ask them?

#### Karma Kanda, Jnana Kanda, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fpNgEkLCq0M?t=632
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The ones who are completely dedicated to Krishna, Adi Narayana, the original Narayana, Krishna, then that path is akutobhaya."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that while Māyāvādīs may be well-behaved, their path (karma kanda and jnana kanda) is not appropriate or fearless. True guidance comes from those dedicated to Krishna (Narayana Parayana), leading to a path free from fear (akutobhaya).

So, Prabhupada is explaining in the purport. Sadricinaha, that means, what they are following is not appropriate. They are following, say, karma kanda and jnana kanda. Karma kanda means innumerable religious activities for the sake of getting something back. Doing idhyas particularly to get some sense gratification. If you see Vedanta Sutra, it has four chapters and two chapters are dedicated to it. One is the jnana kanda. People, not only one category of people are dexterous in performing rituals. You know, they are very, very good. They show all the hand movements and mudras and this and that. They bewilder you. And so, there are certain categories like that. And then there is one more category. They are very good in speculating. They will sit in an armchair. They will relax. They will deliver. They will deliver all kinds of speculations. And then so, this is jnana kanda. If they have some basis, it is jnana kanda. Otherwise, it is just mental speculation. So, these two paths cannot make one shushila, sadhava in the true sense. And then their marga, their path is also fearful. We don't know where we will land up. So, why we have, which kind of shushila, sadhava we have to approach? Narayana, Parayana. The ones who are completely dedicated to Krishna. Adi Narayana. The original Narayana, Krishna. Then, then that path is akutobhaya.

#### Parampara, Confidence, Crime
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fpNgEkLCq0M?t=747
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "That's why I have come here to Chicago to tell you why and what to do."
**Summary:** The speaker recounts an incident where Prabhupada, upon seeing a Time magazine cover about crime, confidently declared his purpose in Chicago was to address the issue, highlighting the confidence that comes from being in the parampara of Nārāyaṇa, Parāyaṇa.

Actually, in 1975, when Prabhupāda was travelling from Denver to Chicago, his disciple Brahmananda is also sitting next to Prabhupāda. And then, he was holding Time magazine. The main title of the magazine, the title is that Crime, Why and What can be done? You know, that's the title of the magazine. So, Prabhupāda also sees the title. And Prabhupāda is very eager. Yes. You know, he wants to address this issue. So, as soon as he gets down in the airport, Ohare airport, many media people approach Sri Prabhupāda. And then, the local temple president, Sri Govinda, you know, he somehow pushes everybody and then able to reach Prabhupāda and introduces the media people. You know, here is the Miss Jones from NBC television. And they would like to ask you something. And then she says, she says, how do you do? She starts like that. And then she says, what is the, what is the purpose for coming to Chicago? You know, everywhere when Prabhupāda goes, you know, the first question sometimes would be, why you have come here? They are all scared. So, as soon as he asked, Prabhupāda said, just now, I saw the Time magazine and the title cover page says, Crime, Why and What to do? And then he says, and then the whole topic is covered in three, four pages in the magazine. See, Time magazine is the leading magazine of those days. And in that three, four pages are allocated for this particular subject. So, must be a very prominent issue. And they're all concerned. And then, so Prabhupāda says, that's why I have come here to Chicago to tell you why and what to do. So, Prabhupāda is very confident. So, this is an important. So, that confidence is because he is coming in the parampara of Nārāyaṇa, Parāyaṇa.

**Key Quote:** "Chant Hare Kṛṣṇa Hare Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Hare Hare Hare Rāma Hare Rāma Rāma Rāma Do Ankit and jubilantly dance. And then if you are little tired, then eat Prasad. That's all."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of following the Mahājanas to be free from fear and experience auspiciousness. He describes the simple path of chanting Hare Krishna, dancing, and eating Prasad as a means to imbibe all the Vedas.

So, here Prabhupāda in the parport, he mentions about the Mahājanas. And he says, if you want to be free from fear, because we are dedicating our life, we are spending time, we are spending energy, all that is it going in the right direction? Yes. Akutobhaya. Panta kṣema. Very auspicious. Not only fearless, it is very auspicious. As we keep growing in this path, you will perceive more and more auspiciousness in the life. So, Prabhupāda is very confident. He presented the conclusion of the parampara. He knows our parampara is following in the footsteps of the Mahājanas. Brahmā, the original, one of the important Mahājanas out of the twelve, we belong to that parampara. And Prabhupāda brought that message to this world. So, and then what is our, you know, the path? Because apparently we don't have too many rituals. And we also don't encourage speculation. And so, and then looks like we are very simple. What is our path? Very simple thing. Chant Hare Kṛṣṇa Hare Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Kṛṣṇa Hare Hare Hare Rāma Hare Rāma Rāma Rāma Do Ankit and jubilantly dance. And then if you are little tired, then eat Prasad. That's all. If you do these three, you are a Chaturvedi. All Vedas are imbibed by you. You are a Chaturvedi.

We should ensure that my actions are authorized. For authorizing, there is a system that Srila Prabhupada has given. So, we should, if you can ensure that our actions are authorized by the system in place, that's all, we are safe, akutha, then we are fearless. But otherwise, we are taking the responsibility. If you do this much, just to conclude, one practical a practical incident we have seen in andhra there was a program called anna canteen so you know we got the opportunity to do that and in vijayawada there is one canteen when this when the canteen was started many the many complaints we started getting you know because there are many drunkards and it's called the blade group you know they they always carry blades that group and then you know that's the blade group so uh that's the group which is there in the canteen and they used to throw the bottles and this and then and then they also used to come and you know eat uh eat the food there and then uh so they were very unruly and then very misbehaved so then um it's going on and after six months or eight months that local supervisor he gave a feedback to us that uh prabhuji uh nowadays they're standing in the line nicely they fell in line and you know they don't create any trouble they nicely take prashad you know they eat and all that so six months they ate the blade group has said in six months you can see there is a change in their entire consciousness and behavior.

#### Chanting, Sensitivity, Consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/fpNgEkLCq0M?t=1772
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Our movement is a consciousness fine-tuning. A person becomes more and more sensitive and fine."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a story about a wealthy donor who initially hesitated to chant but gradually increased his chanting and eventually gave up meditation, demonstrating how chanting sensitizes and refines consciousness, leading to positive changes.

Not only that, I have one experience, one very big donor, he is a very busy man. He has some, I mean, he has some 9,000 acres somewhere in Andhra, that's the kind of, you know, big. So when we went there, of course he is a little devoted man, so we said, no, you should chant. So I said 108 times you have to chant. So then he said. Swamiji, this, you know, he is, so you know, he is looking for excuse, you know. So then I said, okay, 108 may be big for you, you do just 9 times, not 9 rounds, 9 times, just 9 times. He said, that looks okay Swamiji, I will take up. So he started chanting 9 times and then he met me after 2-3 months. That time I saw he has one clicker. Clicker is there. I said, what is that? No, no, I found in one of the temples, there is a clicker like this. Every time, you know, I chant my 9 on this, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, you know, he is coming close to this. And then he also told me, whenever I get a chance, Swamiji, I will again, I will click 9. You know, he is increasing his 9. After another 3-4 months I met him. He told me one more interesting thing. Swamiji. He said, I left meditating, Swamiji said. I said, how is that? I never told, I never told that you have to stop meditating. But he on his own, he left. He said, I somehow felt it's not good. See this felt, these are all, he is sensitizing, he is getting sensitized. Sensitivity is increasing. Consciousness is becoming finer. Our movement is a consciousness fine-tuning. A person becomes more and more sensitive and fine. That is called Sushila. You know, this is, this is our movement. So that, that person felt, you know, his consciousness has developed and then he left all those things. So this is the mystical process that Prabhupada has given. If he, in the earnestness, somebody takes up this chanting, certainly there will be you know, tremendous transformation in that person's consciousness. And slowly, he will accept more and more of, you know, the process that is given by Srila Prabhupada. And then he can perfect his life.

And health is very bad. And in spite of all these things, still he is trying to continue with his materialistic activities. So suppose if he had, has to come to the devotional path. So what will be the remedy for him? Only like, how should he take it and move forward? So our process is appealing to all sections of the society. Whether he is well behaved or not well behaved. That Jeeva Goswami's, that Goswami's prayers, it is mentioned that, what is that? Dheera and Adheera. So it is applicable to all of them. And it requires the preacher's expertise to address the message to the audience. Now here lies the preaching. Prabhupada says, preaching means you should make it appealing to the audience. So if you go to a Karmakandi, you know, that person who is attached to Karmakanda. Now what is that we have to preach? So not easy to preach and convince people towards pure devotional service. #### Srimad Bhagavatam, Vidura's Inquiries, Transcendental Bliss
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Nf71MC-NeJs?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maitreya Muni, being thus invited by Vidura to speak, appeared to be smiling because he actually felt transcendental bliss."
**Summary:** The lecture begins with the speaker reading and translating text 42 from Srimad Bhagavatam, focusing on Maitreya Muni's enthusiastic response to Vidura's inquiries and the transcendental bliss he experienced.

Om Namo Bharavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bharavatam, Tanto 3, Chapter 7, Further Inquiries by Vidura, Text No. 42. Sri Sutra Uvacha. Saitam Aprista Purana Kalpaha. Turu Pradhanena Muni Pradhanaha. Pravrita Harso Bharavat Rathayam. Sanchodita Stam Prahasan Ivaaha. Translation in purport by His Divine Grace, A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada. Jai Prabhupada. Sri Sutra Dev Goswami said, Great learned sages like Maitreya Muni are always very enthusiastic about describing the transcendental activities of the Lord. Maitreya Muni, being thus invited by Vidura to speak, appeared to be smiling because he actually felt transcendental bliss. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Purport.

---
#### Bhagavad Katha, Maitreya Muni, Vidura
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Nf71MC-NeJs?t=117
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Maitreya Rishi got enlivened, very enthusiastic because Bhagavad Kathayam Sanchodita, he got an opportunity to explain about Bhagavad Katha, the transcendental activities of the Supreme Personality of Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the roles of Vidura and Maitreya Muni, explaining how Maitreya Muni became enthusiastic and enlivened by the opportunity to discuss Bhagavad Katha due to Vidura's questions.

So this is the last verse of the chapter titled, Further Inquiries by Vidura. So in the last 41 shlokas, we have seen Vidura asking Maitreya Muni, who is referred to as Muni Pradhanaha, is the chief of the sages. Muni Pradhanaha, and who is Vidura, who is asking all these questions, inquiries, is referred to as Kuru Pradhanena by Pradhana, by one of the chief personalities in the Kuru dynasty. So Kuru Pradhanaha is asking questions to the Muni Pradhanaha. So after this inquiry, the conversation moves back to the conversation between Shukadeva Goswami and Parikshit Maharaj. So here we see Shri Shukavvacha. Shukadeva Goswami is explaining the reaction of Maitreya Muni. After hearing the questions of Vidura, what was the response of Maitreya Muni? He said, He was thus questioned by Vidura. Maitreya Rishi, he knows how to explain Puranas. And then, He became very enthusiastic. Pravaruddha means enhancement. He was extremely satisfied. So by hearing the questions, the response of Vidura, Maitreya Rishi got enlivened, very enthusiastic. Why? Because Bhagavad Kathayam Sanchodita, he got an opportunity to explain about Bhagavad Katha, the transcendental activities of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Bhagavad Kathayam Sanchodita, inspired by, or questioned by, or infused by, or infused by Vidura, Tamprahasan Iva Aaha, he was actually smiling. Tamprahasan is so happy, you can see on his face, that he is actually smiling.

---
#### Transcendental Bliss, Krishna Consciousness, Association with Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Nf71MC-NeJs?t=283
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Just by hearing and explaining, we become, we start associating with these Leelas, with these transcendental activities, with Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the transcendental bliss derived from explaining Bhagavad Katha, drawing from Prabhupada's teachings and highlighting Krishna's complete bliss, knowledge, and permanence, and how devotees can associate with Krishna through hearing and explaining His activities.

Prabhupada writes in the purport, he is smiling because he has actually felt transcendental bliss. The very thought that I have been asked to explain Bhagavad Kathayam, that very thought made Maitreya Muni to smile and be very happy to explain these activities. So we will see in the next chapter, Maitreya Rishi starts answering the inquiries of Vidura. And these answers are nothing but description about Purana Kalpa. These are all descriptions from various Puranas, from the Vedas. Maitreya Rishi answers the questions by Vidura. So the important point is that when an opportunity for preaching is available, a preacher becomes very happy. If somebody asks him a question about Krishna, he becomes very enthusiastic to explain. So if somebody chants nicely, the result is that he becomes very enthusiastic to serve. This is the test. If somebody really chanted and hears, the first thing that you see him is that he becomes very enthusiastic to serve. So those services are all preaching. All services within this organization, they are all preaching. And particularly, if somebody asks about Krishna, he becomes very happy to explain the Bhagavad Kathaya. Why? Of course, Maitreya Rishi is a liberated personality. So he became smiling. He started smiling. So Prabhupada explains that these Kathayams are full of bliss. There is transcendental bliss in hearing and explaining these activities of the Supreme Lord. Krishna is complete bliss. He is complete knowledge. He is pure knowledge. And he is permanent. So that is explained as Sat, Chit and Ananda. It is a notion of bliss. And the activities that he performs, they are also completely blissful. It may be killing of a demon, or it could be dancing with happiness, or it could be dancing with happiness, so anybody who starts associating with these activities, any devotee who starts associating with the Supreme Lord, how? By hearing and explaining. Just by hearing and explaining, we become, we start associating with these Leelas, with these transcendental activities, with Krishna.

#### Preaching, Enthusiasm, Prabhupada's Example
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Nf71MC-NeJs?t=501
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If there is a preaching opportunity, he has so much of energy. He never felt tired. He used to go and preach to the people."
**Summary:** The speaker illustrates the importance of enthusiasm in Krishna consciousness by sharing anecdotes from Prabhupada's life, highlighting his tireless dedication to preaching and his unwavering energy despite travel and fatigue.

So, as the devotee goes on hearing and explaining, gradually he develops transcendental bliss. Gradually, he also enjoys explaining these stories, explaining these Kathas. Actually, we see this in Prabhupada's life. Whenever he used to go to any place, devotees would organize various preaching programs, and then Prabhupada would get ready and then he used to go to one preaching program after one more, continuously used. Like one devotee was recollecting, Prabhupada travels, it's a long distance, he travels by flight, and he comes to that place, and he goes for some program to explain about Krishna to explain about transcendental activities of Krishna, Krishna Katha. And then, there is one more program comes up at 11 o'clock in the night. And he was thinking, my God, Prabhupada traveled so much, already he would have been tired. So, should I ask about this preaching opportunity or not? Finally, somehow he makes up his mind and approaches Prabhupada and says, Prabhupada, there is a radio show at 11 o'clock. Prabhupada says, okay, how many people attend this? Then he says, there will be millions here listening to this. He says, let's go. And then he says, no, no, Prabhupada, you have come all the way, you have traveled, you are tired, you must be tired. I don't want to take advantage of your presence here. Prabhupada says, don't worry. And then he goes to the program, radio program, and he addresses the audience. Actually, Prabhupada never felt tired. If there is a preaching opportunity, he has so much of energy. He never felt tired. He used to go and preach to the people.

#### Preaching, Enthusiasm, Spiritual Life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Nf71MC-NeJs?t=633
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without enthusiasm, there is no chance of spiritual life."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to emphasize the importance of enthusiasm in spiritual life, contrasting it with disappointment and using the analogy of a successful businessman seizing opportunities to illustrate how devotees should eagerly embrace service.

And sometimes disciples will say, I know thousands will come, hundreds will come, and then only some 30, 40 will come. And sometimes 20 will come. But you see, Prabhupada never gets disappointed. He continues to preach, whether it is two, three people, whether it is hundreds and thousands. In India, disciples organized these Pandal programs in Mumbai, in Hyderabad. Thousands would come. Daily 25,000, 30,000 people would come. And then when Prabhupada addresses the audience, there will be thousands sitting and listening. So Prabhupada has seen that. Prabhupada has also seen where they thought 100 people will come, and you will see sometimes one person, two persons would come in the western countries. So, but Pravruddha Harsha, Prabhupada, as you see, he is very enthusiastic. Actually, without enthusiasm, there is no chance of spiritual life. Krishna consciousness or spiritual life means one has to be very enthusiastic. Yes, I want to do this service. Yes, I want to preach. Now, what kind of an enthusiasm Prabhupada gives an example of a businessman earning money. So, if you talk to a successful business person, who is earning a lot of money, you go and ask him, see, look, there is one more opportunity here. And then what will be the response of that businessman? Immediately he will catch on to that opportunity. He will say, yes, let me go there. Let me start one more branch. Let me start one more business venture. That's what he is. He will not be thinking, no, I am only one person. How can I do this? No need. It's okay. Let us leave this opportunity. You can go to some other businessman and give that opportunity. He will never say that. Continuously he will be snatching those, lapping on to those opportunities.

#### Service, Enthusiasm, Vaishnava Tradition
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Nf71MC-NeJs?t=773
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A devotee immediately latches on to doing that one more service. Not that so much service I am doing from the morning and suddenly one more service has come to me. So, you know, how can I do this? No. One should be Pravuddha Harsha. He becomes more enthusiastic. Yes, I want to do one more."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that a devotee should eagerly embrace additional service opportunities, becoming more enthusiastic rather than feeling burdened, and highlights the Vaishnava tradition where the spiritual master appreciates the disciple for providing opportunities for service.

So, similarly, a devotee means he is, if some more service has been offered, one more service, or one more. So, a devotee immediately latches on to doing that one more service. Not that so much service I am doing from the morning and suddenly one more service has come to me. So, you know, how can I do this? No. One should be Pravuddha Harsha. He becomes more enthusiastic. Yes, I want to do one more. Jai Radha Govinda Ki Jai. Sanchojitaha. Infused. Here Vidura is asking questions, so he is infusing that enthusiasm. So, similarly, somebody will come to you and say, do one more service. Actually, it is infusing. One more opportunity is infusing in your life. But generally we become very upset with him. See, so much I am working and one more service. But here you see, he is very happy with Vidura. Actually, we will see his answers in the next shloka. Maitreya Muni glorifies Vidura. Oh, you are so nice. You have given me one more opportunity to talk on the transcendental activities of the Lord. You are so wonderful. You will see he glorifies Vidura first of all. Although he is a spiritual master, Vidura is a disciple, but still he glorifies the disciple. You see this tradition only in Vaishnavism. In Vaishnava Sampradaya, a spiritual master congratulates and thanks the disciple because he has been given one more opportunity to serve Krishna.

#### Spiritual Energy, Humility, Preaching

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Real humility means, how I can preach more and more. This is real humility."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts genuine spiritual energy with material energy, emphasizing that true humility lies in eagerness to preach and serve, rather than artificial humility that avoids service.

This is the mood of the devotees. So here, Sanchodita Prahasan is not answering grudgingly. Oh, one more question. So many questions you have asked. Hours have passed by. Now one more question. Some more questions. No, Prahasan Niva is actually smiling. He is not asking bring me one more plate of some Prasad or something. Already my mouth is dried up. How can I go on answering your question? He is not saying. He is very happy. So this is spiritual life, spiritual energy. If somebody is really absorbed in service, chanting and hearing, if he really hears, if he really serves nicely, actually he gets spiritual energy. He is working under the shelter of spiritual energy. Middle energy means tiredness, boring, no happiness. This is material energy. Spiritual energy means encouraging, pravardha harsha, becomes very enthusiastic and he wants to, and he will be smiling and moving around, doing more and more, more and more. So, particularly in our movement, devotees have to be very enthusiastic to preach. If there is an opportunity to preach, smiling, happy, and he should go and preach. Yes, I am ready. One class after another class. He should not say, no, no, I don't want to. Real humility means, how I can preach more and more. This is real humility. Artificial humility means, no, no, no, I don't want to. I cannot preach. This is artificial humility. Real humility means, he is ready to preach more and more. What do I know to preach? I am not very qualified, so let me not. No.

**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker defines real humility as being guru-sevi, a servant of the spiritual master, and emphasizes the importance of following the guru's order to preach, regardless of one's perceived qualifications.

Humility means, one should be guru-sevi. That means, a servant of the spiritual master. There are three terms here. Guru-sevi, then guru-bhogi. He wants to enjoy guru's facilities. And then, there is, he wants to serve, or he wants to, one more is what? Guru-thiyagi. He gives up the order of guru. Guru's order is what? Preach. And he has shown by his personal example. Preach. Go on preaching. Take advantage of the opportunities. Now there are online opportunities are available. Technology is there. Simply you announce some topic, somebody will come. Maybe two people, three people, somebody will come. Just repeat what you heard. No, no, no, I am not qualified to preach. Then we will become guru-thiyagi. One has to be guru-sevi. Yes, I am not qualified. That's fine. But guru is asking me to preach. That's all. On the order of the spiritual master, I will preach. That's all. Whether I am qualified or not qualified, that's different.

#### Guru's Order, Preaching, Prabhudatta Desha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Nf71MC-NeJs?t=1115
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The places that are assigned, allocated for me, the service that is allocated for me, I have to preach. I have to take that as Prabhudatta."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a pastime about Prabhupada instructing Harikesh Maharaj to preach in a remote place, emphasizing that assigned places and services should be embraced as Prabhudatta desha, allocated for preaching.

There is one pastime where Prabhupada is asking Harikesh Maharaj to go and preach. And that place is some, you know, very corner, some place, some new place. Chaitanya Mahaprabhu also like that. He told his associates, go here, go there, preach. He told Rupa Sanatana to go to Vrindavan. Excavate the places and you preach there. Write books. Like that he has assigned some services to these associates and sent them to various places. Chaitanya Charitamrita says such places should be taken as Prabhudatta desha. The places that are assigned, allocated for me, the service that is allocated for me, I have to preach. I have to take that as Prabhudatta. Datta. In Telugu, Dattata means you have adopted. You have adopted the place to preach. Not for enjoying, not for exploitation, but to preach. So, an opportunity is given, a place of service has been given. One has to be very eager. Yes, I have to take up this and then preach.

#### Preaching, Transcendental Bliss, Surrender to Guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Nf71MC-NeJs?t=1209
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When will my mind be completely surrendered unto your lotus feet? At that time, only by your mercy, I shall be able to get relief from all obstacles to spiritual life."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how preaching Bhagavatkathaya can give transcendental bliss, and emphasizes the importance of surrendering to the spiritual master to overcome obstacles in spiritual life, quoting a verse by Sridhara Swami.

So, preaching Bhagavatkathaya, the transcendental activities of the Lord, they are very prahasaniva. They can actually give us the transcendental bliss. Of course, sometimes we feel very enthusiastic to preach. Sometimes we may not feel so much enthusiastic. Because that is our current condition. As long as the anarthas are there in the mind, in the heart, sometimes we will be very enthusiastic, and sometimes we will not be very enthusiastic. That ups and downs will be there. But, then what is our inspiration to preach then? That Prabhudatta desha. The inspiration is the order of the spiritual master. My Guru has told me, so I have to do it. Then you will get inspiration. Then you will get all the... So, here in one place, Sridhara Swami, one of the original commentators of Srimad Bhagavatam in our parampara, he composes a nice verse. And then, there he says, All merciful spiritual master, representative of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Spiritual master has to be seen as an intimate representative of the Supreme Personality. When will my mind be completely surrendered unto your lotus feet? This is the prayer to the spiritual master. What is that? When will my mind be completely surrendered unto your lotus feet?

At that time, only by your mercy, I shall be able to get relief from all obstacles to spiritual life. Sometimes we feel there are too many obstacles. Not able to move an inch. Not able to proceed further. Not able to progress. We feel like that. So, Sridhara Swami is saying, how we should pray to the spiritual master. So, he is asking when will my mind be completely surrendered unto your lotus feet? At that time, by your mercy, all these obstacles will be relieved. Removed from my life. And I shall be situated in blissful life. Now, this is the secret to get bliss. What is that? When our mind is completely attached to, surrendered unto the lotus feet of spiritual master. What is the meaning? Yes, one is to meditate on the lotus feet of the spiritual master. That is good. But really, the surrenderance means how we can take this movement to as many people as possible. Real surrenderance means how I can preach this message. Prabhupada wanted me to do this. So, if somebody takes the order of the spiritual master very seriously and try to execute that order as much as possible, then how will Krishna see this? If you try to take up Guru's order, how will Krishna see this?

#### Krishna's Observation, Guru's Order, Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Nf71MC-NeJs?t=1425
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Regardless of whatever stage one is in, when I see that one is engaged seriously and sincerely in discharging the duties ordered by the spiritual master... that person becomes most dear to me."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Krishna, as the Supersoul, observes everyone's activities and is most pleased when someone sincerely and seriously dedicates their life to fulfilling the orders of the spiritual master, regardless of their material qualifications or circumstances.

Now, Krishna explains himself. Very interesting. He sees as the super soul of the living entities sitting in everyone's heart. Krishna is present in everyone's heart as the super soul. He is present. So, he is saying as super soul, I observe everyone's activity in every stage and order of life. In every stage. We may be in deep trouble. We may be having a lot of obstacles and this and that. Every stage. So, Krishna is saying I observe everyone's activity regardless of which stage one is in. Now, Krishna is saying you may be in whatever stage. Regardless of whatever stage one is in, when I see that one is engaged seriously, and sincerely in discharging the duties ordered by the spiritual master, then what happens? And is thus dedicating his life to the service of the spiritual master, that person becomes most dear to me. So Krishna is saying, who becomes most dear? If somebody is sincerely and seriously executing the orders of the spiritual master. It doesn't matter whatever is the stage, whatever may be the condition.

---
#### Guru's Order, Devotion, Krishna's Grace
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Nf71MC-NeJs?t=1559
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you are enthusiastic to execute the order and dedicate your time and energy, dedicating life means your time and energy for executing this order of the spiritual master, then He says, He becomes very dear."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to emphasize that Krishna values sincere effort in following the guru's orders, regardless of obstacles, and that dedicating time and energy to this service makes one dear to Krishna, offering a formula for attaining Krishna's grace.

People are not cooperating or I am having health issues, I am having opposition, I am having this, I am having that. There could be innumerable reasons for the mind to tell us not to do anything. This is called RG. We don't want to, Saru, we don't want to cooperate. There could be innumerable issues. But Krishna is saying, I observe all this and I know what kind of a stage exactly He is in. Actually only Krishna understands what is our real difficulty. Nobody else will be able to understand. If you can explain, then other person may understand. And of course if you explain to one more devotee, devotees can understand to a little deeper level. But finally only Krishna knows our actual situation. Because He is observing us. So those and He is saying whatever stage you may be in, because Krishna understands that. But if you are enthusiastic to execute the order and dedicate your time and energy, dedicating life means your time and energy for executing this order of the spiritual master, then He says, He becomes very dear. You want to become very dear to Krishna? This is the formula. The Supreme Personality of Godhead will be smiling on you. If you try to execute the orders of the spiritual master. And for that we need to pray to Guru, offer our prayers. When will by my mind be attached to your instructions? Your orders? When will my mind be very focused on it? Only at that time all my challenges will be removed from my life. So and then if I execute the orders of the spiritual master, then I will be able to enjoy the blissful life.

#### Maya, Guru's Order, Enthusiasm
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Nf71MC-NeJs?t=1711
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Anything that is stopping you from executing the order of the spiritual master that is actually not Shastra. That is Maya."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that any thinking that opposes service or preaching is Maya, even if seemingly based on Shastra, and emphasizes the importance of vigilance and enthusiasm in serving the spiritual master.

Okay, so any any kind of a thinking, which is opposing service or which is opposing preaching, that is Maya. You may have a great reason why we are not preaching or why I am not doing some service or why I am not being very enthusiastic, why I am not trying to cooperate or enhance the services or push on this movement. We may have a lot of reasons and calculations. Apparently sometimes based on Shastra also. But then anything that is stopping you from executing the order of the spiritual master that is actually not Shastra. That is Maya. So that's why one has to be very vigilant, see how much enthusiastic he is to serve. And Prabhupada says in one place, if somebody chants nicely his rounds and hears, then automatically he becomes very very enthusiastic. So let us not lose this opportunity. Enthusiastic the way a business man is very enthusiastic to accumulate more and more money, more and more money. Similarly devotee he is very enthusiastic to accumulate more and more services in his account. More service, more service, more, more, more chanting, more rounds, more reading, any opportunity he takes very gladly. He thanks those people who gave that opportunities. He becomes grateful to Guru. He becomes very happy. And then smilingly he does his services. So this is these are some of the points that we can understand from this purport. Any questions?

Executing our service. Whatever stops, it's Maya. You told sometimes it may be based on the Shastra also. What is the example, Prabhu? Same. I mentioned in the class humility. I am not qualified. #### Guru's Order, Preaching, Purification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Nf71MC-NeJs?t=1935
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada knows, if he follows the order of Guru, that gives the purification. Rest of the things are all external."
**Summary:** The speaker shares the full pastime of Prabhupada and Harikesh Maharaj, emphasizing that following the guru's order is paramount and provides purification, even if it means facing difficult circumstances or compromising external considerations.

Actually I have not completed that past time. Harikesh Maharaj, he sends him to some place for preaching. Some obscure place it is. And Harikesh Maharaj says no, how can I go there? There is no association. Ropa says forget about this association. Go and preach. My order. Prabhupada's order has to be followed. Then he says Prabhupada, I may not get Prasadam there. Forget about it. Go there. And not only that I may not get Prasadam, I may not even get vegetarian food. I may have to eat meat. Doesn't matter. Go. Eat whatever you want. Preach. He says, no if I eat meat and all that, I will fall down. My consciousness will be polluted. Hell with your consciousness. Go and preach. Prabhupada knows, if he follows the order of Guru, that gives the purification. Rest of the things are all external. You see. Why? Krishna is saying personally, how sincerely, how seriously you are following the order of Guru. That's what I see. Krishna is interested to see how much the disciples are following. Then he becomes very dear to Krishna. The Supreme Personality of God. If you want to become dear to Krishna, this is the formula. Keep out all the considerations. Nicely I have to, sincerely I have to execute the order. Of course it doesn't mean that we have to break all the principles. The story and the memory whatever that is told here, Harikesh Maharaj what it means that Guru's order takes the prominence. You may be in many unfortunate stage, conditions. Doesn't matter. Your goal should be, how I can execute that order? How? How? How? Keep praying to Prabhupada. Keep praying to Krishna. You will get the strength. You will get the strength and then jump into the service. Don't think too much. Order has been given, execute. That's all.

#### Guru's Order, Execution, Vaishnava Support
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Nf71MC-NeJs?t=2079
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Guru's order is available. Just simply you have to jump and do. That's all."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing the importance of simply executing the guru's order without overthinking, seeking support from Vaishnavas to execute it nicely, and ensuring that Guru's order is always maintained.

Many times we try to consider the order. Is that order okay? Shall I do this? This and that. You need not have to calculate about what is the order. Guru's order is available. Just simply you have to jump and do. That's all. How to do nicely? Yes. Take help of the Vaishnavas. The senior Vaishnavas, authorities. Take their support. Execute the order nicely and then that will please Guru and Krishna. This is our life. Okay? So that's what I mean when I said apparently it looks Shastric but somewhere he has gone away. How do you come to know that he has wrongly calculated? Just see that if there is an opposition in his mind either to preach or to do some seva or to do some order of Prabhupada, that means calculation is not correct. So that's why we should be very careful. Ensure that Guru's order is always maintained.

### Having Harmonious Devotee Relationships | Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa | SB 3.21.48 | 11.12.2025
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### etiquette, humility, respect
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LSkMjHU7DWM?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Similarly, it was the duty of the hermit to offer blessings to teens to approach his hermitage in the general."
**Summary:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa reads and translates Srimad-Bhagavatam 3.21.48, focusing on the etiquette shown by Emperor Swayambhuva Manu towards the hermit Trathama.

Om Namo Bhadravate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad-Bhagavatam, 10-3, Chapter 21, Verse 48: Athotajam upayatam nridevam pranatam purah Saparyayaprayatrinath pratinandyanu rupayah. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhattivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada: Seeing that the monarch had come to his hermitage and was bowing before him, the sage treated him with benediction and received him as his son. He blessed him with due honor. Purport by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhattivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada: Emperor Swayambhuva Manu not only approached the cottage of dried leaves possessed by the hermit Trathama, but also offered respectful obeisances unto him. Similarly, it was the duty of the hermit to offer blessings to teens to approach his hermitage in the general. Thus ends the Parthivedanta Parvat.

---
#### etiquette, humility, Vaishnava relationships
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LSkMjHU7DWM?t=87
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, this is called etiquette. When a king approaches a sadhu, a hermit, or an exalted personality like Kardhama Muni, how he has to behave, how he has to offer his obeisances, that is explained here."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the importance of Vaishnava etiquette, drawing parallels from the behavior of Swayambhuva Manu towards Kardhama Muni, emphasizing humility and respect in interactions with Vaishnavas.

So we are reading from the conversation between Manu and Kardhama. Manu refers to Swayambhuva Manu who approaches a hermitage of Kardhama Muni. So in this shloka, it is mentioned that Athochatam Upayatam. When the monarch approached the hermitage, immediately, Pranatam Puraha he offered his obeisances. And then, Kardhama Muni also, he received him. He greeted him. And then, Pratinandhya Anurupaya he has offered his honor, Jew honor to the emperor Swayambhuva Manu. Prabhupada is specifically saying in the purport that the cottage of dried leaves, you know, Kardhama Muni, is a hermit, is living in a cottage of dried leaves. Whereas, Swayambhuva Manu, because he is an emperor, he is a ruler, he is living, he must be living in a big palace, which is bedocked with jewels and ornaments. And such an emperor, he comes to a hermitage made up of dried leaves. And he is paying obeisances to Kardhama Muni. So, this is the position of a great devotee, like Kardhama Muni. Swayambhuva Manu is also an exalted personality. He is known as Mahajana, a pure devotee of the Lord. And he, when he approached this hermit, Kardhama Muni, immediately he paid his obeisances. So, this is called etiquette. When a king approaches a sadhu, a hermit, or an exalted personality like Kardhama Muni, how he has to behave, how he has to offer his obeisances, that is explained here.

#### etiquette, Krishna Consciousness, obstacles
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LSkMjHU7DWM?t=261
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So, Vaishnava etiquette is defined, it is mentioned by great authorities, and it is said that etiquette is the ornament for a Vaishnava."
**Summary:** The lecture emphasizes the importance of Vaishnava etiquette for progress in Krishna Consciousness, defining it as the proper way to treat other Vaishnavas, the public, donors, and well-wishers.

So, when we come into contact with this movement, we are coming into the association of a lot of Vaishnavas. And when we approach such Vaishnavas, there is an etiquette that is recommended. One may be a very exalted personality, like Swayambhuva Manu, but when he approaches a Vaishnava, or a great sadhu, he has to follow a certain etiquette. So, Vaishnava etiquette is defined, it is mentioned by great authorities, and it is said that etiquette is the ornament for a Vaishnava. And so, when we follow such etiquette, then we will progress rapidly in Krishna Consciousness. Without following Vaishnava etiquette, we will face a lot of obstacles in our advancement. Now, Vaishnava etiquette means, as a Vaishnava, how should I treat other Vaishnavas? How should I treat the public? How should I treat the donors, the well-wishers, the ones who have started chanting? In various aspects of our life, how we have to conduct ourselves, that is Vaishnava etiquette. So, in this slokas of Srimad Bhagavatam, we come to know these aspects of Vaishnava etiquette.

#### Vaishnava society, senior devotees, gratitude
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LSkMjHU7DWM?t=372
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, one should be very grateful and obliged. And whenever they meet such senior Vaishnavas, we should greet them."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the divisions within Vaishnava society (senior, equal, and junior devotees) and emphasizes the importance of showing respect, gratitude, and avoiding envy towards senior devotees.

So, for example, here, he is such an exalted personality, great ruler, but still, he is offering his obeisances to Kaddhamamuni. So, similarly, in a Vaishnava society, Bhagavatam explains, Prabhupada also explains, that there are three divisions are there. Senior devotees, and then equal devotees, and then junior devotees. And the conduct with respect to senior devotees is that, we have to show our great respect, offer our obeisances, and then show our gratitude, obligation. Somebody who has, you know, who has been practicing this Krishna consciousness for many years, contributing to this mission, somebody who has joined before me, and then instrumental in building this Yatra mission here, and by their hard work, today I am able to practice Krishna consciousness, nicely, comfortably. So many spiritual facilities have been given to me, by the great tapas sacrifice of my senior Vaishnavas. So, one should be very grateful and obliged. And whenever they meet such senior Vaishnavas, we should greet them. The details of paying obeisances, when and where, sometimes they are defined, based on time, place and circumstances. But anywhere, we can always smile, with a great smile we can offer our greetings to the Vaishnavas. Skanda Purana says, when we see a Vaishnava, we should smile, you know, we should be happy to see a Vaishnava. And if we are not happy, and smiling, that means, it is an offense. So, when it comes to senior Vaishnavas, one regulation is that, one should not become envious of the seniors, at any point of time. Apparently seniors may be, apparently looking to be very comfortable, and having many great facilities than, what I could get. But then, as I said, one should also realize, that those Vaishnavas have, their sacrifice and their austerity, also we should remember. And if there, somebody is a senior to me, in a position, one should recognize, that is Krishna's wish, Krishna's sanction, that he is in such a position. So one should not become envious, of the senior Vaishnavas.

#### humility, menial service, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LSkMjHU7DWM?t=591
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Because the whole process of Krishna Consciousness is, how we can become humble."
**Summary:** The speaker explains why new devotees are often asked to perform menial services, emphasizing that it is to cultivate humility and surrender, which are essential for advancement in Krishna Consciousness.

For example, we may see, somebody in the movement, for say 10 years, 20 years, or whatever, but everybody goes through the same process. I remember during our days, we used to do Mandiram Arjuna, for 3 years. What is Mandiram Arjuna? We are supposed to clean toilets, and you know, clean the rooms, of other senior Vaishnavas. So this was the training, it is not that it is happening only to, the new joiners, everybody in this movement, has come to a certain position, through the same process. Now why is that, when we join the movement, or in the beginning days of Krishna Consciousness, why is it that we are asked to do, such menial services? Removing the mats, dusting in the outside, keeping them safely, cleaning the floor, or serving other Vaishnavas. Why is it that this is recommended? Because the whole process of Krishna Consciousness is, how we can become humble. Humility. Humility means to understand that, I am insignificant. I am very insignificant person. And then I have to surrender. I have to surrender to Krishna, I have to surrender to the process, that is given by Krishna, Guru and Krishna. So, that humility begins by doing, you know, these menial services. Otherwise, the feeling inside is, I am such a big person, I am studying so and so, I am an engineer, or I am a doctor, or I am an IT professional. You know, we all feel, that actually I am very superior. But then, when we approach, a temple like this, or when we approach Vaishnavas, one, you know, one has to become humble, and immediately pay obeisances, to the devotees, who have sacrificed, their life, their ambitions, the so called, the material ambitions, to serve the mission of Srila Prabhupada. Actually, by giving respect to these Vaishnavas, by serving them, one becomes quickly advanced, in Krishna Consciousness. In fact, if you want a rapid advancement, better serve and take blessings, of the Vaishnavas first.

Respectful means, although sometimes, apparently some, actions or words, appear to be, not very respectful, but still, one has to, respect such Vaishnavas. Sometimes, seniors are, no, the Vaishnavas may, appear to be very harsh. They may speak little harsh words, or, stern words. Sometimes, when we do some, small mistakes, sometimes, you see, you know, some devotees may, speak little harsh words. So, Prabhupada says, what is the general etiquette, towards the, the seniors? So, he says, it is general etiquette, that superiors are, not to be offered, even a verbal fight. Even if they are, sometimes, harsh in behavior, they should not be, harshly treated. Even if they are harsh, we should not, harshly treat them back. In Vedic, history, we see this, this kind of a behavior. For example, Narada Muni, cursed, Nalakuvera, to become, trees, a very harsh, treatment. But then, we don't hear, that Nalakuvera has, become vengeful, on Narada Muni, and try to treat, Narada Muni, in the same way. No. The etiquette is that, even if, apparently, the seniors, Vaishnavas, become harsh, we should not, harshly treat them back. Rather,

#### blessings, introspection, humility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LSkMjHU7DWM?t=1014
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Rather, we should accept, that as, you know, a blessing, that has come, through, such senior, Vaishnavas."
**Summary:** The speaker encourages listeners to accept harsh treatment from senior Vaishnavas as a blessing and to practice introspection, focusing on their own faults rather than those of others.

Rather, we should accept, that as, you know, a blessing, that has come, through, such senior, Vaishnavas. Look at the blessings, of Nala and, Nalakuvera, I mean, Nalakuvera. What is the blessing? They could, directly, have darshan, of the supreme, personality, of Godhead, Krishna. So, similarly, in, good, intention, sometimes, senior devotees, may chastise, or, may, speak some harsh words, for our benefit, we should take it as, a blessing. Some, so, Lord Brahma, he gives, what should be the mood, whenever there is a, a, harsh, environment, or a, reversal in life, he says, one should take it as, Swayankrutam. It is my own, past, whatever I have done, now it is coming, but because, I am in, Vaishnava association, that reaction is, coming down, in a very small quantity, through that, Vaishnava. If I were to be, outside, mostly, somebody would have taken, a chappal and, beaten me. But then, here, you know, the senior Vaishnava, because he is also, exercising restraint, he knows, the value of, other, other Vaishnava, so, actually, it gets minimized. So, one should, it's all about, how, we take it, in our, mind. Very important, there's so many events, happen on a day to day, basis, but, how you, take that incident, into your heart, that is the most, crucial point. So, if we can, you know, take it, on the basis of, Shastra, then, we are safe. That same, incident, somebody can take it, different. So, if we can, you know, take it, into our, mind. Oh, he's like this, he's like that, he's always like this, he's very harsh, I'm actually, very good man, I'm a very good boy, I'm a very, nice person, but, look at this, you know, so, this is one more tendency, of the conditioned mind, that it always, wants to say, that, I am pakka, but, the problem, entire problem, is on, the other person. We always, look for, faults in the other person, but, very rarely, we try to, look at the faults, in ourselves. Actually, this quality of, trying to look for faults, in us, is called, introspection. And, that is a symptom of, Satuguna.
