#### Srila Prabhupada, respect, Satvaguna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LSkMjHU7DWM?t=1272
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "I could not hold myself. Such an extraordinary person is. He is in full Satvaguna."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates a story about Jaya Govinda's father, a proud and minimizing man, who unexpectedly paid obeisances to Srila Prabhupada upon seeing his majestic presence, recognizing his Satvaguna.

His father, to come and see, Sri Prabhupada, very reluctantly, he agrees. Out of affection, for the son, he comes, to the temple. So, when he came, to the temple, he was asked, to remove, his shoes. Immediately, he said, what is this? You know, I have to remove, my shoes? You know, so, then, Jaya Govinda, instead of, giving some explanation, he said, you know, there is some, Sankirtan going on, why don't you come, you know, participate in Sankirtan, let us go inside, like that. So, the father goes inside, and then, deities are there, devotees are there, Sankirtan is going on, and he, he is just, normally standing, and watching. And, suddenly, the Prabhupada quarters, that door, opens up. And, Sri Prabhupada is, coming out, from the room, with a stick, with a cane, in his hand, and, you know, very, regally, aristocratic, walk. You know, he comes out from this, room, and then he, walks up to, the deities. Then, as soon as, Prabhupada entered the room, all the devotees, they paid obeisances. Now, this man, who is standing, like a stick, he also, immediately paid, obeisances too. He kneeled down, on the floor, immediately paid, obeisances, and then he, Jayagovinda was shocked. What is happening? You know, what is happening? This, this man, never used to be, like this. So then, Prabhupada, when he is paying obeisances, somehow, Prabhupada is coming, that side only. And then, he looked at both, Jayagovinda, and the person, next to him. Prabhupada would have, spotted, some similarities. He, he knows, must be father. And, he smiles at both of them, and then, he walks away.

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of conducting oneself properly as a devotee, as one's behavior reflects on the spiritual master and the Krishna Consciousness movement, and discusses the need for humility and appreciation when preaching to others.

So, later on, Jayagovinda asks, his father, father, why did you pay, obeisances, when Prabhupada came? He says, I could not, hold myself. Such an, extraordinary, person is. He is in full, Satvaguna. Of course, according to his, calculation, he is full Satvaguna, and as soon as I saw, I wanted to pay, my obeisances, to him. See, the experience, that Prabhupada had. Just by seeing him, people used to pay, obeisances, offer respects. In fact, one, in one more, memory, somebody told, that as soon as, Prabhupada, comes out of the car, the way he comes out, you know, the, the majesty, with which he, opened the door, and he comes out, you know, immediately, he decided, this is my Guru. Maybe, it's a very, what to say, the, initial feeling, but, Prabhupada commanded, that kind of a, respect. So, as, practicing devotees, are, the ones, who are practicing, from, staying outside, we should conduct, ourselves, in such a way, that the society, respects, Vaishnavas. Respects, Srila Prabhupada, and respects, our Krishna Consciousness, movement. This is a very important, responsibility, on each one of us. Prabhupada says, in one place, people will look at, your conduct, and they will, comment on me. They will comment on, they will criticize, the spiritual master. Why? If somebody is behaving, in a different way, not against the, against the norms, of the Vaishnava culture, then, they will, they will, they will, they will, they will, they will, they will, they will, they will, they will, they will, they will, they will, they will, they will, they will, they will, they will, criticize. Then, people will criticize. What kind of a guru? What kind of a training? So, one has to be, very careful, that, be, very, properly, behave. What should be, our etiquette, when dealing with, outsiders, because, many of the preachers, are many of the, ones, you know, How we should deal with others, particularly while preaching, it is said that we should approach them with all humility, glorify them, and then when they are a little softened, and then we say, Sarchant Hare Krishna. This should be our process. We should not think, I know much better than him, I know the highest philosophy, I am serving supreme personality of Godhead, I am a servant of him, and then, what is he? He is serving Maya. You know, he is, you know, all kinds of, so, that kind of a behavior will alienate people. They will think, what is this, you know, this person is so, you know, so rough.

#### preaching, humility, appreciation

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Our behavior should be in such a way that that person is not alienated, doesn't become inimical towards us, towards the movement, but rather he becomes very appreciative."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of humility and appreciation when preaching, suggesting that devotees should appreciate others' current positions and devotion before introducing Krishna Consciousness.

So that's why each one of us should learn the way to approach others, and this is the technique given by Prabodhananda Saraswati. When we go and preach, because they are all, they feel that they are very, very exalted, very advanced. So when we go to them, we should actually appreciate them. Appreciate their current position, appreciate that little devotion that they are showing, at least he has allowed me to come and enter into his house, appreciate, and then slowly you introduce Krishna Consciousness. It could be giving a book, or it could be giving, you know, or it could be for seeking some donation, it could be for explaining Bhagavad Gita, whatever it could be, our behavior should be in such a way that that person is not alienated, doesn't become inimical towards us, towards the movement, but rather he becomes very appreciative. In beginning days of Sri Prabhupada's time, once one donor says, I want to see your spiritual master. Then, you know, other disciples, they made arrangements, and then he came. He comes to Sri Prabhupada and says, immediately pays obeisances and says, I wanted to see you, because yesterday one of your disciples came to me, and the way he spoke about you and the Krishna Consciousness is so wonderful, I decided, I want to come and see you. So, when we go and come out from that house, they should feel, yes, I should come to Hare Krishna Mandir.

#### preaching, building bridges, compassion

**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "A preacher's goal should be to build bridge."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the importance of building bridges with people of different philosophies, emphasizing compassion and understanding rather than criticism, to help them cross over to Krishna Consciousness.

So, we should enhance the prestige of the institution, of the spiritual master. So then, it becomes very easy for us to convince him to take the next steps, highest steps in Krishna Consciousness. So, these are the various angles when we are dealing with seniors and outsiders. Sometimes, what happens is, devotees, when they see that others are not worshipping Krishna, or they are having a different philosophy, the tendency is to criticize their philosophies and criticize their worship. Because, after all, whoever comes to Krishna Consciousness, he gets such a higher philosophy, from a high point, he can see mistakes of every other thing, every other philosophy in this world. Automatically, his consciousness is so bright, you know, he can see everything that is happening, what is wrong and what is not correct, he comes to know. But that doesn't give us a license to criticize and minimize that person, and he becomes inimical and alienated from us. Our mood should be to build bridge with that person. That person is in the other side of the, you know, river, Vaitharini, and we are in this side, let us say. Unless you build a bridge, how that poor guy can walk and come? You can go on commenting, you are standing in a wrong position, what is this, what is that? You know, you are like this, you are like that, we can do that. But then where is the chance for that person to really cross that Maya and then come to this side? There is no possibility. So, a preacher's goal should be to build bridge.

#### preaching, building bridges, common ground
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LSkMjHU7DWM?t=1824
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Preacher has to tax his brain to find out how to communicate the message of Krishna consciousness."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the example of book distribution during the Ganesha festival to illustrate the importance of finding common ground and building bridges with people of different faiths to communicate the message of Krishna Consciousness.

We have to build bridges. For example, devotees, they go for book distribution during Ganesha festival. In Hyderabad, it is very big, Ganesha, Maharashtra, of course, throughout India. They make a very tall deity of Ganesha and then they do. So, using our influence, we got us tall, almost close to the deity. Now, who will come to see Ganesha? All Ganesha devotees only. Not that some Krishna devotees will come and purchase that book. All Ganesha devotees. Now, preacher's calculation is, how can I build a bridge? So that these Ganesha devotees also get attracted. Now, this should, otherwise where is the thinking for the preacher? Preacher has to tax his brain to find out how to communicate the message of Krishna consciousness. He has to tax. Where is the taxing? The taxing is in building the bridge. Now, what is the bridge here? You have to see the common point. What is the common point? Now, you want to sell Bhagavad Gita. Now, Bhagavad Gita is from Mahabharata. And Mahabharata is written by Ganesha. So, our devotees will say, Ganesha wrote. So, will you read or not? No, no, we will read. When it comes to Durga, they will say, sister, Krishna's sister. Very obedient sister, not like modern, very obedient. So, how she is obedient? Obedient to Krishna's words. And where are these Krishna's words? They are in Bhagavad Gita. So, you should also become obedient. Like that, we should build bridges. He could be any philosopher. We have to build a bridge, so that he can also cross that obstacle.

Now, equals means, the attitude inside should be, even if he has joined one or two days before me also, he has contributed that much. But generally, whoever got initiated together, so, say, you take them as equals. So, we need equals. We need friends. And then, the other devotees also can share, yes, yesterday I was reading this, yesterday I was reading that. #### devotional service, liberation, bhakti
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/U2Xij9gSPyY?t=39
**Verse References:** BG 4.9
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only by devotional service can one understand the transcendental nature of the Supreme Lord, and after understanding Him perfectly in His transcendental position, enter into the Kingdom of God."
**Summary:** This segment explains how Kardama Muni attained liberation through uncontaminated devotional service and achieved the ultimate goal of life, Bhagavati Gati.

Freed from all hatred and desire, Kardama Muni, being equal to everyone because of discharging uncontaminated devotional service, ultimately attained the path back to Godhead. As stated in Bhagavad Gita, only by devotional service can one understand the transcendental nature of the Supreme Lord, and after understanding Him perfectly in His transcendental position, enter into the Kingdom of God. The process of entering into the Kingdom of God is Tripadabhuti Gati, or the path back home, back to Godhead, by which one can attain the ultimate goal of life. Kardama Muni, by His perfect devotional knowledge and service, achieved this ultimate goal, which is known as Bhagavati Gati in the sense of the Bhagavad Gita. So this is the final shloka of this chapter.

#### renunciation, devotional service, purity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/U2Xij9gSPyY?t=96
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If the motivation is just to please my spiritual master, Guru, then automatically Krishna is pleased."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the renunciation of Kardama Muni and the importance of uncontaminated, pure devotional service, emphasizing that the motivation should be to please Guru and Krishna.

Of this chapter, renunciation of Kardama Muni, and the chapter ends with a blissful ending, Kardama Muni going back to Godhead. It is mentioned here that He went back to Godhead by Bhagavad Bhakti Yuktena, by rendering uncontaminated, pure devotional service. Contamination means these three modes. Uncontaminated means while doing devotional service, not contaminating by either sattva guna, or raja guna, or tamo guna. It has to be pure. What is pure? The motivation to do that service is pleasing Guru and Krishna. That's all very simple.

If the motivation is just to please my spiritual master, Guru, and automatically Krishna is pleased. So, by discharging that uncontaminated devotional service, pure devotional service, there are two symptoms in Kardama Muni. Vihinena, he became free from two forces which act on everybody in this world, that is Ichcha and Dvesha. Ichcha Dvesha Vihinena, Sarvatra Samachetasa. So he became freed from Ichcha, that is desire, desiring something in this world, then Dvesha, hating something in this world. By becoming free from this desiring and hating, he became Samachetasa, equal. He has become equal to everyone.

Illusion. And if you are caught up in such illusions then how we can understand our eternal knowledge and our relation with Krishna. ### How Can I Identify Which Mode of Nature (Guna) Is Influencing Me? | Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### self-awareness, modes of nature, body as a machine
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tdSqa9p5P2o?t=26
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We are not this body, but we cannot be ignorant of this body."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the importance of understanding which mode of nature is influencing us and how the body, referred to as a 'yantra,' continuously influences us, requiring training through sadhana bhakti.

But in a devotee's life, how do we know in which mode we are? How do we reflect that I am being conducted by which mode, whether I am too much passionate, or I am under ignorance, or I am in sattva guna? How do we reflect, how do we know our standing, which mode we are conducted by? Actually, it is very important to understand that we are in which mode. To understand our own body and mind, our own, you know, this yantra, very important. We are not this body, but we cannot be ignorant of this body, because this yantra influences us every moment, everywhere. This yantra is continuously influencing us. And because the yantra was trained up for millions of years towards sense gratification, towards supremacy, towards possessing many things, it is important that we have to know what is this sadhana bhakti, nothing but training this yantra. You have to matter of duty means you're training this yantra, yes, you have to attend sadhana, you have to do your duty. It creates a lot of illusions. So that's why we should not be in ignorance about our own body.

#### eating habits, Krishna consciousness, prasadam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tdSqa9p5P2o?t=122
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "But the problem with us is there is no Krishna consciousness while eating."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of understanding our eating habits and their impact on our consciousness, advocating for eating with Krishna consciousness and being mindful of what suits our body.

We have to gradually understand where I stand. And so I would say that education about understanding ourselves, I would say it begins with our eating habits. It begins with the tongue, our devotional service. So it is good to understand our eating habits, whether this food is suiting me or it is not suiting me, what disturbs my consciousness. See actually, prashadam, if we properly honor with Krishna consciousness, there's no question of any trouble. But the problem with us is there is no Krishna consciousness while eating. We just eat like a normal eating and there is no consciousness maintained that this is Krishna's mercy and it's a transcendental food. There is no such thinking, there is no realization like that. So that's why we have to be careful to understand. Like Prabhupada was asked by that Bob in the book. He says, let us say, if there is a chili, Prabhupada was explaining that whatever is there in the plate you should eat. Everything is prashad. Then he is asking, if there is a chili then if it is, you know, for me it is. Then Prabhupada says, if your vision is that you are seeing chillis and tamarind and this and that, you should eat. You cannot artificially imitate a higher platform that it's all Brahman, it's a transcendent, it's Krishna's mercy. These things are not there. So then yes, you have to be careful. You have to understand what suits you and what doesn't suit you, what agitates your senses and what doesn't. And let us say if I eat many sweets in the night or if I overeat in the night, it has certain repercussions, we should understand. If you eat too much of paneer, then you may get sexually agitated, that you have to understand.

Here, you cannot walk into the Ashram Prasadam hall just like that and then, okay, agaya. You can go inside and so take one more plate of food and then your previously morning breakfast is not digested and you are not still feeling hungry and then you load another one more plate. So this, of course, this is called overeating and it has its own serious implications. So gradually devotee, because he is chanting and performing devotional service, he is able to see, you know. All these implications on his consciousness, how the body is trying to pull him down, he understands. And so gradually he rectifies all those things. #### aging, health, lifestyle
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/tdSqa9p5P2o?t=365

**Summary:** The speaker discusses how eating habits need to change as we age and shares an anecdote about a young man with numerous health issues, highlighting the unfortunate state of health in the outside world due to poor lifestyle choices.

Very, very important. Of course, in the beginning days we can eat well and it gets digested also. But as we grow older, you have to reduce the quantities, number of times and what type of items. Let us say somebody is not very careful about eating sweets, for example. As we grow older, it can lead to many other complications. Of course, nowadays diabetes, not only by sweets, by many other reasons it is coming into the... Now so many diseases are coming. Even youngsters, they are also having a lot of diseases. So very unfortunate. So by carefully understanding our body, what suits and what not suits, we can keep our bodily thing little on a sattvic platform. And then so we can execute our services. One boy joined in Hyderabad. He was, he is just 18 years. And in the application, joining form, we ask, you know, you mention if you have any health issues. And so, you know, he came for interview. And then I was going through his form. And then I came to this page where he has mentioned all his health issues. And I counted those health issues. He is having 18. 18 years and 18. I said, if you become 25, what will happen to you? What will happen to you? Will it become 25 or is it going to be something less? Very unfortunate in the outside world because they have no idea of managing their eating habits, sleeping habits and all this.

So we have to start as basic as that. And then gradually improve our... For example, with respect to work. You can see. When I have to do, let us say the immediate tendency is to postpone the work. That means ignorance is acting. Ignorant person only postpones. He has got a procrastination. Any time when you want to do some work, immediately mind says, No, not now. Later, later, later. So then you know, okay, ignorance is trying to influence me. Or... Or you have a lot of energy to do. But in the end, you are expecting that somebody should recognize me. Somebody should say, yes. Very good. Or at the end of some few years, Yes, I need some designation. No expectation. It may come on its own. You take that as prasadam and go on. But... You are not building up any hankering for it. Like that. This is one more important. So like that gradually you will see that there are lot of things to handle in this machine.

### How Did Mother Sita Nurture Luv and Kush? | Sri Sampati Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### Sita's character, Parenting, Ramayana, Dharma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/gri9cg9Fl8o?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Sita prepared Luv and Kush to go and sing the greatness of Ram's character."
**Summary:** The speaker argues that Sita's upbringing of Luv and Kush, teaching them to glorify Shri Ram, demonstrates her love and understanding of his dharma, contrasting it with modern examples of parents poisoning children's minds against each other after divorce.

If Sita was angry and upset with Shri Ram, why Ram abandoned me to the forest? She would have made Luv and Kush feel against Shri Ram. This is not the truth. If Sita was unhappy that why Ram abandoned me to the forest just because some common people are making some allegations against me, this is not the truth. She was not angry and upset with Shri Ram's decision. It is visible the way she brought up Luv and Kush. Luv and Kush are the first Katha Vachaks. They are the first people to speak Ramayana, Valmiki Ramayana. They were going everywhere, every village, nook and corner of Ayodhya and they were singing the glories of Lord Shri. If Sita was angry and upset with Shri Ram, with his decision, obviously she must have poisoned the children of Shri Ram. She would have made Luv and Kush feel against Shri Ram. Rather, she made them learn the glories of Shri Ram. That shows that Sita always carried love for Shri Ram. She understood the Dharma Sankat of Shri Ram. She was completely okay with Ram's decision because she did not poison the minds of Luv and Kush. Today we see when there is fight between husband and wife and there is some kind of injustice and there is divorce, sometimes the father tries to poison the minds of children against the mother or mother tries to poison the children's mind against the father. But that's not the case in Ramayana. Sita prepared Luv and Kush to go and sing the greatness of Ram's character. That is Ramayana. So Luv and Kush, they never carried any grudge against their father Shri Ram. They were very happy. They always felt that Ram is the greatest father. So that is because Sita happily trained Luv and Kush to sing the greatness of Lord Shri Ram.

### How Does the Naivedya Become Prasadam? | Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### love, devotion, prasadam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/JbMzaaVYTg8?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Even if the Lord glances on the food, that turns into Prasadam."
**Summary:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa explains how offering Naivedya to Krishna develops love through give and take, and how even a glance from the Lord transforms food into Prasadam.

When you go in front of the Lord, the Lord is watching us. He is hearing our prayers. He is accepting our Naivedya. He is giving back Prasad, and He is blessing the devotees. All interactions are going. This interaction means love. Love means there should be give and take. If you want to develop love with anybody, there should be give and take. Now we want to love the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Now you have to give, and He gives, and you should take. So now the devotees want to give some food. So Krishna says, "Asana me, I will accept." With devotion, if somebody offers some Naivedya, Krishna says, "I accept." Again, atheist Christian in this class, they may say, the plate came out as it is. God has not eaten anything. Their idea is that eating means God will eat the way I eat. We eat, we complete the whole plate, but God's eating is not that. His eating is very fine. It is said that even if the Lord glances on the food, that turns into Prasadam. And on that Prasadam we eat, remembering God.

### In-Depth Exploration: Assimilating the Knowledge of Krishna Consciousness I SB 3.25.25 I 07.12.2023
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### devotional service, association of devotees, liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X1y5oEBIu9k?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In the association of pure devotees, discussion of the pastimes and activities of the Supreme Personality of Godhead is very pleasing and satisfying to the ear and the heart."
**Summary:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa begins by reciting the verse SB 3.25.25 and its translation, emphasizing the importance of associating with pure devotees to advance in Krishna consciousness and attain liberation.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, 3rd Canto, 25th Chapter, Text No. 25: Satam Prasangan Mamavirya Samvido Bhavan Thirutkar Narasayanakatha Tajyoshana Adashvapavarga Vartmani Shraddharathir Bhaktiranukramishyati. Translation: In the association of pure devotees, discussion of the pastimes and activities of the Supreme Personality of Godhead is very pleasing and satisfying to the ear and the heart. By cultivating such knowledge, one gradually becomes advanced on the path of liberation and thereafter he is freed and his attraction becomes fixed. Then real devotion and devotional service begin.

---
#### association of devotees, personalism, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/X1y5oEBIu9k?t=71
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without the association of devotees, one cannot understand the activities of the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Srila Prabhupada's purport, stressing the necessity of associating with Krishna conscious individuals for spiritual advancement, understanding the personal aspect of the Absolute Truth, and developing genuine devotion.

Purport by Aishwarya Prabhupada: Teachings of Lord Kapila, the son of Devavati, Verses 25 and 26. The process of advancing in Krishna consciousness and devotional service is described here. The first point is that one must seek the association of persons who are Krishna conscious and who engage in devotional service. Without such association, one cannot make advancement. Simply by theoretical knowledge or study, one cannot make any appreciable advancement. One must give up the association of materialistic persons and seek the association of devotees because without the association of devotees, one cannot understand the activities of the Lord. Generally, people are convinced of this. They are convinced of the impersonal feature of the Absolute Truth. Because they do not associate with devotees, they cannot understand that the Absolute Truth can be a person and have personal activities. This is a very difficult subject matter and unless one has personal understanding of the Absolute Truth, there is no meaning to devotion. Service or devotion cannot be offered to anything impersonal. Service must be offered to a person. Non-devotees cannot appreciate Krishna consciousness. One must have the personal activity of the Lord. The transcendental activities of the Lord are described in the Vedic literature by reading the Srimad Bhagavatam or any other Vedic literature wherein the activities of the Lord are described. They think that these activities are fictional, manufactured stories because spiritual life is not explained to them in the proper mood. To understand the personal activities of the Lord, one has to seek the association of devotees and by such association, when one contemplates and tries to understand the transcendental activities of the Lord and he is freed, one who has firm faith in the supreme personality of godhead becomes fixed and his attraction for association with the lord and the devotees increases. Association with devotees means association with the lord. The devotee who makes this association develops the consciousness for rendering service to the lord and then being situated in the transcendental position of devotional service he gradually becomes perfect. The sense the bhaktivinoda is perfect this is part of the that shloka book also the selected verses that the devotees have compiled.

### Increasing Our Absorption in Service of Lord Krishna | Sri Satya Gaurachandra Dasa | SB 3.2.6 |
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### devotional service, spiritual world, love of God
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lJ3X882D4ag?t=162
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The devotee gardener is in touch with the abode of the Lord even from the material plane by dint of performing devotional service to the Lord simply by hearing and chanting."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the creeper of devotional service growing to Goloka Vrindavana and how a devotee can be in touch with the Lord through devotional service.

The creeper of devotional service gradually grows so high that it passes through the entire material universe and enters into the spiritual sky, growing still higher and higher until it reaches the planet Goloka Vrindavana. The devotee gardener is in touch with the abode of the Lord even from the material plane by dint of performing devotional service to the Lord simply by hearing and chanting. As a creeper takes shelter of another, stronger tree, similarly, the creeper of devotional service, nourished by the devotee, takes shelter of the lotus feet of the Lord and thus becomes fixed. When the creeper is fixed, then the fruit of the creeper comes into existence and the gardener who nourished it is able to enjoy this fruit of love and his life becomes successful. That Uddhava attained this stage is evident from his dealings. He could simultaneously reach this place.

---
#### Krishna consciousness, absorption, bhakti yoga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lJ3X882D4ag?t=222
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The whole process of bhakti yoga is aimed at developing this full absorption in Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Uddhava's devotional ecstasy upon hearing Krishna's name and how bhakti yoga aims to develop full absorption in Krishna, which is hindered by contaminations in the heart.

So we are reading from the chapter titled Remembrance of Lord Krishna. In this chapter, Vidura approaches Uddhava and asks about Krishna's activities and past times. As Uddhava approaches Uddhava, as soon as he hears the holy name of Krishna, he immediately goes into devotional ecstasy. And in this verse, Shanakair Bhagaval Lokan, he enters into the spiritual world by his meditation on the past times of the Lord. And so in this verse we see that Shanakair, slowly he came back from Bhagaval Lokan from the plane of the transcendental world to Nulokam Punaragata. He came back to this material plane and then Vimrujya Netre he wiping his eyes, Viduram, he saw Vidura. And then Vimrujya Netre he wiping his eyes, Viduram, and then he started remembering the Krishna's past times. And in the next slokas we are going to see, the most exalted devotee Uddhava speaking about Krishna to Vidura. So here in the first line Prabhupada says, the third line exerts down my ground is, says when Uddhava was fully absorbed in the transcendental ecstasy of love of God, he actually forgot all about the external world. So here the key word is fully absorbed in the transcendental ecstasy of love of God, so he is so much absorbed that he forgot all about the external world. So the whole process of bhakti yoga is aimed at developing this full absorption in Krishna. As we practice this bhakti yoga, quickly we understand that our mind is not resting on Krishna. Full absorption is only possible intermittently. We are not able to really be absorbed 24 hours in Krishna, but here Uddhava is in a different plane, complete absorption in Krishna. And why we are not able to absorb in Krishna completely? Because there are many contaminations in the heart, the contaminations which are still attractive to us. There are so many contaminations in the heart, they are very, very attractive to us because we are not able to absorb in Krishna, and because of those attractions we are not able to get attracted to Krishna and then develop full absorption. So in this path of bhakti yoga to develop this full absorption on Krishna, we need the shelter of an ecstatic lover of Krishna, a spiritual master who is having such absorption in Krishna, only he will be able to guide us.

---
#### spiritual master, transformation, Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lJ3X882D4ag?t=479
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Anybody who takes comes in touch with Prabhupada, he will for sure conquer his contamination in the heart and gradually develop this full absorption."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a story about a devotee bringing an author to the temple to discuss a project, highlighting the author's smoking habit and the devotee's reaction.

So anybody who takes comes in touch with Prabhupada, he will for sure conquer his contamination in the heart and gradually develop this full absorption. So then next day we reminded the middle man and then he called the author and said, you know, Iskand people are still following up with me. What do you say? Then the author said, if the Swamijis are confident that I can do this work, I can attempt. He has taken a humble approach. And then we said, yeah, we are confident that he can do. So our devotee went to his house to bring him to the temple so that he can have the darshan of the deities and then also we can discuss about the project. So when the devotee went to his house, the house is full of smoke, thick smoke. He is all pervading in his house. That smoke is cigarette smoke. It's not a, what do you call, nice fragrance or something. It's a cigarette smoke. And then the devotee was trying to wade through that smoke to see where the author is sitting. Suddenly he found in the corner most room, dark, little light is there, smoke is coming out from that room. We understood. Okay, this is the source of this man. So he went inside. Already our devotee is coughing, he is trying to cover, he is trying to do all kinds of things. And then so that author said, oh Swamiji, you have come here? Sorry, little bit. No, I cannot resist this. Please excuse me like that he told. And then he came to the temple. Then he had darshan. Then we had a discussion.

---
#### transformation, Prabhupada, inspiration
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/lJ3X882D4ag?t=825
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The education begins with character change this is the impact of a a bona fide spiritual master."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story of the author, detailing how he was encouraged to write Prabhupada's biography, his anxiety about the work, and ultimately, how translating a particular pastime inspired him to quit smoking.

We encouraged him, you know, give a, give a try. So he is, I gave him some ten books of Prabhupada's biographies written by many of his disciples, foreign disciples. And so the biography is available in English, but it has to be, you know, introduced in Telugu. So we gave him. We gave him, you know, some ten books in a big carton box. And then so he went away. And so I told him, you give us fifty pages to start with. And then we will see how it is and then we will take it forward. So then he went back. Really he was, I think he has spent a good time. He finished reading those ten books in one month. And in the next fifteen days he wrote fifty pages. And he has sent it to, you know, our devotee. So I got the hard copy, fifty pages. Somehow in the next one week I got a little busy. I could not read those fifty pages. This author, who is like a seventy-two year old, old man, very famous. He is a personality development guru also in Andhra. A very popular guru. And so he is in great anxiety. He is saying, what happened? Did Swamiji read? And did I pass or fail? Later on he was telling me, I went back to my school days. I was in great anxiety. What will happen? So then, you know, I read fifty pages. I mean, to be frank, he wrote very well. Came out very nicely. So he came and met me after one week. I said. I encouraged him. I said, wonderful job, you know. Please continue. Complete the work. So then he went back, I think within two months or two and a half months. He wrote the entire biography. He completed. And he sent the, again, book to us. Then I read the book. The whole book I read. And the book was narrated in his own style. And the style was so, it's like a suspense thriller, even for me. I read English biography many times. What is going to happen next? Next, next I was reading. And the final pastime, he writes a poem. If somebody really read that book, a new person to Krishna consciousness, if he reads that poem, actually he will cry. There would be tears in his eyes. Such a nice work he has done. So again he has come and met me. Then I said, what could be the best title for this book? Because see you are the author, no, Swamiji, you only decide. I said, see, how can I decide? You decide. You tell me, some two, three options you can give me, I told. So then, finally, he decided one name and then we also liked it and the title title you know what he gave was so then i asked i said this looks like you know conquest of the world i know conqueror of the world he said yes swamiji prabhupada conquered the world with his love not with the weapon so then we liked it that interpretation and then we then i was talking to him and then i asked him see you wrote the biography of such an acharya prabhupada are there any changes in you have you felt any changes then he said swamiji there is one instance in prabhupada's biography where one of his disciples is also into smoking and when i translated that particular pastime of prabhupada somehow it came strongly in my mind that i should also give up this habit and i gave up my 40 year old bad habit of smoking. We have not told that you have to stop smoking stop this stop that on his own he got that inspiration while translating that very particular thing he also got you know fully convinced and then much later after three four months again when i met him i asked him are you okay or are you gone back to your smoking no no swamiji i'm still following.

#### Invocation, Protection, Incarnations
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Cipl8AGhUlM?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "May Kalki Deva, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who appeared as an Incarnation to protect religious principles, protect me from the dirt of the age of Kali."
**Summary:** The speaker begins by reciting a verse from Srimad Bhagavatam, offering prayers for protection from ignorance, misuse of Vedic knowledge, and the negative influences of the Kali Yuga, invoking Vyasa Deva, Buddha Deva, and Kalki Deva.

OM NAMO BHAGWATHE VASUDEVAYA. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 6, Chapter 8, and Text 19. Dvaipayano Bhagavana Prabodhan Buddha Stupashanda Gana Pramadhan Kalki Hi Khalehe Kala Malaat Prapatoo Dharmavana Yoru Kruthavatha Raha. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivadanta Swamishya Prabhupada. Translation: May the Personality of Godhead in His Incarnation as Vyasa Deva protect me from all kinds of ignorance resulting from the absence of Vedic knowledge. May Lord Buddha Deva protect me from activities opposed to Vedic principles and from laziness that causes one to madly forget the Vedic principles of knowledge and ritualistic action. May Kalki Deva, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who appeared as an Incarnation to protect religious principles, protect me from the dirt of the age of Kali.

---
#### Vedic Knowledge, Incarnations, Kali Yuga, False Dharma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Cipl8AGhUlM?t=94
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every day they manufacture a new type of dharma on the plea that whatever one manufactures is also a path of liberation."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the importance of Vedic literature compiled by Vyasa Deva, the role of Lord Buddha in preventing animal killing misusing Vedic principles, and the future appearance of Kalki to vanquish atheists in Kali Yuga, emphasizing the dangers of pseudo-religious principles and the importance of surrendering to Krishna.

Purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivadanta Swamishya Prabhupada. This verse mentions various incarnations of the Supreme Personality of Godhead who appear for various purposes. Srila Vyasa Deva, Mahamuni, compiled the Vedic literature for the benefit of all human society. If one wants to be protected from the reactions of ignorance, even in this age of Kali, one may consult the books left by Srila Vyasa Deva, namely the four Vedas, Sama, Yajur, Ruggha, Natharva, the 108 Upanishads, Vedanta Sutra, Brahma Sutra, Mahabharata, Srimad Bhagavatam, Mahapurana, Vyasa Deva's commentary on the Brahma Sutra, and the other seven, the seventeen Puranas. Only by the mercy of Srila Vyasa Deva do we have so many volumes of transcendental knowledge to save us from the clutches of ignorance. As described by Srila Jayadeva Goswami in his Dashaavatara Stotra, Lord Buddha apparently decried the Vedic knowledge. nindasi yajna vidhe ahaha suci jhatam sadhaya hrudaya dharshita pashu ghatam keshavadruta buddhashitam nindasi yajna vidhe ahaha suci jhatam sadhaya hrudaya dharshita pashu ghatam keshavadruta buddhashitam nindasi yajna vidhe ahaha suci jhatam shareera jay jagadeshahare. The mission of Lord Buddha was to save people from the abominable activity of animal killing and to save the poor animals from being unnecessarily killed. When Pashandis were cheating by killing animals on the plea of sacrificing them, in Vedic hoped the Lord said that if the Vedic injunctions allow animal killing, I do not accept the Vedic principles. Thus he actually saved people who acted according to Vedic principles. One should therefore surrender to Lord Buddha so that he can help one avoid misusing the injunctions of the Vedas. The Kalki Avatara is the fierce incarnation who vanquishes the class of the atheists born in this age of Kali. Now in the beginning of Kali Yuga many religious principles are in effect and as Kali Yuga advances many pseudo religious principles will certainly be introduced and people will forget the real religious principles enunciated by Lord Krishna before the beginning of Kali Yuga namely principles are surrender unto the Lotus Feet of the Lord. Unfortunately because of Kali Yuga only religion doesn't really speak out and if Kali Yuga is actually Yuga, foolish people do not surrender to the lotus feet of Krishna. Even most people who claim to belong to Vedic system of religion are actually opposed to the Vedic principles. Every day they manufacture a new type of dharma on the plea that whatever one manufactures is also a path of liberation.

#### Kali Yuga, Vedic Principles, Ignorance, Knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Cipl8AGhUlM?t=279
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Ignorance means if he doesn't know Vedic knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the increasing influence of false philosophies in Kali Yuga and the role of Kalki Deva in saving devotees. He defines ignorance as the absence of Vedic knowledge, contrasting it with worldly education, and emphasizes that true knowledge comes from Brahma Vidya derived from the Vedas and Brahma Sutras, while Jada Vidya is considered ignorance unless used in Krishna consciousness.

According to this view, there are hundreds of thousands of different opinions in human society and each opinion is a valid religious principle. This philosophy of rascals has killed the religious principles mentioned in the Vedas and such philosophies will become increasingly influential as Kali Yuga progresses. In the last stage of Kali Yuga, Kalki Deva, the fierce incarnation of Keshava, will descend to kill all the Atheists and will save only the devotees of the Lord, the Sanstha Bhaktivedanta Prabhupada. So we are reading from the chapter title, the Narayana Kavaca Shield. This shield is given by one powerful demigod Brahmana, Vishwarupa, to the Kali Yuga. They have killed Jataka, King Indra. So as part of that Narayana Kavaca, in this shloka, protection was sought from three incarnations. Dvaipayano, Bhagavan, Aprabhodha, to save oneself from the ignorance of not knowing the Vedic principles ignorance that is resulting from absence of Vedic knowledge. So here ignorance is defined as absence of Vedic knowledge. If somebody doesn't have B.Tech, M.Tech, B.A., C.A., K.A., K.A., K.A., all those, it is not considered ignorance. Ignorance means if he doesn't know Vedic knowledge. He may be a simple person without any big, big degrees, but if he understands the Vedic principles, then he is in knowledge. Knowledge means this Brahma Vidya, the Brahma Vidya from the Vedas, from the Brahma Sutras. Jada Vidya is not considered as knowledge. But under the expert guidance of a spiritual master, the Jada Vidya can be dovetailed to advance in Krishna consciousness. But otherwise, it is considered ignorance. Jada Vidya is simply ignorance. So if we want protection from ignorance resulting from lack of Vedic knowledge, then one should seek protection from Dvayapayano Bhagavan. So after gaining Vedic knowledge, some people try to misuse it. So here it is recommended that if we want protection from misuse of Vedic knowledge, then Buddhas tu pashanda gana pramadhat. Then one should take shelter of Lord Buddha.

#### Vedic Principles, Lord Buddha, Kalki Avatar, Religious Principles
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Cipl8AGhUlM?t=484
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A human being has no other work than to simply understand his relation with the Supreme Personality of Godhead Lord Shri Krishna and continuously worry about how to reestablish my relationship."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that taking shelter of Lord Buddha can free one from the misuse of Vedic knowledge and laziness in applying Vedic principles. He then describes the role of Kalki Avatar in protecting against the manufacturing of false Vedic principles. He summarizes the essence of Vedic knowledge as understanding who we are, who God is, our relationship with the Supreme Lord, and how to reestablish that relationship.

So that he can be freed from this misuse. There are two things mentioned here, Prabhupada mentions. One is misuse, and one more is laziness. And forget the Vedic principles. Forget to apply the Vedic principles. So in such scenarios, Buddha, seeking Buddha's protection. And then, Kalki kale kālamalāt prapātu. There is one more category. They try to manufacture Vedic principles. In the name of Vedic principles. Nobody can manufacture. But they will manufacture some path. And they will say, this is also a Vedic path. A Vedic principle. To save ourselves from such manufacturing agencies. It is said here, Kalki kale kālamalāt prapātu. One should seek protection from Kalki avatar. So, you can see there are three incarnations safeguarding the Vedic principles. The religious principles which are described in the Vedic knowledge. It is mentioned in Bhagavatam, lokasājānato The world doesn't know these Vedic principles. What are these Vedic principles? Actually, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu has simplified the understanding of this Vedic knowledge. He says, the entire Vedic knowledge has only three divisions. That's all. The first division is known as the knowledge which will tell us who are we and who is God. And then the second section, it will tell you how, what is our relationship with the Supreme Lord. And the third section will tell you how to reestablish that relationship. Famously, these three are known as sambandha, abhidheya and prayojana. That's all. Entire Vedic knowledge is nothing but these three categories of knowledge. A human being has no other work than to simply understand his relation with the Supreme Personality of Godhead Lord Shri Krishna and continuously worry about how to reestablish my relationship.

#### Krishna, Vyasadeva, Living Entities, Satvata Samhita
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Cipl8AGhUlM?t=646
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If I can establish the supremacy of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and establish the supremacy of pure devotional service to Krishna, then the superfluous miseries of the living entities can be mitigated with that intention."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Vyasa Deva's contemplation on the Absolute Truth, his vision of Krishna and His energies, and his compassion for the suffering of living entities in the material world. He explains that Vyasa Deva aimed to mitigate these superfluous miseries by establishing the supremacy of Krishna and pure devotional service, leading him to give the Satvata Samhita, including the Srimad Bhagavatam.

So when Vyasa Deva was contemplating on the Absolute Truth, he saw Krishna along with all His energies. And he also saw Krishna's incarnations, various Amshas. Krishna has two kinds of Amshas, Swa Amsha and Vibhina Amsha. So Vyasa Deva saw all these and he also saw how the Vibhina Amshas are actually suffering various miseries in this material world. And he saw that those miseries are superfluous. Superfluous means they are actually not deeply experienceable. They are not meant for, they are not actually touching the soul. It is like an experience in a dream. Superfluous. So Vyasa Deva thought, if I can establish the supremacy of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and establish the supremacy of pure devotional service to Krishna, then the superfluous miseries of the living entities can be mitigated with that intention. He gave Satvata Samhita. He gave the Satvata Samhitas. Paancharatna, Paancharatras are also called Satvata Samhitas. But most important Satvata Samhita that he gave is the Srimad Bhagavatam. If the Vedic knowledge is compared to a tree, it's a Kalpa Vruksha of knowledge. All divisions of knowledge is present in that. And if you see interestingly, a tree, you know, that's how the knowledge is always organized, as a tree. This organization is visible even in our brains. In our minds, the knowledge is always organized as a tree. Modern people call it as mind maps. But actually, the knowledge will always, should always be organized as a tree.

---
#### Vedic Knowledge, Krishna, Srimad Bhagavatam, Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Cipl8AGhUlM?t=779
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If Krishna is not there in the root, and then you study some little branch here and there, that is Jada Vidya."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Vedic knowledge is organized like a tree with Krishna as the root, and that Srimad Bhagavatam is the ripened fruit of this knowledge. He emphasizes Prabhupada's magnanimous contribution in making the Bhagavatam accessible to everyone, describing Prabhupada's writings as filled with Satchitananda, bringing a flood of love of Krishna into our consciousness.

And what is the root of this knowledge? If the root of knowledge is Krishna, then the tree that that has been built up in our mind or in our brain, that is considered proper knowledge. That is called Brahma Vidya. If Krishna is not there in the root, and then you study some little branch here and there, that is Jada Vidya. So this Vedic knowledge, which is compared to a tree, has a ripened fruit and that is Srimad Bhagavatam. A very magnanimous contribution of Vyasadeva is that he wrote Srimad Bhagavatam for all of us. And even more magnanimous contribution is from Prabhupada who made this Bhagavatam available to us. Available to us means fools and rascals. Available to all of us. In an easily understandable language and depicting all his ecstasies in his writings. Prabhupada said his words are his ecstasies. They are all Satchitananda. So when we read Bhagavatam written by Prabhupada, there is a rain, there is a flood of Satchitananda into our consciousness. Hmm? One one letter is raining of love of Krishna. This is Bhagavatam.

**Summary:** The speaker discusses Prabhupada's extraordinary feat of translating Sanskrit Vedic knowledge into English, highlighting the limitations of the English language compared to the richness of Sanskrit. He notes that Prabhupada expressed his inability to fully convey the depth of the knowledge due to these limitations, but hoped that honest people would accept his descriptions.

So, Prabhupada on the order of his spiritual master, he has given this wonderful knowledge in the form of so many books. And that too, he has written those books in a foreign language called English. Sanskrit to English is actually not easy. It is an extraordinary feat. So Prabhupada has done that. And then he gave us these wonderful books. It is said that Bhagavatam, Sanskrit has lakhs of words. To describe a person, to describe a relationship, to describe internal world, language has to be rich. And if there is a rich language, then you can present properly what is going on inside you or what is going on outside of you. Knowledge, the language, should enable the speaker to express his experience, his ecstasies. So, Prabhupada, you know, he is an exalted Acharya and, but when he wanted to express his realizations, in English, certainly there is a limitation. English has around 5,000, 6,000 words. Now they are all increasing but they are not, they are all technically increasing in the sense, now there are many medical terms describing various diseases. Since diseases are increasing, there are so many words that are increasing, so that's not called development of the knowledge, development of language.

**Key Quote:** "These are not my books. These are written by Vyasadeva. These are the books, this knowledge is coming from Vyasadeva."
**Summary:** The speaker recounts Prabhupada's humility in attributing the knowledge in his books to Vyasadeva and Krishna, emphasizing that the hungry foreigners grabbed these books. He shares an anecdote about Rameshwarabas asking Prabhupada about his ability to write so many books, and Prabhupada's response that the knowledge came from Vyasadeva.

So it's only that many, some thousands, English in thousands, Sanskrit. But Prabhupada, he expresses this inability in the beginning of writing this Bhagavatam. He says, I am writing this valuable knowledge for the sake of the mankind, for the civilization which is on fire, which is burning. Every day we hear about these burning events. One war here, one war there. So, I am presenting this Bhagavatam in a foreign language, so certainly there will be limitations. But, Prabhupada hopes that people who are honest, they will accept these descriptions. So that's what happened and Prabhupada wrote these books. The hungry, you know, particularly the foreigners who are hungry for such knowledge, they just grabbed these books. So, this is the greatest contribution. Once Rameshwarabas asked, telling Prabhupada, Prabhupada, many people are surprised that you wrote so many books, so many volumes of books depicting this knowledge. They're wondering, where did you get this knowledge from? They also said, even your God brothers could not write many books. Prabhupada says, yes, they, they tried to write some books, but then they passed away halfway through. Nobody could complete. And then, so, again Rameshwar says, everybody is really surprised that, you know, you know so much of knowledge. They're all wondering, how did you get this knowledge? And Prabhupada says, these are not my books. These are written by Vyasadeva. These are the books, this knowledge is coming from Vyasadeva. So, Prabhupada always gave credit to his spiritual master, to Vyasadeva and to Krishna for these wonderful books.

#### Vedic Knowledge, Evidence, Ignorance, Suffering, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Cipl8AGhUlM?t=1177
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Anything that you don't connect it to Krishna, it will haunt you. It will come back to you as a misery."
**Summary:** The speaker asserts that Vedic knowledge is self-evident and doesn't require external proof, contrasting this with the modern tendency to seek scientific validation. He explains that ignorance leads to suffering, and that any aspect of life not connected to Krishna will result in misery.

So, Vedic knowledge is evidence by itself. We should not look for proving Vedic statements. Modern mind is so diseased, when we hear something, we immediately try to find a proof for it. You know, we wanted to understand, we want to arrive at some kind of a proof. But actually, Vedic statements are not like that. When any Vedic statement is made, there is, that itself is an evidence. There's no need of any proof. You need not have to search for some other proof. From the scientific field, or some other field, we try to bring some arguments to prove this Vedic statement. No. That is not the proper way of understanding this Vedic knowledge. So, Vyasadeva, who is the incarnation of Krishna, he himself given this knowledge. Anybody who becomes absorbed in this knowledge, so then he is saved from the ignorance. So, one may say, what is the problem with ignorance? Let me live in ignorance. But the problem with ignorance is, it will get you miseries. Why people suffer in this world? Only because of ignorance. Let us say, we have some suffering in our body, or in our mind, or somewhere. It means there is an ignorance. Some aspect, we have not connected it to Krishna. Some aspect, we are dealing separately from Krishna. We are not understanding how to connect it to the Lord. So we are suffering. Anything that you don't connect it to Krishna, it will haunt you. It will come back to you as a misery.

Of course, many of us are, initially began our journey as, prasādam bhaktas, but slowly, we, we started reading Prabhupāda books. Prabhupāda books are easily understandable, after you eat prasād, and after you chant Hare Kṛṣṇa. Once you chant Hare Kṛṣṇa, little bit cleansing happens. And then, vidyāvadū jīvanam, vidyāvadū jīvanam, that very chanting, will become life of all the vidyā, vidyāvadū jīvanam.

#### Chanting, Knowledge, Practice, Auspiciousness, Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Cipl8AGhUlM?t=1450
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The process that Prabhupāda has introduced, the knowledge that he has given, is complete and consistent. Nothing else we have to look at."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that chanting is the basis and life of all knowledge, and that practicing the conclusions of Vedic knowledge in daily life increases auspiciousness. He asserts that Prabhupada's process and knowledge are complete and consistent, contrasting this with the incompleteness of mathematics as proven by Godel's theorem.

So, chanting is a basis. It is a jīvana, life of all the knowledge. If you want to keep the knowledge in, in life, then, chanting is important. That is the vidyāvadū jīvanam. Once you have the knowledge, proper conclusions in the mind, and you are trying to practice them, in your day-to-day life, then, śreyaḥ kāyatava caṇḍrikā vitaraṇam, then, auspiciousness will increase in our lives. We will not be disturbed by these anardhas. anardhopaḥ samam śākṣāt bhakti-yogaṁ adhokṣaje Actually, the process that Prabhupāda has introduced, is complete and consistent. The process that Prabhupāda has introduced, the knowledge that he has given, is complete and consistent. Nothing else we have to look at. By the way, the other day I was kind of reading something. I came across, there is one theorem in mathematics. It is called, Godel's theorem of incompleteness. One mathematician proved, that the mathematics is not complete and consistent. Whereas Prabhupāda's books and knowledge, is complete and consistent. Now you can see the value. So mathematicians themselves proved that, mathematics is incomplete. So by holding on to the incomplete field, how can you learn conclusive truths? That's why such fields of approach to knowledge, they are compared to holding on to the dog's tail, to cross the ocean of unknown science. You have something to cross, but actually it will not take you anywhere. So this is the problem.

#### Prabhupada, Srimad Bhagavatam, Laziness, Knowledge, Surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Cipl8AGhUlM?t=1590
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "After reading so many things, if you don't come to the point of surrender to Kṛṣṇa, that means you have not read."
**Summary:** The speaker urges listeners not to be lazy in understanding Prabhupada's books and Srimad Bhagavatam, emphasizing that intellectual laziness can hinder deeper understanding. He explains that initial readings provide a shallow understanding, but deeper, more thorough readings reveal connections and logic within the knowledge, leading to Swadhyaya, which protects from Maya.

So one should not be, lazy to understand all this. Imagine what a fortune, we came across these books of Śrī Prabhupāda, and we came across Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam, and imagine we are not spending enough time, in understanding these conclusive truths. It's simply, laziness. You may say, no Prabhu, we are actually very busy. Yes, actually it is an intellectual laziness. Bodily you may not be lazy, because some people may be bodily lazy also. But actually, most of us have this intellectual laziness. We don't really go into, deeper aspects of this truth. There is one more important point, that when we read first time, yes, we will come to know, some macro level, branches. Always you should think, knowledge means tree. So you know, the root is there. What is the root of our knowledge, our entire knowledge? Kṛṣṇa, surrender to Kṛṣṇa. That's the root. After reading so many things, if you don't come to the point of surrender to Kṛṣṇa, that means you have not read. So from that root comes, initially when you read, Bhagavad Gita or Bhagavatam, some few branches will come out in your mind. You will be able to understand, okay, Bhagavad Gita has 18 chapters. Mostly you will be able to repeat all the chapter names. And, little more, probably you will come to know, okay, in this chapter, this is there, that is there. You can see, at a very shallow level, you develop some knowledge. Some branches have grown, which is not there previously. But when you read something, some branches have grown. And then, when you read second time, little more thoroughly, then what happens? Now this, whatever bigger branches that have grown, 18 chapters are there, okay, very good. And then you know the chapter names. Now first chapter, what is there? Then you will see some sections inside, some kind of a logic, some kind of a reasoning. There is some kind of a flow. Then you start seeing all those. And then, somebody goes little more further, and then you will try to understand, how these chapters are connected, from here to there, here to there. He will be analysing. If somebody gets into that, trying to understand this knowledge deeply, that is called Swadhyaya, that will save you from all Maya.

---
#### Krishna's Service, Prabhupada's Books, Knowledge, Laziness, Opposition
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Cipl8AGhUlM?t=1756
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As much time as possible, as much time that is available, go on deeper, deeper, deeper, deeper."
**Summary:** The speaker encourages deeper study of Prabhupada's books to attach oneself to Krishna's service, emphasizing that the beauty of this knowledge lies in discovering new branches with each reading. He addresses the challenge of balancing transcendental activities for full-time devotees and encourages prayer to Krishna to remove laziness and opposition to Vedic knowledge, which arises from the conditioning of the modern brain.

If we want to be attached to Krishna's service, then Prabhupada books have to be read like that. You should not be satisfied with shallow level of understanding. As much time as possible, as much time that is available, go on deeper, deeper, deeper, deeper. Then you may say, where is this deep knowledge, Prabhu? I already read all these books. So, when you read, you only touched one level of branches. The beauty of this knowledge, that given by Prabhupada is, when you read second time, you will be shocked to realise, oh, this piece of knowledge is available. You suddenly discover one more little branch. You come to know, the whole tree of Bhagavatam, whole tree of Vedic knowledge. This should be our goal. So, for full time devotees, the challenge is not availability of this knowledge. The challenge is about balancing all the transcendental activities that we do. But, if the devotee is nicely managing his time, then he will get good amount of time for deeper study more and more. So, one should not be lazy. Pray to Krishna, pray to Lord, to remove us from this laziness. And also, opposition to Vedic knowledge. Sometimes in our mind, there is an opposition to Vedic statements. Because, modern brain is trained up in a certain way. And when we hear Prabhupada's statements, automatically brain, in the mind, there is some opposition. It's called Mano Vyasangam. There is an opposition in the mind. Because we are attached to already known knowledge, pieces of knowledge. We are so attached, we don't want to change. In that angle also, we need blessings of Krishna to protect from us, protect us from this laziness and this opposition.

#### Kalki, Atheists, Philosophies, Kali Yuga, Protection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/Cipl8AGhUlM?t=1921
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Kali Yuga, everybody knows, that it is going to be a environment which is vitiated by ignorance."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the fierce nature of Kalki, contrasting it with peaceful, commercialized versions. He warns against the proliferation of so-called philosophical paths, particularly in India, and emphasizes the need for protection from these philosophies, which are spreading rapidly in Kali Yuga, an age vitiated by ignorance.

buddhastu pashantagana brahmadadam kalki kale kala mala Prabhupada is saying, Kalki is a fierce incarnation. Now, we have imagined, but of course, He is supposed to come only in the end of the Kali Yuga. But we see, some so-called Kalki avatar has come, He peacefully sits. And then there is a marriage ceremony, Abhisheka, and you can touch His feet, but you have to pay one lakh. You may be a pashanti, but you can touch. Kalki is not going to be like that. It's a fierce incarnation. He is not going to receive Abhisheka, Abhisheka, and everybody is a pashanti by then. He simply kills everybody. So, we need protection from these philosophies. Already we are seeing there are so many so-called philosophical paths are created in India. Actually, India is not only a hub of IT, it is also a hub of all these philosophical paths. If you want incarnations, available. If you want any theory, yes, available. So many we manufacture actually. If there is a GDP connected to all this, we will be the richest. So, these people, they present some kind of a, they call it as Vedic knowledge. They call it as spiritual philosophy. Sometimes they'll put vigrahas. They quote from shastras. Some of these Yathamat, Tathapat people, if you hear, they will be quoting such shlokas and explaining so nicely, we get agitated. You know, because they're very smart. And then, these people, somehow, they have oratory skills, good ability to understand what's going on in the people's mind. And then, slowly, they'll present their philosophies. And such people, they release their video, or whatever, in the social media channels, there'll be one million, five million, so many millions. And when we release our Bhagavatam, when we release our Bhagavatam class, in the social media channel, 50, 100, you'll have 50 million. Already, you can see, so many of these philosophies are spreading in this age. This is called Kala Mala. Kala. The age itself is, brings, you know, this Mala. For example, this season, automatically, rains will, it'll bring rain, rains. Summer season, it brings, you know, heat and, you know, that kind of a temperature. So it's understandable. So similarly, Kala Mala means, Kali Yuga, everybody knows, that it is going to be a environment which is vitiated by ignorance. So we need protection of a fierce incarnation, Kalki.

#### material nature, suffering, free will
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QSokzBoFGlA?t=169
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The living entity is the cause of his own suffering, but he can also be the cause of his eternal happiness."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the verse, focusing on the roles of material nature, the spirit soul, and the demigods in experiencing happiness and distress, emphasizing the soul's free choice to engage in Krishna consciousness for eternal happiness.

The body, karana, the senses, katrutve, regarding the demigods, karana, karana, the cause, prakrutim, material nature, with who the learned understand, bhoktrutve, regarding the perception, sukha, of happiness, dukkhanam, and of distress, purusham, the spirit soul, prakrutehe, to material nature, param, transcendental, translation, purport by his divine grace, A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swamishya Prabhupada, translation, the cause of the conditioned soul's material body and senses, and the senses presiding deities, the demigods, is the material nature. This is understood by learned men. The feelings of happiness and distress of the soul, who is transcendental by nature, are caused by the spirit soul himself. Please repeat. The cause of the conditioned soul's material body and senses, and the senses presiding deities, the demigods, is the material nature. This is understood by learned men. The feelings of happiness and distress of the soul, are the things we received from this world by understanding the spiritual state, whose müssenbeneit survived, how they you stuck, and how witness a life is past-time or Leela. But here it is clearly stated that the conditioned soul is forced to take a certain type of body and senses under the three modes of material nature. That body is not received according to his own choice. In other words, a conditioned soul has no free choice. He has to accept a certain body, certain type of body according to his karma. But when there are bodily reactions as felt in happiness and distress, it is to be understood that the cause is the spirit soul himself. If he so desires, the spirit soul can change this conditioned life of dualities by choosing to serve Krishna. The living entity is the cause of his own suffering. But he can also be the cause of his eternal happiness. When he wants to engage in Krishna consciousness, a suitable body is required. A suitable body is offered to him by the internal potency, the spiritual energy of the Lord. And when he wants to satisfy his senses, a material body is offered. Thus, it is his free choice to accept a spiritual body or a material body. But once the body is accepted, he has to enjoy or suffer the consequences. The Mayavadi philosopher's presentation is that the living entity enjoys his pastimes by accepting the body. This theory is not acceptable. However, because the word pastime implies voluntary acceptance for enjoyment. Therefore, this interpretation is most misleading. When there is unforced acceptance for suffering, it is not a pastime. The Lord's pastimes and the conditioned soul, conditioned living entity's acceptance of karmic reaction are not on the same level. This ends the Bhaktivedanta.

#### material nature, modes of nature, control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QSokzBoFGlA?t=487
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When the living entity desires to lord it over this world, it wants to become the lord of all that he surveys, he comes under a specific influence which is called the influence of the material energy."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the fundamental principles of material nature as explained by Lord Kapila, describing how the living entity's desire to control the material world leads to being influenced by the three modes of material nature.

So, we are hearing from the fundamental principles of material nature as explained by Lord Kapila to his mother Devahuti. The control of the material nature and the conditioned soul, living entity is been described in these verses. So, when the living entity desires to lord it over this world, it wants to become the lord of all that he surveys, he comes under a specific influence which is called the influence of the material energy. There is a powerful energy acting in this world and that controls each and every action, thought, the thoughts and feelings and actions of the living entity. Of course, the energy doesn't mean there is only one single energy. There are three modes. There are three powerful modes. So, energies are there, Satoguna, Rajoguna and Tamoguna and their combination acts on the living entity.

#### senses, demigods, control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QSokzBoFGlA?t=589
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "That feeling that I am not the controller is a very good realization. Now you can work on really controlling the body, mind and senses."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the verse, explaining that the body and senses are controlled by demigods who are themselves controlled by the Supersoul, emphasizing the importance of realizing one's lack of control as a first step towards genuine control through Krishna's blessings.

So, here it is mentioned, Karya, Karana, Katrutve. Karya means this body, Karana means the senses, body and senses. And Katrutve. Katrutve, that means the controller of this body and senses is the demigods. Karanam, Prakrutim, Viduhu. These three are coming from the Prakruti, material nature, which has got three aspects, three modes. So, we have a body and the body has senses. So, body and senses are different. There is a difference between these two. So, the body and the senses are not free. These are controlled by demigods. Very closely they are controlled. In fact, each and every sense is controlled. It's controlled by one-one demigod. For example, our mind comes under the control of the demigod known as Moon, Chandra. As the Moon movements change, our mental aspects also change. When there is an Amavasya, there is a certain way our mind behaves. behaves and Purnima, our mind behaves differently. There is one Purnima which gives pleasure to everybody, that is called Gaurapurnima, which is coming up very soon. And so these bodily aspects are controlled. Our intelligence is controlled by the Surya. Little bit sit in front of the sun, little enlightenment will come. In fact, that's why we chant one of the Gayatris also. So this, that's why sometimes it is mentioned that while sleeping it is good to face our head towards the east, from which the sun is rising. But then it is good to face our head towards the east. It is good to face our head towards the east. It is good to face our head towards the east. If he doesn't read Prabhupada's books, even if he sleeps also, it's not that he will become very intelligent. So then, like that, blood circulation. These circulations are done by Vayu, then digestion by Agni. So you name any part, it's controlled by one demigod. We are thinking we are the controllers of this body and mind. Of course, if a person comes to Krishna consciousness, he understands that he is not the controller. Particularly if somebody tries to control his body and senses, he understands, okay, I am not the controller. I don't have control on my body, on the senses, on the mind. One feels that that. That feeling that I am not the controller is a very good realization. Now you can work on really controlling the body, mind and senses. And so who are the controllers? The immediate controllers are the demigods. But the demigods are also controlled. They are not the ultimate controllers. They are controlled by the Supersoul, Vishnu. So the real sense control can happen only with the blessings of Vishnu or Krishna.
