Then you may ask, okay, if they eat prasadam nicely, and at that time also, they will become strong and stout, then what will happen, they will develop higher consciousness, they will develop, you know, Krishna consciousness, they give up the lower behaviors. You may say, no, it's all looking theoretical, you know, where is the proof that just by eating prasad, there is a change in the person. We have one experience in during the last government, Andhra government, there was some canteens were operated throughout the state, and then, in Vijayawada area, there are many canteens were operating, and, we got one complaint from one of the canteens, that every day, there are some gang is coming, there is called chaddi gang. #### prasadam, transformation, real-life example
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/FCfBINHoDpw?t=1311
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "They ate that food for say about some ten days and immediately you can see some change in them, this is the difference."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a real-life experience of a 'chaddi gang' (a group of ruffians) who regularly caused trouble at a prasadam canteen, but after consistently eating the prasadam for about ten days, their behavior transformed, and they became peaceful and orderly.

So then, because there is a system, you know, there is a government put system that they they have to stand in a queue, and everybody has to be peaceful, they can they have to pay, initially one or two cases they have not paid also, and then, such a chaddi gang, so then, after two three times when they came, maybe a weeks or weeks time, and then, slowly, we stopped getting complaints from the the manager, and one day our devotees went there to see what is happening, whether manager is still survived, surviving, or is gone, so, we went there to see, and, that time, the manager was telling, yes, even now, the chaddi gang is coming, every day, but now, they are not creating any trouble, and then, somebody was showing the devotees, see, this is the chaddi gang, and, they are very peacefully standing in the queue, they are all coming, five rupees, they are paying, they are taking food, they are eating, they are going, so, now, what exactly happened, it's not that we call we call police, or we put some pressure, nothing, they ate that food for say about some ten days and immediately you can see some change in them, this is the difference, they don't understand this, they think food is food, why it has to be offered in a sacrifice.

### Srimad Bhagavatam I Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa I SB 3.6.34 I 09.03.2022
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### purification, modes of nature, spiritual identity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-Reuhncefm8?t=393
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As a spirit soul we don't like these conditions, we want to be free, we want to be powerful, we want to do whatever we want to."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the concept of purification (atma vishuddhi) as freedom from the influence of the three modes of nature, highlighting the spirit soul's inherent desire for freedom and the tendency to exploit resources for bodily comfort.

Artham, a purification, one has to purify. Now what is this purification? The shastra always talks about atma vishuddhi. What is this purification? So Prabhupada is mentioned here, the purification is the purification of the impact of the three modes, purification of the influence of the three modes. The basic idea is that we are atma, here it is said atma vishuddhi, we are all actually spirit souls, but then there is a contamination. We are under some conditions, the contamination means these three modes, and the three modes will impose conditions on us. But as a spirit soul we don't like these conditions, we want to be free, we want to be powerful, we want to do whatever we want to. And one of the influence of these three modes is that one wants to exploit the resources for his own sense enjoyment. The basic thing that he thinks is, I am this body, let me exploit everything for body's comfort. Always tries to keep the body very comfortable, little bit of discomfort he cannot tolerate, and little bit of pain, little bit of suffering he doesn't want to tolerate. This is the mood of a living entity. In this world everybody is running behind how they can enhance their enjoyment. This is how the body can be. Atma vishuddhi, we are all actually spirit souls. Atma vishuddhi, we are all actually spirit souls. Atma vishuddhi, we are all actually spirit souls.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-Reuhncefm8?t=529
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the importance of brahmanical culture and the varnashrama system, highlighting the role of the brahmana as a spiritual master and the ultimate goal of transcendental service to the Lord for purifying the soul.

In society everybody is simply running behind how to enhance, how to earn more and more money. But actually Lord has created these varnas: brahmana, kshatriya, vaishya, and shudra. These varnas and also He has defined what is the swadharma for the sake of increasing culture, enhancing the culture. And what kind of a culture is that? It is called here brahmanical culture. So here it is said the brahmana is the spiritual master of all the orders of the society and thus brahmanical culture culminating in transcendental service of the Lord is the basic principle of purifying the soul. Everybody is a spiritual master of all the orders of the society. Every sentence is shown as purifying the soul. Now the souls are having this tendency to lord it over and Prabhupada is saying the last snare or the last goal of this tendency is to become God himself. One claims that he is supreme. Prabhupada says how one can be supreme if he is under the contamination? He cannot be the supreme. So this brahmanical culture will help everybody. So what is this brahmanical culture? First of all this division is there. The four divisions should be there: brahmana, kshatriya, vaishya, and shudra. And they all have to accept the brahmana as the spiritual master. All the varnas and all the ashramas are there. So this brahmana culture will help everybody have to work under the direction of the spiritual master, swagurum harim. And they have to worship the Supreme Lord Hari under the direction of the spiritual master. And then what will the brahmana tell? He says the division of the society is not meant for enhancing the bodily comforts but rather to culture the soul, enhance the soul's comfort. This is the difference. Brahmana means one who knows that there is a spirit soul and he knows that the spirit soul has its own comfort. He knows the constitution of the spirit soul. He is called brahmana. Somebody who knows medicine he is called a doctor. Similarly somebody who knows about the soul and its…

Creating facilities will not save you from this contamination, will not save you from this conditions of life. So Western civilization means simply they enhance the money, money, money, in order to cover up the defects of the culture. Big money, lighting, all facilities and machines and this and that. But such a civilization cannot exist. Culture has to be there at the family level, culture has to be there at the civil level. What is culture at the family level? The man, he works honestly to his best capacity and he brings money to the house and the wife, very intelligently, whatever money comes, very intelligently she manages the house. She doesn't complain about how much money has been brought to the house. She will not complain. As soon as the man comes, she will not harass. Why only this much? I want more. Where is my abaran? Where is my saree? Where is my one more cupboard? She rather, whatever money comes, by the honest endeavor of the man, she takes that and very intelligently she manages the family. Even now we see some families, families like that, wife will never complain. She has great respect for the husband and for his hard endeavor and whatever comes, she very expertly utilizes for running the family.

#### purification, deity worship, chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/-Reuhncefm8?t=1497
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Deity worship alone is not sufficient for purification...The recommended process is one has to chant, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare."
**Summary:** This segment explains that the goal is to purify the soul, and that brahmanical culture is created so that people can happily practice worshiping the Supreme Lord. In this age, deity worship alone is not sufficient for purification, and the recommended process is chanting Hare Krishna. Deity worship and chanting should go on parallelly, with deity worship helping to reduce restlessness and chanting enhancing the deity worship experience.

Unless he understands that my goal is Shraddhayatma Vishuddhadham. He understands that my goal is to purify the soul. The tendency to lord it over, that is coming from the three modes. And if I can purify myself, those tendencies will not push me by simply enhancing bodily comforts and bodily enjoyment. That will not satisfy the Atma anyway. So to reduce these forces of the modes, so that he can really change his life and simplify his life. Otherwise we are forced. Nobody will make it simplified. So that's why so nice actually here, Prabhupada is saying, these orders are created. Brahmanic culture is created so that people can happily practice Ajanti, Swagurum Hari. Worship the Supreme Lord. Feed the Supreme Lord. Naivedya. People have no time for their own lives. Now how will they be able to take care of the Supreme Person in the form of a deity? It is not easy. That is why you require Swagurum. Actually in this age, deity worship alone is not sufficient for purification. It is not alone to self-realize or to uncondition ourselves. The recommended process is one has to chant, Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. So this is called Bhagavath Dharma. And deity worship is called Pancharatrika. And both have to go on parallelly. Deity worship is also compulsory, particularly in the beginning days, because it will take us out of agitations in the mind, the agitations and bodily concepts which are agitating our mind. If we want to, restlessness has to be reduced. Then deity worship is essential. And then once you have little restlessness has come down, then you can chant nicely. And when you chant nicely, then you will do deity worship very nicely. You can feel the presence of the Lord. And when you do deity worship nicely, then you will chant nicely. So both the processes will help each other. And then we can go out of the conditions. Otherwise our life becomes complicated. And then we don't find any energy and any life in our day to day existence. Any questions?

### Srimad Bhagavatam I Sri Satya Gaura Dasa I SB 3.6.17 I 11.11.2021
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### hearing, knowledge, Vedic knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QXKCjGn45o4?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The entire Vedic system of knowledge is received by aural reception only and thus sound is the most important source of knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse and purport, emphasizing the importance of hearing, especially Vedic knowledge, as the primary source of knowledge and perfection.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 3, Chapter 6, Text No. 17: Karnavasya Venerbhinao, Dishnyam Svambhivishur Dishaha, Shrotrenam Senashabdhasya, Siddhyam Yena Prapadhyate. Translation in purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada. Translation: When the ears of the gigantic form became manifested, all the controlling deities of the directions entered into them with the hearing principles by which all the living entities hear and take advantage of sound. The ear is the most important instrument in the body of the living entity. Sound is the most important medium for carrying the message of distant and unknown things. The perfection of all sound or knowledge enters through the ear and makes one's life perfect. The entire Vedic system of knowledge is received by aural reception only and thus sound is the most important source of knowledge. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta.

---
#### hearing, perfection, Vedic knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QXKCjGn45o4?t=88
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Siddhi is not possible if one doesn't follow the Vedic knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the verse, explaining that perfection is achieved by hearing perfect sound, which carries the message of the divine and requires following Vedic principles.

So, how the living entities can experience perfection in life? Siddhyam Yena Prapadhyate. If one wants to achieve perfection, then Shrotrenam Senashabdhasya, one has to hear perfect sound. The sound which is carrying the message of the divine, the perfect knowledge should be heard. So, for that purpose Karanavasya Vinirbhinao, the ears became manifested in the gigantic form and the Dishnamsvam Vivasudhishah, the controlling deities of the directions entered into this new department along with the Shrotrenam Senashabdhasya. Along with the Shrotrenam Senashabdhasya, the controlling deities of the directions, they entered into this new department along with the Shrotrenam Senashabdhasya, the Shrotrenam Senah, the hearing principles. Every department comes with its own principles, its own rules and regulations. So, by which Siddhyam Yena Prapadhyate, Nasiddhyam Avaapnoti. So, Siddhi is not possible if one doesn't follow the Vedic knowledge. If somebody doesn't know the Vedic knowledge, if somebody doesn't have the knowledge of the Vedic knowledge, if somebody doesn't know the Vedic knowledge, doesn't take advantage of the sound which is depicted in the Shastras.

---
#### hearing, purity, sinful activities
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QXKCjGn45o4?t=178
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Our attention will be diverted if mind is polluted. And mind remains polluted if the living entities are engaged in the four sinful activities."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that hearing the Shastras with attention leads to perfection, but a polluted mind, resulting from sinful activities, hinders proper hearing, emphasizing the need to follow Acharya's instructions for purification.

When someone reads the Shastras or recites the Shastras, the sound which comes, that has to be heard. So there are some principles. So if somebody follows such principles and then hears that sound, then Siddhim, there is a perfection of life. Generally we will not be able to hear properly. Our attention will be diverted if mind is polluted. And mind remains polluted if the living entities are engaged in the four sinful activities. So that's why Acharya's instructions, they have to be followed. And anyone who follows such instructions of practicing these principles, then he will become pure in mind and he will be able to give full attention to hearing.

#### hearing, importance of ears, knowledge
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QXKCjGn45o4?t=263
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "If you can hear perfect sound, perfect knowledge, then one's life becomes perfect."
**Summary:** The speaker highlights the importance of ears as the last sense to fail at death and as a primary instrument for acquiring knowledge through sound, emphasizing that hearing perfect sound leads to a perfect life.

So Prabhupada is saying here that ears are the most important instruments in the body. It is said that when a person is dying, various parts start failing in his body. Various senses start failing. But the ears, they remain till the last moment. They are the last ones to fail. So when somebody is about to die also, you can just say Hare Krishna. Last moment you can hear Hare Krishna and then die. So this is the most important instrument in the body. Many times we think ears, eyes are the most important. Some other sense is the most important. But actually ears are the most important instruments in the body. And why is that? It can hear sound. Ear is a knowledge acquiring sense. Ear is a knowledge acquiring sense. So through sound, it can acquire knowledge. The ears can acquire knowledge. Now some sound has come. Now you know somebody has entered the temple hall. Now some bells are ringing. We know. Dities are offered, Naivedya. See? Knowledge. So sound is such a powerful instrument, powerful media for gaining knowledge. So Prabhupada is saying, so Prabhupada is saying, if you can hear perfect sound, perfect knowledge, then one's life becomes perfect.

#### Shastra, lust, hearing the truth, Acharya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QXKCjGn45o4?t=373
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The perfect sound comes from the lotus lips of the Acharya."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that not following the Shastra leads to unhappiness due to being driven by lust, and emphasizes the importance of hearing the truth, avoiding rumors, and seeking perfect sound from the Acharya.

Asashra vidhyam usrujya vartate kamakarata. Na siddhim na avaapnauti. So if someone doesn't follow the Shastra, then na siddhim, na sukham. There is no Siddhi, there is no happiness. So now why one doesn't follow the Shastra? Because kamakarata, driven by lust. So when we are driven by lust, we break all Shastric principles. We break all rules. So that's why it is mentioned here that the perfection comes through the ears, when one listens to the perfect message. One of the hearing principles is that you should hear the truth. That is depicted in the Vedic knowledge. One should be eager to hear the truth. One should not hear rumours. One should not hear such kind of sounds, noise. I mean if it happens to be there, then you are forced to hear, that's okay. But then one is not eager to listen to all these things. Eagerness is how can I receive perfect sound? And the perfect sound comes from the lotus lips of the Acharya.

#### Acharya, spiritual master, hearing, Prabhupada's example
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QXKCjGn45o4?t=476
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of listening to the spiritual master and shares Prabhupada's example of prioritizing hearing from his guru over pilgrimage, highlighting the pleasure it gave his master.

The sound that comes from the mouth of an Acharya is the perfect sound. So anybody who is eager to listen to Acharya, he wants to hear from the spiritual master, more and more he is properly situated, he is on the path of perfectional life. Prabhupada explains his own experience, that how he was very eager to listen to his spiritual master. And then when there is an opportunity for even going for some pilgrimage, Prabhupada chose to remain in the class. He wanted to attend the class of his spiritual master and listen to him carefully, very attentively. Prabhupada says that has actually pleased his master. Prabhupada's own example is that. His master Bhakti Siddhanta Saraswati was very pleased with him because Prabhupada was very eager to listen to him. He is listening to him, although he could not understand some big complicated concepts. Bhakti Siddhanta was explaining many things and Prabhupada is, he, Prabhupada says that he could not understand, but still he simply sat there and listened to them. Radha Govinda Ki. He simply sat there and listened to the sound.

**Summary:** The speaker explains that the potency of the Acharya's words is present even if not fully understood, leading to Mantra Siddhi, and emphasizes the importance of submissively listening to the Acharya's words to avoid dissension.

Now this is very important. Whether one understands, doesn't understand, the potency of that sound is there. And that sound, if it penetrates through the ears, through the ears, then the perfection is there. So this perfection through sound is called Mantra Siddhi. Anybody who listens to the Mantra, all Vedic knowledge is Mantras, then there is a Mantra Siddhi. You will attain perfection. Such a simple system it is. Everybody has got ears. All that he has to do is simply listen to this sound attentively, particularly the words of the Acharya. So Acharya's words have to be taken submissively and understand the message carefully, otherwise it can create dissension. Unfortunately, in our movement also, there are two parties.

#### Guru camp, Rithvik camp, arguments, straw arguments
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QXKCjGn45o4?t=677
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "These are all called straw arguments. That means an attempt to divert the attention from the real argument."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the two opposing viewpoints within the movement, the 'Guru camp' and the 'Rithvik camp', and describes the common arguments used by each, labeling them as 'straw arguments' that divert from the core issue.

One is called Guru camp and one more is called the Rithvik camp. Actually it is a loose word, Rithvik, but anyway. These are the two arguments about the Acharya's words. Many times, the Guru camp people argue that you mean to say Prabhupada has not created any pure devotee, that he cannot succeed Prabhupada. You mean to say that Prabhupada is not capable of creating any pure devotees who can become Acharya's next to him. And they will also say, look at this, the Gurus, they are so dedicated, 50 years, 45 years, 50 years, they have been serving Prabhupada's movement. And you mean to say their 50 years of practice, their stability, their commitment to their spiritual master is not sufficient for him to become an Acharya. You are committing an offence if you say they are not qualified to sit in the Acharya Peetha. You are committing an offence, Vaishnava offence. And sometimes somebody will say, you mean to say if Prabhupada can become a Guru, then Rupa Goswami can also become a Guru. Why don't you, I will give you Rupa Goswami Diksha, come. Why don't you, why don't you do that. Actually these are not the arguments. These are all called straw arguments. That means an attempt to divert the attention from the real argument.

See both, both the camps, there are sincere devotees, educated, well versed devotees. And both of them knows the Bhagavatam's purport. One of the verses, Prabhupada mentions about the concept of a current link. I think in 2.9.7, Srila Prabhupada mentions that one should approach the current link in the disciple succession. Both the camps knows. It is not an argument that you know, you can go to Rupa Goswami or you can go to Manu or if possible even beyond, Brahma. Nobody is saying, this is not at all an argument. Current link has to be approached. So, the question is, a departed Acharya, like Srila Prabhupada, can he remain a current link? This is the question. The actual question is, in 1977, Prabhupada has sent in a very important direction to the movement in the form of July 9th letter, 9th July 1977 directive, Prabhupada has sent.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QXKCjGn45o4?t=870

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Did Prabhupada appointed those 11 as Diksha gurus, then that means Prabhupada is handing over the baton to them. He ceases to be the current link. Those 11 will become the current links. Or did Prabhupada appointed those 11 only as Rithwiks? That means Prabhupada remains as the current link."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the crucial importance of the July 9th letter, framing the central question as whether Prabhupada appointed the 11 leading disciples as Diksha Gurus, thus handing over the baton, or as Ritwiks, allowing him to remain the current link.

In that, both the camps accept that 11 of the prominent leading disciples of Prabhupada were chosen in that direction, in that particular directive. 11 people, personalities, were chosen. They were the leading, mature devotees of that time. Now, the question is, did Prabhupada appointed those 11 as Deeksha gurus, as Deeksha gurus, as Deeksha gurus, then that means Prabhupada is handing over the baton to them. He ceases to be the current link. Those 11 will become the current links. Or did Prabhupada appointed those 11 only as Rithwiks? That means Prabhupada remains as the current link. So, this 9th July 1977 directive is such a crucial document. Imagine if it is appointment of 11 Deeksha gurus, the Parampara takes a direction in a certain direction. And when the whole movement is on a crossroads, this way or this way.

#### Guru's order, July 9th letter, organizational directive, final direction
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/QXKCjGn45o4?t=1079
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Once you ascertain what is his final direction, then you bring all kinds of quotes, letters, conversations, everything to support that."
**Summary:** The speaker stresses the importance of first understanding the Guru's order, particularly the final direction in the July 9th letter, and then using other sources to support that understanding, emphasizing that the letter is an organizational directive.

Once you ascertain what is his final direction, then you bring all kinds of quotes, letters, conversations, everything to support that. First is Guru's order, Acharya's wish. And then if you see the 9th July letter, it is not actually a letter. It is a directive that is given to the entire society. It is addressed to the entire movement. It is addressed to the temple presidents and the GBCs. It is addressed to the top management of the movement. It is not directed towards a single individual devotee. It is addressed to the official positions within the organization. It is an organizational directive. One should not think that this is written to some individual devotee and then there something is written so that becomes the basis for ever-perfection of life. No. This is. So, that 20th May came and those things are discussed, Reethvik word came out, disciple of Guru, disciple word came out. There were so many things came out in the conversation. Yea there are many things came out. After that also there were few meetings. There also many things came out. Ultimately, a final direction has come to the entire movement in writing, signed by none other than His Divine Grace. And that is July 9th, 1977 direction.

Stopping something which is going on, something which is already in practice going on. Now what is the direction, additional direction in writing that you got to change such a system? Prabhupada is overseeing such implementation for the last three, four months. The whole world is following that. Now what is that which has triggered you to change the system? So what is Guru's order has to be clearly ascertained. Otherwise, Siddhim Ena Prapadhyate, one will not experience Siddhi perfection. Now you cannot try to bring your own arguments and say, no, no, no, tradition, Guru has to be physically present. No, no, no, tradition, Guru has to be physically present. And for us, the Shastra is Prabhupada's books. Don't bring anything else. Prabhupada's books and whatever Prabhupada has given, from his conversations, from his directions, all that has to be used to prove. All that has to be used to prove.

### Srimad Bhagavatam I Sri Satya Gaurachandra Dasa I SB 6.4.30 I 25.08.2022
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### Krishna as the cause, Supreme Brahman, Oneness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cPLYxqZ1R28?t=59
**Verse References:** BG 9.18
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna, who is known as Govinda, is the Supreme Controller. He has an eternal, blissful, spiritual body. He is the origin of all. He has no other origin, for He is the prime cause of all causes."
**Summary:** The speaker establishes Krishna as the original cause of all causes, referencing Bhagavad Gita, Brahma Samhita, and Madhavacharya, emphasizing His eternal, blissful nature and oneness despite multifarious forms.

He is the ultimate objective, and whether acting or causing others to act, He is the ultimate doer. There are many causes, high and low, but since He is the cause of all causes, He is well known as the Supreme Brahman who existed before all activities. He is one without a second, and has no other cause. I therefore offer my respects unto Him. The Supreme Personality of Godhead, Krishna, is the original cause, as confirmed in Bhagavad Gita. Even this material world, which is conducted under the modes of material nature, is caused by the Supreme Personality of Godhead, who therefore also has an intimate relationship with the material world. If the material world were not a part of His body, the Supreme Lord, the Supreme Cause, would be incomplete. Therefore, we hear, if one knows that Vasudeva is the original cause of all causes, He becomes a perfect Mahatma. In the Brahma Samhita, it declares, 'Ishwara Paramakrishna, Sachidananda Vigraha, Anadiradir Govindaha Sarvakaranakaranam.' Krishna, who is known as Govinda, is the Supreme Controller. He has an eternal, blissful, spiritual body. He is the origin of all. He has no other origin, for He is the prime cause of all causes. The Supreme Brahman, Tad Brahma, is the cause of all causes, but He has no cause. Anadiradir Govinda Sarvakaranakaranam, Govinda Krishna is the original cause of all causes, but He has no cause for His appearance as Govinda. Govinda expands in multifarious forms, but nevertheless they are one, as confirmed by Madhavacharya. Ananyaha sadrusha bhavad eko roopadi abhedatah. Krishna has no cause, nor any equal, and He is one, because His various forms, as formsa and vibhannamsa, are non-different, different from Himself. This is the Bhagavad Gita, parupottoma.

#### Digital depression, Social media, Anxiety
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cPLYxqZ1R28?t=211
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "One young girl, she has committed suicide because her YouTube views are not increasing. So it's a real problem. It's a very big problem. 3% of the population are having it."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the growing problem of 'digital depression' among youngsters and elders, linking it to social media use, anxiety over views and responses, and highlighting symptoms like loss of self-confidence, lamentation, and even suicidal thoughts.

So recently, there was a news, an interesting article about a new tendency that is developing in the youngsters, not only youngsters even in the elders also, and they figured out that about 3% of Indian population are having these kind of tendencies, quite a big number. India is a very populous country. Now what are those symptoms? The symptoms are connected to utilizing this social media, mobiles, laptops, gadgets, which give access to the social media. A young man or a young girl, they take photographs, and then they immediately upload it into the social media. Nowadays they can take photographs of anything, any damn thing and put it up on the social media. There's somebody to see. And then when a boy or a girl or anybody, say he takes a photograph of himself, or herself, and then post it on to the social media, then begins the tension. How many views, how many responded? Nowadays there are more options are there, you know, not only this and that. We used to have only one, one this way, one this way. Now it has changed, you know. There are love symbols and so many other faces probably you can put up. So, it's a tension. How many people are liking it, how many are not liking it. And imagine you say this is the best picture of myself and post it, and nobody is responding. There is a big silence. So this is the anxiety people are going through. Recently it's very surprising in Hyderabad. One young girl, she has committed suicide because her YouTube views are not increasing. So it's a real problem. It's a very big problem. 3% of the population are having it. So, scientists, of course as usual they got into the picture. First thing they do is they will give a name. They can't find any solutions, but, they gave a name. They call this as digital depression. Previously, very near dear ones, say passes away, somebody used to get into depression. So, depression has always been there, but, this is a new depression that has been created, a digital depression. They have mentioned what are the symptoms of this depression. One is they lose self-confidence. Obviously, you know. You know something, and, you have put all your confidence in the hands of others. Obviously, you will lose your own confidence. And then, always they are in a lamenting mood. Lamentation. Then, concentration is disturbed. Decision making power is affected. Suicidal thoughts. Then, lack of sleep. Loneliness. Weakness. These are the symptoms that they have mentioned. This is called digital depression. There is a tsunami of depressed people in the world.

**Summary:** The speaker asserts that any analysis falling short of recognizing Krishna as the cause of all causes is incomplete, and solutions lacking Krishna are more problematic. He explains that not seeing Krishna's transcendental form leads to duality, attraction, and repulsion.

Anything less than this, if somebody analyzing the cause for some issue and if they fall short of reaching this conclusion that Krishna is the cause of all causes, then his calculation is not complete. Like for example they have mentioned all these causes and they have mentioned the solutions. So if the Krishna is not there in the equation and when they come up with the solutions, then what happens? That Prahalad Maharaj explains that such solutions will be much more problematic, much more problematic than the original problems. For example there are solutions about how you should cross over suicidal tendencies. Big, big books are there. And then how to come out of addictions and habits. Big, big books are there. In some of the countries they have a de-addiction center for Facebook alone. Facebook building, then there is a Google building, continuously searching. And then after he comes out of the gadget also the hands are not stopping. So that has become a disease. So they have like that. Somebody may say, no I don't have Facebook depression nowadays because I am using Instagram. That is becoming very popular. Don't worry, very soon Instagram building also will come up. So for each of these symptoms, they are coming up with solution, but those solutions are much bigger problems. So that is why here it is clearly mentioned, sarvakarana karanam. Krishna is also known as karanatmane. His very body, his transcendental body is the cause. As soon as we don't see Krishna's body, then our thinking gets into duality. We get attracted by some things and we get repelled by some things. The first symptom of not seeing Krishna's transcendental form is that we get into duality. Our mind starts thinking in terms of plus and minus or something is attractive and something is not. Consciousness gets repelled by some things, and consciousness gets attracted by some things. This is the phenomenon. That is why some people get attracted to this channel, some people to that channel. Somebody may not have that much of an attraction. Everything can be explained if you understand that if Krishna is not there in the picture, then comes the duality.

#### Duality, Habits, Maya, Krishna connection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cPLYxqZ1R28?t=1373
**Verse References:** —

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Habits are like this, pendulums. We will be forced to do something and then we become detached from it and we move away from it, and again we will be forced in it."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that recognizing duality is an issue is incomplete without understanding Krishna as the cause. He describes attraction and repulsion as a pendulum, defining habits as this pendulum swing, and linking it to Maya and the lack of a Krishna connection.

That is why some people are able to figure out duality is an issue, but their analysis, their final conclusion is not correct. It is incomplete, because they have not yet understood the tad brahma tad hetu ho. So whenever we are caught up in these dualities, getting attracted to something and getting repelled by something, we should know that Krishna is missing in the equation, and that is why we are struggling in our minds. So this attraction and repulsion, they are not static like that. We move between these two. You know, sometimes you may be attracted towards one particular object, a particular person, and then slowly, by your calculation, you may detach yourself and then go to the other extreme of repulsion. No, no, I hate this, you may say. And then hating, hating, hating, after some time, you will see again it will move back towards the attraction. This pendulum, the duality of pendulum, this is called habit. Habits are like this, pendulums. We will be forced to do something and then we become detached from it and we move away from it, and again we will be forced in it. So pendulums indicate that Krishna, somehow we are not able to see Krishna as a cause there. In the Chathashloki Bhagavatam also Krishna explains, if anybody sees something existing without my connection, that is Maya. So that means Maya's first symptom is we will be caught up in this duality. So that's why when we are caught up like that, it is difficult for us to cross over on our own. We have to take shelter of the spiritual master, take shelter of other Vaishnavas. Go and explain about the pendulums in our consciousness. Take their help so that we become fixed in one. Ananyad ekam, everything is one without any second and that is Krishna. So if we can figure out Krishna in every situation, if we can see connection with Krishna in every situation, then only we can be free from these anxieties of dualities.

Here there is one more word is there, yasmai. Yasmai means actually everything is coming from him, everything belongs to him, and then everything has to be offered to him. That is the ultimate conclusion of everything of Krishna conscious life is, our offerings, our offerings to our spiritual master, our offerings to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. #### Pure devotional service, Ignorance, Chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/cPLYxqZ1R28?t=1667
**Verse References:** BG 18.7
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "How to bring in Krishna into our life more and more, into some situation, into our problems? That is made very easy by Prabhupada through this chanting."
**Summary:** The speaker details six aspects of ignorance to avoid mixing into our offerings, based on Krishna's teachings in Bhagavad Gita: future bondage, distraction, violence, disregarding scripture, self-assignment, and illusion. He concludes by emphasizing chanting Hare Krishna as a means to bring Krishna into our lives.

There are six things that Krishna explains in Bhagavad Gita. We should ensure that our offering doesn't have these six items. One is anubhandam. Anubhandam means without future bondage. Before we begin anything, little bit of calculation should be there whether it will bind me. Consequences, whether it will bind me, one has to calculate. Then shayam, shayam means distraction. That means instead of focusing on the service at hand, am I getting distracted, distracting myself. Sometimes if the service is little big, mind will not go towards it. That's the tendency of the mind. Anything big or anything unknown, tries to postpone. Keep the work aside and distract ourselves with browsing something, clicking something and putting something. This is takshayam. And then himsam, himsam means any violence. This action, this offering, a so-called offering, is it going to be creating any himsam, violence. And then anapeksha. Anapeksha means consideration without considering the scriptural directions. Anapeksha, udhasina, anapeksha. We don't consider the regulations, the rules and regulations. And then paurusham. That means it is self-assigned. We assume, okay, this is okay, paurusham. And then mohat, out of illusion. Illusion means impractical. Sometimes we may start something which could be impractical. So yes, offerings are there, we are offering every day, and we wanted to, but we have to offer, ensuring that these six ignorant things don't get mixed up. So then our devotional service will become pure day by day. So how to bring in Krishna into our life more and more, into some situation, into our problems? That is made very easy by Prabhupada through this chanting. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama.

### Srimad Bhagavatam I Sri Satya Guara Chandra Dasa I SB 3.6.35 I 02.06.2022
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### limitations of human perception, potency of God, mental speculation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/JiUwk_OyRrM?t=0
**Verse References:** BG 11.16
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The best path is to become a cruel-headed, submissive devotee of the Lord. Try to hear about the Lord from the bona fide spiritual master, and thus serve the Lord in transcendental loving service, as suggested in the previous verse."
**Summary:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa reads and explains Srimad Bhagavatam 3.6.35, emphasizing the futility of mental speculation to understand the vastness of God's form and potency, referencing Arjuna's experience with the Vishwarupa in Bhagavad Gita.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, 10-3, Chapter 6, Verse 35. Etad Satar Bhadravato Daiva Tarmatma Rupinaha Rahasraddhatyad Upatrartum Yodramaya Balodayam. Translation and perfect by His Divine Grace, Srila Prabhupada. Translation: O Vidura, who can estimate or measure the transcendental time, work and potency of the gigantic form manifested by the internal potency of the transcendental form of the supreme personality of Godhead. Perfect by Srila Prabhupada. The fraudist philosophers may go on with their mental speculation on the subject matter of the Virat, the gigantic form exhibited by the Yodramaya internal potency of the supreme personality of Godhead. But factually, no one can measure such a vast exhibition. In Bhagavad Gita 11.16, Arjuna, the recognized devotee of the Lord, says, planetary bodies, mouths, and eyes in all directions, and they are all unlimited. I cannot find the end of this manifestation, nor do I see the middle, nor the beginning. But Vajritha was specifically spoken to Arjuna, and the Vishwarupa was exhibited before him at his request. He was awarded the specific eyes to see this Vishwarupa, yet although he was able to see the Lord's innumerable hands and mouths, he was unable to see him completely. Since Arjuna was unable to estimate the length and breadth of the potency of the Lord, who else would be able to do so? One may only indulge in miscalculation like the fraught philosopher. The fraught philosopher wanted to estimate the length and breadth of the Pacific Ocean by his experience of the well, well three cubic feet large, and thus he began to puff himself up to become as big as the Pacific Ocean, but at last he burst and died by this process. This is his story. This story is applicable to the mental philosopher who, under the illusion of the Lord's external energy, indulges in estimating the length and breadth of the Supreme Lord. The best path is to become a cruel-headed, submissive devotee of the Lord. Try to hear about the Lord from the bona fide spiritual master, and thus serve the Lord in transcendental loving service, as suggested in the previous verse. Thus ends the Vattivedanta Purport.

#### estimation, material consciousness, comparison
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/JiUwk_OyRrM?t=217
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Always this calculation is there."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses how the material mind is always engaged in calculation and estimation, comparing oneself to others, even within the Vaishnava community, and how this tendency extends to understanding God, leading to flawed conclusions.

So, in our day-to-day life, we will be meeting a lot of people while traveling or while visiting some other place. Sometimes we will be introduced to some unknown people. So, of course, in the modern world, particularly in a corporate world, as soon as we meet one more, the more likely we are to meet one person, the more likely we are to meet one person. If we meet one and the same person, immediately they exchange cards. They exchange visiting cards, and both of them will come to know who are they, will come to know what is the other person, the other person also will come to know. Another is that, we have left for a long time, and we have to be a little more aware of what our true soul is and how the soul of this soul works. We have to be in the state of being. you are not sure how to deal with him or how to relate with him, right? So the mind starts thinking, who is he, what kind of a person is he? The mind starts calculating about the other person. And the process of calculation is that we will be estimating, okay, he may be like this, he may be like that. But if a card is given, you know, this process can stop and then we can make up our mind, okay, looks like he is quite powerful than me or he is quite senior to me and then immediately, accordingly, we will behave. See, our next steps are pending. Next steps are dependent on our estimation of the other person. It's a natural process. Next steps are the process in which our brain works, the material brain works. So, if a person is told about God as a person, God as a personality, then again the mind starts working. How, what kind of a personality is? Now, let us say you are a software engineer and then you come across one more person, he is also a software engineer. Then you will try to see, you will try to position him, okay. You know, is he equal to me or is he bigger than me or he is less than me? Always this calculation. Always this calculation is there. Even in Vaishnava community, we have this division. Seniors are there, juniors are there and equals are there. This calculation is there always. And according to our estimation, if he is a bigger software engineer, that means having a bigger position or whatever, then accordingly we will talk. We will immediately seek the information that we have. Seek to understand what is he doing, how big the project is. Is it double the project that I am doing or triple the project that I am doing? Particularly in terms of the money. You know, to get an idea, you know, how to relate with this person. So, if such people are told about God, about Krishna as a person, then immediately, mind will start thinking, okay, Krishna is a personality. So, how big is the personality? Some people say we are equal. These mayavadis, they'll say, we're all equal. I am God, you are God.

So they, they try to make it equal. They don't want to accept that the Lord can be all-powerful. They have no idea. So Prabhupada gives, he always uses this word called frog philosopher. Even in some religion, they, they know that the supreme person means he must be the original person, the oldest of the persons. And then they depict him as an old man, a very old man with a white beard and all that. Even Brahma Samhita says he is Purana Purusha. Why he is called Purana Purusha, Adhyam? Because he is the origin. Krishna is the origin of everything. He is the origin of everything. Satyamparam Dheemahi, this is a Gayathri word, Satyamparam is the supreme truth, he is the original person. So then he is Purana Purusham, then obviously he is the oldest of the personalities. But immediately it says, Purana Purusham Navayavanamcha, because as soon as the verse says that he is Purana Purusha, oldest, then immediately, our Minds will start painting, an old man with a long beard, the beard must be one mile or something you know, he must be having a lot of...you know all those things. This is...you know the Mind goes on like that decoration. So immediately it says, Navayavanamcha. But, he is always youthful. He is the oldest of persons, but he is always youthful. that is process. It is just miserable. It is same as what we talk about, the process of becoming drunk, the process of becoming strong. it is the process. Prabhupada says at the time of the battlefield, the Kurukshetra battle, Krishna must be about 125 years old, according to material estimation, but He looks like a 16-20 years personality. He appears to be growing from the childhood to adulthood to youth, but then beyond youth He doesn't grow. You saw that? So that means He is a different personality. So you should see the difference. Simply because He has some two hands and two legs, and we also have two hands and two legs, it doesn't mean, you know, both are same. You should see, okay, beyond youth, He is not growing. No, you don't see anywhere Krishna depicted as an old man. Right? That means He is a different personality.

#### frog in the well, estimation, opulences
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/JiUwk_OyRrM?t=668
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Calculating in terms of the feet. How long you can go on? Even million feet is less."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the 'frog in the well' analogy, explaining how limited perception leads to flawed estimations of the Supreme Lord's opulences, comparing this to how people estimate each other based on material wealth and power.

In this world, to come across such a different personality is a great fortune. You don't find, here everybody is a conditioned soul, with so many weaknesses and frailties. To find God in front of you, that is not ordinary. But the frog philosophers, they will not stop the calculation. Prabhupada explains this tendency, and Parvati is explaining that there is one frog in a well of three cubic feet. Just, you know, all over three, three feet well. And one day, one more frog fell into that well. And so obviously this frog, you know, the frog in the well started asking, okay, who are you? Where do you come from? It's like we have an estimation. It is also trying to estimate who this frog is. So he said, no, I am coming from the Pacific Ocean. You know, I live nearby. Actually frogs don't live in oceans. So it saw the Pacific Ocean. And it falls into this. And so it says, you know, I come from a place, the Pacific Ocean. Then the frog in the well says, what is this Pacific Ocean? No, no, very big. Then it says, how big is that? Very big. It's a very vast ocean. And immediately the well frog is saying, how big is that? Is it three feet? No. Ten feet? No. Hundred feet? No. Prabhupada says, ultimately it burst and died. And died by his own process. It is puffing, trying to show how big is that. It is calculating in terms of feet. Calculating in terms of the feet. How long you can go on? Even million feet is less. So similarly, when you see a personality, the personality of Krishna, then the philosophers, they try to calculate like this. Maybe he is little better than me. Generally, we estimate in terms of opulences. Whether he is more stronger than me, or whether he is more powerful than me, or is more wealthier than me, or equal to me. In terms of the six opulences, we will be calculating and trying to estimate where he stands. In terms of money, it is easy. You can put a number and see where you come. That's what the Forbes magazine do every month or every quarter. They put all the wealth of the richest people and then they will say, okay, you are number one, you are number two. It's easy. There is a system like that. So, they compare.

#### inconceivability, universal form, frog philosophy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/JiUwk_OyRrM?t=881
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Nobody can measure the Lord in totality."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the impossibility of estimating Krishna's opulences, even for qualified individuals like Arjuna, and reiterates the futility of the 'frog philosophy' in understanding the Supreme Lord's vastness and potencies.

Now, compare in terms of the opulences to estimate, okay, where he stands. Now, what about the opulences of Krishna? So, can you estimate? The richest man has some hundred billion dollars, for example. That will be for a month. Next month we have to see. And then hundred billion dollars. And then let us say, he starts estimating how much Krishna has. So, that process is called this Kupa Manduka Nyaya, the frog philosophy. Nobody can estimate the opulences of Krishna. You cannot even estimate how big is this universe. How many planets are there in this universe? One cannot even estimate. The most qualified person, Arjuna, he himself is saying, na antam na madhyam na punastha tava adhyam I am unable to perceive your Adhi, Madhyam and Anta. Now, this is, Arjuna is referring to the gigantic Vishwaroopa, Virata form. And when Arjuna asks Krishna, Krishna, I want to see your universal form, the form which is pervading in this universe, then the Lord gives special eyes to Arjuna to see. Because sitting in one place, you cannot see the entire universe. You know. We can only see to a certain extent. And some of us will require the specs to see even, you know, what is there behind. You know, that is our capacity. So, the specific eyes were given to Arjuna so that Divya, Chakshu, so that he can see the entire universe sitting in one place. And when he is seeing, he is also unable to find what is the Adhi, Madhyam and Anta. He himself is admitting that. nāntam na madhyam nāpunastavādhyam So, that's why, paśyāmi viśveśvara viśvarūpā So, Krishna's one universe and his universal form is so difficult to understand. So, here that's what Maitreya is explaining. yogamāyā bhalodayam The form, see, all the verses in the previous, you know, in the previous verses were describing the viśvarūpa. Maitreya Rishi is explaining the viśvarūpa, how various things are getting manifest, various departments of the universal form. And then here he is saying that universal form is manifested by yogamāyā. One single manifestation of yogamāyā. And then, there is not possible to estimate. Upākartum means measure in totality. Nobody can measure the Lord in totality. Lord's own appellances and Lord's various potencies, nobody can appellate. In fact, it is said that even Krishna doesn't, cannot estimate his own appellances and potencies. Sometimes he himself gets surprised about his own appellances. If that is the case, how can an ordinary human being can estimate the potencies of the Supreme Lord? Forget about entire potencies, even one universe, even that is also, in totality, to measure the one universe, that is also not possible.

**Summary:** The speaker advises a cool-headed and submissive approach to understanding Krishna, emphasizing the importance of hearing from the Lord or His representatives. He explains that Krishna's appearance shatters speculations and proves the realized knowledge of great sages, contrasting this with the 'frog philosophy' and the example of the 'God is dead' philosophy.

So that's why Prabhupāda is saying, be cool-headed. When you hear about Krishna, be cool-headed. Don't try to measure, because you will not be successful. And measure and come up with some speculative philosophies, that is a dangerous path. So that's why, actually in Bhagavatam Krishna book it is explained, when the demigods, they know, they came to know about, that the Lord has appeared in the world. When the Lord is in the womb of Devaki, the demigods, they appear in the prison. They were, they were wanting to offer their prayers to Krishna. And, they glorify the Lord, that, my Lord, now you are appearing in this world, in order to shatter all speculations about the Supreme Lord. Whatever speculations are there about the Supreme Lord, are put to rest, because, you know, by seeing you, by seeing your transcendental form, all these philosophies are put to rest. Actually, that is one of the purposes of the Lord's, descent into this world. He wants to show, who the Supreme Lord is. And all the other speculations are, put to rest. So that's why, Krishna's appearance is His mercy, because, the Vijnanam, the, the realized knowledge can, which is been explained by great sages, great Acharyas. So Krishna, by His appearance, He proves that realized knowledge. Like Brahma is the, original Acharya of our Parampara. His realization is this, Adhyam Purana Purusham Navayavanamcha. This is the realization of Brahmachrya. And Krishna's appearance proves, the Vijnanam, the realized knowledge of all these Acharyas. And then simultaneously, put to rest, all the philosophies of these frog philosophers. Like in sixties, when Prabhupada went to America, there was a philosophy, that, God is dead now. Why they are seeing that? Because there are so many problems in this world, that they are saying, God, God must have died by now. Because after creation, all those things, you know, He must be very old, and probably died also. This is their philosophy of, God. So Prabhupada, in one place, he says, everybody used to think God is dead. But now they understood, that the God is dead. They understood, that the God is with Bhaktivedanta Swami. So Prabhupada has, Prabhupada has proven, about God, about Krishna, he established, he, particularly, Prabhupada's famous conversations with his, disciples about the scientists. Particularly, with Swaroop Dham, with Prabhu, and then many other, you know, disciples. They used to ask a lot of questions, to Srila Prabhupada, about scientific, achievements of the, the scientists.

#### frog philosophy, science vs. realized knowledge, submission
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/JiUwk_OyRrM?t=1351
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "They don't go by the realized knowledge of a, of the Acharyas and the sages. They have realized, because they have seen the absolute truth. They have seen Krishna, and they are directly telling, he is now a Yavanamcha, he is a Parabrahma, but they don't want to accept that."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses Prabhupada's use of the term 'Dr. Frog' to describe modern scientists and their limited understanding of life and creation. He contrasts this with the realized knowledge of Acharyas and sages who have seen the absolute truth, emphasizing the importance of submission and hearing from the Lord or His parampara to understand Krishna.

And then Prabhupada, very famously, used to use this word, frog philosophy. And then he coined the word called, Dr. Frog. You know, this is a, actually that's a Prabhupada's, beautiful, way of presentation, to summarize, you know, big, big concepts into one simple words. Prabhupada used to coin these words, Dr. Frog. Like in the conversation, you know, today in this book we are reading, Prabhupada summarizes, what is Vrindavan life? He says, simple living, and high thinking. So just one single word, Prabhupada has summarized, you know, the concept of what is Vrindavan life is. So, this single liners of Prabhupada, very interesting to study. You know, devotees can do little bit of research, and then you will see, lot of single liners like that. Not even single liner, one word, Dr. Frog. That summarizes, what Prabhupada wants to communicate about, the modern scientists. One of the popular philosophy, those days was about, how the life comes from, chemicals. Even now, many people believe, the life is actually coming from, chemical combination. Somehow you mix up some chemicals, that will produce the life. So, Prabhupada vehemently, he refuted this philosophy. In fact, he fondly quotes, his disciple, Swaroop Dharmadhar Prabhu, who challenged the scientists. One of the noble laureate, is presenting this philosophy, that the life has come from chemicals. And then Swaroop Dharmadhar, he is there, in the crowd. And then he says, okay, Mr. Scientist, I will give you all the chemicals, can you produce the life? And the scientist says, no, that I cannot do. I can only stand and give classes, and lectures, and make some philosophies, and get some noble prizes. But I can't do anything, and do anything in practical, you know, in practice. You know, this is the, most of the people, these so-called scientists, they simply theorize. If you talk, if you type, how the universe came, how the creation happened, so many philosophies. Every month, I will see one, one philosophy, one, one theory. But because, it has been proposed by Dr. Frog, people take it very seriously. This is the unfortunate, unfortunate situation. They don't go by the realized knowledge of a, of the Acharyas and the sages. They have realized, because they have seen the absolute truth. They have seen Krishna, and they are directly telling, he is now a Yavanamcha, he is a Parabrahma, but they don't want to accept that. So this is, that's why Prabhupada is saying, he is concluding this pathport, saying that, be cool-headed, and be submissive. So, remain very submissive, and then hear about the Lord. From the Lord only, from the spiritual master. From the Lord, or from the parampara of the Lord, if one hears about Krishna, then, that is the best way of understanding, the science of Krishna.

### Strengthening Our Relationship with Srila Prabhupada | Sri Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa | SB 3.5.18

#### transcendence, devotion, gratitude
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3qiTgotuz2U?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "O Vidura, all glory unto you! You have inquired from me of the greatest of all goodness, and thus you have shown your mercy both to the world and to me, because your mind is always absorbed in thoughts of the transcendence."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the verse and purport, highlighting Maitreya's appreciation for Vidura's transcendental inquiries and their benefit to the world.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Kanto 3, Chapter 5, Vidura's Talks with Maitreya, Text No. 18. Maitreya Vacha. Sadhu Prashtam Tvayasadho Lokan Sadhva Anugranatha Kirtim Vitanva Thaaloke Atmano Adokshajatmanah. Translation and Purport by Siddhiva Ingereshwila Bhavichand Aravinda Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada. Shri Maitreya said, O Vidura, all glory unto you! You have inquired from me of the greatest of all goodness, and thus you have shown your mercy both to the world and to me, because your mind is always absorbed in thoughts of the transcendence. Purport by Srila Prabhupada. Maitreya Muni, who was experienced in the science of transcendence, could understand that Vidura's mind was fully absorbed in transcendence. Adokshaja means that which transcends the limits of sense perception or sensuous experience. The Lord is transcendental to our sense experience, but He reveals Himself to the sincere devotee, because Vidura was always absorbed in thought of the Lord. Maitreya could estimate Vidura's transcendental value. He appreciated the valuable inquiries of Vidura, and thus thanked him with great honor. Thus ends the Bhaktivedanta Purport.

He is saying, Sadhu Prishtam Tvaya Sadho, O good one! Sadho means a good personality. Sadhu Prishtam Tvaya, whatever you have inquired from me, it is in that inquiry, is actually greatest of all goodness. Sadhu. Lokan Sadhu Anugrunatha, you are showing your mercy by asking this question, because it will benefit. It is meant for the benefit of the whole world, and even to me. Keetim Vitanvata Loke Atmano Adhokshaja. And he is saying, because your mind is absorbed in the thoughts of the transcendence. Adhokshaja Atmanaha, mind is absorbed in Adhokshaja, that means some transcendence, or transcendental Lord Krishna. So Maitreya is appreciating, first of all he could recognize, that Vidura is absorbed in transcendence. How is that he could recognize? After all, he is experienced in the science of transcendence. #### humility, devotee, Vaishnava etiquette
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3qiTgotuz2U?t=303
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A pure devotee never considers that he has become a Paramahamsa. He has become a pure devotee."
**Summary:** The speaker shares an anecdote about Prabhupada's humility when asked if he was an incarnation, and then returns to the main point of Maitreya appreciating Vidura's questions, emphasizing the Vaishnava etiquette of exalted personalities appreciating their disciples.

So this is one more important point, when a speaker speaks Krishna consciousness, he speaks on the philosophy of Krishna, he thinks that it is for his purification. Like in Leelamruta we read, a devotee never thinks that he has become a devotee. A pure devotee never considers that he has become a Paramahamsa. He has become a pure devotee. So, once one of the reporter, he asked Prabhupada, he says, we see there are so many, the sadhus, coming from India. And they all claim that they are incarnations. Because everybody, those days, America was flooded with incarnations. Particularly from India. So that reporter was asking, you know, we are, every other day we see one incarnation, so who are you? Are you an incarnation? And Prabhupada says, no, I am not God. And he says, I am a servant of God. And then after one moment, Prabhupada corrects, he says, no, I am not even a servant of God. I am trying to be a servant of God. He says that. That reporter was surprised. First time he is hearing someone saying that he is not an incarnation. So, here, Maitreya is appreciating, Vidura's questions. And he is saying, yes, it is beneficial to me. Lokan Sadhu. It is the benefit of the world. So, when two great personalities are discussing, like Vidura and Maitreya, they are worried about the ultimate good of the world. So, that's why their questions are very important. And the answers are very important. So, this is, Vaishnava etiquette, that we see here, that even, exalted personalities like Maitreya, appreciating his disciple. And we see this, even in Shogadeva's form. He appreciates Parishit Maharaj.

**Summary:** The speaker explains the conversation between Mahaprabhu and Ramananda Rai about Sadhya (goal) and Sadhana (process), emphasizing that the goal of life is to be absorbed in Krishna and the process is devotional service.

So, here, he is saying, Janilun Sadhya Sadhan Nirnai. Yes, Mahaprabhu is asking, Ramananda Rai, that, I want to understand, Sadhya and Sadhana, are two things here. What is Sadhya? Sadhya means, what is the goal of my life? Nirnai, I want to ascertain, what should be the goal of my life? Sadhya. And then, in order to reach that goal, what is the Sadhana? What is the process that I have to follow? This is the question. So, once, one disciple asked Prabhupada, Prabhupada, we want to understand, I want to know, how this, African, black race, came into existence. And then Prabhupada says, what is the use of understanding, how the black race came into existence? He says, we are not these bodies. Why are you curious about understanding these black bodies? So, he is correcting his disciple, because, our inquiry should be, on Adhokshaja, on the science of transcendence. So, when Mahaprabhu asked Ramanandaraya, what is the Sadhya? What is the goal of my life? Our goal of life is, Adhokshajatmana, how we can be absorbed, in Krishna, on Adhokshaja. That should be, that is the goal of life. So, he is asking that, Sadhya, and Sadhana, that will be explained. The Sadhana for reaching our goal, is, the devotional service. So, here it is mentioned, that, a pure devotee, he can understand, the mind, the mind of others. Once, Jadhuran, Govindasi, in the beginning days, Govindasi and, her husband, they were sent to Hawaii, to open a temple there. That is in 1969. Very early days, Prabhupada used to send, one family, or three families, one by one, to various places, and tell them, you have to open a temple there. It is a very, what to say, very interesting phase of, our movement. So, Govindasi and, her husband, they reach Hawaii. They didn't have any contacts, no money. So, she thinks, okay, let me get a job, so that I can work, and slowly build a temple there.

Prabhupada, always understood, what is going on, in my mind. So, this is, an important, understanding, for the disciple. My Guru, is, capable, is omniscient, is as good as, Paramatma, to understand, me, and my mind. Is as good, in His, functional, in His duty, as Guru, is as good, as, Paramatma. He is not, God, but is, empowered, by God, to an extent, that He, understands, what, His, disciples, require.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3qiTgotuz2U?t=1268

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains that the guru knows the consciousness of his disciples and guides them, and that disciples should have faith that Prabhupada is active in their lives even when he is not physically present.

In Bhagavatam, there is, a glorification. Suta Goswami, glorifies, His, spiritual master, Shukadeva Goswami. And, He says, you are like, the air, which can enter, into the, body. You know, go into the body, it can enter, into the heart. Explaining that, the spiritual master, is not an ordinary person. We should never, consider, that the spiritual master, is an ordinary person. Govindas, he thought, why he has come, so early? As if, Guru doesn't know, when to come. He also knows, that they went only, some couple of months back. He also knows. But still, why he went? It's not that, he doesn't know. So, this is a, mistake that, she has done. So, Guru, He knows, the consciousness, of His disciples. And, He knows, how to guide them. How to take them, out of, their, feelings, of duality, feelings of doership, their feelings of, independent nature. He is trying to help them, in their feelings of devotion, to Krishna. As soon as, our life becomes mechanical, Guru will do something, so that, we again, find our way. Again, we find, the happiness, that we used to get, in our Krishna consciousness. Guru is continuously acting. He knows, what is going on, in each one of our minds, in our consciousness. So, a disciple should not think that, because, Guru is physically not present, maybe, He doesn't know, what's going on, in my mind. This is, the next level of Maya. One is to think that, Guru doesn't know, what is happening to me. And, second is to think that, He doesn't know, because, He is not personally present. Of course, here Govindas's case, He personally came to deliver her, to save her. Then, what about us? Prabhupada is not personally present. No. Prabhupada, is powerful enough to, act, through, the system that He has given.

### The Appropriate Way to Utilise One's Power | Lessons from Lord Rama | Sri Sampati Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### power, leadership, compassion, duty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/SnnIRDDzrs8?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Ram uses his power always to help weaker section of the society."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Lord Rama's use of power to help the weak with Wali's abuse of power against his own brother, highlighting Rama's selfless leadership and compassion.

Lord Ram uses his power always to help the weaker section of the society. Those who are being attacked, they will take shelter of Lord Shri Ram. Being powerful, he never misused his power in any manner. Lord Shri Ram is an inspiring figure for everyone because being powerful, he never misused his power in any manner. We see an example of Wali, a very powerful person, king of Kishkindha. What did he do with his power and position? He attacked his own younger brother Sugreeva. He threw him out of Kishkindha. Sugreeva was a loyal citizen of Kishkindha. He was a contributing citizen of Kishkindha. But Wali being angry and out of certain misunderstanding, he attacked his own younger brother, and he used his power and position to make an effort to murder, kill Sugreeva. Lord Ram uses his power always to help weaker section of the society. Those who are being attacked, they will take shelter of Lord Shri Ram. Lord Shri Ram helped Sugreeva by killing Wali. He made Sugreeva the king of Kishkindha. Lord Ram is not thinking, I'm very powerful. I will defeat Wali and I will become the king of Kishkindha. Even though he killed Wali, Lord Ram did not become the king of Kishkindha. He made Sugreeva the king of Kishkindha. So here we see Lord Ram as somebody who is not greedy for power. He is somebody who makes his followers leaders. He is somebody who creates more leaders. He is not somebody who says that I should be the only one who is the greatest. I should be the only one who is the king. I should be the only one who is powerful. He likes creating more powerful, more leaders under him and especially uses his power to help those who are weaker section of the society like Sugreeva who was into trouble because of somebody like Wali who was abusing power. That's why Lord Shri Ram is an inspirational figure for all the leaders to learn the lesson how to use power for what purpose we use power in our life. That's why Lord Shri Ram is an inspirational figure for all the leaders to learn the lesson how to use power in our life. That's why Lord Shri Ram is an inspirational figure for all the leaders to learn the lesson how to use power in our life.
