#### Social Pressure, Personality, Suffering
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZyiAShpZT7k?t=877
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So they move away from their real personality more and more in order to put up a happiness facade, some other personality outside and so they suffer inside."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that people maintain a 'happiness façade' due to social pressure and competition, leading them to suppress their true selves and experience inner suffering, loneliness, and emptiness.

Now, why do people do this? Because, because of the competition, social pressure. There will be social pressure. Let us say, you lived in a, you are using a Ford car. And you are living in a big bungalow. And let us say, situation has become worse. Now, you have to live in a Maruti 800. Not easy. Why? Because we all know, there is a certain social pressure. So, the stars and everybody also, they will be under tremendous pressure to maintain their happiness façade. They maintain their levels, to maintain their facilities. They will all be very conscious of all these things. It is a natural thing in the outside world, in the society. So, when they are like that, trying to be somebody. But there is no way to be somebody. You cannot be like this. You can't be yourself. You cannot be yourself. You cannot be yourself. You cannot be about yourself. You cannot be somebody. And actually it is not that. This is what we see. What are these people trying to do? They want to put on some personality. But actually inside that's not their real personality. So they move away from their real personality more and more in order to put up a happiness facade, some other personality outside and so they suffer inside. They start feeling lonely, empty, hollow. All these issues will be there. But even if you think inside personality, if it is not understood and is only maintaining an outside stuff, he will not be happy. A similar situation happens with all the living entities in this world. By nature, we are all something inside. But we are all trying to be putting up a different personality outside.

---
#### Real Personality, Humility, Sanatana Goswami
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZyiAShpZT7k?t=1024
**Verse References:** BG 15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All these top heroes. See the problem with them is they will not be able to come out of it and ask somebody, I need help."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the real personality as explained in the Bhagavad Gita and highlights the difficulty of those maintaining a façade to admit their weaknesses, drawing a parallel to Sanatana Goswami and Rupa Goswami before meeting Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.

And what is our real personality? That is explained in the Bhagavad Gita, 15th chapter: mamayi vamso jivaloke, jivaloke, bhootha sanatana. All these top heroes. See the problem with them is they will not be able to come out of it and ask somebody, I need help. There is one more issue. When you are trying to put up the whole thing, you don't like to disclose your weaknesses. You don't like to disclose your miseries, your sorrows. You don't want to. Why? Because that is against what people are perceiving about your personality. So, you know, these people are caught up in a kind of a thing and then they cannot come out of it. They cannot even disclose. A similar situation was there for Sanatana Goswami and Rupa Goswami before they came in touch with Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. They were big ministers in the court of the king, that time king, a Muslim king. And so, they were actually very big ministers. They have all the facilities of that age and having it all. They are also having it all. Everything is there. But then they understood that we are actually not happy. And when Sanatana Goswami approached Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, he said, It is actually a Bengali, nice verse. So, Sanatana Goswami is asking this question to Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. This is very important. It requires humility. To be able to submit and tell, Yes, I am feeling like this. I have this trouble. I am like this. What do you think is the solution? So, they went and asked.

---
#### Sanatana Goswami, Intelligent Question, Miseries
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZyiAShpZT7k?t=1156
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Many times we think if I have a lot of money, I can cross over all my miseries, all my unhappiness, my ill health, my this, that, everything I can cross over."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story of Sanatana Goswami, emphasizing his intelligent question to Chaitanya Mahaprabhu about his true identity and the cause of his suffering, despite having material possessions and recognition.

Who am I? What is my internal personality? I am trying to put up a personality like a big minister and a happy man and having it all and all those things. But, Why am I suffering like this? Tapatraya means there are three kinds of miseries every living entity goes through. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. So, Sanatana Goswami is asking this intelligent question. Saying, Yeah, everybody calls me Pandit. Everybody calls me, Oh, you are a great man. You are a great minister and I am having it all but why am I suffering? If I am so powerful, if I am having it all, why am I unable to resolve my miseries? Many times we think if I have a lot of money, I can cross over all my miseries, all my unhappiness, my ill health, my this, that, everything I can cross over. The moment I have everything with me, but then Sanatana Goswami is saying, Yes, I am having everything with me, but nonetheless, I am unable to solve my issues. So, he is asking, Who am I? You know, I am trying to put up something that is fine, but I am not happy. Inside I am feeling hollow, depressed, all these things. You know, tapatraya, all three kinds of miseries are haunting me. But then, who am I? Iha nahi janiya, I do not know. Khe mane hitahai. What is the real welfare, real benefit of me? I don't know. Actually, this question is considered to be a very intelligent question.

---
#### Intelligent Question, Stock Value, Identity
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZyiAShpZT7k?t=1275
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Otherwise, we may be putting up one show and going on in our life. We may not even know what is our innermost desires."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the intelligent question of 'Who am I?' with mundane questions about cricket scores and stock values, emphasizing that the former leads to true satisfaction while the latter can cause anxiety and even health problems.

As I said, we all will be asking a lot of questions in our life. For example, throughout the day, if there is a, say, cricket match, India and Pakistan, cricket match, let us say, somehow you are away from the TV, generally we will be glued to the TV, but let us say you are away from that. What will you do? Continuously, you are checking, what is the score now? What is the score now? Now, this is the question. What is the score now? Is the question. That question's answer will either increase your blood pressure, or it will decrease a little bit. It depends on that answer. Okay, so, similarly, some of us will be asking questions. What is the today's stock value? We have invested in some stocks. What's happening today? As soon as America killed Iran's second man, the topmost second man, the Indian stocks fell hugely. Some 2 lakh crore or 3 lakh crore worth of loss on that day. Just the previous day, the stocks have gone up to the peak. On that day, suddenly, something happened and everything fell down. So, sometimes I see, I hear people looking at this stock thing, sometimes they get heart attacks by looking at those. So, everybody is asking questions and seeking answers. But what is the most intelligent question? This is the most intelligent question. Who am I? Is the most intelligent question.

---
#### Satisfaction, Innermost Desires, Eternal Servant
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZyiAShpZT7k?t=1393
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you don't know who you are, you are trying to satisfy, but then it's not possible like that. You don't even know who you are."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of understanding one's true identity and innermost desires for achieving real satisfaction, and explains that Chaitanya Mahaprabhu revealed to Sanatana Goswami that he is an eternal servant of Krishna.

What can really satisfy me within my core? This is the real intelligent question. Otherwise, we may be putting up one show and going on in our life. We may not even know what is our innermost desires. We may not even know our innermost personality. We will just go on in our life putting up something and then trying to maintain that position throughout our life. That will be our struggle. So, when as soon as Sanatana Goswami asked this, yes, who am I? So, immediately, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu replied that Jeevera Swaroopai Nithya Krishna Das. He says, deep inside, you are actually an eternal servant of Krishna. Eternal servant of Krishna. So, as soon as we understand our identity, then we can work for our real benefit. We can work for the real welfare. We can work for real happiness and satisfaction. If you don't know who you are, you are trying to satisfy, but then it's not possible like that. You don't even know who you are.

**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Unless and until he realizes who is he, he realizes that I am the son of an emperor or a king he will not be having that satisfaction."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a king's lost son raised in the forest to illustrate that true happiness and satisfaction can only be found when one realizes their true identity and acts accordingly.

Sometimes, we hear in some movies, you know, when a king goes to the forest along with his small child and somehow something happens in the forest. The king goes back to the kingdom. The child is lost in the forest. Throws and becomes like, you know, some kind of a tarjan or whatever, you know, you say, you know, becomes like that. Now, I mean, just for the sake of some argument, that boy can be happy only in the kingdom. But now he is in a forest. He will not be happy. Unless and until he realizes who is he, he realizes that I am the son of an emperor or a king he will not be having that satisfaction.

#### Identity, Lion Cub, Grass
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZyiAShpZT7k?t=1539
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "As the lion is growing, it is thinking, I am also a deer."
**Summary:** The speaker shares the story of a lion cub raised among deer, highlighting how the cub, unaware of its true identity, tries to find satisfaction in eating grass like the deer, emphasizing the importance of realizing one's true nature.

One more example. Once you know, one deer was there and then one lion attacked the deer. It's a pregnant deer and then it has conceived the child, the cub, the deer cub and the mother died. And so that cub grew up among the lions. You know, that's the story. The lions will not leave the cub like that. But anyway, the deer cub has grown into a mature or a bigger animal. All the while, sorry, the other way around, there are many lions and one, lion has given birth to a cub lion. And the cub lion grew among deers. As the lion is growing, it is thinking, I am also a deer. It is continuously thinking, I am also a deer only. And like the deer, it is also eating grass. It is also eating some vegetables and it is also moving here and there. Everything is almost like a deer. Once one more big lion came and it is pouncing on all the deers. Everybody is running here and there. And this small lion deer also, it is also running here and there. And finally the big one caught this small one. And the small one is saying, leave me, leave me. Big one is saying, why are you fearful? No, I am a deer. Come on, wake up. You are not a deer. You are actually a lion. So, that child lion has a big revelation. Wow, really? So, you are not going to eat me? No. So, when the lion is there, but like a deer, it is eating grass because it doesn't know who is he.

---
#### Spirit Soul, Devotional Service, Unalloyed
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZyiAShpZT7k?t=1691
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "What satisfies the spirit soul is unalloyed devotional service to Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that just as the lion cub cannot find satisfaction in eating grass, we, as spirit souls, cannot be satisfied by material pursuits, and that true satisfaction comes from unalloyed devotional service to Krishna.

It is trying to take satisfaction in grass, vegetables. Lion's food is different. Internally it is a lion only. Unless it eats that, it cannot have satisfaction. So, similarly we are a spirit soul. You know, there is a display there. Behind you can see. We are a spirit soul inside. As long as we don't know what is spirit soul, what satisfies the spirit soul, you may be doing many other things. Like eating like this lion cub, it is eating grass and many other things to satisfy itself. But it can never be because it is a lion. Similarly, we are behaving, we are taking many, many things. We are trying to satisfy this body. But it will not satisfy us because we are actually a spirit soul inside. So, what is the satisfaction for the spirit soul? Association of the Supreme Personality. What satisfies the spirit soul is unalloyed devotional service to Krishna. You know this metallurgy. Somebody from engineering background, you know. Metallurgy is an alloy. Alloy means, you know, you mix some 2-3 metals and come out with one alloy. It is called. Unalloyed means there is no mix of any other thing. It is pure. Unalloyed. So, what is expected is unalloyed devotional service to Govinda Krishna. That means our devotional service cannot be mixed with any other thing. So, this is also called pure devotional service. This is one important point that we have to understand. Bhagavatam is not presenting Bhakti. It is presenting Ek Bhakti Govinda. It is presenting unalloyed exclusive devotional service to Krishna. It is presenting pure unalloyed devotional service to Krishna alone. Right?

---
#### Ek Bhakti Govinda, Motivation, Sense Gratification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZyiAShpZT7k?t=1813
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To love God for what he is a wonderful beautiful powerful merciful, magnanimous personality for what he is just to love him for what he is that is a that is an extraordinary devotion."
**Summary:** The speaker distinguishes between general bhakti and Ek Bhakti Govinda, explaining that the latter involves pure, unmotivated devotional service to Krishna, without seeking any personal gain or sense gratification.

Otherwise, Bhakti we will see so much. You know, in this world there is so much Bhakti is there. But world is not getting any better. Why? Because what is missing is Ek Bhakti Govinda. Now, what is this meaning? When we do devotional service, if you have an intention that I should get something out of it, that is called mixed Bhakti. Alloyed, not unalloyed. For example, we go to any temple or Tripathi, you know, you take a big list of what to ask God when I go. Right? We are busy reading it out. No, I want this. Meanwhile, they will kick you out. You are out of the temple. You have not finished your... So that Bhakti is okay. It is good that you are coming to the Supreme Lord. But that is not sufficient. Your questions will not stop. After asking God also, you will be anxious. Whether it will come or not. Will Balaji give me? Will Govinda give me? Or not? One more anxiety. No. Bhagavatam is not talking about that Bhakti. It is talking about Jataika Ek Bhaktir Govinda. Exclusive, unalloyed devotional service to Krishna. That means, while rendering service to Krishna, we have no motivation. We are not going to ask anything from the Lord. No motivation. Ahaituki apratihata. No Hetu. Why are you chanting? Why are you coming to the temple? Why are you dancing? Why are you honoring Prasad? No Hetu. An attraction towards the Lord. That's all. No other mix for our own pleasure. We don't look for our sense gratification. We only want to always see Krishna happy.

---
#### Exclusive Devotion, Table Devotion, Unceasing Devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ZyiAShpZT7k?t=1971
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "As soon as we get something, cloud. So that is the issue."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the concept of exclusive devotion to Krishna with common religious practices that focus on seeking material benefits, and emphasizes that true devotion is unceasing and independent of circumstances.

This is something revolutionary for our Indians. Or in fact any other religion you take. Everybody talks about, this prayer. You want this, you do that prayer. In fact in Hindus, we have a weekly timetable. Monday, you go to this temple. Tuesday, you go there. Wednesday, you go here. Thursday, one more song. Friday, some other ritual. In the weekly list, I don't think Krishna is there. As of now, it's not there. And in some seasons, they replace the list with the seasonal rituals will be there. They replace with that. So now, this table devotion, that is everybody knows. It's called table devotion. Vidura, Maitreya is not talking about table devotion. Or seasonal devotion. Suddenly you become big devotee. And after that, devotion is not visible. No. So Ekabhakti means Nityam. Nityam Bhagavata Sevaya. Continuously, our devotion to Krishna is unceasing. Whether you have trouble, no trouble, Lord gives, whether you are up or down, victory, failure, or rich, this, that, anything, our devotion to Krishna should not stop. This is called Ekabhakti Govinda. If you only do Bhakti when we are in suffering, and when we get something it's all in the cloud. Nowadays everything is in the cloud, right? Our Bhakti is also in the cloud. As soon as we get something, cloud. So that is the issue. So as long as this Bhakti cloud is there, then our questions will continue. So here it is said, Jatayikha Bhaktir Govinde Tebhyas Cho Upararama Vidura retired from his questioning as soon as he realized that exclusive devotion to Krishna is the goal of life.

Now how do I practice this? Exclusive? Actually once I was talking to one Muslim woman and I was talking about this. Exclusive Bhakti to God. And then see in any religion you see this is an interesting, a new concept actually. Because everybody goes to God for Tapatraya in order to remove the Tapatrayas. Remove all our anxieties and miseries and all those things. But here Bhagavatam is saying you go to the God, you develop love for God. Because to develop our attraction towards the Lord. We pray to God in order to increase our attraction to Krishna. We pray to God Krishna in order to increase our service to the Lord. Nowhere a devotee will ask Krishna I am having this trouble, you please remove this. He will not ask. Haridasa Thakur was beaten up in 21 places by a whip. He never once asked my Lord you come and you know, relieve me from this suffering. No. Prahalas was put into so much of difficulty. His own father Heranakashyapu has put Prahalad, Bhakt

### Why Bhakta Hanuman is so Famous? | Sri Sampati Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### servant leadership, Hanuman's qualities, dharma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xmjUwmJmQY0?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Hanuman used his power, his intelligence, his courage in the service of delivering dharma and nyaya."
**Summary:** This segment introduces Hanuman as an inspiring example of servant leadership, highlighting his power, intelligence, and courage in upholding dharma and justice.

How to become a servant leader. Ravan also had many powerful intelligent servants. Nobody is remembered today. Hanuman used his power, his intelligence, his courage in the service of delivering dharma and nyaya. Did he ever ask anything in return? What is the reward that Hanuman received? What are the reasons of Hanuman's enduring and ever-increasing popularity and worshipping Hanuman in Sanatan Dharma? Hanuman is an inspiring, motivating character in Valmiki Ramayana that teaches us how to be a good servant leader. Hanuman is considered very powerful, intelligent, courageous and very satisfied by rendering service. When somebody is powerful, intelligent, courageous, smart, they like to become a leader. But we see Hanuman being happy and satisfied being a servant leader. Hanuman is considered very powerful, intelligent, courageous, smart, they like to become a servant leader.

---
#### dharma, justice, compassion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xmjUwmJmQY0?t=82
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Hanuman is popular and worshipped because throughout his life, he used his power, his intelligence, his courage in the service of Dharma, in the service of Nyaya, in the service of delivering Dharma and Nyaya."
**Summary:** This segment illustrates Hanuman's commitment to dharma and justice through examples like supporting Sugriv and rescuing Sita, emphasizing his role as a savior of the weak and distressed.

Because Hanuman stood for dharma and nyaya. Whenever there was injustice done to anyone, Hanuman was there to deliver justice. Example is Sugriv. Sugriv was actually attacked by his own brother, Vali. And Sugriv was thrown out of his own kingdom, Kishkindha. He was wronged. So Hanuman supported Sugriv. He supported weak people. This is the quality of those who are powerful. A power is always used in the service of the weak people. In that way, Hanuman was in the side of Sugriv. When Hanuman came to know about Lord Shriram, whose wife was kidnapped, that means there was injustice done to Lord Shriram by Ravana. Immediately Hanuman came to do saviour. To deliver justice to Shriram, Hanuman also worked very hard to bring justice to distressed women like Mother Sita. Sita was put into a very, very distressful condition by Ravana and Hanuman fought with great valour to give justice to Mother Sita. So Hanuman is popular and worshipped because throughout his life, he used his power, his intelligence, his courage in the service of Dharma, in the service of Nyaya, in the service of delivering Dharma and Nyaya.

---
#### leadership, dharma, legacy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xmjUwmJmQY0?t=180
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Anybody who takes the side of adharma and anyai, they will be dead and they will be forgotten."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts Hanuman's enduring fame with the forgotten servants of Ravana and other historical figures like Napoleon and Hitler, highlighting the importance of aligning with dharma and serving a glorious leader like Shriram.

That is why he is very popular. Another important thing is, Hanuman is popular because his leader is a glorious leader. He became the servant of a glorious leader like Shriram. In Ramayana, we also see Ravana, who is also powerful. Ravan also had many powerful intelligent servants but nobody is remembered today. They all gave up their lives serving Ravan but nobody is worshipped and nobody, we don't even know their names because they were all on the side of adharma and the side of anyai. Anybody who takes the side of adharma and anyai, they will be dead and they will be forgotten. Even in the modern history, there are many so-called powerful people like Napoleon, like Hitler. Do we even remember them or their followers? No, we don't even know who are their followers. But Hanuman, from thousands of years, people remember, glorify, worship him because he stood for nyaya and dharma and that is why he has become an inspiration for everyone to follow.

---
#### selflessness, love, servant leadership
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/xmjUwmJmQY0?t=253
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Hanuman received nothing in return. He received the love of Shri Ram."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes Hanuman's selflessness and the love he received from Shri Ram as his reward, presenting him as an example of a servant leader who serves dharma and justice without expecting anything in return.

Even though Hanuman did so much service to Lord Shri Ram, did he ever ask anything in return? When I become servant of somebody, I am always thinking what is the profit of doing so much of service? What did I get in return? What is the reward that Hanuman received? Hanuman received nothing in return. He received the love of Shri Ram. So here is a selfless person. For whom, the service to dharma, service to nyaya itself is a reward. So I think we all have an inherent attraction to such people who stand up for dharma, who stand up for nyaya selflessly, not for any reward. So Hanuman is that example who selflessly fought for nyaya and dharma for Shri Ram and he remained very happy and satisfied. Servant of Shri Ram. So he teaches an example how to become a servant leader. He is a good servant of Shri Ram and he is a good leader for all of us to follow. That is why Hanuman is popular and his popularity is ever increasing and people love to worship Hanuman.

### Why Great Souls Surrender to Krishna? | SB 3.24.32 | HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### surrender, opulence, Absolute Truth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T-rAHNh1IxY?t=0
**Verse References:** BG 9.34
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you want to be perfect, just always think of Me, become My devotee, worship Me and offer your obeisances to Me."
**Summary:** The speaker reads and explains a verse from Srimad Bhagavatam and a related verse from Bhagavad Gita, emphasizing the importance of surrendering to Krishna, who is full of six opulences.

Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 3, Chapter 24, and Text 32. Uribihi, Tatva, Bubutsa, Ayaddha, Sadha, Abhivadha, Arhana, Padapeetam, Aishwarya, Vairagya, Yashaha, Avabhodha, Dheerya, Shriya, Portham, Aham, Prapadhey. Translation and purport by His Divine Grace A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada. My dear Lord, Your lotus feet are the reservoir that always deserves to receive worshipful homage from all great sages eager to understand the Absolute Truth. You are full in opulence, renunciation, transcendental fame, knowledge, strength and beauty and therefore I surrender myself unto Your lotus feet. Purport by Srila Prabhupada. Actually, those who are searching after the Absolute Truth must take shelter of the lotus feet of the Supreme Personality of Godhead and worship Him. In Bhagavad Gita, Lord Krishna advised Arjuna many times to surrender unto Him, especially at the end of 9th Chapter, Manmana Bhavamad Bhakta. If you want to be perfect, just always think of Me, become My devotee, worship Me and offer your obeisances to Me. In this way, you will understand Me, the Personality of Godhead, and ultimately you will come back to Me, back to Godhead, back home. Why is it so? The Lord is always full in six opulences, as mentioned herein: wealth, renunciation, fame, knowledge, strength and beauty. He is full in everything.

---
#### opulence, Bhagavan, Absolute Truth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T-rAHNh1IxY?t=163
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If anybody wants to understand Absolute Truth, then they are having a desire to understand Tattva, Tattva means the Absolute Truth, the Ultimate Transcendental Knowledge, then they have to offer their worshipable homage unto your Lotus Feet."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on Krishna's six opulences, explaining the meaning of 'Purtam' and 'Asamavoodhva'. He connects these concepts to the definition of Bhagavan and emphasizes the necessity of offering worship to the Lord's lotus feet to understand the Absolute Truth.

And no one can surpass Him. Another one of Krishna's names is Asamavoodhva, which means that no one is equal to or greater than Him. So, Kardama Muni is offering his prayers to Lord Vishnu, and in this verse he says, Aham Prapadhye, I surrender unto you. He says the Lord is full of Purtam, He is full of Aishvarya, that is the wealth, Vairagya, the renunciation, Yashah, transcendental fame, Avabhodha, knowledge, Virya, the strength, and Shriya, beauty. So, the six opulences are listed here in this sloka. A same list is given by Parashara Muni also, explaining how Bhagavan is defined as the one who has all these opulences in full, in infinite quantity, that is known as Bhagavan. And so here, he is saying, if anybody wants to understand Absolute Truth, then, Tatva Bubusyada, they are having a desire to understand Tattva. Tattva means the Absolute Truth, the Ultimate Transcendental Knowledge, then, Twam Suri Bi Sadha Bi Arahana Padapitam, then, they have to offer their worshipable homage unto your Lotus Feet.

#### devotional service, understanding, surrender
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T-rAHNh1IxY?t=1378
**Verse References:** BG 10.10
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I give the understanding by which they can come to me."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts discussing Krishna as Maya with directly worshipping Him. He quotes Krishna from the Bhagavad Gita, stating that those constantly devoted and worshipping with love will be given the understanding to return to Him, emphasizing Krishna's responsibility in providing knowledge to surrendered devotees.

There is no point discussing Krishna as Maya, Vishnu Maya. There are so many such podcasts and interviews will happen and they will all be giving various ideas, how you can become big in this, how you can become good in this, ultimately, it will not work. But just by worshipping and you know, being part of the being, basically, you cannot become You know, just by serving Krishna, everything will happen. That's what Krishna is saying. Tesham satatayuktanam bhajatam prithipurvakam. Satatayuktanam, if a devotee is always engaged, those who are constantly devoted and worship me with love, let us say a pujari is there, he is only worshipping, but he is constantly, satatayuktanam, constantly engaged. 24 hours a devotee is engaged in Krishna's service. That is pure devotee. 24 hours thinking, not that, okay, I can think now up to some level, then I can give one hour for politics, and another one more hour for some other thing, then I will be alright. No, Krishna here is saying, Tesham satatayuktanam, satatam means always, bhajatam prithipurvakam, with love they render service to Krishna, then Krishna is saying, dadhami buddhi yogam tam enamam upayantite, I give the understanding by which they can come to me. Now Krishna is taking responsibility of giving the understanding. Giving the knowledge. So, the question, that if I simply worship Krishna everything will happen? Yes, Krishna is saying, yes, I will give the understanding by which they can come to me. You can go back to Krishna only when you become perfect in absolute knowledge, absolute truth. That's what Kaddamuni is saying, tattva bhavasa, one who wants to understand absolute truth, better they worship the Buddha. And he himself is saying, aham prapadhey, let me surrender. Now Krishna is saying, if somebody surrenders to me and renders services, what will happen? I give the understanding by which he can come to me.

#### compassion, knowledge, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T-rAHNh1IxY?t=1534
**Verse References:** BG 10.11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The sincere devotee engaged in Krishna consciousness, cannot be without knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes another verse from Bhagavad Gita where Krishna promises to destroy the darkness of ignorance with the lamp of knowledge for those who worship Him. He then shares an anecdote about Lord Chaitanya and Prakashananda Saraswati, emphasizing that sincere devotees are helped by Krishna Himself within their hearts, regardless of their philosophical understanding.

Then final shloka he says, Tesham evanukam paddham, aham ajnanajam tamaha, nashayame atma bhavasto, jnanadeepena bhashut. Here Krishna is saying, out of compassion for them, for the devotees who are worshipping, for them, dwelling in their hearts, I dwelling in their hearts, destroy with the shining lamp of knowledge, the darkness born of ignorance. So here, in our hearts there could be ignorance, darkness, but the Lord is assuring that with a lamp of knowledge, I destroy this darkness, because I am dwelling there. Hmm? Prabhupada in the Paripoti writes nicely, when Lord Chaitanya was in Benares, promulgating the chanting of Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare, thousands of people were following him. Prakashananda, a very influential and learned scholar in Benares at that time, derided Lord Chaitanya for being a sentimentalist. Sometimes philosophers criticize the devotees, because they think that most of the devotees are in the darkness of ignorance, and are philosophically very naive sentimentalists. They think, okay, they are doing some Aachana, they are doing some Pooja, okay, study Pooja, you don't know Vedanta. You don't have any learning, you don't have any philosophy, like that sometimes they make a mockery of the devotees, actually that is not the fact. There are very very learned scholars who have put forward the philosophy of devotion, but even if a devotee does not take advantage of their literatures, or of his spiritual master, let us say spiritual master has written a lot of books, and then the devotee is not able to take advantage, seriously, if a devotee does not take advantage of the literature given by his spiritual master, if he is sincere in his devotional service, if he is very sincerely doing every day, he is helped by Krishna himself within his heart. This is the secret of a devotee's knowledge, his philosophy, and the capacity to understand the philosophy. This is the secret. Krishna, he takes the responsibility. He says, I destroy the darkness in their hearts. So the sincere devotee engaged in Krishna consciousness, cannot be without knowledge.

#### Krishna consciousness, Vedanta, chanting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/T-rAHNh1IxY?t=1728
**Verse References:** BG 15.15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you want to summarize Vedanta in one word, that is Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker shares an example of a young boy repeating philosophical truths, emphasizing that the only qualification is devotional service in full Krishna consciousness. He concludes by stating that devotees are mystical personalities, and that chanting Hare Krishna is the perfection of Vedanta, summarizing everything in Krishna.

A sincere devotee who is engaged in Krishna consciousness, he cannot be without knowledge. You see small small children I am seeing, if at all you want to see, see their podcast. Little little children, and then they are asking, how do we live our life? And he is answering. He is this much height, that boy is. Without getting affected by victory or loss, you should completely surrender to Krishna and perfect your life. You know, he is just repeating whatever he has heard, without getting disturbed by the dualities of this world, by your prescribed duties, you worship Krishna and attain perfection. This is what he said and everybody is shocked. So, the only qualification is that, one carry out devotional service in full Krishna consciousness. This is the only qualification. Carry out devotional service in full Krishna consciousness. Actually, devotees are the most mystical, mystical personalities. Their process is completely mystical and transcendental. They look very simple, they just go on, they may be doing some ordinary things, cleaning, mopping and all that, but if you touch them and ask them one question, you will see perfect Vedanta knowledge coming out from their mouths. Even if you think that he doesn't know, he will certainly say, chant Hare Krishna. Whoever is asking, he will say, chant Hare Krishna. Hare Krishna and that is the perfection of Vedanta. If you want to summarize Vedanta in one word, that is Krishna. Vedanta, if you want to summarize in just only one word, that is nothing but Krishna. Krishna summarizes everything. Vedaishya sarvaya aham eva vedyaha, Krishna himself is saying in Bhagavad Gita, by Vedanta everybody is seeking me, searching after me, this is Krishna. So, if the devotee just says, chant Hare Krishna, he is giving full Vedanta knowledge to him. Now what else you want? So that's why, never think that this devotional service, is something less or something inferior to the so called Vedanta and philosophy.

### Why did Lord Ram banish Sita to forest? | Sri Sampati Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Satya Gaura Chandra Dasa

#### leadership, character, public perception
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is very important to have spotless character to continue to be king and queen."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the question of why Lord Ram banished Sita, emphasizing the importance of spotless character for leaders and addressing potential doubts about Sita's fitness as queen.

Did Lord Shri Ram doubt his beloved wife, Mother Sita, especially after she gave Agni Pariksha? Today, we all demand that our leaders should be corruption free. If there are any allegations of corruption, we ask them to resign from your post, just because an ordinary citizen passed in your comments, they had this doubt that whether she is fit. Oh, look how Ram has become so blindly attached to his wife. He is so lusty. It is very important to have spotless character to continue to be king and queen.

---
#### dharma, leadership, morality
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=36
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It was very important for king and queen to have spotless character and that inspires the citizen also to follow the dharma marga, the righteous path."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that Lord Ram never doubted Sita, but banished her due to public perception and the importance of a king and queen maintaining spotless character to inspire citizens to follow dharma.

Did Lord Shri Ram doubt his beloved wife, Mother Sita, especially after she gave Agni Pariksha? Just because an ordinary citizen passed in your comments on Sita's character? No, never. Lord Shri Ram never doubted his wife, Sita. Then how do we understand? Then why did he send Mother Sita to forest just because of somebody doubting the character of Sita? It is because we need to understand Lord Shri Ram and Sita, they are not just husband and wife. They are also the king and queen of Ayodhya. As a king and queen of Ayodhya, it is very important to have spotless character to continue to be king and queen. So when the citizens had this doubt that their queen was with some other man for one year, they had this doubt that whether she is fit to continue to be queen of Ayodhya. Like today, we all demand that our leaders should be corruption free. If we come to know or if we get a doubt that this leader is involved in some gotala, there is some corruption charges, we many times we see people asking them to step down. We ask them resign from your post because you know you have to prove that you are corruption free. If there are any allegations of corruption, you cannot continue to occupy the post of a minister. Similarly, in those days, it is not just corruption, it was also character which was more important, right. So it was very important for king and queen to have spotless character and that inspires the citizen also to follow the dharma marga, the righteous path. If the king and queen themselves have a dubious character, compromised character, immoral, unethical, that will encourage the citizens also to follow adharma.

---
#### dharma, leadership, sacrifice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=177
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So Lord Ram and Mother Sita were teaching that to their citizens by themselves having spotless character."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes that leaders must inspire ethical behavior and that Lord Ram distanced Sita from the throne to uphold dharma, while maintaining his personal relationship with her.

In their life, so it is not just enough for the king and queen to do economic development and give all the facilities in the citizen, there also should be inspiring figures to follow dharma, to follow morality, to follow ethical good behavior, conduct. So Lord Ram and Mother Sita were teaching that to their citizens by themselves having spotless character. So Mother Sita, as a queen, her character was doubted. That is why Lord Sri Ram, till her character was proven again, asked Mother Sita to be away from the politics of Ayodhya or away from the palace, away from the king's life. As a king, he distanced himself from the queen. As husband, he always maintained his highest relationship with Mother Sita. He never doubted Mother Sita's character.

---
#### devotion, leadership, sacrifice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=231
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Rama gave the glorious position of wife to mother Sita only, not to anyone else, not that he rejected Sita."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the example of Rajasuya Yagna to illustrate that Lord Ram never doubted Sita's character as his wife, even while distancing her as queen due to public doubt.

When Ram did Rajasuya Yagna, Rajasuya Yagna or any Yagna can always be done by a married person with his wife, because wife has equal rights to participate in Yagna. At that time, Sita was at the forest. As a ideal king, Lord Sri Ram, distanced himself from Queen Sita, whose character was under question. But, as a loving husband he never doubted his beloved wife Sita. Rajasuya is an example for this. Rajasuya Yajna was done by kings and when Lord Shri Ram was about to do Rajasuya Yajna, the priest in Ayodhya questioned Lord Shri Ram, where is the wife? Because without wife, you cannot perform Rajasuya Yajna alone. At that time, Lord Shri Ram as loving husband got golden statue of Sita, his beloved wife and along with that golden statue Sita, he completed Rajasuya Yajna. If Lord Ram had any doubt about Sita's character, why would he do golden statue of his wife Sita? He gave that glorious position of his wife. In the Rajasuya Yajna, he said that only Sita is eligible to be part of Rajasuya Yajna as my wife. He never rejected Sita as his beloved wife. Yes, queen whose character was under doubt, the public had the doubt, not the king Ram, husband Ram especially. So Rajasuya Yajna episode tells us Rama gave the glorious position of wife to mother Sita only, not to Sita.

---
#### leadership, sacrifice, nationalism
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=349
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Lord Sri Ram is very different leader. He chose nation. He chose the growth of the country first. He chose the citizens' comfort first, not his personal life."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Lord Ram's leadership with modern examples of leaders prioritizing family over country, highlighting Ram's sacrifice and focus on the welfare of his citizens.

Not to anyone else, not that he rejected Sita. And another very important lesson that many people miss out is the topic of Parivarvaat, today we hear. Today we hear how leaders, powerful people and those occupy the leadership and powerful position in our country do everything for their own family, for their family members, right? At the cost of their own family members, they do everything for their own family members. At the cost of citizens at the cost of the growth of the country. They're very happy to see their family growing, the family property growing, and being with family, being together family, they say, no, no, no, I can't be in the jail. I want to be with my wife. I want to be with my children. But what about country's growth? Nobody cares. But Lord Sri Ram is very different leader. He chose nation. He chose the growth of the country first. He chose the citizens' comfort first, not his personal life. He kept his personal life away from the country. Where do we find such great leaders today? Ram is an inspiration for all the leaders.

---
#### leadership, justice, public opinion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=427
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "He said in his kingdom, citizens can without any fear even question the credibility of the king and queen."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts Lord Ram's tolerance of criticism with authoritarian regimes, emphasizing that Ram allowed citizens to question even the king and queen, and that he didn't use his power to silence dissent.

Today, it is very hard to find people who keep their family away and country first. Today, sometimes we hear like in the example of Bangkok in Thailand, there is a monarch dynasty. If you pass any comments, negative comments on the king or that dynasty, you will be jailed. You will be sent to jail. In fact, you may be sentenced. The life sentence be given for those who talk against the royal family. Lord Ram was a monarch. He had all the power in his hand. He could have immediately won. He could have immediately executed those who commented on mother Sita. But he didn't punish those who passed comments on the Sita. He said in his kingdom, citizens can without any fear even question the credibility of the king and queen. He did not threaten the citizen. He did not scare the citizen. I am the powerful person. How dare you talk on my family members? He was okay to be questioned. A king also can be questioned, he said. For that, he could have easily taken the side of Sita. And he said, no, I know the character of my wife Sita. He did not appear to be Parivarwadi. He said, let other authorities justify the Sita's character. Like today, we go to Supreme Court. If there is any corruption charge, the government themselves cannot say we are clean. We are clean. If there is any allegation. A third party like Supreme Court should hear and give verdict. And once the Supreme Court gives verdict, everybody will accept it. If Ram himself speaks in favour of Sita, then people will say, Oh, look how Ram has become so blindly attached to his wife. He obviously speaks in favour of his wife. He is so lusty. He is attached to his wife. So Ram did not take the side of mother Sita. So he distanced himself from the queen.

#### authority, validation, purity

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers

**Summary:** The speaker explains that Valmiki, a respected sage, vouched for Sita's character, similar to a Supreme Court verdict, which allowed Ram to welcome her back as queen.

But as husband, he always gave the position of a great wife to mother Sita. Later, in another authority in the society, in Treta Yuga, the great sages were given the great authority. Like the kings, they were also authorities. When Valmiki comes and he says with his tapas shakti of 60,000 years of tapasya, he says, I vouch for the character of Sita. And everybody now accepts. He accepts the statement of Valmiki because Valmiki does not have any favouritism, right? Because Sita is no way connected to Valmiki. And Valmiki, who was so pure with his tapas shakti, who has that faith and trust in the public, the public was ready to accept the statement of Valmiki. And when he said that Sita is clean, Sita's character is pure, then Ram said she can come back as the queen of Ayodhya. So, like Supreme Court gave verdict, Valmiki gave his verdict that Sita is, she has spotless character.

#### devotion, dharma, leadership
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/zJD7aOfE0tI?t=621
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Sri Ram is being away from such loving wife, just to show to the people, the whole world that how a leader always chooses his duty first, country first, welfare of the citizen first, and family is last."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing Sita's devotion to Ram, her understanding of his dharma, and Ram's ultimate prioritization of duty, country, and the welfare of citizens over personal happiness, making him a true nationalistic leader.

At that time, Ram was ready to welcome mother Sita. If at all Ram mistreated his wife Sita, he doubted the character of Sita, he has hurt the feelings and emotions of Sita, then what was Sita's response to this? The Sita's response itself will tell us, that how she was happy with Sri Ram's action of sending her to forest. At the end, mother Sita asked that life after life, I want Sri Ram as my husband. If Sri Ram mistreated her, why she wants Sri Ram again as her husband? That itself will tell us, clarify all the doubts, as a responsible, mature queen, Sita understood the Dharma Sankat of Ram. Ram has dual roles to play. He has a role of a king, role of a husband. So, the family of Lord Sri Ram always stood by country, stood by the welfare of the citizen, not the personal happiness, personal benefit. When it was about to do Pattabhisheka, become king, at that time also Ram chose Ayodhya first, his personal happiness later. He went to forest in order to demonstrate the entire citizen, here is a government that always keeps up the promise. It never goes away from the words, the promises that we have made, Raghukula Reethi Sada Chaliyai. Even at the cost of being in the forest and going through so much tribulation, even at this case, Lord Sri Ram is being away from such loving wife, just to show to the people, the whole world that how a leader always chooses his duty first, country first, welfare of the citizen first, and family is last. He is never a Parivarwadi, in fact he is a Rashtravadi. He is a great, true, inspiring, nationalistic leader that we all aspiring to have in our life.

## HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

### 2016-01-27 | SB 3.18.13 | HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

#### power of the Lord, demons vs. devotees, fearlessness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=254
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So a demon may become so powerful, but in front of the Lord, in front of Vishnu, in front of Krishna, they are nothing."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a snake charmer and a cobra to illustrate how even the most powerful demons are insignificant before the Lord, emphasizing Varahadeva's unperturbed nature in the face of Hiranyaksha's power.

Because he wanted to show off. So then he approached Varanadeva and he said, I heard you are very powerful, why don't you fight with me? So Varanadeva said, I am old, I am retired, please leave me Baba, don't fight with me, don't worry, soon there will come one person, Vishnu will come, he will take care of you. So from that time, he was searching, where is Vishnu, where is Vishnu, where is Vishnu? So when he came across finally Varanadeva, Vishnu in the form of Varanadeva, so he was thinking that, ah, I am so powerful, I will finish off this Vishnu also, what is this Vishnu in front of me? So until that point of time, nobody had dared to stand up and talk to him even. So when he approached Varanadeva and started insulting him, Varanadeva immediately gave it back to him. So he got angry, how can somebody talk to me like this? Shaila Prabhupada is saying here, the Bhagavatam is saying here, that in the purport, Shaila Prabhupada is saying, that a cobra may be very fierce for an ordinary person. Like I said, if a cobra were to enter into this room, what would happen? Everyone would start running here and there, right? No one will sit here. But if there is a snake charmer, among the monsters, what he will do, he will not fear, he will immediately take out his that he blows, and he will immediately start chanting his mantras and he will subdue the serpent. Simply catch it with his hand and he will take it and go away. Isn't it? Just like in Vandavan, because we are in the midst of open fields, sometimes snakes come into our campus. So when snakes come, then all the staff, everybody starts running around and everybody is yelling and shouting and all that, and we get some snake charmer. So when the snake charmer comes, everybody tells him, see, it's there in that bush, it is sitting there. So he will go there, he will put his hand, take it and he will have a basket, he will put in that, he will take and he will go. And you pay him something for that. So snake charmer is not afraid of the snake. For him, what is there, it is a plaything. So similarly, Hiranyaksha, a demon, might sometimes become very powerful. He may become extremely powerful. Just like this Hiranyaksha, he is so powerful, he may become extremely powerful. Just like this Hiranyaksha, he is so powerful, even the devatas, none of the devatas were courageous enough to stand in front of him. Even Varanadeva said, no, I don't want to fight with you. So a demon may become so powerful, but in front of the Lord, in front of Vishnu, in front of Krishna, they are nothing. So Varahadeva is not perturbed by Hiranyaksha's strength or his power or his reputation.

---
#### source of power, devotee harassment, divine intervention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=427
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So no matter how powerful a demon may become, for the Supreme Lord, he is nothing."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that all power comes from the Lord, using the example of Prahlad Maharaj and Hiranyakashipu, and emphasizes that even when demons harass devotees, the Lord will ultimately vanquish them.

Varahadeva is completely unperturbed because the Lord is the supreme proprietor of everything. He is the most powerful, he is all-powerful. And in fact, all power is coming from him. If the demon is powerful, where is that power coming from? Just like when Hiranyakashyapu, he was trying to kill Prahlad Maharaj, his own son, five-year-old son. Just like when Hiranyakashyapu, he was trying to kill Prahlad Maharaj, his own son, five-year-old son. He tried various means to kill him. Put him into a pit full of snakes, threw him down from the top of a cliff, tried to trample him with elephants, all of that failed. And when that failed, Hiranyakashyapu started getting afraid. And he questioned Prahlad Maharaj, where are you getting this power from? None of the devatas can stand in front of me, you are just a five-year-old kid, and all my attempts to kill you are failing. How have you become so much powerful? So Prahlad Maharaj replied, whatever is the source of your power is also the source of my power, which is Vishnu. So when he heard that, Hiranyakashyapu could not tolerate. That is how he said, where is your Vishnu, show me, is he in this pillar? And we know the rest, what happened. So, no matter how powerful a demon may become, for the Supreme Lord, he is nothing. He is nothing. And these demons, when they become powerful, they try to harass devotees. So sometimes devotees become perturbed that this demon is there, he is attacking, he is creating so much harassment for us. Just like Hiranyakashyapu, so much he harassed Prahlad Maharaj. And Prahlad Maharaj was very disturbed, but he pacified himself by remembering Vishnu and Krishna. By chanting Krishna's holy name, he managed to calm himself. So similarly, in all of our devotees' lives, there will be circumstances where we will see some demon become very powerful. And he will harass devotees. It may, the harassment may last for several years. But finally, when the Lord decides to vanquish him, then nobody can come in the way.

---
#### divine will, Lord's protection, power of devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=562
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Rakhe Krishna Mareke, Mare Krishna Rakheke. Rakhe Krishna, if Krishna wants to protect someone, Mareke, who can kill him?"
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Ravana's defeat by Lord Ramachandra, highlighting that even Lord Shiva could not protect Ravana because the Lord had decided to end him, illustrating the principle that if Krishna wants to protect someone, no one can kill them, and vice versa.

In the purport, Srila Prabhupada is mentioning the pastime of Ravana. Ravana was another demon who was so powerful. He was so powerful that he wanted to build a staircase directly to heaven. He was thinking, what is all this Punya, Girnya and all you have to go to heaven? I will build one staircase, let everyone go. What, only devatas should enjoy heaven or what? So he started building a staircase to heaven. He was so powerful. And he was a great devotee of Lord Shiva. And because of pleasing Lord Shiva, he had become very powerful. So when the battle between Ravana and Lord Ramachandra was going on, the fight was raging. And on one side, Ravana, on the other side, Ramachandra. And arrows were being exchanged, weapons were being aimed at each other. The fight was going on. And one by one, slowly all of Ravana's weapons were getting destroyed. So his chariot got destroyed, his horses were destroyed, his charioteer was finished, his flag was cut off, everything was finished. And then his bow also broke. So then Ramachandra fixed his last bow, last arrow on his bow. And he aimed it at Ravana. And Ravana knew, this is the end. Now, I cannot protect myself. So he immediately remembered Lord Shiva. And he started calling out to Lord Shiva. Go Shiva, please protect me, please save me. So Lord Shiva was sitting in Kailasa. Sitting with Parvati Devi. So Lord Shiva heard Ravana's cry. Ravana's cry reached up till there. But Lord Shiva was unmoved. He was silently sitting. So Parvati Devi, naturally because she is Lord Shiva's wife, so she has a... She has that little bit of familiarity with him. So she said, What is this you are sitting here, your devotee is crying. Go, no, do something. Just like wives will tell husbands, No, what useless fellow sitting there, do something. No, like that. No, like that Parvati Devi will tell, What is this you are sitting here, your devotee is crying. Why don't you go and do something? So Lord Shiva replied, Devi, what can I do? Supreme Lord Ramachandra has decided to kill him. What will I do going to there? The Lord has already decided, I cannot do anything, sorry. So Ravana was crying and crying for Lord Shiva's protection. But in the meantime, Ramachandra fixed his arrow, released it and Ravana was finished. So even Lord Shiva refused to protect Ravana because he knew that now that Ramachandra Bhagavan, he has decided to kill Ravana, even I cannot protect him. Srila Prabhupada used to often quote that saying, Rakhe Krishna Mareke, Mare Krishna Rakheke. Rakhe Krishna, if Krishna wants to protect someone, Mareke, who can kill him? Just like Krishna wanted to protect Prahlad Maharaj, even Hiranyakashipu could not do anything. And on the other hand, Mare Krishna, if Krishna wants to kill someone, just like Ramachandra decided to kill Ramana, Rakheke, who can protect that person? Nobody can protect.

---
#### humility, anger, sense gratification
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=782
**Verse References:** BG 3.62
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So the source of anger amongst non-devotees is the desire for sense gratification."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts the anger of the Lord, which is for the welfare of living entities, with the anger of demons, which stems from frustrated desires for sense gratification, as explained in the Bhagavad Gita.

So, the demons become very puffed up by their own power and strength, thinking that they are all powerful. But devotees, as devotees, we should not become puffed up whenever the Lord invests certain power in us for executing certain services. So as devotees, devotees are always humble and they know that the source of my strength and power is Krishna, is Vishnu. So they always treat that power which is invested in them by Krishna with lot of humility. Another thing that we can notice here is that Varahadeva also became angry and Hiranyakashipu also became angry. Both of them became angry. So what is the difference between the Lord and the demon? So the difference is that when the Lord becomes angry, his anger is for the welfare of the living entity. The Lord for the welfare of the demons, he becomes angry with them. So that by his anger, he can punish them and by that punishment they become purified of their offenses and they can become liberated. Whereas, the source of anger for the asuras, it's something else. Actually, Krishna says in the Bhagavad Gita, in the 62nd verse, 62nd and 63rd verse, Krishna explains what is the source of anger for the non-devotees. So Krishna says in the 62nd verse, While contemplating the objects of the senses, a person develops attachment for them and from such attachment, lust develops and from lust, anger arises. So the source of anger amongst non-devotees is the desire for sense gratification. So because of the desire for sense gratification, when a person contemplates on the objects of the senses, he develops attachment for them, he wants to fulfill his desire to gratify his senses. And then when he, and out of that and from such attachment, when he lusts after satisfying that desire, eventually that lust becomes frustrated. He is not able to fulfill that lust to the fullest extent. Then because of that frustration, anger arises.

---
#### anger, delusion, sense control
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=949
**Verse References:** BG 2.63, BG 2.58
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "From anger, delusion arises and from delusion, bewilderment of memory. When memory is bewildered, intelligence is lost. And when intelligence is lost, one falls down again into the material pool."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the consequences of anger, leading to delusion and loss of intelligence, and contrasts Dhamma (sense control) with Shama (mind control), emphasizing the importance of both.

Now when a normal conditioned soul becomes angry, Krishna explains further in the next verse what actually further happens, what does that anger lead to? From anger, delusion arises and from delusion, bewilderment of memory. When memory is bewildered, intelligence is lost. And when intelligence is lost, one falls down again into the material pool. So we can see here, Krishna is describing the danger of becoming subjugated by anger. Actually in fact, in the little previous to this, in the 58th verse, Krishna mentions One who is able to withdraw his senses from sense objects, as the tortoise draws his limbs within the shell, is to be understood as truly situated in knowledge. So Krishna in the 58th verse, he spoke about Dhamma, controlling the senses. That we all have to control our senses. The senses cannot be freely left to enjoy the sense objects. Now Dhamma entails gross control of the senses. Whereas in the 62nd and 63rd verse, Krishna is speaking actually of Shama, controlling the mind. One is the force of the senses, which impels us to enjoy the sense objects. And the second is the force of the mind. The mind generates certain desires. So if the mind is uncontrolled, then it finally leads to anger, and anger leads to further entanglement in this material world. So, Shama and Dhamma. Dhamma is important, Shama is also important.

---
#### sense control, mind control, devotional practice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=1074
**Verse References:** BG 2.60
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The senses are so strong and impetuous, O Arjuna, that they forcibly carry away the mind even of a man of discrimination who is endeavoring to control them."
**Summary:** The speaker stresses the need for both sense and mind control, noting that while devotees often practice Dhamma (sense control) through following regulative principles, Shama (mind control) is often overlooked, leading to frustration and loss of interest in Krishna consciousness.

If we do not control our senses, if Dhamma is not practiced, Krishna says what happens in the 60th verse. He says, The senses are so strong and impetuous, O Arjuna, that they forcibly carry away the mind even of a man of discrimination who is endeavoring to control them. So even a knowledgeable person, even a person who is a person of discrimination, not an ordinary person, a person of discrimination, a learned person, an intelligent person, a person who is a gyani, even such a person, if his senses are not controlled, what will happen? He will be led to destruction. So, one is Dama, another is Shama as we read in the 62nd and 63rd verses, if a person does not practice Shama, he may very well control his senses, but then, if his mind is not controlled, then that will eventually again lead him to destruction. So both Dama and Shama are important. Now, in the case of devotees, what happens is Dama is very easily practiced. We follow the four regularity principles strictly, then automatically Dama is practiced. And, but then Shama is something which we consciously, we may miss out in practicing. Dama is very conscious, we will consciously follow. I should avoid onion, garlic, I should not eat meat, I should eat only Krishna prasadam, very easily we can follow. But Shama is something which many times may miss our attention, that we have to control the mind also. On one level, controlling the mind means we do not allow the mind to dwell on material sense objects. On a higher plane, Dama also means that we cannot allow the mind to think whatever it wants. Sometimes devotees get carried away, that no according to me it should be like this, according to me we should do like this. And then when things don't happen according to the way they are thinking, they become frustrated. And they start thinking, what is all this, I thought I came to devotional service thinking that everything will be nice here, but there are so many problems here, this is not okay, that is not okay. And finally the person will lose his interest in Krishna consciousness.

---
#### mind control, desire for recognition, humility in service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=1232
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Because the Brajavasis, they have no desire other than to please Krishna."
**Summary:** The speaker warns against the uncontrolled mind generating thoughts that seem like devotional service but are driven by a desire for recognition, and contrasts this with the mood of the Brajavasis, who are solely focused on pleasing Krishna, regardless of the size or visibility of the service.

So, in the case of devotees, we have to be very careful to practice this Shama, this control of the mind. Sometimes this uncontrolled mind will generate some thoughts which will seem almost like devotional service, it will look like devotional service only. Sometimes devotees think, no I should do something big for Krishna and we give them some service, what is this small service you are giving me, I want to do something big, give me some bigger service. So, actually if one has such a mentality, even if one becomes a PR devotee, one will not be able to have permanent residence in the spiritual world. One will have to keep coming back to that. One will have to keep coming back to the material world. Because if we have that desire, I should do something big for Krishna. Krishna will say, okay, you continue doing that in the material world, you be there and you keep doing big things. Such people will generally become devatas. They are pure devotees, but they become devatas because they have some desire to serve Krishna in some big capacity. So Krishna says, okay, remain in the material world and do big things. So, a devotee of the highest order, will generally, will not have this kind of thought. If we look at the Brajavasis, why are they considered the topmost devotees, even above the devatas? Why? Because the Brajavasis, they have no desire other than to please Krishna. If Krishna is pleased by my doing some small service, I will do that small service. I will be happy doing that small service. If Krishna wants me to do some big service, then I will do that big service. I am not attached to doing something big or small, I am attached to pleasing Krishna.

---
#### surrender, pleasing Krishna, example of Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=1341
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Make me a success or a failure as you wish."
**Summary:** The speaker illustrates the mood of a topmost devotee through Prabhupada's example, emphasizing his prayer for Krishna to make him a success or failure as He wishes, highlighting the importance of surrender and prioritizing Krishna's pleasure above all else.

This is the mood of the Brajavasis. And we can see that very strongly reflected in Prabhupada's life also. When Prabhupada was going to the US on the Jaladuta, he wrote the poem, So there, Prabhupada says, he prays for Prabhupada, he prays to Krishna, Prabhupada prays to Krishna, saying that, you have brought me to this land of the mlecchas and yavanas I do not know whether I will be able to convince them about Krishna consciousness. And then Prabhupada says, make me a success or a failure as you wish. You see, Prabhupada did not pray, Krishna I have come here to serve you, please make me a big success. Prabhupada is saying, make me a success or a failure as you wish. Prabhupada says, make me a success or a failure as you wish. And Prabhupada says, make me dance, make me dance, make me dance. You see, this is the mood of a devotee of the topmost order. He is not attached to doing something big or something small. He just sees what Krishna wants. If Krishna is happy by my becoming a failure, I will become a failure. If Krishna is happy by my going to Naraka, I will go to Naraka. If Krishna wants me to go to Svarga, I will go to Svarga. Doesn't matter where I go, the central point is pleasing Krishna. And this is the mood of devotees Therefore, those devotees in Goloka Vrindavan are always respected everywhere. They are glorified by all devotees, everyone glorifies them, they respect them, they look up to them. They aspire to become like them. This is the standard of the highest order of devotees.

#### satisfying Krishna, humility in service, mind's tendencies
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=1452
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Even the smallest thing, just cleaning the floor, you do it nicely, it's enough. Krishna and Pravupa is pleased."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of satisfying Krishna and Prabhupada in whatever service is given, even the smallest tasks, and cautions against the mind's tendency to seek attention and praise through big services.

And the same thing is what Srila Prabhupada taught us, and the same thing Prabhupada demonstrated in his life also. So we have to, we as devotees, following in Srila Prabhupada's footsteps, following the example that Srila Prabhupada said, we should try to satisfy Krishna and Srila Prabhupada in whatever service we are given. It's not that we have to do something really big to satisfy Even the smallest thing, just cleaning the floor, you do it nicely, it's enough. Krishna and Pravupa is pleased. If Pravupa and Krishna are pleased by my cleaning the floor, I'll keep cleaning the floor all throughout my life, no problem. This is the real mood of a devotee and one has to train one's mind to accept this mood and practice this mood. Otherwise mind always wants to do something which will gain lot of attention, lot of puja, pratishtha, labha. So if I do some big service, many people will come and congratulate me, many people will come and praise me, that is very pleasing for me. Therefore I want to do big service. So the mind always tends towards these things. So actually in our condition stage, if we think that I want to do something big for Krishna, actually the motivation is not pleasing Krishna. The motivation is I want to do something big so that everybody will appreciate, people's attention will be there. Right now I am doing something nobody comes to know about it. What is this? I am going inside the something I am scrubbing, nobody knows, nobody sees, nobody appreciates. So that's not the point. So we should be careful. So this will look almost like Krishna consciousness. We are serving Krishna only, what is wrong? But actually behind that there is another thing going. So we have to be careful about this, what we think, we have to control, we have to train the mind to be satisfied

#### satisfaction in service, material duties, self-realization
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=1575
**Verse References:** BG 3.17
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Until and unless we come to the point where one is completely satisfied in performing whatever duties are assigned to one by one spiritual master, then one cannot, until that point one cannot be free from the obligations to perform one's material duties."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that devotees in Kali Yuga primarily serve Krishna, but if they don't find full satisfaction in their assigned devotional service, they become liable for not performing their material duties, as described in the Bhagavad Gita.

Actually Krishna explains in the third chapter of the Bagavat Gita, I will read that verse. So for a person who is engaged in devotional service, what happens is we devotees, we do not practice any other duties that we are supposed to perform according to the Vedas. According to the Vedas we have so many rnas to fulfill, rnas to parents, rnas to one's forefathers, rnas to the devatas, rnas to the teachers, rnas to the rishis, rnas to the rishis, so many people, we have so many kinds of rnas. But then what happens is we don't follow any of those, actually in Kali Yuga nobody follows any of those duties. Instead, it is said that in Kali Yuga we have to simply serve Krishna. Right? Now if we don't perform our duties which are enjoined in the scriptures, actually one becomes liable to liable for the reactions to those sinful activities. It is considered a sinful activity not to perform one's duties. Now as devotees what happens is if we do not find full satisfaction in performing one's devotional service whatever is assigned to us, then actually we will also become liable for not performing those material duties. That is why Krishna says means a person does not have to perform those duties. Who is the person who does not have to perform those duties? This is the seventeenth verses. One who is taking pleasure in the self, number one. Number two, one who is illumined in the self. And number third, one who rejoices in the self, number three, and is satisfied with the self only, fully satiated, for him there is no duty. So, until and unless we come to the point where one is completely satisfied in performing whatever duties are assigned to one by one spiritual master, then one cannot, until that point one cannot be free from the obligations to perform one's material duties. So as devotees we should train our mind to be satisfied, to take satisfaction only in performing the assigned duties to us in whatever service we are doing. If we are not feeling that satisfaction, then we have to understand that I am going to get the reaction for all, for not performing all my material duties.

#### mind control, sense gratification, anger
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/KTHudZWO2Ac?t=1760
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "And when our mind is not engaged in trying to satisfying the sense objects, there is no question of frustration of one's attempts leading to Krodha or anger."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating the importance of practicing Shama (mind control) to avoid seeking satisfaction in sense objects, thus preventing frustration and anger, and opens the floor for questions.

So, this is another important thing that in the practice of Shama, that devotees have to diligently follow. So, if one practices controlling the mind in this fashion, if one practices Shama, then naturally one does not, one's mind will not try to find satisfaction in the sense objects. And when our mind is not engaged in trying to satisfying the sense objects, there is no question of frustration of one's attempts leading to Krodha or anger. And thus one can be safe from the dangerous effects of Krodha. So these are some of the points which we can glean from this. If there are any questions or comments, we can discuss.
