#### detachment, dharma, boons
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/8X-VhBG8mRc?t=543
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He was always treading the path of Dharma."
**Summary:** Yamaraja offers more boons, and Yudhishthira prioritizes dharma and asks for success in their incognito exile and freedom from negative qualities. Yamaraja grants these boons, acknowledging Yudhishthira's inherent righteousness.

And after this, Yamaraja asked Yudhishthira Maharaj, My dear son, I am not satisfied with giving you this boon. This is hardly anything. Please ask something more from me. Actually, Yudhishthira Maharaj could have asked Yamaraja for the kingdom, to get back the kingdom that they had lost. But Yudhishthira Maharaj was so fixed in Dharma that he saw that at the present moment, to get back that Brahmana's firewood sticks was the most important thing for them. Otherwise, they would not be upholding Dharma. And that is why he asked for that. Yudhishthira Maharaj was not so much interested in occupying the throne, having a kingdom, and getting back his lost kingdom. I should take revenge upon Duryodhana. Those things were not there in his heart at all. He was always treading the path of Dharma. And as far as he was concerned, he was quite happy living in the forest, leading an austere life, wearing tree bark for clothes, devoid of all the royal opulence and servants and all the army. And all such things. He was happy as long as he was able to follow the path of Dharma. As far as the kingdom was concerned, in due course of time, according to the law of karma, if they had to get back the kingdom, they would get it. If they would not get, if they were not destined to get it, they would not get it back. So, he was, Yudhishthira Maharaj was not so keen. He was not so concerned about getting back the kingdom. Now, when Yamaraja asked him to ask for one more boon, then Yudhishthira Maharaj replied to him that if you want to grant us one more boon, then please bless us that now our 12th year of forest exile is getting over and next year, one year we have to live incognito. If you so wish, please bless us that we may be able to carry out this exile in incognito successfully. Immediately, Dharmaraj replied that, so be it my son, I grant you your boon. In fact, I bless you that even if you roam around in all the prominent cities, dressed in the same way that you are currently as the Pandavas, nobody will be able to recognize you. Even if you go without any disguise, even if you present yourself as you are, nobody will be able to recognize you. This is my boon to you and therefore, I am blessing you that the next one year which you have to live incognito, go to the kingdom of Virata and spend your one year there. And then Yamaaraj said to Yudhishthira Maharaj that I am not satisfied with just giving you two more boons. I want to give you one more boon. Please ask. To which Yudhishthira Maharaj replied, please bless me that I will always tread the path of dharma and I will never be overcome by kama, krodha, lobha, mada, moha, matsarya, these six enemies, which are there in the heart. And I will always be able to tread the path of truthfulness. Yamaaraj replied that my dear Yudhishthira Maharaj, right from your birth, by your very nature, you are always on the path of dharma. You are always truthful and kama, krodha, lobha, mada, moha and matsarya have no place in your heart. But still since you are asking, I bless you that you will continue to be free from these voids. And so being blessed by Yamaaraj like this, the Pandavas became very happy.

#### preparation, exile, travel
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/8X-VhBG8mRc?t=779
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The Pandavas left all on their own to Viratanagara."
**Summary:** The Pandavas return the firewood, spend the rainy season in the forest, and then discuss their plans for incognito exile. They decide to go to Viratanagara, dismissing their servants and priest before departing.

Then taking the Brahmana's Arani firewood, the five Pandava brothers they returned and they handed over the firewood to the Brahmana and they continued to live in the forest for the next four months which were the rainy season. Generally in North India, the months starting from July up till about September, these four months are the rainy season. And during the rainy season, during those days, the practice was that people would not travel anywhere, they will stay wherever they are to avoid the troubles of walking through muddy patches of land and also the rainy season is the time when the forest become infested with snakes etc. It's not safe also to travel during the rainy season. So the Pandavas stayed in the Dwaitavana itself for the remainder of the rainy season and once the rainy season was over, the Pandavas started discussing amongst themselves that now the time has come that we have to live incognito. Now where do we go? After discussing various options, they decided that as per what Yamaaraj had blessed them with, they should go to the place known as Viratanagara. This Viratanagara was so called because it was being ruled over by King Virata and the kingdom was also known as the kingdom of Matsya. And this Matsya kingdom, some people say that it is in Rajasthan and Viratanagara is about 60 kilometers from Jaipur. Some other people say it is a place called Bhairhat in Bengal. Either way, it was quite a distance from wherever the Pandavas were there and they undertook a long journey by foot to cover the distance. Before leaving for Viratanagara, the Pandavas, they sent whatever servants and maidservants they had, they sent them all back to Hastinapura and they requested Dhaumya also to... Dhaumya was their family priest who was staying with them. They requested leave from him also. And the Pandavas left all on their own to Viratanagara.

#### disguise, planning, roles
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/8X-VhBG8mRc?t=922
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Now we know what all we will become."
**Summary:** Upon reaching Viratanagara, the Pandavas discuss their disguises and roles. Yudhishthira will be an advisor, Bhima a cook and wrestler, Arjuna a eunuch and dance teacher, Sahadeva a cowherd, and Nakula a horse caretaker.

Having reached Viratanagara, the Pandavas started discussing amongst themselves, how they would disguise themselves and what they would present themselves as. Although, Yamaraj had blessed them that even if they were to walk around as it was, as they were, nobody would recognize them. Still, they thought it, they thought it better to disguise themselves. Yudhishthir Maharaj told his brothers that, I will become Kanka and I will become an advisor to Virat Maharaj. I will introduce myself to Virat Maharaj, saying that I used to be an advisor in Yudhishthir Maharaj's court. And now that Yudhishthir Maharaj and the Pandavas are in forest exile, I don't have a job, so I have come seeking a job with you. Then he looked at Bhima and he said, what will you do? Bhima said, I am very good in cooking. I will go to Virat Maharaj and I will tell him, I was a cook in Yudhishthir Maharaj's kitchen and I also know a little bit of wrestling. So please appoint me as a cook in your kitchen and I will make wonderful dishes for you. And also whenever some free time is there, I will wrestle some lions and elephants and all that. And I will keep you entertained. Next, Yudhishthir Maharaj turned to Arjuna and asked him, my dear Arjuna, what will you present yourself as? At that time, Arjuna remembered when he had been to the heavenly kingdom on the invitation of Indra, Urvasi had approached him, but on the plea that Urvasi was the wife of one of the ancestors of Arjuna's dynasty, the King Pururava, whose wife was Urvasi, Arjuna said, you are like my mother and therefore I cannot see you in any other manner. And being rebuffed like this, Urvasi being frustrated in her advances towards Arjuna, she cursed him that for one year he will have to become a eunuch. Now Arjuna remembered that curse of Urvasi and he said, now is the time for Urvasi's curse to take place. I'll become a eunuch. And because I know good music and dancing, I will teach all the women folk in Virat Maharaj's palace, all the different forms of singing and dance. So the brothers were surprised to hear this. Arjuna, the great warrior, he is going to become a eunuch for one year. Anyway, this is all destiny. The Lord's leelas are like this. Then Yudhishthira Maharaj turned to Sahadeva and asked him, what will you do? Sahadeva replied, I will become a cowherd by the name Tantripala and I will take care of the cows in Virat Maharaj's palace. And then Nakula said that I will become Granthika, a person who takes care of horses, because Nakula was very expert in identifying horses, taking care of them, breeding them, etc. And therefore Nakula said, I will take a place in the stable of Virat Maharaj. Having discussed like this, all the Pandavas decided that okay, now we know what all we will become.

#### weapons, disguise, strategy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/8X-VhBG8mRc?t=1134
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Nobody will become curious enough to go and unpack those weapons."
**Summary:** The Pandavas decide to hide their weapons in a wrapped bundle on top of a semi-tree near the crematorium, using a story about a corpse to deter anyone from investigating.

And so therefore, now what to do with all our weapons? If the cook Bhima, who is going to be the cook named Vallabha, if he is going to go with his Ghada into the assembly, then nobody will believe that he is a cook. And if Arjuna goes with his Gandiva and says, I am a unit, nobody is going to believe him. Therefore, all the Pandavas wrapped up all their weapons in a nice piece of cloth, completely covered it up. And when they approached Virat Nagar, at the entry to the Virat Nagara, they came across a big crematorium. And outside the crematorium was a big semi-tree. They climbed onto the semi-tree and on the top of the semi-tree, they kept all the packed weapons. And they left the place. Some people were there who were coming to the crematorium. They came across some people and they told them in our custom, all dead bodies we cover in cloth and keep it on top of the semi-tree so that the soul goes to heaven. And our mother recently passed away. So we have put her corpse, we have covered it in a cloth and kept it on the top. When the people heard, they said, oh my God, corpse up there, we should not touch it. And this way, the Pandavas thought, nobody will touch those. Nobody will become curious enough to go and unpack those weapons.

#### infiltration, disguise, employment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/8X-VhBG8mRc?t=1218
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I hope very quickly that gets over."
**Summary:** Yudhishthira, Bhima, and Draupadi enter Virata's court and secure positions. Virata welcomes Yudhishthira as an advisor, Bhima as a cook, and Draupadi as a maidservant to the queen, Sudeshna, after addressing Sudeshna's concerns about her beauty.

The first day, Yudhishthir Maharaj entered the court of Virat Maharaj and he introduced himself as Kanka. And he told the king that he had been an advisor in the court of Yudhishthir Maharaj. And when Virat Maharaj heard this, Virata was actually a great front of the Pandavas. When he heard that this Kanka had been an advisor in Yudhishthir Maharaj's palace, he very warmly welcomed Kanka and he told him, please welcome, please be an advisor to me also. I don't know how the Pandavas are living in the forest. It is a very unfortunate situation. I hope very quickly that gets over. He was also feeling a little bad about that. The following day, Bhima entered the court of Virata Maharaj. And he introduced himself as Vallabha, the cook. And he told the king that please appoint me in your kitchen. I used to cook in Yudhishthir Maharaj's kitchen. And I know a variety of dishes and every day I will cook very fine dishes for you. The king was very pleased to see this huge able-bodied cook. And he said, okay, since you are coming from Yudhishthir Maharaj's kitchen, you must definitely be a good cook. So right away, I am appointing you. The next day, Draupadi entered the kingdom. And she approached the king Virata's wife, Sudeshna. And she introduced herself as a class of maidservants known as Sairindri. And she informed Sudeshna that she had been earlier a servant of Draupadi as well as Satyabhama. And now that Draupadi had gone into the forest in exile, she had lost her job and she was looking for a job. And that's how she had come to meet Sudeshna seeking an employment. Sudeshna, when she looked at this woman, she saw that she was extremely exquisitely beautiful. She told her that if you have served as Satyabhama, Krishna's wife and Draupadi, definitely you must be very qualified. I will be very happy to have you with me. But seeing your beauty, I am very afraid that the king may get carried away and he may think of making you the queen. Then what will become of me? Then this Sairindri maidservant replied to Sudeshna that please do not worry. I am married to five Gandharva husbands and I am completely devoted and chaste to my husbands. I will never look at any other man. As far as you are concerned, you protect me from other men. I will be only with you. I don't want to be in any place where the men will be able to see me. When Sudeshna heard this, she said, okay, in that case, I am quite pacified that you will not try to woo my husband and take over my place. Then I am willing to appoint you as a maidservant in my palace. And then Draupadi told Sudeshna two conditions I want to put before you. One is that you will not make me wash anybody's feet and I will not eat any leftover food. Sudeshna immediately agreed. She said, no problem. I will ensure that you get the best food and you won't have to wash anybody's feet.

#### disguise, acceptance, infiltration
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/8X-VhBG8mRc?t=1433
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Very, very nice."
**Summary:** Arjuna, as Brihannala, is inspected and accepted as a eunuch to teach dance and singing to Uttara. Sahadeva and Nakula also gain employment as a cowherd and horse caretaker, respectively, completing the Pandavas' infiltration of Virata's kingdom.

The next day, Arjuna walked into the court of Virataraj and everyone was astonished to see this huge bodied man dressed in a sari and hands full of bangles. And walking like a woman, although externally it was obvious that it was a man. And the King Virata was also astonished. And he asked, who are you? Where have you come from? And why have you come to my court? Then this Brihannala, this eunuch introduced herself saying that my name is Brihannala. I am a eunuch and I have no parents. I know the art of singing. And dance. So I am requesting you. Please appoint me to teach the art of dancing and singing to your daughter Uttara. The King Virata also looked at Brihannala and then he said that if you are truly a eunuch, I don't mind appointing you. And so he had a few maidservants from his palace inspect Brihannala. They took away Brihannala for some time and they came back and they reported to the King that they have tested him. He is clearly a eunuch. He is not a male. Then Virata, Virata Maharaj was also happy to appoint Brihannala as a teacher of dance and singing for his daughter Uttara. And because of Urvashi's curse, Arjuna in the form of Brihannala felt no lusty desires awakening in his heart, even when he was in the midst of all the women folk in the palace. The next day, Sahadeva entered the cow shed of Virata Maharaj and he approached the King Virata saying that he would like to be appointed as a cowherd and the King Virata had one lakh cows in his cow shed and he was very glad to appoint Sahadeva. And the day after that, finally, Nakula entered the stable of the King Virata and King Virata happened to see Nakula in his horse stable and when he started inquiring from him, Nakula said that he had been earlier serving in the stable of Yudhishthira Maharaj. And so now he would, he was seeking employment and Virata Maharaj was very happy. Oh, one more person from Yudhishthira's palace has come here. Very, very nice. So you are welcome and he appointed him also. And like this, all the five brothers and their wife, managed to enter undetected into the kingdom of Virata Maharaj and they positioned themselves as different ordinary servants.

#### incognito, disguise, future
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/8X-VhBG8mRc?t=1609
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Thank you for joining us today."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by posing a question about the success of the Pandavas' incognito life and invites the audience to join the next episode.

Now, how did the one year of their incognito life in Virata go and how were they successful in maintaining their disguises for the whole period of one year which was agreed upon in the game of dice? Please join us in the next episode to know about this. Thank you for joining us today. Hare Krishna.

---
#### disguise, strategy, danger
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mbHtRZ49ssY?t=93
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In case of any danger and in case of need of any help, they decided on code names by which they would call each other."
**Summary:** The speaker summarizes the previous episode, detailing the Pandavas' decision to go incognito in the kingdom of Virata and the code names they adopted to signal danger to one another.

All of you are welcome to this next episode of the Mahabharata. In the last episode, we have seen how the Pandavas had completed their 12 years of forest exile and now it was time for them to go incognito. And fortunately, they also happened to receive the blessings of Yamaraj, by which they were assured that they would be able to successfully go back to their native land. They were able to successfully pass the time of one year incognito without anyone recognizing them. Having now obtained the boon from Yamaraj, still the Pandavas felt it wise to disguise themselves and as advised by Yamaraj, they decided to go to the kingdom of Virata. And in the kingdom of Virata, the five Pandavas entered Virata separately. Yudhishthir Maharaj became Kanka, an advisor to the king. Bhima became Vallabha, a cook in the royal kitchens. Draupadi became Sairindri, a maid-servant who dresses the hairs for the queen of King Virata, Sudeshna. Arjuna became Brihannala, a eunuch, and he was appointed by King Virata to teach art of dance and music to his young daughter Uttara. And then Nakula became Granthika, who was going to take care of the horses in the stable of King Virata. Sahadeva became Tantripala, who was going to take care of the cows of King Virata. After entering the kingdom of Virata, the Pandavas had a secret code by which they would call each other if any one of them were to be in any kind of trouble or danger and they needed the help of the others. In case they were in danger, because they were all entering separately and they were not in their disguise going to reveal that they were related to each other in any way, they would not be able to call out the names what they had assumed, nor could they call out their original names. So, in case of any danger and in case of need of any help, they decided on code names by which they would call each other. So, they gave themselves the names of Jayanta, Vijaya, Jaya, Jayat Sena, Jayat Bala. Like this, they gave each other names by which they could call out, any one of them could call out the other's names, and then immediately the others would understand that there is some danger and this person needed help.

---
#### intrigue, politics, danger
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mbHtRZ49ssY?t=270
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Although Keechaka was a disturbance, he was also a great protective shield for Virata's kingdom."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the peaceful life of the Pandavas in disguise, the efforts of Duryodhana to find them, and the character of Keechaka, the commander-in-chief of Virata's army, who was both a terror and a protector.

Now, the Pandavas, they had entranced themselves in their respective positions and things were going quite smoothly. Nobody had detected them. In the meantime, Duryodhana had started sending spies all over the place. They were going to various kingdoms and they were observing who are the kind of people there, whether the Pandavas are there or not. Because Duryodhana's intention was, if somehow or the other he is able to uncover their disguise, then again the Pandavas would have to go into another 12 years of forest exile. So, he could very comfortably, without any opposition, he could enjoy the kingdom of the Pandavas as well as his own kingdom which was Hastinapura. Now, although the spies were going everywhere, they could not find any trace of the Pandavas. And so, the Pandavas were able to keep up their disguise very effectively for more than 10 months. During these 10 months, the Pandavas were able to live peacefully. More or less, they were avoiding all attention. And Bhima would also, because he was a cook, he was in the kitchen, whenever he would cook some delicacies, he would come in the night secretly, meet with his other brothers and share with them. And sometimes Tantripala would get some cow milk products, he would share it with his brothers. And sometimes Yudhishthira would get some gifts from the king, he would come and share it with the brothers. So, like this, they were managing to pass their time. Although they were not very happy being servants to subordinate kings, it was not the ideal position to be in, but they were not in any kind of trouble also. Now, like this, after more than 10 months had passed, they were into the 11th month. This queen Sudeshna had a brother and the name of this brother was Keechaka. He was extremely powerful and very well built and he was very expert in fighting and in the art of warfare. Therefore, King Virata had appointed Keechaka as his commander-in-chief of his army. Now, this Keechaka was quite a terror and he had created lot of problems for all the surrounding neighboring kingdoms and even for the Kauravas, this Keechaka was a headache. But then he was quite powerful, so nobody dared to oppose him openly like that. Nobody wanted a direct confrontation like that. So, to maintain peace, they would tolerate his, he would sometimes encroach on them. He would encroach into others' kingdoms. He would go and sometimes pillage a town or something like that. Those kind of things he was off and on doing. But rival kings were keeping distance because they didn't want to antagonize him. And Virata was also heavily dependent on Keechaka for the protection of his kingdom. Although Keechaka was a disturbance, he was also a great protective shield for Virata's kingdom. Otherwise, the surrounding neighboring enemy kings could easily swallow the kingdom of Virata. Virata, King Virata himself was not so powerful. So, he was quite lenient and he was, he used to not take too much notice of the wrongdoings of Keechaka.

---
#### lust, infatuation, dharma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mbHtRZ49ssY?t=483
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Actually, you should be in a much better condition than this. Why don't you become my queen?"
**Summary:** Keechaka sees Draupadi (Sairindri) and becomes infatuated, expressing his lustful desires and proposing to her, which alarms Draupadi.

Now, this Keechaka one day happened to visit his sister Sudeshna and there he happened to see Draupadi who was a Sai Rindri. Now, when he saw her, immediately this Keechaka, he was infatuated with her. And he was not a man of great any good morals or something like that. So, he immediately developed lusty desires towards Sai Rindri. And he started asking Sudeshna, Who is this girl? I have never seen her before. Where has she come from? What is she doing? And all these kind of things. And then when Draupadi, Sai Rindri came to Sudeshna for some work, immediately Keechaka started talking to her. Oh, my beautiful girl. Who are you? Where are you from? I have never seen you before. I am Keechaka. I am the commander of the king's army. So, you are serving like a maid servant in my sister's palace. Actually, you should be in a much better condition than this. Why don't you become my queen? Like this, he started talking. Now, Sai Rindri immediately, she got alarmed. What is this man saying? All these months, she had managed to keep a low profile and avoid the gaze of any men. And somehow, she was managing. Because Draupadi was exquisitely beautiful. That was the fear that Sudeshna also had. Now, Keechaka's eyes, unfortunately, fell on Sai Rindri. Now, Sai Rindri, immediately she was taken aback. And she said, Oh, my... Oh, king, please don't talk like this. Because I am already wedded to another person. So, it is not appropriate for a person like you, who is from a royal family, to set his eyes upon a woman who is already married. This is against the principles of Dharma. So, please give up such thoughts. And please avoid thinking of me in such way. And give up any hopes like this if you have towards developing any relationship with me. Like this, Sai Rindri said. And she immediately turned and walked away. Now, Keechaka was not someone to give up so easily. Although he was rebuffed by Sai Rindri, he was not, in the least, he was not deterred. He spoke to Sudeshna and he said that somehow I have to get her. How to get her hand? How to woo her? How to make her agree to my proposal? So, Sudeshna looked at her brother. And because he was, after all, her brother, she said, Don't worry, because in front of everybody, she might have felt a little shy. So, she might have spoken like that. I will create some excuse and send her to you for some small work. At that time, in a secluded place, you propose to her. Maybe, if you nicely talk to her, she may change her mind also. Sudeshna said like this. Because in those times, in the royal family, sometimes the kings would have relationships with some of the maid servants also. Although they may not be wedded with the maid servants. And this was not so uncommon. And so, Sudeshna thought, Maybe, Sairendri may become interested. If she becomes interested and both adults are consenting, then what's the problem?

#### devotion, protection, divine intervention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mbHtRZ49ssY?t=672
**Verse References:** BG 9.11
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is the source of all the Devatas. The Devatas are all created by Krishna himself, by Vishnu."
**Summary:** Sudeshna sends Draupadi to Keechaka's chambers, despite Draupadi's protests. Draupadi prays to Suryadeva for protection, leading to a discussion about the relationship between devotees, demigods, and Lord Krishna.

So, then, after a few days, Sudeshna found some opportunity. And she told Draupadi, Sairendri, that you please fetch some rice and some other things from my brother's chambers. You please go and get it. Now, Sairendri immediately told Sudeshna, Oh, my dear lady, you have already seen that day, how he was talking to me. And he has these kind of lusty desires to me. Now, if I go to his chambers, God alone knows what is going to happen. So, please don't put me into this kind of a dilemma. Please don't put me into any embarrassing situation like this. Now, Sudeshna, she dismissed her. She said, don't worry. You tell him that I have sent you. So, he will not try anything wrong with you. He will not misbehave with you. Don't worry about that. He is not going to have that much guts to do something. Then, I have sent somebody. So, don't worry. Like that, Sudeshna, somehow she brushed aside Sairendri's objections and sent her. Now, Sairendri, what to do? Now, she was almost on the verge of crying. She knew what was going to happen with Keechaka. And so, as she stepped out of the place and then she was proceeding towards the Keechaka's chambers, she looked up at the sky and looked at Suryadeva. Suryadeva, the sun was shining very strongly. So, she looked up at him and she prayed, please protect me, like that, and she went. It was a very informal kind of prayer from her side, from Draupadi's side. But then, what we have to understand is, all the Pandavas, including Draupadi, are great devotees of Lord Krishna. And in the Rig Veda also it is said, OM TAD VISHNOH PARAMAM PADAM SADAPASHYANTI SURAYAH DIVIVA CHAKSHU RATATAM TAD VIPRASO VIPANYAVO JAGRAVAMSA SAMINDATE VISHNO ORYAT PARAMAM PADAM Vishnu's lotus feet are considered the supreme destination, even for the Devatas. So, even the Devatas are constantly waiting, when can we have darshan of the lotus feet of Lord Vishnu. So, all the Devatas are always trying to please Lord Vishnu. They are trying to please and serve Lord Vishnu in some way or the other. Why? Because Lord Krishna says in the Bhagavad Gita, AHAM ADHIRHI DEVANAM Krishna is the source of all the Devatas. The Devatas are all created by Krishna himself, by Vishnu. So, naturally, Krishna, so Vishnu being the supreme creator, all the Devatas are always looking for opportunities to serve Vishnu.

---
#### devotion, protection, divine intervention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mbHtRZ49ssY?t=837
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you want to love me, then you should love everyone else that I also love."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of loving someone's dog to explain that devotion to Krishna includes respect for His devotees. Suryadeva responds to Draupadi's prayer by sending a Rakshasa to protect her.

Now, there is a saying that, If you want to love me, first learn to love my dog. The idea behind this saying is that, If you want to love me, then you should love everyone else that I also love. You cannot say that I love you, but I hate your father and mother. I am a big enemy of your brother, but I love you. You cannot say that. If you have to love me, then you have to love all the people that I love also. This is generally saying like that. So, all the Devatas, the demigods, they are all great devotees of Vishnu. They are also very respectful and devoted to all the devotees of Vishnu. Now, the Pandavas are great devotees of Lord Krishna. They are eternal associates of Lord Krishna. Therefore, all the Devatas, all the demigods have the greatest of respect for the Pandavas. And naturally, Suri Deva also, when he saw that, Draupadi has asked for my protection, then he felt obliged. Oh, she is a great devotee of Krishna, so I have to provide protection to her. Although, she asked very casually, just in a passing, she asked Draupadi. But still, for Suri Deva, it was like a great prayer for him. Because, Draupadi was a great devotee of Lord Krishna. And so, immediately, Suri Deva summoned one Rakshasa and told him, Please go and always, whenever there is a problem, Draupadi steps out of her residence, always be with her. And constantly protect her. And so, this Rakshasa, he invisibly started following Draupadi, who walked into Keechaka's chamber.

#### assault, divine intervention, humiliation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mbHtRZ49ssY?t=942
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A woman's honour is being violated. That too, right in front of you in your assembly."
**Summary:** Keechaka attempts to assault Draupadi in his chambers, but she escapes and runs into the court of King Virata. Keechaka drags her by the hair and kicks her, but the Rakshasa sent by Suryadeva intervenes, disorienting Keechaka.

Now, when Draupadi walked into Keechaka's chamber, Keechaka was very happy to see her. He was very glad to see her. Ah! Sairendri has come. Of course, he could not see the Rakshasa who was following close behind, whom Surya had sent. And so, he said, Oh! My Sairendri, my dear lady, you have come. Please tell me, what have you come for? What is the purpose? Sairendri said, Sudeshna has asked for some things, which I have to take from here. Please provide to me. The queen is also waiting. I should not keep her waiting. Quickly, I have to go. Please, as soon as possible, please hand over the things. Keechaka, she was carrying a golden pot in her hand. Keechaka took the pot from her. And he gave it to one of his servants who was standing there. And he said, Don't worry. My servants will take care of it. Fill it and send it to Sudeshna. And he said, You don't worry. You sit with me. You sit down. I want to talk to you. And he started all kinds of sweet talks, which Sairendri was not happy to hear. And he said, Sorry, I have to take this. I have been given the order by Sudeshna. So, please don't prevent me. Please give me the pot. I want to take it and go. And if you are not giving it to me, I will go myself. And then, Keechaka, what he did? He caught hold of Sairendri. And he started pulling her to him. Now, Sairendri got alarmed. Now, what to do? She gave Keechaka one push on the chest. And this caught Keechaka completely unaware. And because he was not expecting it, he got off balance and he fell down. Taking the opportunity, immediately Draupadi turned and she fled out of the room. And Keechaka managed to pull himself together. Then he got up. Then he started chasing Sairendri. Hey, where are you going? Stop. Wait. I am telling you to stop. Like that. Now, Sairendri, she got very afraid. Now, this Keechaka is not leaving me. He is pursuing me. Now, where shall I go? What shall I do? She ran into the court, into the assembly of King Virata. Where Virata was sitting, all his ministers were sitting. And it so happened that Kanka and Vallabha were also in the assembly. At that point of time. Sairendri ran into that assembly. And from behind, Keechaka is pursuing. And the moment she entered the assembly, Keechaka caught hold of her hair and he started dragging her. And when Draupadi tried to resist, Keechaka gave her a mighty kick on her back. And Sairendri fell down on the floor. Now, when this happened, the Rakshasa, who was sent by Surya, he could not tolerate. He went and gave one strong kick to Keechaka on his chest. And Keechaka fell over and he got thrown behind. He was flung behind. And he went and landed on the floor. And the impact was so huge that he got completely disoriented. And then he started looking around. He was completely dazed. What had happened? Suddenly, from somewhere, some foals came and flung him on the floor with a huge impact on the chest. And he was not able to figure out what happened. And nobody could figure out what happened suddenly to Keechaka.

#### humiliation, restraint, disguise
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mbHtRZ49ssY?t=1136
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I have got five Gandharva kings. They are very powerful. Unfortunately, they are not taking any action right now."
**Summary:** Draupadi pleads to King Virata for justice, indirectly criticizing Yudhishthira and Bhima for their inaction. Bhima is enraged but Yudhishthira restrains him to protect their disguise.

And seeing the opportunity, that Keechaka was dazed and he was, for a moment he had forgotten about this Sairendri, she got up and started pleading to Virata, O my king, please see what kind of thing is happening in your kingdom. A woman's honour is being violated. That too, right in front of you in your assembly. This Keechaka has come and kicked me in front of you. In front of you in the assembly. And because I resisted his advances. He wanted to own me, although he knows I am a married woman. And he, because I resisted, he has insulted me, humiliated me like this. How can such a thing happen in the presence of such great kings? Indirectly, Draupadi was referring to Yudhishthir Maharaj and to Bhima, who are also there. I have got five Gandharva kings. They are very powerful. Unfortunately, they are not taking any action right now. Otherwise, if they were to take cognisance of this, they would completely annihilate all the people like Keechaka, would have no chance to continue living. So like this, Draupadi indirectly started telling Yudhishthira and Bhima. She was criticizing them. Now, when Bhima saw this, his fury went beyond his control. His eyes went red. His whole body was trembling. And then he stood up to go and attack Keechaka. He would have finished Keechaka right there. But then, that would have blown the disguise of the Pandavas. So immediately, Yudhishthira Maharaj gently tapped Bhima on his hand. And when Bhima looked down and he saw Yudhishthira Maharaj motioning him to sit down, with great difficulty, Bhima restrained himself and sat down.

#### diplomacy, restraint, disguise
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mbHtRZ49ssY?t=1239
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is neither the time, nor the opportunity, nor the place for you to, to air your grievances."
**Summary:** King Virata hesitates to act against Keechaka. Yudhishthira advises Draupadi to leave, and she complies, while Keechaka storms out of the room.

And then King Virata now, he was in a dilemma. He knew that what was happening with Saiyarendri was unjust. At the same time, he did not want to antagonize him. He did not want to antagonize Keechaka because his whole kingdom was resting on his protection. So, Virata, very diplomatically, the King Virata, he said, you please see, maid servant, without hearing both the parties, how can I take a decision? I have not heard what is Keechaka's side of the story. I have heard what you said, but I have to hear what he also has said. Otherwise, how can I decide something? How can I decide that he has behaved unjustly to you? Like this, he started saying. Now, in the meantime, Keechaka had picked himself up and slowly, he was, he was, he was gathering himself and he was coming back to his senses and gathering what had happened, what was going on right there. Now, Yudhishthir Maharaj took advantage of this and he said, maid servant, O Saiyarendri, this is neither the time, nor the opportunity, nor the place for you to, to air your grievances. Please go back to your chambers and you discuss this with Sudeb. This is not the place for you to air all your grievances. Please carry on. This is not the, you should not continue to be here. The situation is not favorable. Immediately, Saiyarendri also took the cue from what Kanka had said and she left the palace and went back to Sudeshna's chambers. So, by the time, Keechaka had collected himself, Saiyarendri had already left. And now, what she had already left? So, he also stormed out of the room and he went away.

---
#### worry, planning, future
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/mbHtRZ49ssY?t=1326
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Something has to be done."
**Summary:** Sudeshna expresses anger at Keechaka's actions. Draupadi, fearing future attacks, contemplates a plan to protect herself from Keechaka, setting the stage for the next episode.

Virata got very angry. I never expected this from my brother. If he has done this, I will tell the king and see that he is put to the gallows. I will see that he pays for this with his life. Like this, Sudeshna also got very upset, angry because she had not expected that Keechaka would force himself upon her. She had thought he will propose to her. Mostly she may agree. If she doesn't agree, she will come away. Like that she had thought that he would go to this extent of humiliating her, trying to forcibly capture her and all these kind of things. So, it came as a surprise for her also. Then Draupadi told her, no, no, no need for you to complain to King Virata and all that. My Gandharva husbands are there. They will take care. Like that she said and with still tears flowing from her eyes, she went to her chambers and then she started taking rest. And although she went back to her chamber, she took bath because she wanted to take rest. She took bath because she wanted to. She was feeling so bad that Keechaka had touched her with his own hands. So, she felt, you know, let me take bath and purify myself. And after taking bath, she also felt fresh and little pacified. And then she was sitting on her bed and she was thinking, now this much has happened. This Keechaka is not going to keep quiet. He will try somehow or the other to capture me and he will try to violate me. So, this is not going to end here. The matter is not going to end here. Something has to be done. And she also saw how King Virata was helpless in front of Keechaka. So, the king, she could not expect any protection from the king also. And as far as Sudeshana was concerned, she was a simple woman and unlikely that she would be able to withstand the manipulations of Keechaka. Keechaka would again come and try somehow manipulate her and she would, he would mislead her and he would take away Draupadi. So, all these were possibilities that could happen. Now, Draupadi was very worried. What will happen now? How could she protect herself? What to do? How to save herself from Keechaka? And she started thinking. And then slowly a plan dawned upon her. She thought of a plan by which she could escape from the clutches of Keechaka. So, what was the plan that Draupadi had thought of? Please join us in the next episode to know about this. Thank you for joining us today. Hare Krishna. Jagatguru Srila Prabhupada Netaikor Premanande. Hari Hari Bol.

---

### Mahabharata | Part 46 | Sairindhri saved from Kichaka | His Grace Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa.
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

#### disguise, strategy, deception
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/i0A30lhRM-0?t=420
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, I have thought of an idea."
**Summary:** To protect the Pandavas' identity and end their exile, Draupadi, under Bhima's plan, feigns interest in Keechaka and arranges a secret meeting in the dancing hall.

You have to vanquish him. The only way is to kill Keechaka. We have no other alternative because if the Pandavas were going to tell that we are Pandavas, this is Draupadi, then their disguise is blown and they will have to go again for another 12 years in the forest exile. Now, there is no other option but to kill this Keechaka. Bhima said, okay, all right, let us do like this. You go back to your quarters now. Tomorrow morning, you go to Keechaka and then Bhima delineated to her a plan which he had formulated, what Sairindhri has to do. When Sairindhri heard this, she also felt this is a good idea and she said, okay, all right, I will do like what you have said. And so, see, she took leave of Pallava and she went back to her quarters. The night passed, the sun rose, Sairindhri woke up, she bathed, finished all her morning ablutions, dressed herself and then she went to Keechaka's chambers. Now, Keechaka, when he saw Sairindhri coming, he was surprised and he thought maybe she has changed her mind and he started singing to her, oh, Sairindhri, you have come out of your own accord. Good, good, good, come, come. You saw what happened in the assembly. You saw how King Virata did not say anything. So, although Virata is king, he is king in name only. Actually, I am the king. I am the de facto king. So, you do not worry. If you become my consort, I am telling you, I will take care of you better than my queen. You will have the position of a queen and I will take care of all your comforts. You do not have to be a servant to anyone in future. Your future is secure. And I am Keechaka. I am practically, I call all the shots here. So, do not worry at all. Like this, Keechaka started talking to Sairindhri. And Sairindhri also acted as if she was favorable towards him. And she told him, you see, that day, I had to behave like that. It was not actually something which I wanted to do. But somehow, things went out of hand that way. I understand your desire also. My worry was that if my Gandharva husbands come to know that we both have associated, then it will create trouble for you as well as me. So, I just want to avoid that. That is all. So, I thought over and I also desire to have a union with you. So, how to do it was what was bothering my mind. So, I have thought of an idea. So, immediately, Keechaka got very interested. He said, oh, really? Oh, this was the situation. Then no problem. Tell, we will find some solution. We will work out. Then Sairindhri told him, you see, if both of us, we meet in an isolated, secluded place in the night, when nobody is watching and we unite, nobody should come to know about it as long as it is a secluded place and not in your chambers where all servants are coming and going, already people have seen me coming in and nobody should get a whiff of our relationship. So, if my Gandharva kings, my Gandharva husbands, don't come to know about my relationship with you, then things are no problem, not an issue. So then Keechaka said, so what do you suggest we do? Sairindhri said, there is the dancing hall. There is that hall where all the princesses, the princess Uttara and her companions and all, they go for learning dance and music and all that. They, during daytime, they learn all their dance and music and all that and during night time it is closed and nobody is there, nobody goes there because it is dedicated for the princess and so nobody else goes there and the princess comes only during the daytime for learning, night time she doesn't come there so night time is completely deserted. So tonight why don't you come there? I will also come and then I will try to satisfy you to the best of my ability. Keechaka was thrilled. This was something which he had not expected at all. What a wonderful turn of events. He said, don't worry Sairindhri, I'll come. I will come at the appointed time. Don't worry, I will be there. You also, you go now and we will meet in the evening. I am very happy with your proposal. So, like this, Sairindhri went back.

#### anticipation, revenge, confrontation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/i0A30lhRM-0?t=689
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You will not go back with your life."
**Summary:** Sairindhri, unhappy with the situation, waits for evening while Keechaka eagerly prepares for their meeting; Bhima hides in the dancing hall, and when Keechaka arrives and attempts to embrace Draupadi, Bhima confronts him, vowing to avenge Keechaka's offense.

She did not like what she was doing. She was very unhappy, but what to do? This man had put her into such a situation. And so, somehow she passed the time till late evening. And here, Keechaka in great anticipation, for him also time was not moving. He was every now and then looking at his watch or clock or whatever and seeing time is not moving, time is not moving. And then he dressed himself up very nicely and put lot of perfume and worn fresh silk cloth and put on nice ornaments and all that. And finally, the sun had set and the time, the appointed time came. Now, Sairindhri went to the dancing hall. After Sairindhri went itself, Bhima, all this was being done according to the plan, which Bhima had made. So, Bhima had reached earlier and he hid himself behind some curtains and all that and he was waiting, when will this Keechaka come? Now, at the appointed time, Draupadi came and a few minutes later, Keechaka also entered the hall. And then he saw at the end of the hall, Draupadi, this Sairindhri is waiting and he became very happy. He closed all the doors, all the curtains are pulled, nobody can see. And he said, ah, Sairindhri, now I have come, come, let us enjoy each other. And at that moment, Vallabha stepped out of the curtain. And then Keechaka was surprised. He said, hey, what is this? Vallabha, what are you doing here? Why did you come here? And then Vallabha said, you are such a vile person, you want to enjoy somebody else's wife. This will not be allowed today for your offence that you are committed towards Sairindhri. You will not go back with your life. Now, Keechaka was not an ordinary person. He was also extremely powerful. And he was a courageous warrior also. He was not going to get frightened by these kind of threats. He said, ah, who are you to threaten me? Come, you want to kill me? I will show you, I will finish you today.

#### battle, strength, victory, wrath
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/i0A30lhRM-0?t=819
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "And finally, Keechaka's whole body turned into an unrecognizable lump of flesh."
**Summary:** Bhima and Keechaka engage in a fierce battle, with Bhima ultimately overpowering and killing Keechaka in a fit of rage, disfiguring the body beyond recognition before Draupadi urges him to leave before anyone discovers what happened.

And then both of them started a battle. And both of them, hand to hand, wrestling and punching and kicking and using their hands, legs. They were pushing each other, pulling and trying to throw each other off balance. And using their fists, using their head, headbutting, punching, everything was going on. And Keechaka initially thought he had a lot of, he was very arrogant, thinking that he is very powerful. So he thought in no time I will finish off this fellow. But as the fight progressed, Keechaka realized that his opponent was not some pushover. At the same time, even Keechaka was also not a pushover. He was also a quite powerful guy. So both of them were battling back and forth, going back and forth. Sometimes Bhima is gaining the upper hand. Sometimes Keechaka is gaining the upper hand. And the fight was going on for several, almost quite a long time. As time went by, gradually Keechaka started getting fatigued. And his strength started flagging. Whereas Vallabha's, Bhima's strength was, he was getting only, he was only getting stronger and stronger. And he continued to pommel Keechaka with the same vigor as when they had begun the fight. Whereas Keechaka had already started getting tired and his blows were not as powerful as it was in the beginning. Now gradually, Bhima started gaining the upper hand. And finally, he caught hold of Keechaka's throat and started throttling him. And then he finally put him, flung him to the floor. And he landed on his chest. And he finally vanquished Keechaka. And Keechaka gave up his life on the floor of that dancing hall. Now Bhima was so angry, so furious that the way Keechaka had humiliated Draupadi, that he was not satisfied with simply killing Keechaka. He continued pommeling. Pommeling this dead body of Keechaka and with such great force that all his hands, legs, everything got smashed. And finally, Keechaka's whole body turned into an unrecognizable lump of flesh. And finally, Draupadi also came and Bhima finally, he had vented his anger and he cooled off. And then Draupadi told Bhima, now better you go quickly before anybody comes looking for Keechaka. And then Bhima also silently, he slipped away and through some back door or something, he went out without anybody noticing and he went back to his chambers.

#### deception, blame, mob rule, injustice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/i0A30lhRM-0?t=991
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Keechaka wanted to violate me. And he has met his death at the hands of my Gandharva husband."
**Summary:** Draupadi announces Keechaka's death, attributing it to her Gandharva husbands. Keechaka's followers, seeking revenge, blame Draupadi and demand her death, leading King Virata to concede to their demands to avoid a rebellion.

Once Bhima had left, Sairindhri stepped out of the hall and she started walking towards her chamber. And she came across some people, some soldiers or some servants were passing by. And she said, please go to the dancing hall. Keechaka wanted to violate me. And he has met his death at the hands of my Gandharva husband. Like that she said and she walked away. When the servants heard what had happened, they looked at each other in great surprise and they rushed to the hall. And they found Keechaka lying in a pool of blood and his whole body turned into a mass of, unrecognizable mass of flesh. They could not make out where is his hand, where is his leg, nothing. It was just a, simply a mass of flesh, that's all. The news started spreading everywhere and everybody was shocked. Keechaka, somebody has killed Keechaka, somebody has killed Keechaka. And this news also started spreading that the Sairindhri's Gandharva husbands have come and killed Keechaka. Because no ordinary man could have killed Keechaka. Keechaka was quite a powerful guy. And next day, the, as the sun rose, then people all started assembling. And they decided that okay, now whatever happened, Keechaka has to be, his final rites have to be performed. And then Keechaka had many, many followers. And all these followers, they, when they heard the news, they all came and they started making preparations for the cremation of Keechaka. They were all die-hard fans, they were all die-hard loyal servants, followers of Keechaka. When they heard that Sairindhri's Gandharva husband has killed Keechaka. All of these servants, they went to, went in front of King Virata and said, see, we have heard that Sairindhri is the cause of all this, whatever mishappening has taken place. And Keechaka has got killed only because of her. So, we should kill her also, we should not leave her, we should not leave her, let her go scot-free. So, please give us permission, we want, when we are doing the final rites of Keechaka, along with him, we want to burn her also to death. Now, these followers of Keechaka. These followers of Keechaka were also very strong and powerful, and Keechaka had become very powerful because of them. Now, Keechaka was already killed. Now, Virata was thinking that, who will now protect my kingdom? And now, these are his people, if he does not pacify them, they will turn, they will rebel against him. So, he said, okay, do whatever you want. Now, for the sake of one maidservant, he is not going to jeopardize his own position. So, all these people, the followers of Keechaka, they came. And they forcibly caught hold of Draupadi and started pulling her and taking her away to the crematorium. Saying that, because of you, Keechaka desired you, and because of you, he had to meet his death. So, Keechaka is going to be burned, you also go with him.

#### desperation, code, rescue
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/i0A30lhRM-0?t=1173
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "May my Gandharva husbands, Jayanta, Vijaya, Jaya, Jayat Sena, Jayat Bala, let them all, may they protect me from the danger I am in."
**Summary:** As Draupadi is dragged to the crematorium, she remembers the code names and calls out to her Gandharva husbands for help, prompting Bhima to spring into action to rescue her.

So, like that, they started dragging her also to the crematorium. And they started taking the procession of the dead body of Keechaka to the crematorium. Now, Draupadi is in a great anxiety. Now, what will happen to her? She never expected. She never expected this turn of events. So, now what to do? Then she remembered the code names that they had agreed upon to call each other in case of any great danger. Because she cannot now call out to Bhima. If she calls out to Bhima, their disguise is blown. She cannot call out to Vallabha. She cannot call out to Brihannala. Because if she does that, then again people will start thinking, why is Saiyarindri calling these people? Why are they coming to protect her? Then also their disguise will be blown. So, then she remembered the code names that they had decided upon. And she started calling out, may my Gandharva husbands, Jayanta, Vijaya, Jaya, Jayat Sena, Jayat Bala, let them all, may they protect me from the danger I am in. Otherwise, I will die. Like that, she started shouting. Now, when she started shouting like this, Vallabha, who was in the kitchen or somewhere, he heard her shouting and immediately understood, oh, this is Draupadi's voice and she is in great danger. Now, I have to save her. Now, thinking like this, immediately Bhima sprung into action. Now, what did Bhima do? And was he able to protect Draupadi before she was taken to the crematorium and forcibly put onto the burning pyre of Keechaka? Please join us in the next episode to know about this. Thank you for joining us today. Hare Krishna.

#### strategy, disguise, Bhima
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oFoQ8ZSav-I?t=202
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Bhima wrapped a cloth around his face so that nobody could recognize him like a mask and through a back door, he went out of the kitchen"
**Summary:** Draupadi calls out using code names, and Bhima, hearing her cries, disguises himself and heads to the crematorium to rescue her.

Now, what could she do? She called out to the five code names which the Pandavas had discussed amongst themselves which they would call out in case of any danger because at that point of time, she could not call out the original names of the Pandavas. If she did that, their disguise would be blown. And if she called the names by which they were known in Virata, Kanka, Vallabha, Brihannala, Granthika, Tantripala, if she were to call out these names, then also the question would arise how Sairindhri knows them and what is their connection that would again lead to their disguise being blown. So, she called out the code names, Jaya, Vijaya, Jayanta, Jayat Sena, Jayat Bala, like this she called out to them saying that she is in danger. Now, Bhima who was known as Vallabha, he heard Sairindhri's cries for help and he realized what was happening that she was being taken away by the Pandavas. He realized that she was being taken away by Keechaka's, his people. So, what did Bhima do? Bhima wrapped a cloth around his face so that nobody could recognize him like a mask and through a back door, he went out of the kitchen and then through some other path, through some other shortcut, he went to the crematorium where Keechaka's followers were bringing Sairindhri as well as Keechaka.

#### Bhima, violence, Draupadi
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oFoQ8ZSav-I?t=289
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "In short, in a short period of time Bhima finished all of them."
**Summary:** Bhima confronts Keechaka's followers at the crematorium, killing all 300 of them and rescuing Draupadi.

When the followers of Keechaka, dragging Draupadi along and then carrying Keechaka, naturally we can expect they are taking the procession of a dead body. So, they would have passed through various streets in the city because Keechaka was one of the prominent people in the city. He was the commander-in-chief of the army. Naturally, they would have slowly, the procession would have proceeded slowly. They would have given an opportunity for everybody to see him and pay their final respects and all that. So, gradually, slowly they reached the crematorium. When the whole retinue reached the crematorium, they found one huge tall man standing there with one uprooted tree in his hand and wearing a mask around his face who was actually none other than Bhima. And so, these Keechaka's understood that this man is not in a peaceful mood. He is standing in a fighting mood and they guessed who is this man who is stopping our path and they guessed, oh, this must be one of the Gandharva husbands of Sairindhri. Come on, let us kill him also. And they took up their arms and they rushed towards this man who was standing like that personified. And these people, the followers of Keechaka were not small in number. Nowadays, when we see some demonstration happening, we see some 10 people gathered together. Media comes and takes their photo from one angle and say there are a lot of protesters and all that. But this was not like that. There were about 300 of them. Not a small number. Big number. And they were all warriors. They all fell upon Bhima with a vengeful spirit because they wanted to avenge the death of their leader. And now Bhima was also not in a, he was not in a conciliatory mood. There was so much pent up fury inside him also. So many years over, they have been living in forest exile and they come to this one, to Virata to live in incognito. So much humiliation. He would not take out his, vent out his frustration and anger. It had all been building up. So now was a great opportunity for Bhima to vent out his frustration and anger built up over almost 13 years. So Bhima also fought furiously with them. And in no time everywhere there were hands, legs, heads strewn around, torsos with, headless torsos. There was blood flowing around. In short, in a short period of time Bhima finished all of them. And then after he killed all of them. Bhima released Sairendri and then he took the shortcut again and unnoticed by anyone he went back to his place in the kitchen. And finally Draupadi as Sairendri she silently walked back to the city. And suddenly the people are seeing that just now this big procession of 300 people they had taken Sairendri with an intention to burn her with Keechaka. And now she is coming back unharmed. And people started questioning what happened? What happened? Then slowly news started coming in that there was this Gandharva husband of Sairendri who came and he has killed all the followers of Keechaka also. Now this news started spreading like wildfire.

And the news reached the King Virata also. So Virata now what to do? There was nothing much he could do. He made arrangements for the cremation of Keechaka and his followers properly. Then after that he approached his wife Sudeshna and he told her see this Sairendri she is creating lot of problem. Because of her Keechaka got killed. Now my commander in chief is gone. Now his 300 followers who are the strongest and most powerful warriors in our army they are also finished. Now to keep Sairendri with us I am very afraid. Tomorrow something by chance something some mistake I tell something or I look at her. I look at her in some manner which she takes differently. Then I will also be finished. So it is too risky to keep her. So you please tell her that the King came and told. King wanted to come and talk to you. But King is very afraid to speak to you directly for the fear of offending you in any manner. And then taking the reaction of your husband. So King has said please we are all. We cannot live in this kind of dangerous situations. So you please go wherever you want. We will not create any problem for you. So many years so many months you have stayed with us. So please the King has requested. You please go somewhere where your Gandharva husbands will be peaceful and they will not disturb anyone. Like this he told his wife Sudeshna.

After 13 days my Gandharva husbands will come and they will take me away. So I will not trouble you anymore. Please you have given me shelter. You have given me shelter for more than 11 months. Now only less than 2 more weeks. Please accommodate me. And Sudeshna said okay no problem. You can continue for 2 more weeks. It is not an issue. You can stay. Why? Why did Draupadi say ask for only 13 days? Because after 13 days the period of time of 1 year for the Pandavas to live incognito was getting over.

**Summary:** News of Keechaka's death reaches Hastinapura, and Duryodhana becomes suspicious of the circumstances, suspecting the Pandavas' involvement.

So they were in the final stage of their exile. Now while all this was going on this news about the death of Keechaka spread everywhere not only within Virata but even to all the neighboring kingdoms also the message started spreading. Soon the news reached Hastinapura. And the Kauravas also came to know like this Keechaka has been killed. Keechaka was a very powerful warrior. And he had avoided the Virata kingdom coming under the subjugation of the Kaurava rulers. So they had always felt that somehow we are not able to subdue him. Now when the news came that Keechaka is dead he is no more. They were all very relieved. Now this very easily King Virata is now weakened. We can subjugate him. Like this they were thinking. Now the news also reached them. About how Keechaka got killed. So when Duryodhana heard that there was this one Sairindhri one maid servant. And she has five Gandharva husbands. And one of the Gandharva husbands killed Keechaka in the middle of the night when nobody was there. He got a little suspicious. Why is it like this? Why would even Gandharvas also don't come and they are not cowards. They will not fight in the dark when nobody is around and all that. Why did they not openly challenge Keechaka and fight with him. Like this some doubt started running in Duryodhana's mind. But more important thing for him. What was occupying his mind was. Less than two weeks were left. For the time of the one year exile of the Pandavas living incognito was getting over.

Now Duryodhana was getting worried. Because all his spies were coming back and saying that we are not able to find trace of anywhere. Where the Pandavas are there. Now if they finish their period of exile successfully. Then he would have to return their kingdom. Which was never Duryodhana's intention anyway. He always thought very easily. I will be able to discover their disguise. And we will send them another twelve years in forest. He was expecting that. But that's not coming true. And less than two weeks are left. He was in great anxiety about this. So he called all the spies and told them. Put redoubled effort. Put redoubled efforts. Within two weeks I want all of you. Somehow or the other. You have to find out where the Pandavas are. And we have to blow their disguise. We have to uncover their disguise. And we have to send them again into forest exile. Somehow or the other this has to happen. So he encouraged all his spies. And sent them out again.

In the meantime. In the assembly of the Kauravas. Along with Dhritarashtra, Drona, Bhishma. All these people also. Duryodhana started discussing. How to discover where the Pandavas are. And he was telling how these spies are coming back. And they are reporting that they are not able to find him. Find the Pandavas. All these things were going on. Then Bhishma gave an idea to Duryodhana. Bhishma spoke to Duryodhana. And he told him in the assembly. I will tell you where to find Yudhishthira and the Pandavas very easily. So Duryodhana's eyes spread up. Okay. So how to find? Please tell me. Bhishma told. The easiest way is. You find out which kingdom. The citizens are all very Dharmic. They are all righteous. They are very pious. Peaceful. And the kingdom is very prosperous. Without any diseases. Without any problems. Very peacefully they are able to carry on their activities. Wherever Yudhishthira is there. Because he is Dharma personified. Therefore. Wherever he lives. All the citizens will be very Dharmic. There will be no Adharma. People will all be living very righteously. And all. They will not be facing any of the problems of material nature. So find out such a place. You will find that the Pandavas are there. And you call the Pandavas. Beg for forgiveness. Return the kingdom to them. Return hand over Hastinapur also to them. And subjugate yourself to them. Bhishma said like this.

#### suspicion, Duryodhana, Draupadi
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oFoQ8ZSav-I?t=891
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Looks like Virata may be the place where the Pandavas are living in disguise."
**Summary:** Duryodhana dismisses Bhishma's advice and expresses his suspicion that Sairindhri is Draupadi and that the Pandavas are hiding in Virata.

Duryodhana flared up. He said. What is this? I am asking you for a. For a. How to discover the Pandavas and send them back and forth. And you are telling me to give the kingdom to them. Now. He cannot. Of course. Duryodhana could not fight back with Bhishma also. But. He was naturally. He was. He was angry. But he couldn't say anything. Then. Duryodhana said. I have got a. Somehow. Again. In his mind. The. The whole incident of Keechaka's death came back to his mind. And he said. I have somehow got a doubt. That. This. Sairindri. Because of whom. The. Keechaka got killed. Looks like she is Draupadi. And looks like Keechaka must have been killed by Bhima. Because. Nobody was more powerful than. Than Bhima. In front of Keechaka. Keechaka was. So powerful. There was only Bhima who was more powerful than them. And. The way he has been killed in the middle of the night. When nobody was there. And nobody saw the whole fight. Means that. Bhima must have done it. So that his disguise is. Safe. It is not uncovered. So. Then. So. So. Looks like. Virata may be the place where the Pandavas are living in disguise. Like this. Duryodhana started surmising.

#### cows, protection, Vedic culture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oFoQ8ZSav-I?t=1033
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Even the cows are supposed to be given special protection by the kings."
**Summary:** The speaker explains the importance of cows in Vedic culture and the king's duty to protect them, contrasting it with modern governments' policies.

So. The very next day. The allies of Duryodhana. They attacked the kingdom of Virata. They attacked the kingdom of Virata. And they started taking away his cows. They were at the outskirts of the city. And so the cows had come there for grazing. And the cows are like the citizens of the kingdom. Even the cows are supposed to be given special protection by the kings. In the Vedic civilization, elderly people, women, children, cows and the priestly class of people. These five classes of people had to be given special protection by the king under all circumstances. It was the duty of the king. Not like modern days. In modern days, the government thinks of protection only of the human citizens. They are not bothered about the animals who live in the kingdom. The animals are slain to the slaughterhouse and the human beings should be protected. This is the current day government's policy all over the world. But in the Vedic times, every living entity who takes birth in a kingdom is called Praja. Praja. Jayate. Jayate means to take birth. Whoever takes birth in the kingdom of Jayate. Whoever takes birth in the kingdom of Jayate. Whoever takes birth in the kingdom of Jayate. Quem is left morning, that Timelike. So, he is not allowed to take birth inside his kingdom. Safet Κhilep ruins. Father King. Foods are confiscated. It is important for visitors of the kingdom. So, the person who is allowed to take birth inside the kingdom ofда kingdom, is called the citizen of the kingdom. Singha. So even animals take birth within the kingdom, they also are considered citizens. Citizens in one respect? In respect of the receiving the, having the birth right to receive the protection from the king, from bodily harm and as well as for their welfare activity. Sure not? So it was the duty of the king to protect.

Of course he did not take brahman nala because she is a eunuch nobody expected her to have any fighting ability so virata took all of them took his army and they came to the border of the city and a huge fight between the two armies on the one side there are the opposing kings and this side virata along with kanka and vallaba and granthika and tantripala the fight was raging very furiously and the the the virata was also fighting very valiantly but he was finding it difficult so when yudhishthira saw that the the they had to support the king it was not right that they keep quiet so he told bima please go forward and help the king so he took the army and they came to the border of the city and they forgot their position at the same time don't overexpose yourself so that people start if they see your full power then people will immediately guess who you are so show a moderate power at the same time you have to help the king to overcome the uh the the enemy and so with uh with a lot with with a lot of restraint bima also jumped into the field and even with restraint he was he was he was there was nobody in the opposing enemy who could match them their heads were rolling, soldiers were falling and by evening the Virata and all his army along with these four Pandava brothers, they had routed the enemy army and the enemy army fled away.

#### gratitude, customs, Virata
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/oFoQ8ZSav-I?t=1362
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "I have noted your bravery and your valor and your capability in fighting all four of you have fought wonderfully, especially Vallabha has been very good in the field."
**Summary:** Virata expresses his gratitude to the Pandavas for their help in the battle and they follow the custom of resting in the battlefield overnight. Upon returning, they learn of another attack by the Kauravas.

So since it was already evening, Virata looked back at all these four people who had accompanied them and he said, I have noted your bravery and your valor and your capability in fighting all four of you have fought wonderfully, especially Vallabha has been very good in the field. I am very happy with all of you. So I want to reward you also once we go back to the kingdom, I will reward you also. I am very thankful that you came and accompanied me and the Pandavas were also, no, no, it was our duty. You have given us protection. So when you are in danger, it is our duty to be standing by your side and all those kinds of things. And it was the custom that the evening when a battle happens, the Kshatriyas who are engaged in that battle. The victorious. The victorious army, they rest in the battlefield for the night and next day they go back to the kingdom. This was the common, normal practice. So all of them put camp, they put tent and they stayed for that night in the, in the battlefield itself. Next day morning, the sun rose and after the sun rose, they took some, they took their bath and finished all their morning ablutions and they started back to the kingdom of Virata. So they came back to the capital city. And when they entered the palace, they were very victorious. They were received very well by all the people. Oh, our King Virata has come back victorious. There was great joy. And when he came back to the palace, the citizens informed him while you are away today, early morning, another group of army headed by the Kauravas attacked the city from the opposite side.
