Actually what had happened was, while in the morning, the Virata and the army had not yet returned, the Bhuminjaya was administering on behalf of his father. His father, while leaving to handle the opposing enemy who had taken away the cows. He had told Bhuminjaya, in my absence, you officiate on my behalf. So Bhuminjaya was sitting on the throne and morning, early morning, he got the news that the Kauravas have attacked Virata from the opposite direction. So Bhuminjaya told, oh what to do, now the entire army has gone away. And he said, I myself will fight very strongly with all these Kauravas, I am ready to fight, but I don't have a charioteer. There is nobody who can act as my charioteer. Then at that time. Brihannala sent a message to Bhuminjaya through somebody, saying that, this Brihannala acted as a charioteer of Arjuna, so she knows how to drive the chariot. So the information reached Bhuminjaya, then everybody, even Sairindri also spoke to Bhuminjaya and told him, he is very capable, you take him, don't worry at all, he will help you win over the Kauravas and all that. So now Bhuminjaya could not go back on his words. They already had spoken. So Bhuminjaya. Like that, Bhuminjaya was very boastfully, so now what to do? Now he said, okay, Brihannala come, we will go. And he clad on his armour, took his bow and all that. Come on, Brihannala, we will see. Orkayi paathir la, we will see who these Kauravas are there. I will show my valour today

---

### Mahabharata | Part 48 | Brhannala to the rescue | His Grace Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

#### Arjuna's prowess, Duryodhana's plan, Karna's boasting
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/stjh4gHOQGk?t=150
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Now that the disguise of the Pandavas was discovered, Duryodhana became very happy."
**Summary:** The segment describes Duryodhana's plan to uncover the Pandavas' disguise, Karna's boasting about defeating Arjuna, and the reactions of Kripacharya.

On the first day when the ally of Duryodhana attacked the kingdom of Virata, the king Virata, accompanied by the four brothers, Yudhishthira, Bhima, Nakula, and Sahadeva, managed to defeat them. And when they were planning to return to the kingdom of Virata the next day, the next early morning, Duryodhana and his party attacked the kingdom of Virata. On the other end, in response to which, King Virata's son Bhuminjaya, along with Brahmanala, went to counter that attack. Now that this Brahmanala was Arjuna was unknown to anyone, but Brahmanala revealed to Bhuminjaya when he was completely taken aback by the force of the Kaurava army. Brahmanala revealed to him that he was Arjuna himself, and then Bhuminjaya agreed to become his charioteer. And thus, Brahmanala retrieved the weapons of the Pandavas, which were hidden in the sami-tree next to the crematorium. Now, having retrieved his Gandiva bow, Arjuna sat on the chariot and he blew his conch shell, the Devadatta. By the sound of the conch shell itself and looking at the Gandiva bow, the Kauravas understood that this was Arjuna. Now that the disguise of the Pandavas was discovered, Duryodhana became very happy. And why? Because according to the conditions of the Game of Dice, if the Pandavas were discovered during their period of one year of living in incognito, then they would have to go again to the forest in exile for another 12 years. And the whole purpose of Duryodhana coming to Virata was that only, to uncover the disguise of the Pandavas. Now Duryodhana became very happy and Karna also became very happy and Bhishma and Dronacharya they advised Duryodhana that it was unlikely that Arjuna would reveal himself unless the time for the period of their living in incognito was getting over. Duryodhana then told Bhishma Deva you please do a calculation and see when what is the exact date on which their period of one year living in exile incognito is getting over. In the meantime Karna started boasting he told Duryodhana why are you worrying Duryodhana the Pandavas have been living in the forest in exile for 12 years by now Arjuna would have become very weak and even if it is even if this person who is standing in front of us is really Arjuna don't worry very easily with my arrows I'll finish him off today. When Kripacharya heard Karna boasting like this he told Karna what are you talking.

---
#### Karna's arrogance, Bhishma's pacification, Arjuna's strength
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/stjh4gHOQGk?t=383
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Challenging Arjuna to battle, if anyone who challenges Arjuna to battle, he must really have gone off his senses."
**Summary:** Kripacharya and Ashwatthama criticize Karna's boasting, highlighting Arjuna's superior strength and past victories, leading to Bhishma pacifying the situation.

He continued his boasting and he said don't worry come on let us fight him. Kripacharya looked at him and he said you are talking of fighting Arjuna, you have gone mad. Challenging Arjuna to battle, if anyone who challenges Arjuna to battle, he must really have gone off his senses. It's like a man thinking of tying a heavy stone around his neck and trying to swim the ocean, not possible, the guy will drown. Or it is like a man with his bare fingers trying to take out the poisonous fangs of his knee, it will only kill him. So like that you are trying to rush into battle with Arjuna, you've gone off your head. Even if all six of us together, Bhishma, Dronacharya, myself, Kripacharya, Karna, you, Duryodhana, all of us together combined if we fight against Arjuna, we may stand a slim chance, that also you cannot say one person chances where we may be able to counter Arjuna, but that also no guarantee and you are saying that you want to fight him all by yourself. When Ashwatthama heard this he also jumped in he said Karna you are only used to boasting all the time where have you proven yourself in battle did you win the game of dice in which fight with other kings did you win. Draupadi that you pulled her into the assembly and gave the great idea of disrobing her in the middle of the assembly in which battle did you ever fight the Pandavas and win. You have never really won any battle on ground and proven your words you are simply going on boasting that's it. Now Karna became very upset and insulted now but he wouldn't say anything because whatever Kripacharya was saying and whatever Ashwatthama was saying both of them are actually correct and then Bhishma Deva had to pacify everyone and then Bhishma Deva spoke up and he said no no don't worry Ashwatthama Karna said like that so that we would all become enthused to fight against Arjuna otherwise we are all we are all talking only of Arjuna's prowess and we are not thinking of fighting him so he wanted to encourage us and to inspire us to fight he spoke those words so don't take it too badly whatever you have spoken is correct whatever Kripacharya spoke is also correct but let us not let us not insult or in fight amongst ourselves so then Dronacharya and Kripacharya also said okay whatever happened.

Let us now think of how to face the Arjuna who is standing before us and then Bhishma told Duryodhana I've done my calculations the one year of living incognito which the Pandavas had to complete got over yesterday so today that is why Arjuna has revealed himself now Duryodhana when he heard this he was mortified but now there was nothing he could do then Dronacharya told Duryodhana do one thing you take all these cows which were belonging to the king the kingdom of Virata you take them and you go we will somehow try to stop Arjuna so because Duryodhana is the prince and his life should not be put into danger so the other Kaurava soldiers the Kaurava army Dushasana and other brothers of Duryodhana Bhishma Dev Dronacharya Ashwatthama Kripacharya all these people took up their formations in the battle to face Arjuna because Arjuna had already blown his conch and then Duryodhana in the meantime he started taking away the cows. Now Arjuna shot two arrows and those two arrows came and landed at the feet of Dronacharya and Bhishma Dev and Kripacharya and then Dronacharya and Bhishma Dev looked at it and they said Arjuna is sending his respects but now having given having submitted his respects now he is not going to hold back so let us get ready so all of them got ready and in the meantime Arjuna saw how Duryodhana's chariot was moving away immediately he understood the plan of the Kauravas and he told Bhuminjaya look look look Duryodhana is trying to get away let us go and corner him so immediately Bhuminjaya instead of going into the cover of army took it off on a detour and cut off Duryodhana. And Arjuna also used his arrows to quickly cut off Duryodhana. And Duryodhana did not have the courage to face Arjuna alone in battle. And when he saw that Arjuna's chariot was coming towards him, he dropped his plans of trying to get away all by himself. And he went back and joined the ranks of the Kauravas, thinking that I stand a better chance when I'm in the midst of Bhishma Dev and Dronacharya and Karna. And so the cows also, Duryodhana could not hold them back. And they all went back into the kingdom of Virata. So now the Virata kingdom's cows had been protected. Now Arjuna came back to the battlefield. And then Karna immediately jumped into the battle. And he started abusing Arjuna, saying that, oh, Arjuna, today you will see my arrows will wound you and they will kill you and you will lie on the battlefield and so on and so forth, as Karna was usually always. He engaged in boasting. Similarly, he continued in the same way.

#### Arjuna's victories, Respect for gurus, Bhuminjaya's role
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/stjh4gHOQGk?t=731
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Arjuna is sending his respects but now having given having submitted his respects now he is not going to hold back so let us get ready."
**Summary:** Arjuna defeats Karna, Dronacharya, and Bhishma, but spares Duryodhana due to Bhima's vow, and then instructs Bhuminjaya to take credit for the victory.

Arjuna did not hold back. Arjuna started releasing his arrows. Karna also started releasing his arrows. And both of them engaged in a pitched battle. But gradually, Arjuna's superiority came to the fore. Slowly, Karna got wounded everywhere. His bow was broken. His arrows had pierced his arms, his thighs, his chest. And finally, he told his charioteer, I cannot stand fighting Arjuna. So he had to run away from the battle. And then when Karna was beaten back like this, then Dronacharya came forward. And when Dronacharya came forward, Arjuna told him that, oh, my guru, you are my guru. I will not take up a weapon against you unless you draw the weapon first. So Dronacharya smiled and he drew his bow and released his arrow. And when Dronacharya released his arrow, then Arjuna started counterattacking. So the fight went on between them, the guru and Arjuna. Dronacharya, of course, his full heart was not there in the battle. So he was not exerting himself fully. So very quickly, Arjuna managed to overpower him. And Dronacharya being hit by the arrows of Arjuna fainted. And when Dronacharya's charioteer saw that Dronacharya has fainted, he took him away from the battlefield. Now came forth Bhishmadeva, Arjuna. Now, between Bhishmadeva and Arjuna also, the fight started in good earnest. And because of the Kshatriya spirit, both of them were in a really angry mood and they were exchanging arrows. But again, even Bhishmadeva also could not face, he could not, he could not counter all of Arjuna's weapons. And Bhishmadeva also soon, he fell, he fell unconscious and his chariot also had to be taken away. Now, Karna had recuperated and he jumped back into the battle. And he came in front of Arjuna again, and again, he started fighting. But very quickly, again, he was defeated. Again, he had to run back from the battle. So like this, the battle between the Kaurava army and Arjuna went back and forth. And on this side, Arjuna was all alone. And on the other side, all these Maharathis, the great generals, Kripacharya, Ashwatthama himself, Dronacharya, Bhishmadeva, all these people, Dushyasana, and all these people fighting against Arjuna. But none of them were able to overpower him. Finally, Duryodhana himself came forward and started fighting. Now, Arjuna very quickly and very easily defeated Duryodhana, but he did not want to kill him because he remembered Bhima's vow to kill all of Duryodhana and his brothers. So he did not kill him, but he very quickly destroyed his bow and all his arrows. Everything got exhausted. And Bhishmadeva was and Duryodhana had also to retreat so finally when they were when the entire Kaurava army was beaten back like this the the Kaurava army decided that we will not be able to face Arjuna let us go back and so all of them left the battlefield and went back to Hastinapura so we can see how Arjuna was so powerful all these warriors together could not face Arjuna in battle but Arjuna did not pursue them Arjuna on the other hand now that they had been defeated and they had left the kingdom of Virata he collected all the what it as the general practice used to be he collected all the gold and the various ornaments of the dead soldiers who were lying on the battlefield and he gave it to Bhuminjaya and he told him that when we go back to the kingdom of Virata don't tell anyone that I am Arjuna and I am the one who fought the battle and won it you tell that you won it at the right time we will inform everyone who I am Bhuminjaya agreed he said Arjuna you are my superior whatever you command me I will follow.

Well this hill is now being looted by the golemos and so Bhuminjaya said no I don't know where he has gone um so I am unable to tell where he has gone I don't know where he where he came from and where he went anyway King Virata was very happy that now his kingdom had been protected and double celebration from two sides the kingdom was attacked but however even the devata seemed to be helping helping King Virata and protecting himself although Keechaka was his commander was now no more.

#### Pandavas' revelation, Virata's shock, Draupadi's role
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/stjh4gHOQGk?t=1120
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Oh my God, this one year you people have been staying here. Sir, always I used to think that somewhere I have seen you, but I couldn't place you."
**Summary:** The Pandavas reveal their true identities to King Virata, who is shocked but happy to have hosted them, and they also disclose Draupadi's role and Keechaka's fate.

Like that the evening was spent the next day morning King Virata came to the assembly and he sat down on his throne and when he was sitting on his throne suddenly he saw five young Kshatriyas clad in silk and wearing all gold ornaments walking into his assembly and he saw these people seem to be familiar oh this is Kanka and this is Vallabha my cook and there is Granthika and he this is Tantripala the the guy who was taking care of my horses and here is the looks like this person somewhere I have seen this person the fifth guy I am not able to place him then he said that Arre Kanka Vallaba all of you how come you are dressed like Kshatriyas and you are walking into my court so in such a regal manner not like the earlier way used to be and then they all introduced themselves and they said that Yudhishthira introduced himself and he said, I am Yudhishthira and we are all the Pandavas. So, and this is Bhima, this is Nakula, this is Sahadeva. Then Virata said, who is this other person? This is Arjuna who was here as Brihannala. Everybody in the court was taken by surprise. They were all shocked. And Virata said, oh my God, this one year you people have been staying here. Sir, always I used to think that somewhere I have seen you, but I couldn't place you. How could I ever not recognize you people? I just, I am not able to understand. Anyway, I am very happy that all of you were there here. And King Virata was a close friend of Yudhishthira. He respected the Pandavas a lot. And so he was very happy that the Pandavas had stayed with him for one year, although he had not recognized them. And then the Pandavas introduced Draupadi also. And they said, this is Draupadi. Draupadi, she was here serving your wife, Queen Sudeshna, as the Sairindri maid servant. And then they also informed King Virata that Keechaka had lustfully approached Draupadi and it was Bhima who had killed Keechaka. Anyway, Keechaka was not such a great popular guy. And Virata also did not have such, he did not have great affection for him. But then out of necessity, out of helplessness, Keechaka had to tolerate him, so he didn't bother that Keechaka was no more.

---
#### Virata's guilt, Arjuna's integrity, Uttara's marriage
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/stjh4gHOQGk?t=1269
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I have been her teacher so as a teacher I am in the position of her father I cannot marry her."
**Summary:** King Virata feels guilty for treating the Pandavas as servants and offers his daughter Uttara to Arjuna, who declines due to their guru-disciple relationship but proposes her marriage to his son Abhimanyu.

And on the other hand, King Virata was very happy with the Pandavas. And then he thought, all you Pandavas, you are all highly glorified and exalted devotees of Krishna. But I have engaged you as servants in my palace, in my kingdom. So I feel very guilty. The Pandavas had no such feeling because the Pandavas themselves had wanted such a position. The Pandavas themselves had wanted such a position to live incognito. So they were not, they had not taken it otherwise. But King Virata remembered all those instances during their relationships when he would order them to do this, do that. Just like he would order any other servants. And so he felt guilty that he had mistreated the Pandavas. And so he said, he offered that my daughter Uttara, I want to give her in marriage to Arjuna. That my daughter Uttara I want to give her in marriage to Arjuna. Now Arjuna immediately responded no no no how can how can that be possible I have been her teacher so as a teacher I am in the position of her father I cannot marry her and if I marry her today people will speak ill of our relationship we have had no illicit connection between ourselves till date and we have had just the very very respectable relationship of a guru and a disciple because as Brahmanala Arjuna was her guru for dance and so Arjuna said but if you want to establish such family relationship with our dynasty then I have a grown-up son Abhimanyu who is about 16 years old now he is more or less of the same age as Uttara if you want we can marry both of them being Brahmanala was extremely happy and satisfied and he said oh this is a very nice proposal from your side and I am ready for this so from this we can see the unimpeachable character of Arjuna although Uttara was offered to him he saw her just as his own daughter even though he had moved with her very intimately taught her the art of dance and in teaching of the art of dance very close interactions would have been there but his heart had entertained no lusty desires towards her and so therefore Uttara became the daughter-in-law of Arjuna.

#### Abhimanyu-Uttara marriage, Pandavas' alliance, Future plans
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/stjh4gHOQGk?t=1404
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Now that our exile is over how do we get our kingdom back."
**Summary:** The marriage of Abhimanyu and Uttara is arranged, solidifying the alliance between the Pandavas and Virata, and they begin discussing how to reclaim their kingdom.

So immediately Virata called all the priests and the following Purnima was decided to be the good day for the marriage of Uttara and Abhimanyu and the king Virata immediately made a lot of grand arrangements for the wedding and he sent invitations to all their allies and other people to invite her to the wedding and she was invited to the wedding and as well as the allies of the Pandavas informing all the Kings that the Pandavas had now come out of incognito they had completed their 13 years of exile 12 years in the forest one year incognito and now their Arjuna's son Abhimanyu was getting married to his daughter Uttara so various Kings came from different places they brought various kinds of gifts and they came to express their solidarity and support for the Pandavas Krishna and Balarama also came and the wedding was conducted in a very grand manner and after the wedding got over and all the celebrations were over the Pandavas sat down along with Krishna Balarama King Virata and other Kings their supporters who had come such as Drupada and others and now they started discussing now that our exile is over how do we get our kingdom back. Now what was their discussion and what was the decision taken by them please join us in the next episode of the Mahabharata to know about this thank you for joining us today Hare Krishna.

#### Pandavas, Marriage, Discussion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NCl2zvTHTwQ?t=91
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So like this all of them had assembled there and after the wedding celebrations they all got together to discuss."
**Summary:** The speaker summarizes the events of the previous episode, including the Pandavas revealing themselves, Arjuna's role as a teacher, the marriage of Uttara and Abhimanyu, and the assembly of kings to discuss the next steps.

In the last episode we have seen how the Pandavas have come out of their living one year in the kingdom of Virata under disguise incognito. And having completed their one year term, Brahman Nala revealed himself to be Arjuna to all the Kauravas who had come to attack the kingdom of Virata and they were all defeated by Arjuna all alone and they had to go back empty handed. And after that we have seen the unimpeachable character of Arjuna when King Virata coming to know of the real identity of the Pandavas he offered his daughter Uttara in marriage to Arjuna. Arjuna having been her teacher very clearly said that he was in the role of her father. And he could not marry her and instead it was decided that Arjuna's son Abhimanyu will marry Uttara. And in this manner the marriage of Uttara and Abhimanyu was conducted in the kingdom of Virata. And many people had come to attend it even King Drupada had come, Krishna and Balarama had come. So like this all of them had assembled there and after the wedding celebrations they all got together to discuss.

#### Deceit, Planning, Character
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NCl2zvTHTwQ?t=183
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "We can see how the Pandavas were always very steadfast in following whatever word of honour they had given, whereas Duryodhana was always seeing how to not keep his promise and this was Duryodhana's natural, his evil-minded character, which was shared by Shakuni, Karna and Dushasana and all his other brothers."
**Summary:** The speaker describes Duryodhana's reaction to the defeat, Shakuni's manipulation, and Duryodhana's plan to refuse returning Indraprastha, contrasting the Pandavas' integrity with Duryodhana's deceitful nature.

So what their discussions led to is what we will look at today. Before we get there, what had happened after the Kauravas returned to Hastinapura Duryodhana was despondent about how they had been defeated by Arjuna and not only that as told by Bhishma Deva period of the Pandavas living incognita had gotten over just the previous day so they had by a whisker missed that opportunity to expose the disguise of the Pandavas by which they could have been sent for another 12 years of forest exile so while while Dushasana Karna Shakuni and Duryodhana all these people were were mulling over what had happened Shakuni came up with a convoluted calculation by which he proved or seemed to prove that the Pandavas had actually not completed their one year of living incognito so armed with this fresh explanation or twist given by Shakuni Duryodhana got back a little bit of his confidence that there was some grounds on which they could reject giving back the Indraprastha kingdom to the Pandavas so they they they knew that sooner or later the Pandavas are going to come asking for they asking for their kingdom back so we have some grounds to now refuse them so like this Duryodhana had already started preparing his mind as well as his followers about what should be the response and how to prevent giving back Indraprastha to the Pandavas we can see how the Pandavas were always very steadfast in following whatever word of honour they had given, whereas Duryodhana was always seeing how to not keep his promise and this was Duryodhana's natural, his evil-minded character, which was shared by Shakuni, Karna and Dushasana and all his other brothers.

#### Discussion, Diplomacy, Conflict
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NCl2zvTHTwQ?t=305
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Krishna, he spoke up and said that, we all know that Yudhishthira Maharaj is always situated in virtuosity. He will never abandon the path of virtue."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the discussions in the kingdom of Virata, including Krishna's call for an emissary, Balarama's conciliatory approach, and Satyaki's argument for war, highlighting the differing opinions on how to reclaim Indraprastha.

Now when in the kingdom of Virata, when the Pandavas and Krishna and Balaram, Drupada and all these people assembled together, they started discussing on the next course of action. Lord Krishna, he spoke up and said that, we all know that Yudhishthira Maharaj is always situated in virtuosity. He will never abandon the path of virtue. Now that they have rightfully completed their term of Yudhishthira Maharaj, they will never abandon the path of virtue. Now that they have rightfully completed their term of Yudhishthira Maharaj, they will never abandon the path of virtue. Now that they have rightfully completed their term of 13 years in exile, 12 years in the forest and 1 year incognito, they have all the right to go back and get back their kingdom of Indraprastha. So we should send an emissary to ask for a return of the kingdom of Indraprastha to the Pandavas and the message should be clear that in case they refuse to give back the kingdom of Indraprastha to the Pandavas, then war would be inevitable. Now this, whatever Lord Krishna said, was echoed by others in the assembly. Lord Balarama, however, took an opposite approach and he said we should take a conciliatory approach. We should approach them humbly and if we go with an aggressive attitude, it may not be conducive. After all, ultimately, Yudhishthira Maharaj also made a mistake in playing the game of dice when he was not expert at it. So like this, Balarama gave an opposite view, which was countered by Satyaki, who was the commander-in-chief of Lord Krishna's army of the Yadavas. And he countered Balarama and he gave a more favorable argument for the Pandavas saying that Duryodhana's nature was such that we cannot expect him to return the kingdom, so we should be ready for war. So after all these back and forth discussions were going on, some were in favor of peace, some were in favor of going. And approaching Duryodhana and Dhritarashtra in a humble mood, others were saying no, we should take a strong approach and all those kind of things.

#### Diplomacy, Strategy, Assessment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NCl2zvTHTwQ?t=441
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So I suggest, Drupada said, I suggest that I have my priest who is a qualified Brahmana, very austere, lives very simply, is very truthful, very humble, so this kind of person if he send to the Duryodhana."
**Summary:** The speaker details Drupada's suggestion to send a priest to Hastinapura to gauge the mood and the assembly's agreement, followed by Krishna and Balarama's departure for Dwaraka.

Finally, Drupada Maharaj spoke up and being an elderly person, being astute in the matters of political affairs, his word carried a lot of weight. So Drupada Maharaj said that I feel, I suggest that what we should do is, we should gauge the mood in Hastinapura. We should gauge the atmosphere. We should gauge the mood of Duryodhana as well as Dhritarashtra and others before we come to any conclusion about what is the next step we should take. Suppose by chance Duryodhana is agreeable to return the kingdom because of the influence of Vidura, Bhishma and all those things, then we have achieved our end. We don't have to create any hostilities. On the other hand, if Duryodhana continues his inimical attitude, then we have to take steps to assemble our people. We have to build up our army and all those things because Duryodhana is quite powerful. So we should start talking to our allies, we have to build up our army also. So unless we have, we know what is his mood, we will not be able to do, we will not be able to take the right decision. So I suggest, Drupada said, I suggest that I have my priest who is a qualified Brahmana, very austere, lives very simply, is very truthful, very humble, so this kind of person if he send to the Duryodhana. The assembly of Hastinapura, everyone will respectfully receive him and because he is very intelligent, he will be able to assess the mood of the people and give us the right pulse of what is happening there. Then when everybody heard this, everybody was in agreement with whatever Drupada said and they said, yes, what you are saying is absolutely right. Let us go ahead with this plan. Then Lord Krishna and Balarama informed the Pandavas as well as Drupada Maharaj. So you please go ahead with your plan. Whatever help you need. In case there is war, we are with you. So don't worry. So we will go back to Dwaraka and wait to hear what happened to the messenger going and coming back because for the messenger to go from the kingdom of Virata to Hastinapura, meet all the Kauravas there, come back with his message, it will all take time. So in the meantime, we will proceed to Dwaraka.

---
#### Message, Strategy, Division
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NCl2zvTHTwQ?t=574
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So your goal in going there should be twofold. One is we should assess what is the mood in their camp? What steps are they taking? Are they preparing for war or are they remaining quite normal and peaceful?"
**Summary:** The speaker explains Drupada's instructions to his priest, emphasizing the dual goals of assessing the Kauravas' mood and creating dissension within their ranks by highlighting the righteousness of the Pandavas' cause.

Like this Krishna and Balarama left for Dwaraka and Drupada Maharaj called his priest, his Purohit and he spoke to him. He told him, see, this is what we want. You have to approach the Kauravas with a message from the Pandavas. Now this message, Drupada Maharaj using his worldly wise wisdom, he told the Purohit that we have to, how the message has to be positioned. So he told the Purohit, it is unlikely that Duryodhana will be agreeable to return the kingdom of the Pandavas. Then what is the point? What is the point in going there? So your goal in going there should be twofold. One is we should assess what is the mood in their camp? What steps are they taking? Are they preparing for war or are they remaining quite normal and peaceful? So that is one thing that you have to assess. Another thing that you have to also assess is that, another thing that you also have to achieve is that in case Duryodhana is refusing. If Duryodhana is refusing or Duryodhana is refusing is disagreeable to peace then you have to speak in such a way explaining how Dharma is on the side of the Pandavas so that people who are good people in that assembly of the Kauravas such as Bhishma, Drona, Kripacharya, Vidura, all these people, they will differ from the view of Duryodhana and so we would have created dissension in their camp. Which will bring down their morale.

#### Assessment, Message, Dharma
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NCl2zvTHTwQ?t=693
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Now this priest, he started explaining how the Pandavas had been cheated in that game of dice, how against the principles of Dharma, Duryodhana, Karna and others had tried to disrobe Draupadi and by way of use of cheating methods, Yudhishthira was unwilling to participate in that game of dice."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the priest's journey to Hastinapura, his observations of Duryodhana's military preparations, and his presentation of the Pandavas' message, emphasizing their adherence to Dharma and their desire for the return of Indraprastha.

So this is one of the age old myths. I will tell you later. About the Parachuram techniques of political craft, which, which Drupada Maharaj was using, Sama, Dama, Danda, Veda, if Sama, Dama, Danda doesn't work, you have to use Veda, if you create division in the camp of the enemy, you have broken his strength, so Drupada Maharaj advised his Purohit, so these are the two things he had to achieve and sent him to the assembly of Hastinapura, this Brahmana, he travelled all the way to Hastinapura, took him quite a few days, he reached Hastinapura, when he reached Hastinapura, he noticed that Duryodhana had started assembling an army, he had started sending messages to his known people, his allies, his friends and started intimating them that we have to prepare for war with the Pandavas, so you send your army to Hastinapura and we have to start assembling an army, so we have to start building our forts, so the Brahmana could see that, okay, this, this thing is, this army is building up, so he made his way and entered the court of Hastinapura and when everybody came to know that he is a messenger coming from the kingdom of Virata and he is the Purohit of Drupada, he was well received, respectfully and after all suitable procedures had been completed, formalities had been completed with respect to his welcoming, they asked him what message he had brought. Now this priest, he started explaining how the Pandavas had been cheated in that game of dice, how against the principles of Dharma, Duryodhana, Karna and others had tried to disrobe Draupadi and by way of use of cheating methods, Yudhishthira was unwilling to participate in that game of dice. He was forced to participate. And unrighteously he was defeated in the game of dice and sent on the, on, on, into the forest in exile. And now that the Pandavas had completed their term according to the, to the promise that they had given, now according to the principles of Dharma, Indraprastha has to be returned to the Pandavas, otherwise according to the principles of the Dharma, Pandavas will be left with no other option than to declare war.

#### Peace, Conflict, Arrogance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NCl2zvTHTwQ?t=816
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The Brahmana also mentioned to the assembly addressing Dhritarashtra that the Pandavas want peace. They are not interested in a war. That is not what they want."
**Summary:** The speaker continues with the priest's message, highlighting the Pandavas' desire for peace and the Brahmana's mention of it to Dhritarashtra, followed by Bhishma's support for the Pandavas and Karna's arrogant opposition.

The Brahmana also mentioned that the Pandavas were not ready to fight. The Brahmana also mentioned to the assembly addressing Dhritarashtra that the Pandavas want peace. They are not interested in a war. That is not what they want. So therefore, please return the kingdom of Indraprastha and they will promise that they will forget all their past hostilities, whatever, whatever humiliation they have undergone, they will forget all that and live peacefully in cooperation with the Kaurava dynasty. Now, when Yudhishthira, Yudhishthira, Yudhishthira, Yudhishthira, Yudhishthira, Yudhishthira, Yudhishthira, Yudhishthira, Yudhishthira. When the assembly heard this, then Bhishma Deva started speaking and Bhishma Deva said, yes, what you have said is absolutely right. Dharma is fully on the on the side of the Pandavas. Whatever they had to undergo due to the cheating and due to the due to the evil machinations of Duryodhana and Karna and Shakuni and others, now the time has come that we should return Indraprastha and seek forgiveness from them. So, please return the kingdom of Indraprastha. Please return the kingdom of Indraprastha. And once again, he was talking about the power of Arjuna and his Gandiva as well as the power of Bhima's mace. So it would not behove us to engage in battle with them in any manner. We stand no chance in front of them. So, when Bhishma spoke these kind of words, Karna could not tolerated because Karna was always very arrogant and he had a he was very conceited he had a very high estimation. An unjust .. If you can see. unjustifiably high estimation of himself So he got up and he started speaking very angrily. What are you talking? Oh brahmana? What do you think of yourself the point of us are coming from exile? What what do they think of themselves according to us there? They have not completed their term of the for the living incognito one year So the they should simply give up everything and go back to 12 years of forest exile After that let them come back and request us and if Duryodhana is pleased he will return the kingdom to them Otherwise the Duryodhana will not give them even an inch of land forget it. We are not interested in giving anything Like this Karna spoke up very strongly

**Summary:** The speaker describes the reactions to Karna's speech, Dhritarashtra's deceptive message of peace without commitment, and the Brahmana's return to Virata with the news of military preparations.

When Karna spoke like this Dronacharya Bhishma, they Vidura they all censured him while Dushasana and others They said oh what Karna has said is right and all these kind of things then Duryodhana spoke something Dhritarashtra Spoke something finally Dhritarashtra Gave the final verdict. He told the brahmana that please take our message back to the Pandavas. We want peace We don't want war. So please tell them whatever is required for peace Let us work cooperatively and let us achieve that let us not engage in any sort of hostility and also Please tell them that if we engage in war it is going to be only destruction of our own family So let us not do this. So like this Dhritarashtra very tactfully he He he gave a message as if he is wanting peace But he never gave a commitment that they will return Indraprastha and so like this the brahmana also Understood what Dhritarashtra's mind was what was the mood of Duryodhana Karna and others and what was happening in Hastinapura and he returned back to The kingdom of Virata with the news Now when he brought back the news he told what exactly? Had happened and he told that the Dhritarashtra's message is that they want peace But he has given no commitment of returning the kingdom of Indraprastha And he also explained what kind of army they were gathering and what kind of military preparations were going on in Hastinapura which he witnessed with on with his own eyes and Drupada also started gathering gathering that information about the strength of the enemy army so Duryodhana was Quite crafty and he was quite experienced so he Had he achieved what he wanted to do he he wanted an estimation of the kind of military Activity that was happening in Hastinapura and he managed to get what he wanted and so then he told the Pandavas see

#### Strategy, Dilemma, Messenger
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/NCl2zvTHTwQ?t=1071
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Dhritarashtra did not have the guts. He did not have the courage to go against Duryodhana. So he was being overwhelmed by what Duryodhana was saying."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the episode by describing Drupada's advice to prepare for war, Dhritarashtra's dilemma between his sons and the Pandavas, and his decision to send Sanjaya with a message, setting the stage for the next episode.

this although he Dhritarashtra is saying he wants peace, but the activity suggests otherwise so we should also not keep quiet We should immediately start informing our allies And we should start getting People on our side let us immediately send out Messengers from our side and ask our allies to come and join us The Pandavas also agreed. Yes. This is a good strategy to adopt We should not waste any more time and immediately Drupada started sending out messengers in different directions to those who are friendly to the Pandavas and they knew that they would join them in the in the fight of righteousness in the meantime a Dhritarashtra also told one more thing to this brahmana. He told them Please give us a little time. We will think over and give our final conclusion We will the final message will send through one of our messengers after a little while So the Pandavas also said okay while we assemble we are while we gather our military strength Also, let us wait for the final messenger to come from the side of the dhritarashtra So they were waiting What Dhritarashtra would do the dhritarashtra thought over? for some time. He consulted Vidura. He consulted Bhishma. They were all naturally in favor of the Pandavas and telling him to return the kingdom of Indraprastha to the Pandavas. Whereas Duryodhana was totally against it. Duryodhana was not interested in giving back anything. Duryodhana was sticking to his stand that the Pandavas have not completed their one year of living incognito. So they have to go back to their 12 year of exile. So he was, Duryodhana continued to be unreasonable and Dhritarashtra did not have the guts. He did not have the courage to go against Duryodhana. So he was being overwhelmed by what Duryodhana was saying. At the same time, Dhritarashtra knew that although Karna was so boasting and all those kind of things, Karna was not going to be able to protect Duryodhana. Although Duryodhana also had great faith in Karna's prowess. But Dhritarashtra had a good, good idea. He had a proper understanding of the reality and he knew that if there was a war, all his sons would be killed. So now what to do? He was, Dhritarashtra was in a dilemma. He could not go against his son Duryodhana and return the kingdom to the Pandavas in which case the war could be fully averted. At the same time, he did not want the war. Now what to do? So he thought of sending Sanjaya with a message to the Pandavas who were living in Virata. Now, what was that message which Dhritarashtra sent through Sanjaya and what was the response of the Pandavas to this? Please join us in our next episode to know about this. Thank you. Hare Krishna. Jagat Guru, Srila Prabhupada ki Jai, Netaayi Gaur, Premanande Hari Hari Bol.

**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So Dhritarashtra wanted to send a message which would not be hostile, which would not incite war, but at the same time somehow broker peace."
**Summary:** The speaker summarizes the previous episode and sets the stage for the current one, focusing on Dhritarashtra's desire to avoid war while Duryodhana remains unwilling to return Indraprastha.

In the last episode of the Mahabharata, we have seen how Drupada Maharaj sent his priest as a messenger to Hastinapura to convey their intent and requesting the Kauravas to return Indraprastha to the Pandavas, while also assessing what was the mood of the Kauravas and what all preparations they were undertaking. And we also saw how the Kauravas were completely against returning the kingdom to Indraprastha. Duryodhana, being the envious, greedy person that he was, he had no intention of returning the kingdom. Shakuni had made his own calculations, some convoluted calculations he had done to convince others that the Pandavas had not yet completed their term of exile, living incognito, before they were discovered. And on that basis, they should be asked to go back for another 12 years of forest exile. Although in reality, just as Bhishma Dev had done his calculations, the Pandavas had completed their exile period successfully. Then after this, Dhritarashtra thought of sending Sanjaya, who was his charioteer and his personal secretary, to the Pandavas with a message which he, after hearing out Duryodhana and understanding his intentions, that he did not want to return the kingdom of Indraprastha. So Dhritarashtra wanted to send a message which would not be hostile, which would not incite war, but at the same time somehow broker peace. That was Dhritarashtra's thinking. So what did Sanjaya convey to the Pandavas? What was the message he took to them? What was their reaction? Is what we will see today.

---
#### strategy, Krishna's influence, Duryodhana's ambition
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=244
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So he thought, let me quickly go to Dwaraka and seek the support of the Yadava army."
**Summary:** The segment describes Duryodhana's strategy to secure the Yadava army's support, recognizing their invincibility and Krishna's influence, leading him to personally visit Dwaraka.

In the meantime, what had happened was, when after the marriage of Uttara and Abhimanyu, when all the kings had got together and they were strategizing what should be the next steps to be taken, and they finally decided to send Drupada's priest to Hastinapura to seek a return of the Pandavas' kingdom, Lord Krishna and Balarama had returned to Dwaraka, giving confidence to the Pandavas that whenever they would be needed, they would be there. This news that Krishna had returned to Dwaraka reached Hastinapura. Now Duryodhana had already started intimating his allies, gathering different armies, and he was building up his army very well aware that his refusal to return Indraprastha to the Pandavas, which was rightfully theirs, would inevitably result in war. Now the Yadava Sainya, which was called Narayani Sainya, it was known to be an army which was invincible. The Narayani Sena of Krishna had defeated Jarasandha innumerable number of times. They had defeated Shalva, and many such battles had been very easily won by the Narayani Sena. And all over the world, everybody knew that the Yadava army was practically invincible. Now it was Duryodhana also knew that Krishna always was favorable towards the Pandavas. So he thought, let me quickly go to Dwaraka and seek the support of the Yadava army. While to all other allies, Duryodhana had sent his different emissaries to seek the support of the different kings. When it came to the Yadava army, because it was so important, Duryodhana personally went to Dwaraka. Duryodhana came to Dwaraka and he informed the people there that he had come to meet Lord Krishna because he knew, although Ugrasena was the king of the Yadavas, still Krishna's word was always considered final in the Yadava assembly. So he thought it is wise to meet Krishna and if Krishna, if he seeks Krishna's support and Krishna agrees, then all the rest of the entire Yadava clan would definitely support him. So with this intention, Duryodhana had come to Dwaraka.

#### devotion, relationships, divine arrangement
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=427
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Arjuna had also come seeking the support of Krishna practically at the same time."
**Summary:** This segment describes Satyaki joining the Pandavas, Duryodhana's arrival at Krishna's chambers, and Arjuna's simultaneous arrival, highlighting the close relationship between the Kauravas and Yadavas.

In the meantime, what had happened is, before Duryodhana came to Dwaraka, Satyaki, one of the commander-in-chiefs of the Yadava army, he was a disciple of Arjuna. He had learnt the art of archery from Arjuna himself and he was always very much in favour of the Pandavas because of their righteousness. Satyaki, without waiting for an invitation from the Pandavas, took one Akshauhini, one division of army and went to Virata, the kingdom of Virata and he joined the Pandavas. Now the rest of the Yadava clan was still in Dwaraka by the time Duryodhana had reached Dwaraka and now when Duryodhana came and sought audience with Krishna, he was told that Krishna was taking rest within his chambers and so he was escorted, Duryodhana was escorted towards Krishna's chambers. Because Duryodhana, the entire Kaurava dynasty were closely related with the Yadava dynasty because Kunti Devi was from the Yadava dynasty and therefore she was the aunt of Lord Krishna and that way the relationship was quite thick between the Yadava household and the Kaurava household. So therefore, Duryodhana had access to Krishna's private chambers. When Duryodhana arrived, it so happened that Krishna was actually resting. He was sleeping in his inner chambers and because he was a close family member, he was escorted to Krishna's bedroom. As Duryodhana was entering Krishna's bedroom, he saw there was one more visitor who had arrived at the same time. It was none other than, Arjuna. Arjuna had also come seeking the support of Krishna practically at the same time. Now Duryodhana and Arjuna saw each other, they greeted each other and Duryodhana entered first into Krishna's chamber because he had come first and also he was the elder one, Arjuna was younger. Giving due respect according to his age, Arjuna followed Duryodhana into the chamber.

#### Krishna's diplomacy, devotion, Vedic principles
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=576
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But when I opened my eyes, the first person I saw was Arjuna."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the scene of Duryodhana and Arjuna waiting for Krishna, Krishna's awakening, and his diplomatic offer to divide his support between them, giving Arjuna the first choice.

As soon as Duryodhana entered, he saw Krishna lying on the bedstead and next to Krishna near his head was a small chair. Duryodhana went and sat there at Krishna's head waiting for him to wake up. Arjuna in the meantime, he also entered and he saw Krishna is sleeping and he went and stood very respectfully at the lotus feet of Krishna. Arjuna, he is a great devotee of Lord Krishna, and for him his heart is filled with love for Krishna. Yes, he understands the great position of Krishna as the Supreme Personality of Godhead. So he felt it appropriate to stand at the lotus feet of Krishna. And both Duryodhana and Arjuna were silently waiting, not wanting to disturb the sleep of Krishna. After a little while, Krishna slowly opened his eyes. And when Krishna opened his eyes, he saw Arjuna standing at his feet. And then slowly Krishna raised himself up and he said, Oh Arjuna, you have come here. And then when Krishna got up and sat up on his bed, he saw Duryodhana is sitting next to him. And he said, Oh Duryodhana, you have also come. Then Krishna greeted both of them warmly and he asked them, Why have you both come at the same time together? At the same time to meet me? Then Duryodhana replied to Krishna and he said, Oh my dear Krishna, we both have come here seeking your support and the support of your Yadava army for any eventuality of any war that may take place between us. And you are related to the Kaurava dynasty. So you are equally related to me as well as to the Pandavas. Therefore, you should be equal to both of us. And since I have come here first, therefore I have the right to first ask your support. Lord Krishna replied to Duryodhana, My dear Duryodhana, I agree what you have said is right. You came here first. But when I opened my eyes, the first person I saw was Arjuna. Not only that, Arjuna is younger to you by age. And according to the Vedic scriptures, according to Dharma, the younger person is always given the first opportunity. And so therefore, I give Arjuna the opportunity to seek my support.

#### devotion, Krishna's supremacy, Arjuna's choice
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=781
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna, doesn't matter that you don't lift arms, you don't do any activity, you just be by my side. That's enough for me."
**Summary:** Arjuna chooses Krishna over the Yadava army, expressing his devotion and prioritizing Krishna's presence above all else, much to Duryodhana's delight.

But at the same time, what you have said is right, Duryodhana, that I am related equally to both of you. This is a family quarrel. And so, what I will do is, on one side, I present myself unarmed. I will not take up arms in the battle. I will not fight in the battle. But I will be there giving good advice and support. Moral support and advice will be there from my side. On the other hand, my entire Narayani Sena, the Yadavasanya will be available. So between the two of you, you can choose one of these two. And Arjuna, I give you the first opportunity please choose whom you want. Arjuna, without batting an eyelid, without hesitation, immediately said, Krishna, doesn't matter that you don't lift arms, you don't do any activity, you just be by my side. That's enough for me. Krishna said, okay, as you wish. Then he turned to Duryodhana and he said, Arjuna has chosen me. So I am afraid that you have to be satisfied with the Narayana. So I am afraid that you have to be satisfied with the Narayani Sena. Duryodhana, internally, was delighted. He had thought that oh, Arjuna has got the first right, he will choose the Narayani Sena and I will be left with Krishna who is anyway not going to lift weapons. What is the point of having him then anyway on our side? Duryodhana was elated that Arjuna had made the foolish decision. And he thought, good, Arjuna has somehow, by some fluke, Arjuna has chosen wrongly.

#### Krishna's grace, devotion, Lord-devotee relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=922
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You are the goal of my life. So I don't want anything else. Everything else is secondary. If you are there with me, for me, everything else is achieved."
**Summary:** Arjuna explains his choice, emphasizing Krishna's supreme position and his desire for Krishna's presence above all else, leading Krishna to agree to be Arjuna's charioteer.

So Duryodhana, but externally, he was a greatly diplomatic person. He maintained a very calm exterior and neutral countenance he maintained. And he said, I think I don't have a choice now. Arjuna has already chosen, you have given him the first right. So I have to be satisfied with your Narayani Sena. Okay, all right. I'll be satisfied with that. Okay, thank you both of you. I'm leaving. And he said, he got up and he walked out. Now when Duryodhana walked out, Krishna looked at Arjuna and he said, Arjuna, I said, I will be, I will not take up arms. Then why did you choose me? You should have taken the army. The entire Yadava army was at your disposal. Arjuna immediately said, Oh Krishna, you are the supreme personality of Godhead. Wherever you are there, Lakshmi Devi is also there. That means all opulence is also there. All victory is also there. I am not bothered about winning or losing this battle. I want you. You are the person whom I want. You are the goal of my life. So I don't want anything else. Everything else is secondary. If you are there with me, for me, everything else is achieved. So Krishna was overwhelmed by this love from Arjuna. And he said, and he told Arjuna, Arjuna, I am, I am indebted to you for the love you have shown me. So please tell me how I can help you in this battle. Then Arjuna told Krishna that Krishna has a great desire. I want you to be my charioteer in this battle. Krishna readily agreed. He said, is that all? Definitely I'll be your charioteer. You can see Krishna is the supreme personality of Godhead. Krishna's lotus feet are worshipped even by Lord Shiva and Lord Brahma. All the devatas are waiting for an opportunity to have darshan of the lotus feet of Krishna. Krishna is such an exalted personality. He is the supreme personality of Godhead. There is nobody equal to him, nobody above him. Such is the greatness of Krishna. He is bhoktaram yajna tapasam sarva loka mahishuram. All the universes, all the planetary systems are emanating from Krishna. Krishna is the supreme creator. And therefore everything belongs to him. And all the ajnas, all the sacrifices, all the Vedas are meant for pleasing Krishna. And that Krishna is agreeing to become the charioteer of Arjuna, his devotee. These are the loving dealings between the Lord, between Lord Krishna, the supreme personality of Godhead, and his devotees. Although Krishna is the supreme person, he is the master of everyone. Still Krishna loves to become subordinate to the commands of his devotee.

#### Krishna's expansions, Ananda, Lord-devotee relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=1062
**Verse References:** SB 1.2.6
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "By nature, the supreme lord being a person, he desires to enjoy."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Duryodhana's next move to seek Balarama's support and then delves into the philosophical concept of Krishna's expansions for the purpose of enjoyment and relationship.

In the meantime, Duryodhana had still not given up. His mind was constantly working. He thought, okay, let's refuse to partake in the battle. But what about Balarama? He is the next most powerful personality. While Arjuna and Krishna were discussing like this, Duryodhana had left Krishna's room and he had gone to approach Balarama. The added advantage for Duryodhana with Balarama was Balarama was his guru as far as learning the art of fighting with the gatha, with the mace was concerned. So Duryodhana already had a good relationship with Balarama. And very quickly he approached Balarama before Arjuna could approach. And Duryodhana approached Balarama and he told him that like this, I have come here seeking your support and I approached Lord Krishna also. But Krishna has agreed to become, he told that he would decide, he would divide the Narayani Sena and himself. One side he will be there without arms and the other side will be his army. And Arjuna chose Krishna. Now I have come to you seeking your support. Now Duryodhana did not know the actual position of Balarama. Actually Krishna is the supreme personality of Godhead. Now it is stated in the Vedas that Anandamayo Abhyasat, by nature, the supreme lord being a person, he desires to enjoy. And when he desires to enjoy, what does he do? Just like in this material world, whenever we want to enjoy, what do we do? We enter into relationships with other individuals. We enjoy in relationship with our families and with our friends and relatives etc. We do not enjoy if we are all alone. If we have got something nice to eat, then we want to call a few friends and we want to share it with them. If we want to watch a movie, we want to go with our family. If we want to go on a vacation, we want to take our wives and children along with us. This is the nature of a person, of an individual person. This nature of human beings is actually a result of being of the individual nature. Being a part and parcel of the supreme lord. Because the supreme lord has a propensity to enjoy in relationship with others, we being emanations, we being part and parcel of that supreme lord, we also have, we also possess similar propensity to enjoy with other individuals.

---
#### Balarama's devotion, Krishna's expansions, Lord-devotee relationship
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=1240
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Balarama is very very possessive of Krishna and he will not think of doing anything other than what Krishna likes."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining Krishna's expansions, focusing on Balarama's role as Krishna's first expansion and his unwavering devotion, leading Balarama to refuse to oppose Krishna in the upcoming war.

Now, Krishna when he desires to enjoy, he expands himself. He is not dependent upon individuals other than himself to enjoy. He expands himself into other personalities. He expands himself into first as Balarama. And that Balarama further expands into the Chaturvyuha expansion of Vasudeva, Sankarsana, Pradyumna, Aniruddha. And from that Sankarsana again expands Narayana. From that Narayana, the second quadruple expansion of Vasudeva, Sankarsana, Pradyumna, Aniruddha come out. And from that second Sankarsana come the Vishnu avataras within this material world. So like this, the lord expands himself into innumerable forms of the Vishnu tattva as well as his incarnation. And the lord also expands himself into his devotees. And then the lord expands himself into all the living entities that we all are. And so like this, the lord expands himself into unlimited forms to engage in wonderful loving relationships with different individuals. Now Balarama is the first expansion of Krishna. And he is imbued with love for Krishna. When the lord descended as Ramachandra, the same Balarama descended as Lakshmana. And in the past times of Lord Ramachandra, Lord Ramachandra had to undergo so much difficulty. And Lakshmana being a younger brother, many times he had great desire to partake, to express his anguish whenever he saw Lord Ramachandra suffering. But he could not express because he was a younger brother and he was inhibited by that subordinate position. So when the Supreme Lord Krishna personally descended in this world, Balarama came as his elder brother so that he could express his anguish whenever Krishna would feel any sort of distress. And also Balarama could play a protective role and prevent anybody from causing any harm or distress to Krishna. So Balarama is very very possessive of Krishna and he will not think of doing anything other than what Krishna likes. The moment when Balarama heard that Krishna has already given himself to the Pandavas, Balarama told Duryodhana, I cannot even dream of opposing Krishna in any manner. And because this is a family quarrel, I will not participate in this. Let Krishna give support to the Pandavas. I am giving up my Kshatriya Dharma. I am retiring from this Kshatriya duties of fighting and engaging in battle and all these kind of things. I am going on Teertha Yatra. Balarama decided to go on pilgrimage.

#### diplomacy, peace, duty

**Content Type:** story

**Key Quote:** "Return Indraprastha to us. Matter will be settled."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes the episode of Duryodhana seeking support and transitions to Sanjaya's arrival at the Pandavas' camp with Dhritarashtra's message of peace.

And so Duryodhana thought, okay, anyway Balarama did not come to my side, but he is not going to fight on the side of the Pandavas also. So there is no loss. Then he said, okay, fine, alright. If you don't want to fight, no problem. And Duryodhana went back to Hastinapura. And in this manner, Krishna became the charioteer of Arjuna. And now Arjuna took Krishna with him and went back to the kingdom of Virata for further consultation and seeking Krishna's advice on what they should do next. After Krishna and Arjuna had reached the kingdom of Virata, they came to know that Sanjaya has come there, bringing the message of Dhritarashtra. The Pandavas, they all assembled and they called forth Sanjaya. Sanjaya came there. After receiving all the welcoming hospitality, Sanjaya came forward into that assembly and Yudhishthira Maharaj received him very warmly because the Pandavas knew Sanjaya for a long time. And Sanjaya had also been always very favorable towards the Pandavas. Although he was the secretary of Dhritarashtra, he knew that the Pandavas were righteous and he was always favorable to them. This Sanjaya came before the Pandavas and Yudhishthira Maharaj asked about the welfare of all the senior people in the Kaurava household like Bhishma Dev, Dronacharya, Dhritarashtra, Gandhari, etc. And Sanjaya also told Yudhishthira and the Pandavas that they were all doing well and that he has brought a message from Dhritarashtra. Yudhishthira Maharaj said, please go ahead, please let us know what message you are bringing from Dhritarashtra. Then Sanjaya told Yudhishthira that Dhritarashtra, King Dhritarashtra wants peace. He does not want any kind of fighting, infighting. And he has said that he is ready to consider all options to avoid any kind of war or any kind of skirmish, and let us peacefully settle the matter. And he has also said that there is no point in fighting, infighting between the family members. If the family members kill each other, then what are we achieving? We are not achieving anything. It is not good for either party. So let us avoid fighting. This is the message.

#### justice, Dharma, war
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=1591
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "You please first consider what is the duty of a Kshatriya."
**Summary:** Yudhishthira responds to Sanjaya's message, demanding the return of Indraprastha and asserting the Pandavas' readiness for war if justice is not served, leading to a debate on Dharma and the duty of a Kshatriya.

So Yudhishthira replied to Sanjaya, See, if you want peace, the solution is very simple. Return Indraprastha to us. Matter will be settled. I assure you that we will forget all animosity, whatever the Kauravas, they have insulted us, whatever humiliation they have done towards us, how they cheated us in the game of dice, we will forget all that and we will live as friends. I assure you this. On the other hand, if you do not hand over Indraprastha to us, then we are not sitting here idly. Arjuna is having the Gandiva in his hand and Bhima is having his Gada in his hand and you will have to reply to these two people and no force in this world can withstand the force of these two weapons of my brothers. When Sanjaya heard this, he was surprised. He said, Yudhishthira, how is it that you are so much inclined to war? You should be thinking of how to get peace. A righteous person is never inclined towards war and to quarrel and such things. He should always think of how to maintain peace and harmony in the society because wars always bring untold suffering to humanity. Everyone knows this. So how is it that you are behaving like this adharmically? When Sanjaya said this, Yudhishthira said that you please first consider what is the duty of a Kshatriya. Duty of a Kshatriya is to fight or not in the face of injustice. In the face of injustice, is a Kshatriya supposed to keep quiet? And what is happening with us here? Is it just or is it unjust? So on whose side is Dharma? You please consider this. And then Yudhishthira Maharaj turned to Krishna and he said, Lord Krishna, you decide whether what I am saying is right or wrong. You please tell us whether I am wrong in expressing that in case the kingdom is not given, there has to be war. Am I a war mongering person? Am I going against the principles of Dharma? Then Sanjaya also looked towards Krishna because Sanjaya also knew that Krishna is the Supreme Lord.

---
#### justice, Dharma, Krishna's anger
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=1758
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Now what right do you have to speak of Dharma?"
**Summary:** Krishna responds to Yudhishthira and Sanjaya, expressing his anger at the Kauravas' past injustices and firmly stating that the kingdom must be returned, or there will be war.

Krishna responded that Yudhishthira, whatever you have said is perfectly according to the codes of Dharma. You have always lived righteously and your demand for the return of your kingdom is also righteous. And Sanjaya suddenly, everybody could see Krishna's face becoming very grave and they could sense Krishna's rising anger. Krishna said, Sanjaya, how did the Kauravas get hold of Indraprastha? Do you remember? They cheated Yudhishthira Maharaj in that game of dice. Although Yudhishthira Maharaj was saying in that assembly that he did not wish to play that game of dice, still he was goaded into playing that game of dice. Although he was not so keen on playing that game of dice, unfairly Duryodhana made Shakuni his partner and he cheated Yudhishthira of his kingdom, of his brothers and his wife. And when Yudhishthira had lost his kingdom, at that point of time, what were you all doing? You were all sitting in the assembly keeping quiet? You did not stop? What was going on? The game of cheating dice? And then what happened? Draupadi was pulled into that assembly and she was attempted to be disrobed. When her honour was being violated, which person in that assembly stood up and spoke? Except for Vikarna. Did Bhishma oppose it? Did Drona oppose it? You were also there. Did you oppose it? So at that time, you did not think of Dharma. Now what right do you have to speak of Dharma? You have no right to talk about what is right and what is wrong. You have no right to talk about Dharma and Adharma. Forget it. You keep it aside. Whatever the kingdom has been taken by force by the Kauravas has to be returned. Otherwise there will be war and Duryodhana and all his brothers will bite the dust. So Krishna's anger was something which was unexpected. And everybody was taken aback, including Sanjaya. But Sanjaya also felt ashamed because whatever Krishna had said was true. In that assembly, Dushyasana was trying to disrobe Draupadi. When Karna was inciting Duryodhana to drag Draupadi into that assembly, none of them had opposed. Sanjaya had not opposed. Sanjaya had kept quiet. This was wrong on his part. Sanjaya accepted that. So he bowed his head and kept quiet. So finally, after little more discussion like this, Sanjaya asked the Pandavas, Please give me your message. What is the message I have to give to Dhritarashtra? Yudhishthir Maharaj told him, Our message is very simple. Please hand over Indraprastha, which is rightfully ours, back to us. We will forget all hostility and we will live as friends and brothers. On the other hand, if you wish to, if you do not give the kingdom back to us, then there has to be war. And even if you give five villages, one for each of five brothers, we are ready to even accept that because we are Kshatriyas. Our duty is to rule. As long as we have got a place to rule and some citizens to administer, then we have no issues. We are ready even for that. This is our message.

#### peace, revenge, compassion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2DMK7tb1W6M?t=1957
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "I am waiting to fulfill my vow."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the responses of Arjuna and Bhima to the message, highlighting Bhima's surprising plea for peace, followed by his reaffirmation of his vow for revenge, ending with a cliffhanger about the Kauravas' reaction.

And then Krishna spoke to Arjuna. Krishna asked Arjuna, Arjuna, what is your response? And Arjuna responded, He said, Whatever my brother Yudhishthir Maharaj has said is fully right and I stand fully with him. If the Kauravas are ready to give back Indraprastha, we will maintain peace. Otherwise, my Gandiva will speak. We will not speak anymore. Then Krishna looked at Bhima and he said, Bhima, what do you have to say? Now what was Bhima's response? Normally, we would expect Bhima to be the most angry of the lot and the person who was most thirsting for revenge. Surprisingly, Bhima spoke very peacefully and Bhima said, My dear Sanjaya, we want peace. We don't want any fight. Please tell Dhritarashtra that somehow please convince Duryodhana to give us at least five villages. We are happy with that. We don't want any fight. We don't want any battle. Why we should fight and kill each other unnecessarily? If we on one side and you Kauravas with Bhishma, Drona and all on the other side fight, what is the result which is going to happen? We will simply kill each other. I don't want to see such bloodshed. Let us avoid all this. Let us maintain peace. Krishna was surprised and Krishna started laughing. Krishna said, What is it that I am hearing Bhima? Looks like you have become afraid when the time for battle has come. What happened to all those words of revenge and all those things you were speaking? Then Bhima became very grave and he said, Krishna, it is not that I have become afraid. I was feeling compassionate towards the Kauravas because if there is battle, definitely they will be killed. And I thought, why they have to go through all this pain and Dhritarashtra and Gandhari will have to suffer seeing their sons killed before them. So I was being compassionate and thought, let us avoid it. But otherwise I am not afraid. I am waiting for battle. Let the Kaurava brothers come. I am waiting to fulfill my vow. I have vowed to kill Dushyasana and rip his heart out and drink his blood. I am waiting for the battle to happen between both of us. So Krishna smiled and said, and he said, This is the Bhima we know. Fine. So like this, everybody's message was given and Sanjaya started back to Hastinapura. And when he reached Hastinapura and how was the message from the Pandavas received? What was the reaction in the Kaurava? What was the reaction in the Kaurava palace? Please join us in the next episode to know about this. Thank you for joining us today. Hare Krishna. Jagat Guru Shri La Pravupada Ki Jai Netai Gor Prem Anandae Hari Hari Bol
