**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

#### diksha, initiation, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When we are taking initiation from Srila Prabhupada, we are accepting Srila Prabhupada as our Diksha Guru, initiating spiritual master, and Prabhupada is also accepting you as his disciple."
**Summary:** This segment introduces the concept of Diksha initiation, emphasizing Srila Prabhupada's role as the Diksha Guru and referencing relevant verses from the Chaitanya Charitamrita.

Namah Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale Srimate Bhaktivedanta Swamin Nithinamine. Namaste Saraswati Deve Gauravani Pracharine Nirvishesha Sunyavadi Paschatya Deshatarine. Jaya Sri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu Nithyananda Sri Advaita Gadhadhara Sri Vasadi Gauravakta Vrinda. Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare. So today is a very significant day in the lives of the devotees who are taking Diksha initiation. When we are taking initiation from Srila Prabhupada, we are accepting Srila Prabhupada as our Diksha Guru, initiating spiritual master, and Prabhupada is also accepting you as his disciple. It is important to understand what Diksha is, what is initiation. In the Chaitanya Charitamrita, Madhyalila, Chapter 4, Chapter 24, verse 333, purport, Srila Prabhupada says that, as far as Diksha is concerned, initiation is concerned, one must refer the Chaitanya Charitamrita, Madhyalila, Chapter 15, verse 108. So in that purport to verse 108 of Chapter 15 of the Chaitanya Charitamrita, Madhyalila, Srila Prabhupada elaborately explains about Diksha or initiation.

---
#### diksha, divya jnana, spiritual progress
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=136
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So the process of divya jnana, of Diksha started the day when you started hearing from Srila Prabhupada."
**Summary:** This segment defines Diksha as the revealing of transcendental knowledge and the destruction of sinful reactions, explaining how this process begins with hearing from Srila Prabhupada and following his instructions, leading to gradual purification.

Srila Prabhupada quotes Srila Rupa Goswami and he says that, divyam jnanam yathodadyat kuryat papasya sankshayam, diksheti sa chokta deshikais tatva kovidaihi. What is the definition of Diksha? The definition of Diksha, the word Diksha is taken from two words, di is taken from divya jnana, and ksha is taken from kuryat papasya sankshayam, kshaya. So divyam jnanam yathodadyat, the process by which transcendental knowledge is revealed within the heart, divyam jnanam yathodadyat. Kuryat papasya sankshayam, and by the process by which sinful reactions are destroyed in one's lives. These two things constitute the process of Diksha. So all of you who are taking Diksha today, you have already received divya jnana in your heart to some extent. The day you came in touch with devotees and somebody gave you a book of Prabhupada and you started reading Prabhupada's books, divya jnana started revealing within your hearts, isn't it? So the process of divya jnana, of Diksha started the day when you started hearing from Srila Prabhupada. And when you started hearing from Srila Prabhupada and you decided to follow his instructions, you might have begun with chanting Hare Krishna Maha Mantra a few times, one mala, then slowly, slowly when you read, you increase your number of malas, then you come to four malas, you are still reading Prabhupada's books, so many things you are understanding. In the beginning, you may not have the understanding that Krishna is Supreme Personality of Godhead, but anyway, I am told to chant Hare Krishna, I am chanting. Some mantra, we chant like that. But as we go on reading Prabhupada's books, we understand, oh yes, Krishna is Supreme Personality of Godhead. Krishna is not like other devatas. So divya jnana has started getting revealed within the heart. And also you understand, I am not the body, I am a spirit soul, I am Atma, I am not the Deha. Divya jnana is getting revealed within the heart. And slowly what happens, you go on reading Prabhupada's books, you keep chanting, by the time you come to chant four rounds, you feel like giving up coffee and tea. And then continue reading Prabhupada's books, continue chanting, by the time you come to eight rounds, onion and garlic goes out of your life. And then slowly, slowly, you come to the stage of chanting sixteen rounds, you start wearing Kanti Mala, you start wearing Tilak, you start fasting for Ekadashi and other festival days. Isn't it?

---
#### fasting, self-control, kuryat papasya sankshayam
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=318
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "But then after reading Prabhupada's books, I realized, no, we have to fast."
**Summary:** The speaker shares a personal anecdote about his initial experience with fasting, highlighting the struggle between bodily desires and the determination to follow Prabhupada's instructions, illustrating the principle of Kuryat Papasya Sankshayam.

Before coming in touch with Prabhupada and Prabhupada's instructions, some of us might have been fasting, but many of us would not have fasted in our life. I, for that matter, never fasted in my life. Whole life, whenever I wanted to eat anything, I was eating. But then after reading Prabhupada's books, I realized, no, we have to fast. So, first time I fasted on Gaurapurnima day. And I didn't realize, I didn't know what is fasting. Fasting karna hai, fasting karo. By that time, it was afternoon, my stomach was growling. And by evening, I was like, you know, I am going to fall dead now. And I was working in office. Then I had to leave the office. Somehow, I drove the scooter all the way to the temple. I don't know how I drove. And then when I reached the temple, Abhishekam, all those things happened. And then finally reached the Prasadam hall, sat down with great, ah, now relief. Now I am going to eat Prasadam. Half my life is already gone by that time. And I am sitting in front of the plate, waiting when will Prasadam come and fall in the plate. Suddenly, one devotee comes and tells me, what are you sitting there for? Get up, come and serve. Another 25% of my life went out. Serve ah, what? Serve. Anyway, he is calling, I could not say no. I got up and served somehow. And then finally, he sat and had Prasadam. And I was like, oh my God. First time I was, what is this fasting? Never had, had any idea. But you see, when we, when we start following Prabhupada's instructions, we start fasting. Even though the body is demanding, body is craving, I want food, I am hungry. Body is all feeling so tired, so fatigued. No, determination has come. No, I must fast. Why? Because I am not the body. I am not given to the body's demands. So you see, Divya Jnana has started getting revealed within the heart. Meat eating goes out of the life. The coffee and tea goes out. All intoxicants go out. So, this is Kuryat Papasya Sankshayam. Papasya Sankshaya also has started happening.

---
#### karma, sinful reactions, prarabdha, aprarabdha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=451
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Papa or the sinful reactions in our life are of two kinds. One is the propensity to perform sinful activities which comes by our, because of our ignorance."
**Summary:** This segment delves into the nature of sinful reactions, categorizing them into the propensity to commit sins and the suffering that results, further dividing the latter into Prarabdha Karma (manifested results) and Aprarabdha Karma (unmanifested results in seed, sprouting, and unripe fruit forms).

So why? Because, Papa or the sinful reactions in our life are of two kinds. One is the propensity to perform sinful activities which comes by our, because of our ignorance. And because of our ignorance, that thinking that I am the body, I am not a spirit soul, we have committed so many sinful activities in our past life. So we get the propensity to perform sinful activities in our current life also, which is the result of sinful activities in our past lives. And second, the way the sinful reactions manifest is in the form of suffering. So there is suffering and when we speak of suffering on account of our accumulated sinful activities, reactions in our past lives, it again manifests in two types. One is Prarabdha Karma and another is Aprarabdha Karma. Prarabdha Karma means the results which are already manifested. This body which I have got is the result of my past activities. It is Prarabdha Karma. I have got a certain mind and intelligence that is the result of Prarabdha Karma. I might be suffering from some disease that is the result of my Prarabdha Karma. That means it is already manifest. I am already, I am already either enjoying or suffering the fruit of my past activity. The second type of sinful reaction is Aprarabdha Karma. Aprarabdha Karmam Kutam Bijam Falon Mukam. This Aprarabdha Karma is of three kinds. One is, it is in seed form. Another is sprouting. Another is the fruit which is not yet ripe. So the, when the sinful reactions in our life are accumulated like a mountain, they are all like the bija, like the seeds which you have accumulated within a jar of seeds. So when you keep the seeds in the jar, they will remain as it is. Now you take out the seed and put it into the soil, put a little water, put a little manure. What will happen? After a few days, you will see it sprouting. So that is called Kuta. Bijam Kutam Falon Mukam. Kuta, bija is in the seed form. When the situations in your life become favorable for that seed to start sprouting, it will become like a, it will be, it will start sprouting.

---
#### karma, diksha, spiritual master, commitment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=602
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So real initiation begins from the day you start hearing from the spiritual master, from Srila Prabhupada."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of engineering college admission, the speaker explains Fala Unmukha, and emphasizes that the spiritual master gives Divya Jnana and removes sinful propensities, leading to real initiation when one is determined to follow instructions.

That sprouted plant is called Kuta. And then, the plant will grow. And after a little while, it will start giving fruits. That fruit, when it is still unripened, it is called Fala Unmukha. That means, the result is going to come. So just like suppose, I go and admit myself in engineering college. So I have just admitted myself in the college. That means in four years, the fruit of my admitting in the engineering college is going to come in the form of the degree. So, the moment I admit myself into the engineering college, my engineering degree is Fala Unmukha. It is going to come. And then finally, there is the ripened fruit, which is Prarabdha Karma. So, when we, when, so the spiritual master in your life until now, he has been giving you Divya Jnana. And he is also Kuryat Papasya Sankshayam. He is taking away the sinful reactions in your life by removing your propensities, to perform sinful activities. That is why you have started following the four regulated principles. No meat eating, no intoxication, no gambling, no illicit sex. Before you came in touch with Prabhupada's books, Prabhupada's instructions, Prabhupada's teachings, we might have been engaging in all those sinful activities. But after reading Prabhupada's books, we feel, no, I should not do that. That means the propensity was there in you, but that has been removed by Srila Prabhupada. So therefore, he has already given, put you on the, into the process of Diksha or initiation. So real initiation begins from the day you start hearing from the spiritual master, from Srila Prabhupada. And when you take the determination, when you are determined, when you read Prabhupada's books, you have to chant 16 malas, you have to follow the four regulated principles, you have to fast on Ekadashi, what happens? You start, you feel, yes, I should follow that. That determination actually constitutes Diksha or initiation. And what ceremony we are undergoing right now, where there is a fire sacrifice, you will receive a new spiritual name, you will receive beads chanted on by the Ritvik on behalf of Prabhupada, this is the formal part or the official part of Diksha.

---
#### diksha, commitment, spiritual master, eternal bond
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=747
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Your commitment to Srila Prabhupada goes a step higher."
**Summary:** This segment addresses the question of why formal Diksha is necessary if one is already following Prabhupada's instructions, explaining that the ceremony strengthens commitment, creating an eternal bond with Prabhupada as the sole Diksha Guru, even in the spiritual world.

So somebody may say, then I can undergo real initiation, I will read Prabhupada's books, I will follow all his instructions, why do I have to undergo formal Diksha? Why do I need to undergo the ceremony? I will keep following whole my life. Prabhupada says that if you, as long as you are following Prabhupada's instructions and in your heart you have accepted Prabhupada as your spiritual master, as your Diksha Guru, you are as good as his disciple, but you are not yet his disciple. So when you undergo Diksha, undergo the official ceremony, what happens? Your commitment to Srila Prabhupada goes a step higher. When you are sitting in the formal ceremony, what are you committing? You are committing that, I am going to accept Prabhupada as my Diksha Guru, life after life, eternally. Eternally, Prabhupada is my only sole Diksha Guru. This is the commitment you are making from your side. There is no going back from here. There is no point of no return. You cannot go back tomorrow and say that, no, I made a mistake, I want to take Diksha from someone else. No. Not in this life, not in a hundred life henceforth, not even after you go back to the spiritual world. Now from this point onwards, once you have taken formal official Diksha, even when you go to the spiritual world, there also Srila Prabhupada will be guiding you as your Diksha Guru. That is the meaning of eternally you are accepting him as your Diksha Guru.

---
#### diksha, karma, punishment, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=837
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada takes away all your sinful reactions."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Prabhupada takes away all sinful reactions (prarabdha and aprarabdha karma) during formal Diksha, but this also makes the disciple liable to punishment for future sinful activities, unlike before initiation when only sukruti was gained.

And from Prabhupada's side, he is acting as your Diksha Guru. What he does, the moment you are sitting for this formal Diksha, Prabhupada says, when you take the first initiation, Prabhupada takes away all your sinful reactions. How does Prabhupada take away all your sinful reactions? Already he has taken away all your propensity to perform sinful activities. No. Kuryat Papasanksham has already done to one extent. But all your accumulated sinful reactions in all the past lives, aprarabdha karma, sitting as Bija, Kuta, Phalamukha, where will that go? And your prarabdha karma, you have got a body and a mind and intelligence, where will that go? That also has to go, when you take formal initiation from Prabhupada, at that time, Prabhupada will take away all the prarabdha and aprarabdha karma also. Not only the propensity to perform sinful activity, Prabhupada will also take away all your accumulated sinful reactions. So therefore, a disciple, once he has taken formal diksha from Prabhupada, once you have formally sat in the ceremony and you have taken diksha from Prabhupada, what happens? One is the eternal bond is created. Second is Prabhupada takes away all your accumulated sinful reactions. Third, what happens is, therefore, you become liable. You become liable to be punished if you commit sinful activity again. See, until you undergo formal initiation, until you undergo formal diksha from Prabhupada, suppose you followed all the four regulative principles, you were chanting sixteen malas for one year, after that, somehow, you fell down. You gave up. You started, you stopped following the four regulative principles, you stopped chanting sixteen malas. What will happen? Whatever you have done for that one year is to your eternal credit. You will get some sukruti. That's all. And loss of karma will take over. Whatever activity, punya karma, papa karma you do, go on. That much only will happen. You will get an eternal credit. You will get sukruti for whatever you have followed. But once you take formal diksha, after that, if you don't chant your rounds or you break the regulative principles, what happens? You are liable to be punished. If you don't follow, if you break, you will be punished by your diksha guru, Prabhupada and by Krishna. Until that point of time, no punishment. You are only getting sukruti. You are only getting blessing. Now blessing will be hitting also.

---
#### diksha, holy name, consciousness, parampara
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=990
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The holy name is officially getting delivered to you from the parampara."
**Summary:** This segment explains the significance of the Ritvik chanting on the beads, emphasizing the delivery of the holy name from the parampara, creating an eternal bond between the disciple and Prabhupada, and resulting in a shift from material to spiritual consciousness.

If you disobey, if you break your vow, because in a formal ceremony what you are doing, you are sitting in front of Agni, you are sitting in front of all Vaishnavas, you are sitting in front of the Deity, and you are taking a vow that you will follow Prabhupada's instructions without fail. So therefore, you become liable to be punished. And Prabhupada also says, when the Rithvik has chanted on your beads, until now you are chanting, you are chanting on beads only. But what is the significance of the Rithvik chanting on the bead and that bead is being given to you? There are two significances. One is, the holy name is being delivered to you from the parampara. Until now you are chanting the holy name. Okay, no problem. The holy name was acting in your life. But now, the holy name is officially getting delivered to you. From the parampara. Therefore, the holy name will act more powerfully in your life. Now, from now on when you chant Hare Krishna Mantra, that holy name will have a much more powerful effect in purifying you and taking you back to Godhead. Second thing is, that when the Rithvik has chanted on the beads and that bead is given to you, what actually it creates is, it creates an eternal bind between you and Prabhupada. As Prabhupada is your spiritual master and you as his disciple. This is the significance of the Rithvik chanting on the beads on behalf of Prabhupada and giving it to you. And as a… Now that all your prarabdha and aprarabdha karma is taken away from you, you are actually getting a new birth. You may not feel it. You may think, I am feeling the same. It is not there. It is not a matter of feeling. It is a matter of consciousness. Because you have to understand, until now, before we took initiation from Prabhupada, our consciousness was different. What was that? Sense gratification is the goal of my life. Now, when you are taking diksha from Prabhupada, what is your consciousness? Your life and substance is Prabhupada's vani, Prabhupada's instructions. Whatever Prabhupada has ordered, that is my life. And just like life cannot be separated from the body and if the life is separated from the body, then the body becomes useless. It becomes a dead body. Like that, my soul existence, I exist now only for carrying out Prabhupada's instructions because he is my diksha guru now. So my Guru Maharaj, Prabhupada's instructions are my life and soul now. For me, material sense gratification doesn't matter, material name, fame, wealth, these things are immaterial for me now. For me, what is now soul thing in my life is carrying out the instructions of my spiritual master, my Guru Maharaj, Srila Prabhupada. So your consciousness has changed, you see. Your consciousness has gone from material consciousness to spiritual consciousness.

---
#### subtle body, brahmana, diksha, second birth
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=1175
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada has taken that away and given you a subtle brahmana body."
**Summary:** This segment explains that Diksha changes the subtle body from material to spiritual, giving a brahmana body and a new spiritual name, signifying a second birth where the goal is to go back to Godhead.

How is this possible? Until and unless your subtle body is changed. Your subtle body until now was materially working. Now your subtle body is working spiritually. Goals have changed. Your purpose of life has changed. Your outlook towards life has changed. The way you perceive the material world and other people around you has changed. How this is possible? That's because your material subtle body which you have got because of your prarabdha karma is now removed and you are given a new spiritual body. You are given a brahmana body. Actually you are given a brahmana body now. So you are able to think like a brahmana because now you think in terms of shastra, right? You see, when you have a when there is a in sanatana dharma when there is a shudra, vaishya, kshatriya they all think in terms of the body. They think in terms of sense gratification. They think in terms of fame. Shudra, simply sense gratification. Vaishya is more into hoarding money. He is interested in wealth. Kshatriya is interested in name and fame. So all revolving around the body. What does brahmana think? Brahmana's thinking, his consciousness is I have to attain moksha. I have to attain liberation. I have to know who that brahman is. So his thinking is on the spiritual platform. So now you are also thinking like that. What is your thinking? Your thinking now is my goal in life is I have to go back to Godhead. So this is how a brahmana thinks. A brahmana is not interested in sense gratification. Brahmana is not interested in doing any sinful activity. He very rigidly follows the shastra. For him, shastra is the most important. Vani of his guru is most important. So now your life is like that. So that means Prabhupada has taken away your praarabdha karma also. Praarabdha karma, Prabhupada is anyway taking away. Kootam, bijam, phalaunmukam. Prabhupada is also taking your praarabdha karma away. Because externally, the gross body that we have got, that's not the real thing. The real thing is the subtle body. How the subtle body works. So the subtle body has been your current subtle body. In Kali Yuga, everyone is born as Shudra. Even if somebody is born in a brahmana family, he is also a Shudra only. Kalau Shudra Sambhava. So you were born in Kali Yuga with a Shudra body. Now Prabhupada has taken that away and given you a subtle brahmana body. So this is your second birth. And so therefore, to signify the second birth, you are given a spiritual name. You are given a new name. You got this praarabdha karma of this body, with its subtle body, gross and subtle body, by taking birth from your mother and father. So you got a name from them. Now, Prabhupada is giving you a new birth by taking away all your past praarabdha and aprarabdha karma. And so therefore, you are getting a new spiritual name. So this is a second birth for all of you who are taking initiation.

---
#### diksha, commitment, spiritual master, obedience
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EMAA-YunHpw?t=1356
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Prabhupada's order is your life and substance."
**Summary:** Using the analogy of college admission, the speaker emphasizes that formal initiation is like enrolling in college, requiring adherence to the spiritual master's instructions without question, making Prabhupada's orders the disciple's life and soul.

And Prabhupada says that this formal initiation ceremony is like, it is like getting, filling up the admission form when you go to school or college. So when you go to school or college, you fill in an admission form. And after filling in the admission form, like most of you would have done, I also did, start bunking classes. And tell one of our friends, go and put proxy attendance. Then, although you have got admitted in the college, you really are not a student. If you are really a student, you should go and sit in the classes. You have to be attentive to the classes. You should be absorbing the lessons. You should be learning. That mood of yours, that determination of yours, I have come to the college to learn, not to bunk and go out and party with my friends. That's not my goal. I have come to the college, I have filled in the admission because I want to sit and learn something. Then you are a real student. Similarly, formal initiation also, it is a formality. It is like getting admitted into the college. Now, don't bunk. Right? Don't bunk and go and party with friends. You should follow all the rules and regulations. You should follow the instructions of the spiritual master. Order of Guru Maharaj, Srila Prabhupada is your life and soul now. Alright? So, whatever Prabhupada has ordered, you have to follow that implicitly. No question. No question. There is no question of asking. Until now, you could have asked. Prabhupada says this. So, let me find out whether it is asked for Shastra or not. Until now, you could have asked that question. From now onwards, you cannot ask that question. Prabhupada's order, end of it. You cannot ask for Shastric evidence and all that. Where is Shastric evidence for Prabhupada's order? Where is tradition in Prabhupada's order? Now, you cannot ask that question. Prabhupada's order is your life and substance. Shastra, tradition, your understanding, not understanding, all your logic, your arguments, you want to understand how it is correct, incorrect, all that is secondary. Whether you understand or not, whether you are going to be delivered or not, are you going back to Godhead or not, all those considerations are thrown out of the window. No more consideration. Your only consideration now is, I have to follow Prabhupada's orders in my life. Period. This is the meaning of Diksha.

---
#### diksha, loyalty, guru, bhakti

**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This unquestioning paraabhakti towards Guru, is what pleases Guru."
**Summary:** This segment concludes by emphasizing that true initiation involves unquestioning loyalty to Prabhupada's instructions, pleasing the Guru and ensuring Krishna's pleasure, leading to effortless progress back to Godhead.

If you are determined like this, then, you are not only formally initiated, you are also actually initiated. Otherwise, if you get, if you sit in the formal Diksha ceremony, got initiated, after initiation, if you say, Prabhupada is saying like this, but Shastra is saying something else, science is saying something else, who knows whether it is right or not, then, you are only filled in the admission form, but you are bunking the classes. So, you are not, you are not really eligible to sit in the exam to go back to Godhead. You understand? So, therefore, you have, you have to understand the significance of formal Diksha. It involves many things. It involves your commitment to Prabhupada and Prabhupada's commitment to you to take you back to Godhead, provided you fulfill all the conditions from your side. It is a two-way contract, eternally binding you to Prabhupada. So, remain determined to follow Prabhupada's warning, Prabhupada's instructions, Prabhupada's orders and that is your life and soul from this point onwards and Prabhupada is your whole, you know, is your soul Diksha Guru, life after life and you will definitely progress on the path back to Godhead. You don't have to worry about going back to Godhead, whether I will go back to Godhead or not. None of those considerations have to be there. Simply, right now, from now on, your only thinking and duty is, how to execute the orders of Srila Prabhupada. That's all. How to purify you, when to take you back to Godhead, how many lifetimes after which to take you back to Godhead, will I take three lifetimes like Jaya and Vijaya or will I take two lifetimes like Bharat Maharaj or will I go back within six months like Dhruva Maharaj, you don't have to consider all that. Your own, no matter, even if I have to take one thousand lifetimes or one million lifetimes again and again, I have to take birth within the material world, no problem. Even in every single of those million lifetimes also, I'll keep following Prabhupada's instructions, my Guru Maharaj's instructions. This should be your determination. This is the, this is what really makes you a disciple and this is what pleases Prabhupada as your Diksha Guru. This attitude of a devotee, unquestioning loyalty, this unquestioning paraabhakti towards Guru, is what pleases Guru. And if Prabhupada, your Diksha Guru, is pleased with this, yasya prasadad bhagavad prasadad, then Krishna will be pleased with you. And if Krishna is pleased with you, your going back to Godhead is not an issue at all. Comfortably, without your putting any endeavor, you'll go back to Godhead. So please keep all these things in mind and firmly remain determined in following the Prabhupada's instructions, His orders, and you will, you'll remain firmly situated as a true disciple of Prabhupada and you will be able to access the true meaning of Diksha and you'll find yourself going back to Godhead very quickly. So, Vishwaguru Sheela Prabhupada ki jai, Shishi Gauranitha ki jai, Shishi Krishna Rukmini Satyabhama ki jai, Netai Gaur, Premanande Hari Haribol.

### Srimad Bhagavatam 3.17.5 | HG Suvyakta Narsimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

#### omens, demons, material nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=389
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "When the breeze was blowing, it was very uninviting to the touch."
**Summary:** This segment discusses the omens that manifested during the descent of Jaya and Vijaya as Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu, focusing on the unpleasant changes in the natural elements, particularly the wind.

So here in this chapter, we are seeing the descent of Jaya and Vijaya into this material world, and when they descended into the material world, they descended as enemies of Vishnu. So when such enemies of Vishnu or demons take their birth, what happens? What kind of portents one can see in this world? What kind of symptoms you can see? Those are being described here. There are many omens that were manifest when Jaya and Vijaya came down as Hiranyasha and Hiranyakashipu. So it is described here, Vavavvayu. Vayu, the air. A breeze which blows. Winds which blow. Normally in Chennai what happens, it's very humid. So when we stand outside in the tree shade, there is a breeze that blows. It feels very pleasing. It feels very nice, cooling, comforting. But the kind of breezes that started blowing when Hiranyakashipu and Hiranyakashipu appeared, they were Suddhusvarsha and they were Phutkaaraan Eerein. When the wind used to blow, when Hiranyakashipu and Hiranyakashipu were going to take birth, the breeze used to give a hissing sound. And just like you would see in if any of you before joining you have seen these horror movies, sometimes in the night they will show one window is closing, one door is closing and there is a kind of sound that comes which is very fearful. Those kind of sounds are not pleasing to the ear. And Suddhusvarsha, to the touch also, the breeze was not pleasing. So, especially when you are in Chennai, you don't have experience of breeze which is unpleasant. Isn't it? Any time breeze blows, it's welcome. But when Karthik Maas comes, when Chaitanya Maas comes, that time, the breeze is very pleasing. So, but then, when Hiranyaksha and Hiranyaka Kshapu were taking birth, the entire climate itself became so bad, that the breeze became distrustful, became uninviting to the touch.

#### omens, Krishna's appearance, pure environment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=878
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "A pure breeze began to blow, pleasing the sense of touch, and bearing the aroma of flowers."
**Summary:** This segment contrasts the omens at the time of Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu's birth with those at the time of Krishna's appearance, highlighting the pure and pleasing environment that manifested when Krishna was about to appear.

So, it is described here, when the breeze was blowing, it was very uninviting to the touch. That's how it is described when Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu were going to take birth. Because they were great demons. They were enemies of Vishnu. Now, on the other hand, in the tenth canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam, there is a description of what was the prevailing situation when Krishna was going to appear. So, the same words, Vavavvayuhu is used even when Krishna appeared. But when Krishna appeared, I'll just read that verse. When Krishna appeared, the Srimad Bhagavatam, it says, Vavavvayuhu suhasparsha. You see, when Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakashipu came, Vavavvayuhu sudhasparsha. And when Krishna was going to appear, Vavavvayuhu suhasparsha. And then, Punyagandhavahasuchihi, Agnayastha, Dvijatina, Chandastatra samindhata. So, what it means is, I'll read the translation. A pure breeze began to blow, pleasing the sense of touch, and bearing the aroma of flowers. Can you imagine that? It was bearing the aroma of flowers. And when the brahmanas, engaging in ritualistic ceremonies, ignited their fires, according to Vedic principles, the fires burned steadily.

#### Vedic civilization, sense gratification, exploitation

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers

**Summary:** This segment contrasts the Vedic civilization's focus on spiritual advancement with the modern world's emphasis on exploiting material nature for sense gratification, leading to ecological imbalance and natural disasters.

Actually, human society is supposed to live in this manner, in harmony with material nature. And in the Vedic times, the whole civilization was, the consciousness of the civilization was how to utilize this human form of life to advance in spiritual life. So, whereas in the current day world, the whole consciousness of the civilization is how to exploit material nature and maximize my sense gratification. So, material nature, if we live according to the laws of material nature, there is ample opportunity for sense gratification, but not for unlimited, unrestricted sense enjoyment. In a limited, restricted way, in the manner it is mentioned in the Vedas, sense enjoyment is there, not that it is not there. But then, as it is said, the earth produces enough to satisfy the needs of a man, but not the greed of a man. Now, the whole civilization, human civilization has become so greedy for sense gratification that all the laws of material nature are discarded. And we try to exploit and exploit and exploit material nature because of what happens? Material nature also starts reacting.

#### sinful activities, reactions, Kali Yuga
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=1134
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "We are accountable for our actions. Whatever I do, there will be some reaction involved."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the accountability for actions, both individually and as a civilization, explaining that sinful activities lead to negative reactions from material nature, making the world an unbearable place to live, especially in Kali Yuga.

So, as Prabhupada is mentioning, the demoniac people who are accustomed to all kinds of forbidden sinful activities, they will create such a situation that the material world becomes an unbearable place to live in. So, we have to understand that we are accountable for our actions. Whatever I do, there will be some reaction involved. If the civilization is engaged in sinful activities, then material nature will give suitable reaction. We will not be able to enjoy. We will not be able to enjoy very happily. Just like if there is a criminal, and if he thinks, I will engage in all sorts of anti-social activities, and I will steal, I will rob people, and with that money, I will enjoy. It is not possible. Why? Because the police department will start running after him. And they will not allow him to live peacefully. They will go behind him until he is behind the bars. So, in a similar way, if the whole civilization is trying to go against the orders, against the order of material nature, which has been given by Krishna or Vishnu, then what will happen? Then we will not be allowed to live peacefully. We will have to. There will be various reactions that will come. We may increase our comforts, our conveniences. We may invent many, many material, scientific inventions we may create, to give us more comforts, to give us more sense gratification. But what will happen? Material nature will make the situation worse and worse. Even though our conveniences, material conveniences increase, what will happen? Material nature will also keep increasing the pressure on us, by which we will not be able to happily enjoy those conveniences. Some pandemic will come, some cyclone will come, some flood will come, some tsunami will come. Actually, if you see, the amount of sinful activities in this material world is growing. That's how Kali Yuga is. In Kali Yuga, the sinful activities of the people keep on increasing.

#### sinful activities, human life, material nature
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=1346
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The human life is meant for a higher purpose."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes that sinful activities at individual and civilizational levels lead to a lack of peace, highlighting that human life is meant for a higher purpose, and aligning with that purpose allows material nature to support our progress, while opposing it leads to vehement opposition from nature.

So, we have to understand, that, at the civilizational level, whether at the global level, or at the national level, or at the individual level, even an individual, if he engages in sinful activities, he cannot expect to remain happy with the results of those sinful actions. So, this is, actually, this is the real fact, which people become blind to, people are blind to, they are not able to appreciate, they are not able to see this. That if I commit sinful activities, they will have to suffer. And, if as a civilization, we engage in sinful activities, thinking that we will make all the arrangements for sinful activities, and we will manage the show, no. Whether at the individual level, or at the civilizational level, it is incumbent upon us, to live our life, free of sinful activities. Otherwise, we are bound to be in a situation where, we will not be peaceful. So, what we have to understand is, that, the human life, is meant to, meant for a higher purpose. And, that higher purpose, is achievable only in the human form of life. And, if we live our life, according to that goal, then, what happens is, the whole material world, the material nature, will align itself, to our activities, it will, it will, it will only, enhance our efforts, progress in that direction. It will not oppose us. When we engage in sinful activities, then material nature will oppose. It will oppose very vehemently. Whereas, if we engage in, achieving the ultimate goal of life, as described in the Vedas, then what happens, material nature will become very favorable, and it will help us, to progress in that direction. It will not oppose us.

#### goal of life, materialism, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=1473
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "Ultimate goal of life, in today's world is, money, money, money, sweeter than honey."
**Summary:** This segment laments the modern world's ignorance of the ultimate goal of life, reducing it to materialism and fleeting pleasures, and introduces the conversation between Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Ramananda Raya about the true goal of life.

So, what is that ultimate goal of life? Unfortunately, today, in the modern day world, people don't know, what is the ultimate goal of life. Ultimate goal of life, in today's world is, money, money, money, sweeter than honey. Unfortunately, that's our, eat, drink, and be merry. You can eat and drink, but you cannot be merry. If you drink also, next day morning, when you get up, you will have such a headache. Where is merry? All gone. And when you are fully drunk, you don't know, what you are talking, what you are doing, nothing you will remember. Where is merry? So, you may say, eat, drink, and be merry, but actually, you cannot be merry. So, what is the ultimate goal of life? Actually, when Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, met Ramananda Raya, on the banks of the Godavari, so, he said, Krishna Balram, so, when Chaitanya Mahaprabhu met, Ramananda Raya, on the banks of Godavari, Ramananda Raya, Ramananda Raya, was the governor, of Vidyanagara. Vidyanagara, at that time, is today's, I think, this one, Vishakaparnam. Am I right? So, there, he happened to, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, happened to meet, Ramananda Raya. So, Ramananda Raya, because he was the governor, naturally, governor will not come, you know, alone to the Godavari, people used to go to the river, to take bath, and all that. So, Ramananda Raya, came there. And, naturally, he was, being carried in a, palanquin, and there were lot of Brahmanas, and Vedic mantras, and there were all his assistants, and all those kind of people, who had come, along with him. And so, then, there, he saw Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, had also come there, and both of them, met there. And then, but then, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, did not want to discuss, too much with him, surrounding him, and he had come, in a very official manner. So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, invited, invited Ramananda Raya, you come to, I am staying in one Brahmana's house, here. So, you come and meet me, in the evening. So, Ramananda Raya, in the evening, came and met Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, all alone. Just one assistant, he brought with him, and he came all alone, and met Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. So, when Chaitanya, so, actually, who was this Ramananda Raya? Ramananda Raya, is, the niece, of, of Krishna. So, Lalitha and Vishakha, are the, are the, are the, after Radharani, Lalitha and Vishakha, are the next Gopis. So, Vishakha Gopi, had incarnated as, Ramananda Raya. So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, asked Ramananda Raya, what is the ultimate goal, of life? It's a very important question, because, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu knows, in the earlier, Vedic civilization, everybody knew, goal of life means, we have to attain Mukti, we have to go back, to Godhead, that used to be, a common knowledge, but in Kali Yuga, nobody knows, what is the ultimate goal, of life. So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu asked, Ramananda Raya, can you please tell me, what is the goal of life? It is not that, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu knows, but he wanted, an answer from, Ramananda Raya, for the benefit of, the general mass of people. So, Ramananda Raya, replied, that, Varna Ashramacharavata, Purushena Parapuman, Vishnu Aradhyate, Nanya Tattoshkaranam. He said, following, the Varna Ashrama system, is the way to, satisfy, Lord Vishnu, and satisfying, Lord Vishnu, is the, highest goal of life. So, if we follow, Varna Ashrama Dharma, then, we are, actually, we will be, achieving, the highest goal, of life.

---
#### surrender, bhakti, goal of life
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=1683
**Verse References:** BG 18.66
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Give up all other kinds of Dharma, that means, this Varna Ashrama Dharma, and all these things, you give it up, and surrender unto Me."
**Summary:** This segment continues the conversation between Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Ramananda Raya, where Ramananda Raya initially suggests following Varna Ashrama Dharma as the highest goal, but Chaitanya Mahaprabhu rejects it, prompting Ramananda Raya to quote Bhagavad Gita 18.66 about surrendering to Krishna.

So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, immediately said, Eho Bahiya, Aage Kaho. He said, this is external, I reject this. Why? Because, Varna Ashrama Dharma, when you are, following, all the duties, of Varna Ashrama Dharma, are, to engage one, in material activities. The, duties, of a Brahma, of a Kshatriya, of a Vaishya, of a Shudra, of a Brahmachari, a Grihasta, a Vanaprastha, Sanyasi, these are all material. So, this is not the highest thing. Aage Kaho, you know, tell something better. So, immediately, Ramananda Raya replied, that, to give up, all these duties, to renounce, the highest goal of life. So, immediately, Ramananda Raya quoted, the, famous words, from the, 66th verse, of the 18th chapter, of the Bhagavad Gita. Krishna advised Arjuna, give up, all other kinds of Dharma, that means, this Varna Ashrama Dharma, and all these things, you give it up, and surrender unto Me. So, this is the, actually, this is the ultimate instruction, of the Bhagavad Gita. So, Ramananda Raya said, this is the ultimate, goal of life. So, what did, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu reply? Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said, Aage Kaho, Bahiya. This is external, rejected. Aage Kaho, tell something better.

---
#### Jnana Misra Bhakti, glorifying Krishna, association of devotees
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=1780
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "To hear Krishna's glories, being described by devotees, that is the highest goal."
**Summary:** This segment continues the dialogue, with Chaitanya Mahaprabhu rejecting the idea of simply renouncing material duties, emphasizing the need for positive engagement. Ramananda Raya then suggests Jnana Misra Bhakti, but Chaitanya Mahaprabhu rejects that too, leading Ramananda Raya to quote Lord Brahma about the importance of hearing Krishna's glories in the association of devotees.

So, Ramananda Raya then replied, with another verse, from the Bhagavad Gita, Brahma Bhuta Prasannatma, Na Sochati Na Kaanshati, Sama Sarveshu Bhuteshu, Madh Bhakti Labhate Paran. So, why, why did Chaitanya Mahaprabhu reject? Because, simply renouncing the material duties, is not going to help one. It is not going to, it is not going to really, there is no positive engagement, you are just renouncing. Simply by renouncing, what is the use? There has to be some positive engagement. So, then, he said, Ramananda Raya replied that, Jnana Misra Bhakti is better, than, Karma Misra Bhakti, or, renouncing completely one's duties. So, he said, better than both of these is, Jnana Misra Bhakti, because on the platform of Jnana, you know, there is, you become qualified to, execute Bhakti. So, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, for this also, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said, Eho Bahiya, this is external, rejected, Aage Kaho, tell something better. So, then, Ramananda Raya, quoted a verse from, 10th canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam, which was spoken by, Lord Brahma. Jnane Prayasam, Udapasi Namantayeva, Sanmukharitam, Bhavadiyava Vartam. What is that, after that? Sthane Sthitah, Tanuang Nobher, Prayaso Ajitajitopya, Sitayis Trilokyam. So, Brahma Ji said, Jnane Prayasam, to engage in the process of Jnana, is, Prayasam, it is simply hard labor, there is no, result that comes out of it. No, real spiritual benefit that comes out of, undertaking the path of Jnana Yoga. Udapasi, so reject the process of Jnana Yoga. Jnane Prayasam, Udapasi, Namantayeva, so, to surrender to Krishna, by rejecting Jnana Yoga, and, Sanmukharitam, Bhavadiyava Vartam, to constantly hear your glories, submitting his prayers to Lord Krishna. So, to hear Krishna's glories, being described by devotees, that is the highest goal. And, by doing this, what happens is, Thane Sita, wherever you are situated, you may be a Brahmana, you may be a Kshatriya, you may be a Vaishya, you may be a, you may be, you may be a Sanyasi, you may be a Grihasta, you may be Brahmachari, Manaprasad, whatever you are, whichever situation you are, engage in glorifying Krishna, in the association of devotees, and by this, what happens is, Krishna is Ajitha, Ajitha Jithopiasi, Krishna is Ajitha, Ajitha means, nobody can conquer Krishna, that unconquerable Krishna, becomes Jitha, Ajitha Jithopiasi, he becomes conquered, simply by, glorifying Krishna, in the association of devotees. So, when Chaitanya Mahaprabhu heard this, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu said, Eho Yaha, now what you are saying makes sense.

---
#### Kali Yuga, association of devotees, ISKCON
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=1977
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In Kali Yuga, the process of spiritual emancipation is to glorify Krishna, in the association of other devotees."
**Summary:** This segment emphasizes the power of glorifying Krishna in the association of devotees, especially in Kali Yuga, highlighting how the Srimad Bhagavatam reiterates this principle and how Prabhupada established ISKCON to facilitate this practice.

Why? Because, now there is some activity, spiritual activity. What is that? To glorify Krishna. So, what happens is, this is a very powerful process, especially in Kali Yuga. What is that? That, we engage in glorifying Krishna, Satamprasangat, in association of other devotees. Satamprasangat means, in the association of pure devotees, and in a group, as a group, we engage in glorifying Krishna. So, this process, is very, very powerful. So, that is why, the Bhagavatam, actually if you see, the Bhagavatam, is, in many, many places, you will find, from the beginning till the end, the Bhagavatam is pushing this, principle, that is, is exposing this principle, is again and again, reiterating this principle, that, in Kali Yuga, the process, the process of spiritual emancipation is, to glorify Krishna, in the association of other devotees. In the earlier ages, we could perform spiritual activities, all alone. Bharat Maharaj, he gave up his whole kingdom, and he went to the forest, and practiced Bhakti, all alone. And he reached the stage of Bhavabhakti. And later on, of course, he again took the birth of Jadabharata, and continued. He did not join, and, you know, in a group, and became a Bhakta, and started getting trained, and all that. You know, he went to the forest, and he practiced. Dhruva Maharaj, at the age of 5 years, he went to the forest, all alone, and practiced. And within 6 months, he achieved perfection. And, we all in Kali Yuga, you know, we come and join, he is gone 24, 26 years old, and 20 years will pass, but still, we will, you know, we will be in, you know, we will never, you know, what he achieved. But then, Dhruva Maharaj did it all alone. He went to Dhrotila, in Vrindavan, and he practiced. In 6 months, he had Darshan of Vishnu. You take the case of Narada Muni, Narada Muni also, for a few months, during Chaturma, for 4 months, he served all the pure devotees. After that, he went to the forest, and practiced on his own, all alone. Whereas, in Kali Yuga, if you try to do that, all these great personalities, they went to the forest, executed Bhakti, and overcame Maya. If in Kali Yuga, we try to do, first of all, you will not find a forest. Even if you find a forest, you go there, he will not overcome Maya, Maya will overcome us. So, it is not possible in Kali Yuga, to practice Krishna Consciousness, all alone. So, in the olden ages, it used to be possible. That's why, in the olden age, there was no ISKCON, there was no organization. It's not that, Panacharya had to create an organization, and everybody would come and practice. This is a very novel thing, which is happening only in Kali Yuga, which was started by Shri Krishna. And this is not unique only to our Sampradaya, Gaudiya Vaishnava Sampradaya. Even Madhavacharya had an institution. Even Ramadhyacharya started an institution. Because, in Kali Yuga, you have to have an institutional place, where devotees can all come together, and jointly they can practice. And that is how Prabhupada made a whole program, where we all get together.

---
#### Sadhana, spiritual strength, Maya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/eL2zRCTsgDM?t=2170
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "This is actually building up our spiritual strength."
**Summary:** This segment describes the ISKCON Sadhana program, emphasizing how it provides a protected environment and builds spiritual strength through collective practices like chanting, hearing Bhagavatam, and Guru Puja, making it easier to progress in spiritual life and resist the forces of Maya in Kali Yuga.

In the morning we get up, all of us come together in the temple hall, assemble together, and then we sing Samsara Dhaman Leda. And then what we do, we all sit in the temple hall, and together we chant Hare Krishna Maha Mantra. And then, together we do Guru Poja. Together we all sit and hear Bhagavatam class. When, you know, in the olden ages, where was this kind of system? Dhruva Maharaj or Bharat Maharaj, they were not sitting in the forest and, you know, gathering lot of people, and together let us, you know, have Bhagavatam class daily. No, they were not having. Okay, only the sages got together in Naimisharanya, because they were conducting a special sacrifice. So, they together all got together and they heard. Because anyway it was the beginning of Kali Yuga. So, they actually set the precedence. That in Kali Yuga, all of, all the sages got together and heard Bhagavatam. Bhagavatam is there all the time. So, only in Kali Yuga, hearing Bhagavatam, process of hearing Bhagavatam is Satamprasangat. You have to do it in the association of others. Alone, we will not be able to do it. We will not be able to sustain. And that is why, Prabhupada has created a whole Sadhana program, where we all come together, and together we hear Bhagavatam, together we chant Hare Krishna Mantra, together we do Sankirtana. And this is the process for Kali Yuga, which is very, very powerful. And simply by engaging in this process, what happens is, we gain strength from each other, and we will be able to very quickly make progress in spiritual life. Otherwise, in Kali Yuga especially, it is very, very difficult to progress in spiritual life. To be able to protect ourselves from the forces of Maya is very, very tough. It is not at all easy. Maya is very powerful, and we are very weak. In the earlier ages, what used to happen, Maya used to be less powerful, and people used to be much stronger spiritually. Now the roles have reversed. So, therefore, we have to, you know, together we have to face the force of Maya. So we stay in the same ashram, we come and practice spiritual life together. So what happens is, in the temple, temple is a very protected environment, where the force of Maya is practically not there. So, this association of devotees, we have to appreciate, and we have to value that, you know, what Prabhupada has given us in the form of this institution, in this form of, in the form of the opportunity to be able to, be able to practice the principles of the Bhagavatam, in the manner it is described in the Bhagavatam. In the association, in a group, every day, day after day, we go on practicing. This is actually building up our spiritual strength. And this is what is required for Kalyuga. So, with these few words, I will stop. If there are any questions or comments, we can discuss.

---

### The Difference Between Material and Spiritual Activity | Sri Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

#### material fatigue, spiritual satisfaction, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/2pjhe6-QyLg?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But because devotional service to Krishna is completely transcendental, therefore, we never get satiated."
**Summary:** The speaker contrasts material activities, which lead to burnout and require breaks, with devotional service to Krishna, which is transcendental and provides constant satisfaction.

Whenever somebody is engaged in some activity, that person after a little while needs a little break. Otherwise, he'll get burnt out. That's why every week when you go to office, there is a weekend. Saturday and Sunday are off. Shut off your mind from your work and relax and take out all the stress. Go out somewhere. Nowadays, people are talking that the five-day week should be reduced to a four-day or a three-day week. Every alternate day, they should have holiday. That will increase the productivity of the people. And once in a year, some long holiday also is required. Go on vacation. In the case of a devotee, what happens is, Anisham constantly is engaged only in thinking of Krishna, speaking of Krishna, rendering service to Krishna. Nataraptaha, he never gets satiated. He feels whatever I'm doing is not enough. I want to do more. I want to do more. Even 24 hours in a day is not enough. I want more time. I want to do more. This is the characteristic of devotional service because it is not material. Anything material becomes hackneyed. You get fed up with it after a little time. But because devotional service to Krishna is completely transcendental, therefore, we never get satiated.
