### What Is The Role Of Diksa Guru In ISKCON? | His Grace Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

#### ISKCON history, Guru system, Zonal Acharyas
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In 1978, after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance, the GBC of Vizcon decided to implement an unauthorized guru system by elevating 11 devotees as zonal acharyas and diksha gurus, declaring that they had overnight become pure devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the topic by discussing the implementation of an unauthorized guru system in ISKCON after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance in 1978, and the subsequent failures and changes made by the GBC.

Hare Krishna! Vishwaguru Srila Prabhupada ki Jai! In 1978, after Srila Prabhupada's disappearance, the GBC of Vizcon decided to implement an unauthorized guru system by elevating 11 devotees as zonal acharyas and diksha gurus, declaring that they had overnight become pure devotees. Over the years, this unauthorized guru system has failed the devotees, with almost 75 gurus falling from grace till date.

---
#### GBC Resolutions, Guru Puja, Srila Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=39
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "It is considered an essential devotional practice for all ISKCON devotees to observe or perform Srila Prabhupada's guru puja daily, either in an ISKCON temple or, if not possible, in one's home."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses GBC Resolution No. 405, passed in 1999, which mandates daily guru puja for Srila Prabhupada for all ISKCON devotees, regardless of their diksha guru.

In reaction to the constant guru fall-downs, the GBC has been chopping and changing the unauthorized guru system as well as their understanding of the functions, qualifications and characteristics. In the year 1999, the GBC passed Resolution No. 405, titled Standards of Guru Worship in ISKCON, and the GBC made it into a law in ISKCON. It has become an ISKCON law. As part of this ISKCON law, the GBC resolved, under subsection C of this Resolution No. 405, in order to offer appropriate respect to Srila Prabhupada as founder acharya and pre-eminent spiritual master of ISKCON. It is considered an essential devotional practice for all ISKCON devotees to observe or perform Srila Prabhupada's guru puja daily, either in an ISKCON temple or, if not possible, in one's home.

---
#### Guru Puja, Shiksha Guru, Diksha Guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=130
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "With an aim to focus more fully on Srila Prabhupada, and every devotee's special relationship with him and to correct any imbalance in application between the respect offered to shiksha gurus, diksha gurus or other senior Vaisnavas contributing to a devotee's spiritual progress, an ISKCON shiksha usingacao or diksha guru may public Guru Puja, Aarati and or foot bathing in person once a year on ISKCON property as a Vyasa Puja."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that while daily guru puja is mandated for Srila Prabhupada, guru puja for diksha gurus is optional and limited to once a year. The GBC also instructs that all disciples should consider Srila Prabhupada as their pre-eminent Shiksha Guru.

While the GBC mandated daily guru puja for Srila Prabhupada, in subsection D of the same resolution of the same ISKCON law, the GBC resolved this about their unauthorized diksha guru puja. the GBC resolved this about their unauthorized diksha guru puja. With an aim to focus more fully on Srila Prabhupada, and every devotee's special relationship with him and to correct any imbalance in application between the respect offered to shiksha gurus, diksha gurus or other senior Vaisnavas contributing to a devotee's spiritual progress, an ISKCON shiksha usingacao or diksha guru may public Guru Puja, Aarati and or foot bathing in person once a year on ISKCON property as a Vyasa Puja. So, the disciples of the unauthorized Gurus may offer, may offer means it's not mandatory, it is optional. The disciples may offer Guru Puja to their Gurus in person only once a year in ISKCON. Further, in subsection J, the GBC law states, at the time of diksha initiation, all disciples will be further instructed by their initiating guru that Shaila Prabhupad is their pre-eminent Siksha Guru as ISKCON's founder Acharya, ever present in His books and teachings. Thus, they have a recognized Siksha Guru.

#### Shiksha Guru, Diksha Guru, Guru Vandana

**Verse References:** —

**Summary:** The speaker analyzes the Sri Guru Vandana song, which is sung daily for Srila Prabhupada, and argues that the song's content implies that Srila Prabhupada is acting as the Diksha Guru for all devotees, despite the GBC's designation of him as a pre-eminent Shiksha Guru.

So, the disciples of the Unauthorized Gurus may offer Guru Puja to their Gurus in person link to Shaila Prabhupada. That means, in ISKCON, every devotee is supposed to consider Shaila Prabhupada as his pre-eminent Shiksha Guru, except, of course, for those initiated before 1978. So, for their pre-eminent Shiksha Guru, Shaila Prabhupada, the devotees during the Guru Puja, sing the Shri Guru Vandana song penned by Shri Launarothamadas Thakur daily, every day, mandatorily. In this song, that every devotee in ISKCON must compulsorily sing for Shaila Prabhupada every single day of his life, it begins with Shri Guru Charanapadma Kevala Bhakati Sadma, meaning, Shaila Prabhupada's lotus feet are the shelter of Shri Guru. In the Chaitanya Charitamrita, Madhya Leela, Chapter 15, verse 108 purport, Shaila Prabhupada quotes Shri Launarothamadas Thakur from his Bhakti Sandarbha, where Shri Launarothamadas Thakur says, Deeksha Vidhanena Shri Guru Charana Sampaditam. Shri Launarothamadas Thakur translates this as, Receiving the Grace of a bona fide spiritual master through initiation. That means, receiving the mercy of Guru or Shri Guru Charanapadma happens through initiation. In other words, Shri Guru Charanapadma in the Guru Vandana refers to the Deeksha Guru, not just to some preeminent Deeksha Guru. And then, the devotees, have to sing, Vandohmui Savadahana Mate, meaning, I bow down to the lotus feet of Shaila Prabhupada with great awe and reverence. So, every devotee in ISKCON has to take shelter of the lotus feet of Shaila Prabhupada. What is the meaning of taking shelter of the lotus feet of Shaila Prabhupada? We will come to that a bit later. towards the end of the Guru Vandana. Please bear with us till then.

#### Guru Vandana, Diksha Guru, Material Existence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=374
**Verse References:** CC Madhya 9.61, NOI 7
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So clearly, it is the responsibility of the Diksha Guru to deliver the disciple from the material existence."
**Summary:** The speaker continues analyzing the Sri Guru Vandana, focusing on the lines about crossing over material existence by Srila Prabhupada's grace. He questions why devotees are mandated to sing about Srila Prabhupada delivering them from material existence, when scriptures state that it is the Diksha Guru's responsibility.

For now, let us go on to the next line. And what is that next line? Meaning, by Srila Prabhupada's grace, because we are singing this song for Prabhupada, this is an offering to Prabhupada, so by Srila Prabhupada's grace, we can cross over the material existence and we can attain Krishna. That means, every devotee in ISKCON must sing every single day of his life that he can cross over the material existence and attain Krishna by Srila Prabhupada's grace. Now, the question arises that who takes the responsibility to deliver the physical life of the human being? Of course, the one who takes the responsibility to deliver the devotee across the material existence to the lotus feet of Krishna? Is it the responsibility of the Shiksha Guru or is it the responsibility of the Diksha Guru? Let us see what Srila Prabhupada says in the Chaitanya Charitamrita, Madhya Leela, Chapter 9, verse 61, purport. There, Srila Prabhupada says, it is the spiritual master who delivers the disciple from the clutches of Maya. How? By initiating him into the chanting of the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra. So clearly, it is the responsibility of the Diksha Guru to deliver the disciple from the material existence. In the Nectar of Instruction, purport to verse 7, Srila Prabhupada says, if one is sincere, he is initiated and this stage is called Bhajanak Kriya. One then actually engages in the service of the Lord by regularly chanting the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra 16 rounds daily and refraining from illicit sex, intoxicants, meat-eating and gambling. By Bhajanak Kriya, one attains freedom from the contamination of materialistic life. So if the Diksha Guru delivers one from material contamination, why are the devotees in Eskan mandated to sing every day that it is Srila Prabhupada who delivers them from material existence? Instead of their Diksha Guru, they can sing about the Diksha Guru only once a day in a year. Whereas, they are singing every day to Srila Prabhupada 365 days a year. That it is Srila Prabhupada who delivers them from material existence. It is Prabhupada who delivers them from there, from the material existence. So how is it that the Shiksha Guru is delivering the disciple from the clutches of material existence whereas the Shiksha Guru is not doing it? While you ponder over that point, the whole situation becomes more complicated when the devotee sings the next line, Jai Prasad-e-Pure Sarva Asha, meaning that by Srila Prabhupada's song, all our desires for spiritual perfection will be fulfilled.

---

As if that was not enough, the devotees have to further sing to Srila Prabhupada. Srila Prabhupada says, Krishnadekar, Krishnadekar, Krishnadekar, Krishnadekar. Guru Karunasindhu that Srila Prabhupada is the ocean of mercy. Again, in the Sri Chaitanya Charitamrita, Antalila, chapter 7, verse 12 purport, Srila Prabhupada says, the true Acharya, the spiritual master of the entire world, must be considered an incarnation of Krishna's mercy. Srila Prabhupada explains in Srimad Bhagavatam, canto 4, chapter 21, verse 31 purport, as Krishna takes away all the sinful reactions of a person, immediately, upon his surrender unto him. Similarly, the external manifestation of Krishna, the representative of Krishna, who acts as the mercy of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, takes all the resultant actions of the sinful life of the disciple, immediately after the disciple's initiation. So, it is the spiritual master who gives initiation to the disciple. So, it is the spiritual master who gives initiation to the disciple. The Dikshaguru, who is the mercy incarnation of Krishna. So, why has the GBC mandated to all the devotees in ISKCON to glorify Srila Prabhupada daily, that Srila Prabhupada is the ocean of mercy, the Karuna Sindhu, meaning the manifestation of mercy of Krishna, or in other words, the Dikshaguru, that Srila Prabhupada is the Dikshaguru, and not one of the unauthorized Gurus, indicated no objection by the GBC.

#### Shelter, Initiation, Diksha Guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=897
**Verse References:** SB 4.29.55
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So, taking shelter of the lotus feet of Srila Prabhupada, haha means to take initiation from Srila Prabhupada. So that means Srila Prabhupada is acting as a diksha guru for all the devotees in ISKCON by giving them shelter at his lotus feet."
**Summary:** The speaker analyzes the final lines of the Sri Guru Vandana, where devotees seek shelter at the lotus feet of Srila Prabhupada. He argues that taking shelter means taking initiation, implying that Srila Prabhupada is acting as the Diksha Guru for all ISKCON devotees.

So, if this much wasn't enough, next comes the killer punch. Going further, the devotees have to sing, Haha Prabhu Karo Daya Deho More Paadachchaya, meaning, Srila Prabhupada, our spiritual master, please give us the shelter of your lotus feet. Haha Prabhu Karo Daya Deho More Paadachchaya, meaning, Srila Prabhupada, our spiritual master, please give us the shelter of your lotus feet. Like the Sri Guru Vandana began with Vandomui Savadhana Mate, I bow down at your lotus feet with great reverence. Similarly, once more, the prayer here is seeking shelter at the lotus feet of Srila Prabhupada. So, we had told you at the beginning that let us wait towards the end of this Sri Guru Vandana to understand what is this seeking the shelter of the lotus feet mean? In the Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 4, Chapter 29, Verse 55, purport, Srila Prabhupada explains this. Any person who is seriously desirous of achieving real happiness must seek out a bona fide spiritual master and take shelter of him by initiation. Not only that, in the Nectar of Devotion, Chapter 6, Srila Prabhupada says, all together, Srila Rupa Goswami mentions twenty items and all of them are very important. Out of the twenty, the first three, namely accepting the shelter of a bona fide spiritual master, taking initiation from him and serving him with respect and reverence are the most important. So, taking shelter of the lotus feet of Srila Prabhupada, haha means to take initiation from Srila Prabhupada. So that means Srila Prabhupada is acting as a diksha guru for all the devotees in ISKCON by giving them shelter at his lotus feet.

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=1027
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If Srila Prabhupada is just a shiksha guru for your group, then why have you made it mandatory for all the devotees to sing Sri Guru Vandana prayers for Srila Prabhupada? Which means, line after line, they have to pray to Srila Prabhupada as their diksha guru."
**Summary:** The speaker questions the GBC's designation of Srila Prabhupada as a 'pre-eminent shiksha guru,' a term he couldn't find in Srila Prabhupada's writings. He asks why the GBC mandates the Sri Guru Vandana for Srila Prabhupada, which implies he is the Diksha Guru, while forbidding daily singing of the same for their so-called diksha gurus.

On the one hand, ISKCON Mumbai Group's GBC has declared that Srila Prabhupada is only a pre-eminent siksha guru. Although, Krishna alone knows what that means because the GBC has not defined what the word quote-unquote pre-eminent siksha guru means. I personally have tried searching for this term pre-eminent siksha guru in the Veda base. But honestly, I couldn't find even one reference where Srila Prabhupada uses this term pre-eminent siksha guru. In case you happen to come across any instance of Srila Prabhupada using using the term preeminent shiksha guru, please do share with us and we will be grateful to you for that. Until then, some questions for the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai and its unauthorized gurus and their supporters. If Srila Prabhupada is just a shiksha guru, preeminent or whatever term you want to call it, if Srila Prabhupada is just a shiksha guru for your group, then why have you made it mandatory for all the devotees to sing Sri Guru Vandana prayers for Srila Prabhupada? Which means, line after line, they have to pray to Srila Prabhupada as their diksha guru. And the GBC has said, every day the devotees have to do this. Why has the GBC forbidden the devotees in ISKCON from singing Sri Guru Vandana for their so-called diksha guru's daily and instead the GBC has told them to direct it to Srila Prabhupada?

#### Diksha Guru, Functions, Characteristics
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=1136
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So actually, by the GBC's own rules and laws, Srila Prabhupada is acting as the diksha guru for all the devotees in ISKCON. And they are affirming that by singing Sri Guru Vandana for Srila Prabhupada day in and day out."
**Summary:** The speaker outlines the functions and characteristics of a Diksha Guru and argues that the GBC's own rules and laws, by mandating the Sri Guru Vandana for Srila Prabhupada, affirm that Srila Prabhupada is acting as the Diksha Guru for all ISKCON devotees. He questions the role and functions of the unauthorized diksha gurus.

Isn't this incongruous? Why this confusion? Actually, if we see the functions and characteristics of a diksha guru, what are they? The functions and characteristics of a diksha guru are, number one, he is a shiksha guru. Number two, he is a shiksha guru. Number three, he is a pure devotee. Number two, to give shelter to the disciple by initiating him or her. Number three, to remove all the sinful reactions of the disciple, kuryatpapasesankshayam. Number four, to reveal transcendental knowledge within the heart of the disciple, divyam jnanam yathodadyat. And then, number five, to be the spiritual master for the disciple, life after life, eternally. And number six, to be the spiritual master for the disciple, life after life, eternally. And number six, to be the spiritual master for the disciple, to act as the manifestation of the mercy of Krishna. All these are functions of the diksha guru. And what does the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai group do? They have mandated that all the initiated devotees in ISKCON, irrespective of who their diksha guru is, they should sing daily to Srila Prabhupada all these aspects, which are meant actually for the diksha guru. And simultaneously, they have prohibited the disciples from singing the same thing to their so-called diksha gurus, except for one day in a year. And that too, optionally, not mandated. So actually, by the GBC's own rules and laws, Srila Prabhupada is acting as the diksha guru for all the devotees in ISKCON. And they are affirming that by singing Sri Guru Vandana for Srila Prabhupada day in and day out. Well, if Srila Prabhupada is performing the diksha guru for all the devotees in ISKCON, they are performing the all these functions of a diksha guru. Then, what are the unauthorized diksha gurus doing in ISKCON? What are they doing? What are their functions towards their disciples? What are their characteristics? Do they really do anything for their disciples at all? Or do they simply give a mala and a name in a ceremony and that's it? But that is what a Rithvik does, not what a diksha guru does. So, do the unauthorized gurus perform all these functions of a diksha guru?

---

**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=1272

Well, the only thing that the GBC has ever said about its unauthorized gurus is in their resolution 409 of 2004, where they have said, When the GBC allows a devotee to take up the service of initiating, it does not thereby endorse him as an uttamaadhikari or pure devotee or certify his having achieved any specific state of realization. The GBC simply said in one line that none of the diksha gurus in ISKCON have any of the characteristics or perform the functions of a diksha guru. Whereas, All of those functions and characteristics are attributed to Srila Prabhupada by the GBC by asking all devotees to mandatorily, compulsorily sing Sri Guru Vandana to Srila Prabhupada daily without fail. Irrespective of where they are, whether at temple or at home, you have to sing daily. And the GBC further, in resolution 409 of 2004, has informed all the devotees, saying that, Therefore, each prospective disciple, before initiation, should become familiar with the qualifications mentioned in Srila Prabhupada's books as those that a spiritual master ought to have, and decide for himself which senior devotee, if any, to approach for initiation. Within this context, the choice of whom to select as one's spiritual master is ultimately not that of the GBC. The GBC has also surfaced since the dawn of the GBC's failure to support the practice of possibility within the GBC. All devotees, including those who have not yet ascended into GBC, are gaining the knowledge of GBC and the GBC'sThat they have not yet attained connections to GBC. res пригilLEY in particular. #### Diksha Guru, Comparison, Srila Prabhupada
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/vmSxJKkqMyA?t=1391
**Verse References:** SB 1.1.15
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "An intelligent devotee can only conclude that it is Srila Prabhupada alone in ISKCON who is actually the Diksha Guru."
**Summary:** The speaker urges devotees to compare the GBC's statements with the position of Srila Prabhupada as portrayed in the Sri Guru Vandana. He presents a comparison table highlighting how Srila Prabhupada fulfills the functions and characteristics of a Diksha Guru, while the unauthorized gurus, according to the GBC, do not.

So, every devotee in the ISKCON Mumbai group, it is your responsibility to determine whether the Diksha Guru in ISKCON is qualified to be a Diksha Guru or not. And well, if you, the devotees in ISKCON Mumbai group were to compare these two statements of the GBC with the position of Srila Prabhupada through the GBC authorized and mandated Sri Guru Vandana, then an intelligent devotee can only conclude that it is Srila Prabhupada alone in ISKCON who is actually the Diksha Guru. Just take a look at this comparison table. Here we have in the first column, the functions and characteristics of Diksha Guru. So, the first point is that Srila Jeevagaswami in Bhakti Rasamrita Sindhu says, Diksha Vidhanena Shri Guru Charana Sampaditam, receiving the grace of a bona fide spiritual Master through initiation. And in the Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 1, Chapter 1, verse 15 purport, Srila Prabhupada says, anyone therefore who takes shelter of the lotus feet of a pure devotee by accepting the pure devotee as his spiritual Master can be at once purified. So, in the Shri Guru Vandana that the devotees have to sing to Prabhupada, what do we sing? Shri Guru Vandana. Shri Guru Vandana. Shri Guru Vandana. Shri Guru Vandana. Shri Guru Vandana. Shri Guru Vandana. Shri Guru Charana Padma Kevala Bhakati Sadma. The lotus feet of the spiritual master are the abode of pure devotional service. So, this aptly fits Prabhupada according to this definition given by Sri Jiva Goswami and Srimad Bhagavatam. And so, Srila Prabhupada is fulfilling these functions and characteristics. And what about the unauthorized gurus as declared by the GBC? The answer is no, they don't perform this function because as per GBC Resolution 409 of 2004, they have declared that none of the Diksha Gurus in ISKCON are pure devotees or Uttama Adhikaris or have achieved any state of specific state of realization. So, they do not fit this characteristic. Then the next one, the Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 4, Chapter 29, Verse 55, purport, Any person who is seriously desirous of achieving real happiness must seek out a bona fide spiritual master and take shelter of him by initiation. And as far as Srila Prabhupada is concerned, every devotee has to sing every day, Vandho Mui Savadana Mate, I bow down to those lotus feet with great care and attention. And also, Haha Prabhu Koro Daya Deho More Padachaya. We have to sing to Prabhupada, O Master, be merciful unto me and give me the shade of your lotus feet. So, this condition is fulfilled by Srila Prabhupada. What about this function by the unauthorized Gurus? Well, they give Diksha. This much they do.

### Who Initiated Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur? | His Grace Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

#### guru, initiation, official guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The real deeksha or initiation involves a bona fide guru and surrender from the disciple."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces the topic of who initiated Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur and Srila Gaukishor Das Babaji Maharaj, contrasting real initiation with official or pseudo gurus.

Who initiated Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur and who initiated Srila Gaukishor Das Babaji Maharaj? In the Vedic culture, the father, the sanyasi, and the brahmana are automatically given the respect and position of a spiritual master. But when such a natural spiritual master turns out to be an atheist like Hiranyakashipu, then he is nothing more than an official guru or a pseudo guru. The real deeksha or initiation involves a bona fide guru and surrender from the disciple.

#### guru, demon, jnata-guru, loki guru, paramarthi guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=189
**Verse References:** SB 7.9.14
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "We have accepted somebody as guru, but he's a demon. You should be rejected immediately."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada on rejecting a guru who is a demon and discusses the system of jnata-guru (hereditary guru), contrasting it with a paramarthi guru (spiritually advanced guru) as recommended by Jiva Goswami.

In another lecture on Srimad Bhagavatam, 7th canto, 9th chapter, verse number 14, recorded on 21st of February in 1976, Srila Prabhupada says, we have accepted somebody as guru, but he's a demon. You should be rejected immediately. Immediately. Just like in India, there is a system of jnata-guru, the guru by car. By family-wise, they become guru. One family is attached to another guru family by hereditary rules, the sons after sons accepting guru, the other party, and there is regular business. So such kind of guru is not wanted. See, Jiva Goswami has recommended that loki guru, this is loki guru. Parityagela adhyatmik paramarthi guru accepted. Guru has to be accepted who is actually paramarthi, advanced in spiritual knowledge, not the formality. That is seconded by Jiva Goswami.

#### official guru, laukika guru, caste guru, hereditary guru
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=288
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Sometimes a caste guru says, You are a caste guru."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to discuss official, judo, laukika, and hereditary gurus, quoting Srila Prabhupada from the Chaitanya Charitamrita on the topic of caste gurus and the Vaishnava rejection of hereditary considerations.

So here, Srila Prabhupada is mentioning another example of an official, or a judo guru, or a laukika guru. A guru who is accepted according to hereditary rules, which are simply material rules. This type of hereditary guru is mentioned by Srila Prabhupada in the Chaitanya Charitamrita also. There in the Madhya Lila, Chapter 8, verse number 128, purport, Srila Prabhupada says, sometimes a caste guru says, You are a caste guru. That ye krsna-tattva-veitase guru-haya means that one who is not a brahmana may become a siksa-guru or a vartma-pradarsaka-guru, but not an initiator-guru. According to such caste gurus, birth and family ties are considered foremost. However, the hereditary consideration is not acceptable to Vaishnavas.

#### official guru, initiation, surrender, diksha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=376
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The official initiation has no meaning. Unless one was fully surrendered to guru, there is no question of initiation."
**Summary:** The speaker summarizes Srila Prabhupada's statements on official gurus and initiations, emphasizing that real initiation requires surrender from the disciple and that official initiation is ineffective.

According to caste, etc., these are all not real spiritual masters. And initiation taken from such gurus is only official initiation and is not the real initiation. Such official initiation is not effective. Initiation or diksa may become official either if the guru is an official guru or judo guru or if the disciple is not committed to the diksa. Srila Prabhupada in a lecture on the Srimad-Bhagavatam, 1st canto, 2nd chapter, verse number 20, recorded on 24th of July, 1974, says, The official initiation has no meaning. Unless one was fully surrendered to guru, there is no question of initiation. Dibhagyana hrde prakasi. Dibhagyana means transcendental knowledge. So, to play tricks with guru, to become diplomat and introvert, to become a guru, to become a leader, these rascals will not help. You can get some other things, some material profit, but spiritual life will be spoiled.

#### diksha, surrender, bona fide guru, preceptor, acharya
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=456
**Verse References:** SB 1.1.5
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The real heart of initiation or diksa is surrender by the disciple."
**Summary:** The speaker reiterates the importance of surrender in diksha and defines the real initiation as involving a bona fide guru, contrasting it with official or judo gurus. He then introduces the term 'preceptor' as used by Srila Prabhupada.

This means that the real heart of initiation or diksa is surrender by the disciple. Therefore, the real diksa or initiation involves a bona fide guru, not an official or judo guru. And without these two things, the initiation is only official initiation, which is meaningless. Now we have understood what is meant by official guru and official initiation. We have also seen that the surrender of the disciple is what really constitutes initiation. Next, let us look at one more terminology that Srila Prabhupada uses, which is preceptor. In the Srimad-Bhagavatam, purport to 1st canto, 1st chapter, verse 5, Srila Prabhupada says, Sri Vyasadeva is the original spiritual preceptor for all men, and all other preceptors are considered to be his representatives. Obviously, here Srila Prabhupada says spiritual preceptor or preceptor to be his representative. It is important to understand this principle.

---
#### acharya, preceptor, guru parampara, disciplic succession
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=530
**Verse References:** SB 1.13.15, SB 3.19.32
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Someone who is in the Guru Parampara."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that Srila Prabhupada uses the term 'preceptor' as an equivalent to 'acharya' and emphasizes the importance of receiving the transcendental message through disciplic succession (parampara).

He says, to mean the Acharya or spiritual master like Vyasa Deva. That means someone who is in the Guru Parampara. Again, in Srimad Bhagavatam, purport to Canto 1, chapter 13, verse 15, Srila Prabhupada mentions about Vidura. He was quite competent to occupy the post of an Acharya or spiritual preceptor. Here also, Srila Prabhupada uses the word spiritual preceptor as an equivalent of Acharya. Again, in the purport to Srimad Bhagavatam, 3rd Canto, 19th chapter, 32nd verse, Srila Prabhupada again uses the word preceptor in the same sense of Acharya. So, there Srila Prabhupada says, Thus, he accepted as bona fide. The system of Parampara or receiving the transcendental message in disciplic succession. Unless received by this bona fide process of hearing from a spiritual master, the statement of an Acharya or preceptor cannot be valid. So, like this, in many places, we can see, Srila Prabhupada uses the word preceptor as an equivalent of an Acharya.

#### Bhaktivinoda Thakur, Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, siksha guru, diksha guru, official
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=621
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Thakur Bhaktivinoda was not the official spiritual master of Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj."
**Summary:** The speaker begins to address the central question of the lecture, quoting a letter from Srila Prabhupada to Dayananda, which states that Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was not the official spiritual master of Srila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, but was treated as a preceptor due to his elevated spiritual understanding.

Now, let us come to what Srila Prabhupada says on the matter of initiation of Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur and Srila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj. In a letter that Srila Prabhupada wrote to Dayananda, written on 1st of May, 1969, Srila Prabhupada says, So far as your second question, Thakur Bhaktivinoda was not the official spiritual master of Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj. Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj was already renounced daughter, Paramahamsa. But, Thakur Bhaktivinoda, while he was even playing the part of a householder, was treated by Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj as preceptor, on account of his highly elevated spiritual understanding. And thus, he was always treating him as his spiritual master. The spiritual master is divided into two parts, namely, Shiksha Guru and Diksha Guru. So, officially, Bhaktivinoda Thakur was like Shiksha Guru. He was like the Shiksha Guru of Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj. So here, Srila Prabhupada is clearly mentioning that Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was not the official, please underline, official spiritual master of Shila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj. But, officially, again underline, officially, he was like his Shiksha Guru. Like his Shiksha Guru means, he was not actually Shiksha Guru.

**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj treated Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur as his Diksha Guru."
**Summary:** The speaker interprets Srila Prabhupada's statement, arguing that since Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was not really the siksha guru of Srila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, and Srila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj treated him as a preceptor, then Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur must have been his diksha guru.

From official point of view, that means in material point of view or from the point of view of custom or tradition that was prevalent in the society at that time, it appeared like Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was the Shiksha Guru of Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj. This was only on the official front. But Prabhupada says Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj treated Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur as his preceptor, which means spiritual master or acharya, as we have seen how Shaila Prabhupada uses the word preceptor elsewhere, which means a Diksha Guru in the parampara or acharya. In other words, Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji. Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj treated Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur as his Diksha Guru. So, Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj surrendered to Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur, accepting him as his Diksha Guru, treating him as his Diksha Guru. Not only that, Shaila Prabhupada here mentions that spiritual master are of two kinds, Diksha Guru and Shiksha Guru. So, if Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was not really the Shiksha Guru of Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, then, because Prabhupada says officially he was like the Shiksha Guru, that means he was not a Shiksha Guru, but Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj accepted him as his preceptor. Then, what kind of spiritual master was Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur? Obviously, he was not the Shiksha Guru. So, then the only alternative left is that of Diksha Guru. So, Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was the Diksha Guru of Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, because Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj treated him like that. He accepted him like that. He surrendered to him like that.

---
#### Bhaktivinoda Thakur, Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj, initiation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=851
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur initiated Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Srila Prabhupada from the Chaitanya Charitamrita and Back to Godhead, stating that Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur initiated Srila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, and that Srila Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj initiated Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur.

In the Chaitanya Charita Amrita, Adi Lila, Chapter 1 Summary, Shaila Prabhupada says this, Shaila Vishwanatha Chakravarti Thakur accepted Shaila Jagannath Das Babaji, who initiated Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur, who in turn initiated Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji. So, here, Shaila Prabhupada clearly mentions, unequivocally, that Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur initiated Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj. In the same vein, Shaila Prabhupada here says that Shaila Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj also initiated Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur. And this is confirmed by Shaila Prabhupada in the March 20, 1960 edition of Back to Godhead, Volume 3, Part 16, where Prabhupada says, Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was initiated and Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji, the spiritual master of Om Vishnupad, Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Prabhupada, the divine spiritual master of our humble self. Again, here also, Shaila Prabhupada says that Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was initiated by Shaila Jagannath Das Babaji.

---
#### diksha guru, official, spiritual consideration, Jiva Goswami
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/ChSfOU-DUP8?t=938
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Such official Diksha has no relevance, no meaning in spiritual life and therefore such official Diksha Gurus find no mention in the teachings of Srila Prabhupada."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes that from a spiritual perspective, Srila Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj was indeed the diksha guru of Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur, and Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was indeed the diksha guru of Srila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj, emphasizing that official diksha based on material considerations is irrelevant and should be rejected, quoting Srila Jiva Goswami.

So, from all of this, it is clear that officially or from material point of view, in terms of maybe social custom or tradition or hereditary practices or caste or whatever practices were prevalent in the society at that time, Shaila Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj might not have appeared to be the diksha guru of Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur. And Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur... In turn, might not have appeared to be the diksha guru of Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj in the eyes of the material people. But from spiritual consideration, Shaila Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj was indeed the diksha guru of Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur and Shaila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was indeed the diksha guru of Shaila Gaur Kishore Das Babaji Maharaj. This... is the conclusion we can draw. The official Diksha Guru, the Diksha Guru accepted according to hereditary customs or traditions or whatever. The official Diksha Guru of Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur, who may have been accepted in terms of social customs, tradition, hereditary practices, caste or any other material reason, whatever it is. It might have been someone else, not Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj. But for all practical purposes, Srila Prabhupada quotes Srila Jeev Goswami to emphasize that such official Diksha has no relevance, no meaning in spiritual life and therefore such official Diksha Gurus find no mention in the teachings of Srila Prabhupada about who were the official Gurus of Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur or of Srila Gaur Keshavar Das Babaji Maharaj. Because they are simply not relevant and they have no meaning. So, in fact, Prabhupada says such official Diksha Gurus are to be rejected.

As Srila Prabhupada quotes Srila Jeev Goswami, which brings us to the current unauthorized Guru system in the ISKCON Mumbai group. Their GBC has based their self-appointed Guru system on Srila Prabhupada. The Bhaktivinoda Thakur's official acceptance of Diksha from someone who was not an Acharya in our Parampara or who was not a pure devotee. So, the GBC quotes this to justify their stand that the Diksha Gurus in ISKCON need not be pure devotees. But as we have seen until now, such official Diksha Gurus are accepted. The Bhaktivinoda Thakur's official acceptance of Diksha Gurus from someone who was not an Acharya in our Parampara or who was not a pure devotee. So, the GBC quotes this to justify God by Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was Laukiki. It was only official and had no spiritual significance, no spiritual meaning. And Srila Prabhupada quoted Srila Jeevakaswami to say that such Laukika Guru is to be rejected. Parityagena. And a Paramarthika Adhyatmika Guru has to be accepted for Diksha. So this means that all of ISKCON Mumbai group's Diksha Gurus who are indicated no objection by the GBC are based on the official or Laukika Diksha Guru example of Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur which means as per Srila Jeevakaswami, they are all to be rejected. Parityagena. All the Diksha Gurus in ISKCON are only official or Laukika Gurus. Then who is the Paramarthika Guru? Who is the Paramarthika Guru to be accepted as Diksha Guru in ISKCON? The answer is that is only His Divine Grace Vishwaguru Abhay Charanarvinda Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada. He is the sole Diksha Guru of ISKCON as declared by Srila Prabhupada himself in his July 9th 1977 Directive which is followed by ISKCON Bangalore group. Vishwaguru Srila Prabhupada Ki Jai. Hare Krishna.

### Who delivers the disciples of ISKCON Gurus? | His Grace Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

#### Guru Parampara, Diksha Guru, Deliverance, Spiritual Master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/73UzAo_MtgY?t=0
**Verse References:** SB 3.25.8, BG 18.66
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The spiritual master is therefore worshipped as one who delivers the disciple from the mire of ignorance with the light of the torch of knowledge."
**Summary:** The speaker analyzes a GBC resolution stating Krishna delivers devotees through various means, contrasting it with Srila Prabhupada's teachings emphasizing the Diksha Guru's role in deliverance, citing Srimad Bhagavatam and Chaitanya Charitamrita.

The GBC of ISKCON Mumbai Group, in its Resolution No. 303, titled GBC Statement on the Position of Srila Prabhupada, has stated that Lord Krishna, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, is the original Guru, whose divine grace descends through the medium of the Guru Parampara. As such, a devotee is ultimately delivered by Sri Krishna, acting through a combination of various manifestations of His mercy. These include, but are not limited to, the Chaitya Guru, Srila Prabhupada, the Guru Parampara, one's Diksha Guru, other Siksha Gurus, the Holy Name, Shastra, and the nine processes of devotional service. So, according to this Resolution of the GBC, who delivers an ISKCON devotee from material entanglement? This is the question before us today. According to the GBC, it is Sri Krishna, acting through a combination of various manifestations of His mercy, such as the Chaitya Guru, Srila Prabhupada, the Guru Parampara, one's Diksha Guru, other Siksha Gurus, the Holy Name, Shastra, Navaveda Bhakti, and many more like this. So, we will see, is this statement of the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai Group, backed by Srila Prabhupada's teachings and Shastra? Continue listening to find out. In his purport to verse 8 of chapter 25 of the third canto of the Srimad Bhagavatam, Srila Prabhupada says, This verse is very instructive, since it indicates the relationship between the spiritual master and the disciple. The disciple or conditioned soul is put into this darkest region of ignorance and therefore is entangled in the material existence of sense gratification. It is very difficult to get out of this entanglement and attain freedom. But if one is fortunate enough to get the association of a spiritual master, like Kapila Muni or his representative, then by his grace, one can be delivered from the mire of ignorance. The spiritual master is therefore worshipped as one who delivers the disciple from the mire of ignorance with the light of the torch of knowledge. The word Paragam is very significant. Paragam refers to one who can take the disciple to the other side. This side is conditioned life. The other side is the life of freedom. The spiritual master takes the disciple to the other side by opening his eyes with knowledge. We are suffering simply because of ignorance. By the instruction of the spiritual master, the darkness of ignorance is removed and thus the disciple is enabled to go to the side of freedom. It is stated in Bhagavad Gita that after many, many births, one surrenders to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Similarly, if after many, many births, one surrenders to the Supreme Personality of Godhead, one is able to find a bona fide spiritual master and surrender to such a bona fide representative of Krishna, one can be taken to the side of light. So, please note Shaila Prabhupada's opening statement of this purport that these words indicate the relationship between the spiritual master and the disciple. These are Prabhupada's words. In other words, these words are specifically about the relationship between the Diksha Guru and his disciple. Therefore, this purport which we have just now gone through is very relevant to our current discussion. And in this purport, Shaila Prabhupada says no less than five times that it is the Diksha Guru who delivers the disciple. The first place, Shaila Prabhupada says, it is very difficult to get out of this entanglement and attain freedom. But if one is fortunate enough to get the association of a spiritual master like Kapilamani or his representative, then by his grace, whose grace, the spiritual master's grace, one can be delivered from the mire of ignorance. So, here clearly, Shaila Prabhupada says that the Diksha Guru is the one who delivers the disciple. Then again, Shaila Prabhupada says a second time in the purport, the spiritual master is therefore worshipped as one who delivers the disciple from the mire of ignorance with the light of the torch of knowledge. Again, Diksha Guru is worshipped as the one who delivers the disciple. Then a third time, Shaila Prabhupada says, Paragam refers to one who can take the disciple to the other side. This side is conditioned life. The other side is the life of freedom. The spiritual master takes the disciple to the other side by opening his eyes with knowledge. So, that's the third time. As if that were not enough, a fourth time, Shaila Prabhupada says, by the instruction of the spiritual master, the darkness of ignorance is removed and thus the disciple is enabled to go to the side of freedom. So, it is the instruction of the Diksha Guru that removes the darkness of ignorance. That removes the darkness of ignorance of the disciple, enabling him to achieve freedom. Then, a fifth time, Shaila Prabhupada says in this very purport, it is stated in Bhagavad Gita that after many, many births, one surrenders to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Similarly, if after many, many births, one is able to find a bona fide spiritual master and surrender to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. If one is able to surrender to such a bona fide representative of Krishna, one can be taken to the side of light. Only if one gets a bona fide Diksha Guru, can one be taken to the side of light. Let me remind you that Shaila Prabhupada began this purport, how? By saying that this verse is very instructive about the spiritual master and his disciple. Hence, we have to understand that wherever in this purport, Shaila Prabhupada is taking into account the spiritual master, it must refer to Diksha Guru because that is the specific subject of discussion in the verse and the purport.

---
#### Holy Name, Diksha, Empowerment, Responsibility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/73UzAo_MtgY?t=600
**Verse References:** CC Madhya 9.61, SB 4.24.31-32, BG 13.8-12, CC Madhya 15.108
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Only when the devotee surrenders unto a bona fide Diksha Guru, will the Diksha Guru take responsibility to deliver the disciple."
**Summary:** The speaker continues to refute the GBC resolution, asserting the Diksha Guru's sole responsibility for delivering the disciple through initiation into the Holy Name and devotional practices, emphasizing the Guru's empowerment by Krishna.

So, very clearly from this purport, we can see that Shaila Prabhupada taught that it is the Diksha Guru who delivers the disciple, not the Siksha Guru or anyone else. So, then the question may arise, what is the role of the holy name and all the other items mentioned as the combined deliverers in the GBC resolution like the holy name, the Siksha Gurus, etc. Well, Shaila Prabhupada says this in the purport to the 61st verse of chapter 9 of Madhya Leela in the Chaitanya Charita Amrita. It is the spiritual master who delivers the disciple from the clutches of Maya by initiating him into the chanting of the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra. So, we can understand that the deliverer is one. And who is that? The Diksha Guru. To deliver the disciple, the Diksha Guru initiates him into chanting the holy name, performing Navaveda Bhakti, worshipping the Deity, etc. Yes, all these are essential in the deliverance of a conditioned soul. But if we ask, who delivers the devotee? The answer is not that it is Krishna through a combination of Diksha Guru, Siksha Guru, holy name, Navaveda Bhakti, etc. as erroneously resolved by the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai group. On the other hand, the responsibility of delivering the disciple is taken by the Diksha Guru, solely by the Diksha Guru. We have to understand that everyone's responsibility is nobody's responsibility. The holy name may be there. Krishna may be there. Siksha Gurus may be there. Navaveda Bhakti may be there. Deities may be there. But one person has to take the responsibility of delivering the disciple. It cannot be left to some vague combination of everyone together delivering the devotee. That one person who takes the entire responsibility of delivering the disciple is the Diksha Guru. For delivering the disciple, the Diksha Guru is empowered by Krishna to engage the disciple in worshipping the holy name by chanting a prescribed number of rounds, in worshipping the deity, in executing Navaveda Bhakti, etc. In other words, if one chants the holy name, executes Navaveda Bhakti, hears many Siksha Gurus, but doesn't have a Diksha Guru, then none of them will take responsibility to deliver the devotee. Only when the devotee surrenders unto a bona fide Diksha Guru, will the Diksha Guru take responsibility to deliver the disciple. In the Srimad Bhagavatam, canto 4, chapter 24, verses 31 and 32 purport, Srila Prabhupada says this, When a mantra is chanted by a great devotee, the mantra becomes more powerful. Although the Hare Krishna Mahamantra is powerful in itself, a disciple, upon initiation, receives the mantra from his spiritual master. For, when the mantra is chanted by the spiritual master, it becomes more powerful. So, it is clear that the holy name, Navaveda Bhakti, Shastra, the Guru Parampara, etc. are all acting to deliver the devotee. But, without the grace of a bona fide Diksha Guru, it is impossible to be delivered. Eventually, as Srila Prabhupada said, if a devotee doesn't have a bona fide Diksha Guru, but is chanting the holy name, executing Navaveda Bhakti, etc., then, the Paramatma will guide the devotee to a bona fide Diksha Guru, and then, that bona fide Diksha Guru will take the disciple back to Krishna, back to Godhead, delivering him from the material entanglement. On the other hand, if one has everything, the holy name, Navaveda Bhakti, Shastra, Parampara, etc., but has no bona fide Diksha Guru, then, he cannot be delivered. Srila Prabhupada says, in his purport to the verses 8 through 12 of the 13th chapter of the Bhagavad Gita, because a bona fide spiritual master is a representative of Krishna, if he bestows any blessings upon his disciple, that will make the disciple immediately advanced without the disciples following the regulated principles. Or, the regulated principles will be easier for one who has served the spiritual master without reservation. If the Diksha Guru bestows his blessings upon his disciple, then, the disciple becomes immediately advanced without following any of the regulated principles like Navaveda Bhakti, chanting prescribed number of rounds, etc. That is the power of the bona fide Diksha Guru. And where does the Diksha Guru get such power? From Krishna Himself. Because it is Krishna who has directly empowered the Diksha Guru like that. But, the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai group has diluted the power of the Diksha Guru by saying that it is not the Diksha Guru who delivers the disciple. Rather, it is Krishna through many many manifestations of His mercy.

---
#### Srila Prabhupada, Diksha Guru, ISKCON, Deliverance
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/73UzAo_MtgY?t=979
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** practical
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The deliverer in our group is His Divine Grace AC Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada, who delivers all the devotees in our group, because He is our sole bona fide Diksha Guru, empowered directly by Krishna to be the deliverer."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by contrasting the GBC's resolution with the belief within their own ISKCON group that Srila Prabhupada is the sole deliverer, empowered by Krishna as the bona fide Diksha Guru.

Let us go through that resolution once again. What does the resolution say? It says, As such, a devotee is ultimately delivered by Sri Krishna acting through a combination of various manifestations of His mercy. These include, but are not limited to, the Chaitya Guru, Srila Prabhupada, the Guru Parampara, one's Diksha Guru, other Siksha Gurus, the Holy Name, Shastra, and the nine processes of devotional service. Do you see how ridiculous this resolution is when you look at all of Srila Prabhupada's instructions on this matter? This is a whole new spin that the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai group is giving to the process of deliverance of a disciple by a bona fide Diksha Guru. Isn't this statement, isn't this resolution by the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai group an obfuscation of the truth? Isn't the GBC contorting the truth to suit its own self-centred agenda? Has the GBC passed such a ridiculous resolution which contradicts Srila Prabhupada's teachings because the GBC believes that its Diksha Gurus are not bona fide, that they are unauthorised. And because they are unauthorised, the ISKCON Gurus are not empowered by Krishna to deliver their disciples. Is it because the GBC knows that the ISKCON Diksha Gurus are not really performing the role of Diksha Guru, which is to illuminate the disciple's heart with transcendental knowledge and to deliver him from all sinful reactions, which is the definition of Diksha Vedana, or in other words, to deliver the disciple from the material world. Just as Srila Prabhupada quoted Srila Jeeva Goswami in Chaitanya Charita Amrita, Madhya Leela, chapter 15, verse 108 purport. Is this resolution an attempt by the GBC to reconcile the different opinions that exist in ISKCON as to who delivers the devotees? Do all the Gurus and their disciples in ISKCON agree and accept the GBC's version of who delivers the disciple? Very unlikely, because there are many, many divided opinions amongst the Gurus and their disciples in ISKCON on this subject. Everyone in ISKCON has his own opinion as to who delivers the disciples. Somebody says Krishna delivers, somebody says Prabhupada delivers, somebody says the Diksha Guru delivers, etc. Does this resolution number 303 of the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai group not smack of hypocrisy, calling its devotees Diksha Gurus, but openly declaring that they are incapable of delivering their disciples? On the one hand, the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai group accuses Srila Prabhupada's Rithvik system of initiations of making Srila Prabhupada impotent. But on the other hand, what does this resolution 303 of 2014 speak of the potency of the ISKCON unauthorized Gurus? Are the unauthorized Diksha Gurus in ISKCON not potent enough to deliver their disciples? We are not sure if the GBC has any answers at all to these questions, as well as the innumerable questions we have raised on the unauthorized Guru system in all our videos till date. Their deafening silence can mean only one thing. What is that? That they have no answer to any of our questions. As far as we at Srila Prabhupada's ISKCON group are concerned, you can ask any devotee in our group. Write from a newly joined Bhakta to the senior most GBC in our group, as to who delivers the devotees in our group. You will instantly get one and only one spontaneous and unambiguous answer everywhere without fail, without any confusion, without any doubt. What is that answer? The deliverer in our group is His Divine Grace AC Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada, who delivers all the devotees in our group, because He is our sole bona fide Diksha Guru, empowered directly by Krishna to be the deliverer. Hare Krishna Vishwaguru Srila Prabhupada Ki Jai!

---

### Why is the world facing so many natural disturbances | HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Suvyakta Narasimha Dasa

#### omens, natural disturbances, Hiranyakasipu
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/a4nlkCW7T7k?t=17
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "But the kind of breezes that started blowing when Hiranyakasipu and Hiranyakasipu appeared, they were Suddhusvarsaha and they were Pudkaran Iren, Muhuhu."
**Summary:** The speaker describes the inauspicious omens, specifically the unpleasant and fearful nature of the wind, that manifested at the time of Hiranyakasipu's appearance, contrasting it with the pleasant breezes usually experienced in Chennai.

There are many omens that were manifest when Daya and Vijaya came down as Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakasipu. So it is described here, Vavavvayu, Vayu, the air, a breeze which blows, winds which blow. Normally, in Chennai what happens, it's very humid. So, when we stand outside in the tree shade, there is a breeze that blows, it feels very pleasing, it feels very nice, cooling, comforting. But the kind of breezes that started blowing when Hiranyakasipu and Hiranyakasipu appeared, they were Suddhusvarsaha and they were Pudkaran Iren, Muhuhu. When the wind used to blow, when Hiranyakasipu and Hiranyakasipu were going to take birth, the breeze used to give a hissing sound. And just like you would see in, if any of you before joining, you have seen these horror movies, you know, sometimes, you know, in the night, they'll show one window is closing, one door is closing, and there is a kind of sound that comes, which is very fearful. You know, those kind of sounds are not pleasing to the ear. And Suddhusvarsaha, to the touch also, the breeze was not pleasing. So, especially when you are in Chennai, you don't have experience of breeze which is unpleasant, isn't it? Any time breeze blows, it's welcome.

---
#### weather, Vrindavan, personal experience
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/a4nlkCW7T7k?t=118
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "As soon as I got out of the car, I immediately got back inside."
**Summary:** The speaker shares his personal experiences of extreme weather in Vrindavan, both in winter and summer, contrasting it with the milder climate he was accustomed to in South India, particularly Bangalore.

But you know, I have also been, you know, I was born and grew up in South India. So, when I went to Vrindavan, I went in 2005. So, and unfortunately, I went in February. You know, January, February is the coldest season in Vrindavan. So, I had no idea how winter is in North India. I never went out of South India. You know, Andhra, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, these were the areas which I used to roam, until I was, until I joined the temple and then went to Vrindavan. So, I had no idea how winters are in the North India. Maximum winter, I had got exposed to was in Bangalore, you know. Maximum would go down to 20 degrees, 22 degrees. And in Vrindavan, you know how winters are. The maximum, the maximum daytime temperature at 1 o'clock or 2 o'clock in the afternoon will be around 5 degrees or 6 degrees. That's the kind of winter. So, I had no idea. So, you know, we got into a car from the airport and drove down to Vrindavan. And our Vrindavan Chandra Uday Mandir site that is there. At that time, of course, it's not like this currently. It was just plain land. There was absolutely nothing. The torus which you see now, it was still coming up. So, I went and landed there. Got out of the car. As soon as I got out of the car, I immediately got back inside. There was a very cold... As it is, the temperature was cold. On top of that, a cold breeze was blowing. The moment it touched my skin, that's all. I couldn't get out of the car. I immediately jumped back inside the car. I said, I am not going to get out. Then, you know, with me, other Vyopat Prabhu and other devotees were there. They said, hey, if you don't get out, you look very bad. I see all the embassies, they are standing there. Come on, get out. So, with great difficulty, I managed to get out. So, there are breezes also which are not pleasant. So, sometimes, you know, in the extreme winter, when the breeze blows, it is not pleasing.

**Summary:** The speaker continues sharing his experiences in Vrindavan, describing the intense heat of summer and relating it back to the unpleasant breezes associated with the birth of Hiranyaksha and Hiranyakasipu, emphasizing the overall deterioration of the climate.

Similarly, my first summer in Vrindavan, I still remember. Summer also, I had never experienced, you know, in Bangalore, temperatures those days, maximum 28, 29. 30 was unheard of in Bangalore, those days. Nowadays, of course, it goes to 35, 36 and all that. So, when summer came, June, May, June, temperatures were sizzling, 48 degrees centigrade. I had never experienced in my life. And those days, we were not staying in the campus. We were staying outside. So, we used to come every day and go. And we used to come by bike. So, another devotee used to drive the bike and I used to sit behind. So, in the afternoon for lunch, Prasadam, we had to go back. So, we used to stay in that Keshav Kunj, which was opposite the Krishnabal Ram temple. So, from, you can imagine, from Chattikara till there, we had to go in 48 degrees centigrade. And we were, when we were driving, the breeze was blowing and I was feeling somebody has set fire to my body. So, at that time, breeze was not pleasing. I was thinking, when will this breeze stop? So, sometimes, breezes are not pleasing to the skin. So, it is said here that when Hiranyaksha and, of course, in certain climates, certain weathers, the breeze may not be pleasing. But when Karthik Maas comes, when Chaitanya Maas comes, that time, the breeze is very pleasing. So, but then, when Hiranyaksha and Hiranyaka Kshapu were taking birth, the entire climate itself became so bad, that the breeze became, became distrustful, became uninviting to the touch.

#### natural disturbances, karma, exploitation, Krishna's will
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/a4nlkCW7T7k?t=352
**Verse References:** —

And the sound that they were giving was also not pleasing to the ears. And what was happening, Vataaniko, Vaatya means, there was a, you know, these cyclones and hurricanes had become very common. So, which is what we are experiencing now. Every other day, there is a cyclone. Recently, just recently, you know, Chennai has gone through one cyclone, still not got over the, still the cyclone hangover is still going on, still continuously raining. And few years back, we had the big tsunami. And I think couple of years back, we had a big flood also here. So, these kind of natural disturbances are actually, if you see, they are not meant to be in this world. It is not that this world is like this. No, this world is not like this, not supposed to be like this. This world, Krishna has created in such a way, that it is supposed to be very pleasing and giving lot of peace and comfort to us in our minds, as long as we are executing spiritual activities. The moment we go against the will of Krishna, we go against the laws of nature, we start trying to exploit material nature as well as other living entities. When that becomes prominent in this material world, what happens is, all the entire system around which this whole material universe is designed, gets disrupted. And when that gets disrupted, all these kind of natural disturbances become very prominent.

#### Guru Parampara, Acharya, Qualifications
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9e4-r0G6jcw?t=61
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Such an elevated Paramahamsa, chosen by Krishna Himself to represent Him, is identified by the predecessor Acharya and appointed as the next Acharya."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the argument that Bhaktivinoda Thakur took Diksha from someone outside the Acharya line and contrasts this with the qualifications and appointment process of an Acharya in the Guru Parampara.

The oft-quoted example is that of Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur. It is said that Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur took Diksha from one Bipin Bihari Goswami. But this Bipin Bihari Goswami is not an Acharya in the Madhavagodiyas of Vaishnava Sampradaya. There are other such examples too. Hence, those Vaishnavas in good standing, who are not Acharyas in the Guru Parampara, who may not be Uttama Adhikari, Paramahamsa Vaishnavas, can give Diksha, like Bipin Bihari Goswami, who is not part of the Guru Parampara. This argument is put forward by the supporters of the self-appointed, unauthorized Guru system in ISKCON. They put forward this argument to justify their GBC's unauthorized, no-objection indication of unqualified devotees as Diksha Gurus in ISKCON. To clear the air of the confusion created by this argument, we need to first understand that there are two separate and unrelated processes being compared here. The first process is that of the appointment of an Acharya to the Guru Parampara. An Acharya has to be self-realized, he has to be liberated, and he must be an Uttama Adhikari, Paramahamsa Vaishnava. Such an elevated Paramahamsa, chosen by Krishna Himself to represent Him, is identified by the predecessor Acharya and appointed as the next Acharya.

For example, in this conversation, an Indian man asked Srila Prabhupada, When did you become the spiritual leader of Krishna Consciousness? Srila Prabhupada replied, What is that? Brahmananda clarified, He is asking, When did you become the spiritual leader of Krishna Consciousness? Srila Prabhupada immediately replied, When my Guru Maharaj ordered me. This is Guru Parampara. The Indian man is still pursuing his question. Did it? Srila Prabhupada tried to understand. Don't go very speedily. A Guru can become Guru when he is ordered by his Guru. That's all. Otherwise, nobody can become Guru. In another quote, in the Srimad Bhagavatam, Prabhupada says, One should take initiation from a bona fide spiritual master coming in the disciplic succession, who is authorized by his predecessor spiritual master. This is called Diksha Vidhana. And yet, in another room conversation in San Diego, recorded on June 29, 1972, in this conversation, Atre Rishi asked Prabhupada, How many qualifications does a spiritual master have in terms of being a spiritual master? And Prabhupada replied, One qualification. He is a devotee of God. That's all. Atre Rishi continued, Also, is he designated? Does he have to be designated by the former spiritual master? Prabhupada immediately, Oh yes! Oh yes! So, Atre Rishi, he has to be a devotee. He has to be both surrendered and designated. That is, identifies disciplic succession, both surrender and designation. And in another lecture on the Nectar of Devotion, Srila Prabhupada said this, Similarly, bona fide Guru means, he must be authorized by the superior Guru. Just like Chaitanya Mahaprabhu says, Amarajnaya Guruhaya Tare Desha. He must receive the order from superior. And the superior must be bona fide. Then he is bona fide, not self-made. So, from this, what we have to note is,

So, although ISKCON Mumbai's group claims that Srila Bhaktivinoda Thakur was not initiated by Srila Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj, Srila Prabhupada says that Bhaktivinoda Thakur was initiated by Srila Jagannath Das Babaji Maharaj. Nowhere in his entire teachings does Srila Prabhupada talk about a person called Bipin Bihari Goswami. #### Diksha, Pure Devotee, Order vs. Possibility
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9e4-r0G6jcw?t=444
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Mention of a possibility does not constitute an order."
**Summary:** The speaker addresses whether taking Diksha from a non-pure devotee is acceptable, using Prabhupada's letter to Kirtananda and an analogy of the Prime Minister and army chief to distinguish between a possibility and an order.

So, let us stick to Srila Prabhupada's terminology. That's safe. But still, does that mean that taking Diksha from a Guru who is not a pure devotee is alright? Well, in the same letter to Kirtananda on 25th of January 1969, Srila Prabhupada elaborates this. Regarding your question about the disciplic succession coming down from Arjuna, it is just like I have got my disciples. So, in the future, these many disciples may have many branches of disciplic succession. Now, here, let me clarify. Prabhupada is talking about a possibility. So, talking about a possibility is not an order. So, this sentence, this statement by Prabhupada that just like I have got my disciples, so in future, these many disciples may have many branches of disciplic succession, does not mean that they have to initiate on their own. For example, the Prime Minister of India may say, there is a possibility of war with Pakistan. Does that mean that the army chief immediately says, Oh, Prime Minister said that there is a possibility of war. So, immediately go out and attack Pakistan. No, mention of a possibility does not constitute an order. So, Prabhupada here is just mentioning a possibility. It may be possible. So, this does not constitute an order.

#### Disciplic Succession, Arjuna, Pure Devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9e4-r0G6jcw?t=533
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "All the Gurus in the Guru Parampara are pure devotees."
**Summary:** The speaker continues with Prabhupada's letter, using the examples of Narada, Vyasadeva, and Arjuna, and the analogy of leaves from the same tree, to emphasize that all Gurus in the Guru Parampara are pure devotees.

So, Prabhupada further goes on to say, So, in one line of disciples, we may not say another name coming from a different line. But this does not mean that person whose name does not appear was not in the disciplic succession. Narada was the spiritual master of Vyasadeva and Arjuna was Vyasadeva's disciple, not as initiated disciple, but there was some blood relation between them. So, there is connection in this way. And it is not possible to list all such relationships in the short description given in Bhagavad Gita as it is. Another point is that disciplic succession does not mean one has to be directly a disciple of a particular person. The conclusions which we have tried to explain in our Bhagavad Gita as it is, is the same as those conclusions of Arjuna. Arjuna accepted Krishna as the Supreme Personality of Godhead and we also accept the same truth under the disciplic succession of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. Things equal to the same thing are equal to one another. This is an axiomatic truth. So, there is no difference of opinion of understanding Krishna between ourselves and Arjuna. Another example is that a tree has many branches and you will find one leaf here and another leaf there. But if you take this leaf and the other leaf and you press them both, you will see that the taste is the same. The taste is the conclusion and from the taste, you can understand that both leaves are from the same tree. So, this example given in this letter by Srila Prabhupada is that of Arjuna and Vyasa Deva. Arjuna might not be the initiated disciple of Vyasa Deva, but still, he could also be an Acharya. Now, does that mean Arjuna was initiated by someone who was not a pure devotee? Definitely not. Because Srila Prabhupada gives the example of two leaves from the same tree. Meaning that, one devotee may be initiated by a pure devotee coming down from one branch, while the predecessor Guru might be coming down from another branch. The predecessor Guru might order this devotee who is initiated from a different branch to become the next Guru in his line. So, both are initiated by pure devotees. Both are pure devotees themselves. In that way, the Guru in the Guru Parampara may not be initiated directly by the predecessor Guru, but he still is initiated by a pure devotee in some other line. Therefore, all leaves taste the same. Meaning, all the Gurus in the Guru Parampara are pure devotees.

---
#### Guru Parampara, Diksha, Liberation
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9e4-r0G6jcw?t=694
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The only way a conditioned soul can get connected to the Guru Parampara is the process of Diksha."
**Summary:** The speaker summarizes the process of appointing a Guru and then explains the second process: the deliverance of a conditioned soul through Diksha from a Paramahamsa Acharya of the Guru Parampara.

So, the criterion for appointment to the Guru Parampara as the next Acharya or Diksha Guru is not Diksha by the predecessor Acharya or Siksha, but rather, order by the predecessor Acharya, as we have seen from the previous quotes of Srila Prabhupada which we shared in the beginning. So, this is the process of appointing the Guru. Now, we come to the second process. The second process is separate and unrelated from the above process. What was the above process? The first process is the process of appointing a Guru by the predecessor Guru. So, this is the first process. Now, the second process which is separate and unrelated to this process of appointment of a Guru by the predecessor Guru is that of the deliverance of a conditioned soul. The process of deliverance of a conditioned soul happens by the combination of revelation of transcendental knowledge in his heart and the destruction of all accumulated past sinful reaction. This process, as all of you know, is called Diksha. Divyam Jnanam Yathodadhyat and Kuryat Papasya Sankshayam. For a conditioned soul to obtain Diksha requires that he must search out an Uttamaadhikari, Paramahamsa Acharya who is part of the Guru Parampara and the devotee has to surrender to him and take Diksha from him. Such a conditioned soul cannot be delivered back to Godhead without taking Diksha from a Paramahamsa Acharya of the Guru Parampara. The only way a conditioned soul can get connected to the Guru Parampara is the process of Diksha.

---

They do not come in discipling succession but becomes Guru, teacher. Therefore, everything is topsy-turvy. Nobody has got fixed idea what is God. Everyone has created his own philosophy. That means the Guru who gives the initiation must be a person who is part of the Sampradaya or who is in the Sampradaya or Guru Parampara. Now, in another quote Prabhupada says, otherwise one becomes Guru. Anyone, just like nowadays they become, they are manufacturing Guru. Guru is not manufactured. Guru is in the discipling succession. #### Disciplic Succession, Guru Parampara, Diksha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9e4-r0G6jcw?t=900
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Diksha taken from such a Guru results in taking the disciple back to Godhead."
**Summary:** The speaker clarifies that a Guru must be one of the 32 personalities in the Guru Parampara and that Diksha from such a Guru leads back to Godhead. He also states that a Guru should not initiate if they cannot take the disciple back to Godhead.

So, in this last quote, Prabhupada makes it clear that Guru is in the discipling succession. And in other places, Prabhupada says, the Guru comes down from that discipling succession. In the discipling succession or from the discipling succession means that the Guru has to be one of the 32 personalities mentioned in the Guru Parampara. It cannot be otherwise. Diksha taken from such a Guru results in taking the disciple back to Godhead. So, Prabhupada says in the Srimad Bhagavatam, according to Shastra, the duty of the Guru is to take the disciple back to Godhead. If he is unable to do so and instead hinders the disciple in going back to Godhead, he should not be a Guru. In other words, from this quote, unless the Guru has the capability to take the disciple back to Godhead, he should not give initiation. Thus, we can clearly see that one process of liberation for a conditioned soul involves Diksha from an Acharya in the Guru Parampara, while the other process of appointment of a successor Acharya does not necessarily involve Diksha. Both processes are unrelated. One is the process of appointment of a Guru and the other is the appointment of a disciple. Both are separate, independent and cannot be compared. We cannot apply the principles of appointment of a Guru to those of the appointment of a disciple or vice versa. Hence, irrespective of whether our Parampara is Diksha Parampara or Shiksha Parampara,
