#### Guru, Krishna Consciousness, Qualifications
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9e4-r0G6jcw?t=1219
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "One who has understood Krishna as the supreme controller, the supreme person, the supreme maintainer, and everything, the supreme ultimate, then he can become Guru. Otherwise, don't try to become Guru."
**Summary:** The speaker quotes Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Prabhupada to emphasize that only someone who fully understands Krishna can be a Guru and that conditioned souls cannot be Gurus.

Srila Prabhupada said in a lecture, Therefore, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu confirms, Ae Krishna Tattva Veta Sai Guru Haya. One who has understood Krishna as the supreme controller, the supreme person, the supreme maintainer, and everything, the supreme ultimate, then he can become Guru. Otherwise, don't try to become Guru. Impersonalist, half understood, partially understood, he cannot become Guru. This is the Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's formula. Therefore, first of all, try to understand Krishna. You will understand in such a way that you can refute all others' argument, all others' opposition. There are so many opposing elements. Then you are Guru. Otherwise, you cannot become a Guru. Guru is not so tiny thing or trifle thing that everyone becomes Guru Maharaj. No, that is not Guru. Samahatma Vasudevaha Sarvamiti Samahatma Sudurlabha. So, you heard what Prabhupada said. Half understood, partially understood, he cannot become Guru. Only an Uttamaadhikari Guru in the Guru Parampara can claim to know full and not partial. Conditioned souls cannot know fully. They can only know partially. In other words, conditioned souls cannot become Guru. To become a Guru and to give Diksha is not a trifle thing. It is an extremely serious affair because Guru has to take the disciple back to Godhead.

---
#### Rithvik System, GBC, Authority
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/9e4-r0G6jcw?t=1304
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without a direct order from Srila Prabhupada, ISKCON's self-appointed no objection Gurus are not in the Guru Parampara."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by reiterating that there is no evidence Prabhupada ordered the GBC's Guru nomination system, that the system is unauthorized, and that the Rithvik system is the authorized process for taking initiation from Prabhupada.

So, there is not a single shred of evidence that Srila Prabhupada ordered Gurus to be nominated with a no objection indicated by the GBC body. How long will the GBC stubbornly refuse to accept the truth and keep coming up with various concoctions just to fulfill their megalomaniacal ambition to become Diksha Guru? How long will the GBC continue to slaughter innocent devotees' spiritual lives to quench their thirst for honour, prestige and worship? Without a direct order from Srila Prabhupada, ISKCON's self-appointed no objection Gurus are not in the Guru Parampara. Thus, the Guru nomination system of ISKCON is unauthorized, while the Rithvik system authorized by Srila Prabhupada in writing via the July 9, 1977 Directive, fulfills all the conditions stipulated for a devotee to take initiation from a Mahabhagavata in the Guru Parampara and go back to Godhead. The Rithvik process is independent of whether the Guru Parampara is Siksha or Diksha Parampara. Whether the Guru Parampara is Siksha or Diksha Parampara, has no relevance to the process of taking Diksha from Srila Prabhupada via the Rithvik system personally instituted in ISKCON by Srila Prabhupada. Thus, the Rithvik system does not violate any principle, even if our Guru Parampara is considered to be based on Siksha and not Diksha. All devotees are requested to reject the self-appointed no objection Guru system promoted by the GBC of ISKCON Mumbai. Instead, please take shelter of the Rithvik system ordered by Srila Prabhupada on July 9, 1977 and followed by the GBC of ISKCON Bangalore. Hare Krishna, Vishwaguru Srila Prabhupada ki Jai!

---

## HG Vasudev Keshav Dasa

### 2016-09-29 | SB 3.25.14 | HG Vasudev Keshav Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Vasudev Keshav Dasa

#### scripture, rules and regulations, perfection
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EWe6Kf0JoCQ?t=600
**Verse References:** BG 16.23
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Krishna is clearly declaring in the Bhagavad Gita that if you do not follow the rules and regulations mentioned in the scriptures and follow something out of your own whims and fancies, then you will not achieve perfection."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that following scriptural injunctions is essential for attaining perfection, happiness, and the ultimate goal of life, as stated in the Bhagavad Gita.

Our own lust and things like that. If you do that, then what will happen? Na sa siddhim avaapnoti na sukham na paramgatim. What is the goal in life? The goal in life is to be happy, is to attain perfection, right? That is all what we are striving for. That is our aim in life. Krishna is clearly declaring in the Bhagavad Gita that if you do not follow the rules and regulations mentioned in the scriptures and follow something out of your own whims and fancies, then na sa siddhim, you will not achieve perfection. Na sukham, you will not attain happiness. Na paramgatim, you will not attain the ultimate goal of life. No happiness, no liberation, no ultimate goal of life if we discard the scriptural injunctions.

#### hearing, attachment, scripture
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EWe6Kf0JoCQ?t=1200
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The first qualification is that the hearer should be very inquisitive, should be very anxious to hear instructions from the Vedic scriptures."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of being anxious to hear Vedic scriptures, drawing a parallel with people's eagerness for material news and highlighting the need to cultivate attachment for hearing about Krishna.

And then in the next verse he says, so what will happen if we follow the scriptural injunctions? One should tasma shastram pramarnam te, one should therefore understand what is duty, what is not duty, by the regulations of the scriptures. Knowing such rules and regulations, one should act so that he may gradually be elevated. So if we want to elevate ourselves, if we want to progress in this human body, in this human form of life, if we want to progress towards spirituality, we simply have to follow the scriptures. All instructions are there intact. And we needn't worry about anything else. So here, Lord Kapila is further saying to his mother that I am going to instruct you this yoga prokta puratanaha. And he says that in this verse, here Prabhupada writes in the purport, that one qualification that Mother Devahuti has, is she is very anxious to hear. He tells his mother, I shall just explain the ancient yoga system because Mother Devahuti, if you remember, in one of the previous verses, she said, I am sick and tired and disgusted with this material existence. These are the three words Prabhupada used to explain one particular Sanskrit shloka. I am sick of this material existence, I am tired of this material existence, and I am disgusted. So she is very eagerly waiting, she is hankering for Lord Kapila to talk about devotional service, and she is eagerly waiting to hear it. And Prabhupada writes here that Kapila explained, he says, I shall just explain the ancient system which I once explained to great sages because they were also anxious to hear about it. So this point of the qualification necessary to receive Vedic instructions. And Prabhupada writes, the first qualification is that the hearer should be very inquisitive, should be very anxious to hear instructions from the Vedic scriptures. That is a very important qualification.

#### hearing, Krishna consciousness, attachment
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EWe6Kf0JoCQ?t=1800
**Verse References:** SB 2.1.5
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Without hearing sufficiently and properly, no one can make progress by any method of practice."
**Summary:** The speaker discusses the importance of hearing about Krishna always and everywhere to develop attachment and taste for Krishna consciousness, emphasizing that hearing is the most important function in bhakti yoga and that sufficient and proper hearing is essential for progress.

Now, we are not very anxious to hear the Vedic scriptures. We are very anxious to receive instructions from the Vedic literatures. Why? Because we have not developed an attachment for them. We have not developed a relationship with God, with Krishna. Early morning, in the material world, even when we were outside, we are very anxious to just waiting when the newspaper will come. And very anxiously waiting. And we can't start our day without a newspaper. It is something odd if a newspaper doesn't come on our table. And then we don't read it. Why is that? Because we have somehow we have developed attachment for these material things. So we are eager. Now as devotees, we have no attachment for these newspapers and all. Right? Because somehow we have gotten detached from them. A little bit is okay for some service, but otherwise not. So we have to cultivate our attachment for hearing. That anxious hearing eagerly. Right now we are not able to hear. The point that is being discussed here is that we have to hear eagerly. So how do we come from the platform of no attachment, no taste from hearing, to hearing anxiously, hearing eagerly, hearing attentively? How do we come from this platform to that platform? Here, in one verse, Prabhupada is mentioning, this is the verse which Pariksit Maharaj is telling, Shukadeva Goswami, and then Prabhupada writes one important line. So this verse says, Tasmat, Shukadeva Goswami is instructing about the science of Krishna consciousness to Pariksit Maharaj, and then he says, Tasmat Sarvatmana Rajan Harihi Sarvatra Sarvada. Very important instruction. Tasmat, therefore, Sarvatmana Rajan Harihi Sarvatra Sarvada. Three words are being used. Sarvatmana, Sarvatra, Sarvada. And what you should be doing? Shrotavya. You should be hearing. So now, who should be hearing? And how he should be hearing? Is mentioned here. The first thing, Sarvatmana. Pariksit Maharaj is being told, everybody, Atmana means spirit, soul, human being it's referring to. Sarvatmana means all human beings. All human beings must be Shrotavya, they must be hearing. Now, how he must be hearing? It is said, Sarvatra and Sarvada. Sarvatra means everywhere he should be hearing about Krishna consciousness. And Sarvada means always we must be hearing about Krishna. So, all living entities, all human beings, always, everywhere, should be hearing about Krishna. We have developed an attachment for material subject matter because practically we see outside world always, everywhere, people are hearing about material subject matters. Outside they go for office, it's all about material subject matter. When they come out, no case he means, everything is material. And that's why we have developed attachment for them because always, everywhere, everyone is hearing about them. So the same thing, always, everywhere, all of us must be hearing about Krishna. Then it is possible to develop that taste, that attachment for Krishna. And Prabhupada writes here that in the purport, he writes, out of all the nine different methods, the first one, namely hearing, is the most important function in the process of bhakti yoga. The most important function in the process of bhakti yoga is hearing. And then he writes, that sarvatmana, that always, he writes here, without hearing sufficiently and properly, no one can make progress by any method of practice.

---
#### hearing, Krishna Katha, spiritual world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EWe6Kf0JoCQ?t=2400
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Every second we can be producing one newspaper. So much information we have of the spiritual world. But, the problem is, people are not eager to hear."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the need to give more time to hearing Krishna Katha to develop a relationship with Krishna, sharing a story about Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur and the potential for endless spiritual news, but highlighting the challenge of people's lack of eagerness to hear.

So if you want to hear properly, that means if you want to develop that anxiousness to hear, that eagerness to hear, Prabhupada is pointing out and this verse is pointing out, we have to hear sufficiently. Unless and until we hear sufficiently about Krishna Katha, that anxiousness to hear, that eagerness to hear, will not come about. This is the important point. It's very important that we develop this thing in us, this qualification in us that, you know, I am very eager to hear. We should be longing, we should be waiting for that day, that when will I be? We should be very eager to hear Srimad Bhagavata, Mahabhagavata Gita, for that matter, any topics of Krishna. Is that eagerness, am I so eager to hear Krishna? If yes, if the answer is yes, then that means we are making progress in Krishna consciousness. If I am having some taste to hear, I am seeing that I am developing some eagerness. But if that eagerness is not coming in, if that taste is not coming in, then there is something that we need to reflect and we need to hear more and more. Sarvada, always, and sufficiently, it has to be heard. So, because, the important point that comes out is we have to basically give more time in hearing Krishna Katha. That is important. And in today's world, people have no time, they have not, and when you give more time, you develop a relationship with Krishna, and then Krishna Katha will also be very tasty, very nice, you know, very attractive, and then automatically, we'll get attracted, and then we'll hanker to hear more and more. We know that story of Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur, where one politician asked him, Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati Thakur was publishing, this is about 100 years back, he was publishing, or not even 100 years, approximately 100 years back, he was publishing one newspaper, Nadia Prakash, and that was a daily newspaper, all about Krishna. So, the politician asked him, you can publish a daily newspaper of Krishna consciousness, you have so much subject matter, that politician thought that daily newspapers are only meant for political news and some material news. He couldn't realize that there could be so much spiritual news. So, then Bhaktisiddhanta told him, explained to him, that how he can publish a daily newspaper. He explained that now you have one city, Calcutta city, you have two, three newspapers, and there are two, three newspapers, and there are so many cities in India, you will have so many newspapers all across India, and then India is just one country, you have so many countries. So, you have so many newspapers every day being published from the small planet Earth. So many newspapers. And, this small planet Earth is just one small speck of dust in this, you know, in this universe. And like this, there are many, many millions of universes. So, you can imagine, in these many, many millions of universes, which have millions and billions of planets, how much, how many newspapers must be getting published for all the material news. Maybe about, you know, billions of newspapers must be getting published. And, this entire material creation is just one-fourth of the actual spiritual world. Spiritual world is three-fourths. Spiritual world is ekapada, tripada. Spiritual world is three times the material world. Spiritual world is three times the material world. And, this Nadia Prakash, which is just one daily newspaper, is the message from the spiritual world, which is three times the material world. So, if in material world you can have millions of newspapers, I can produce not just one newspaper every day, I can produce a minute edition. Every minute, one newspaper. In one more place, Prabhupada says, not a minute edition, I can produce a second edition. Every second we can be producing one newspaper. So much information we have of the spiritual world. But, the problem is, people are not eager to hear.

#### hearing, action, spiritual master
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EWe6Kf0JoCQ?t=3000
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Hearing means you should act. That hearing should react in our heart."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the importance of hearing from a bona fide spiritual master and acting upon the instructions received, highlighting that hearing should lead to a change of heart and that it is more powerful than seeing in spiritual realization.

So, it is very important that we are very conscious of this and that we are always thinking, Prabhupada has left behind so many books with him, for what? For all of us to hear. If you calculate, every day we must be here reading one hour, that is what the minimum that Prabhupada recommended. If we read also every day one hour, you know, it will take us, to read all the books of Srila Prabhupada, it will take us probably, you know, once, maybe one cycle or two cycle or something like that is what we will be able to cover. So, we have enough subject matter, we have been blessed with so much of interesting nectarian topics about Krishna and we should always be hankering about increasing our taste. For hearing. And hearing means what? Prabhupada in one more place he explains that hearing means you should act. That hearing should react in our heart. That is the meaning of reading attentively and anxiously, being anxious to hear. Just like a stone, suppose a stone is here, you know, the style of the stone. That is also hearing. Whatever we are talking, this is also hearing. But there is no reaction in the stone. It remains a stone. But when we hear, there should be a change in the heart. Meaning when we hear, we must act also according to the instructions that we hear or we receive. Then, that hearing will be very effective. And then when we hear from a bona fide spiritual master, then it is, it is, I mean, we always have to hear from a bona fide spiritual master, then that hearing is very, very important and that when we hear from him, it will immediately change the heart. So the heart change, if it is required, we have to hear from a speaker who is qualified to give proper instructions and then we must act according to his instructions for our consciousness to be uplifted.

---
#### hearing, spiritual realization, devotee
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/EWe6Kf0JoCQ?t=3600
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The eagerness to hear about God is the first qualification of a devotee to be eligible to enter the kingdom of God."
**Summary:** The speaker concludes by emphasizing the power of hearing through an analogy of milk, highlighting that hearing about God is more powerful than seeing and is the first qualification for entering the kingdom of God.

Prabhupada gave an example of how hearing is so powerful. He said, now suppose you have got a glass of milk. Now if you see the milk, you will see it is white in color. If you taste it, you can taste it is sweet in nature. If you touch it, you will feel it is liquid, it is cold. If you smell it, you can smell, some aromatic flavor is there. But if you just hear, this is milk, that's all, you don't have to do anything else, no seeing, touching, tasting, nothing. Just by hearing this is milk, you will immediately know, oh, this is milk, that means it must be white in color, that means it must have this flavor, that means it will be sweet, that means it will be liquid. All this information immediately you get just by hearing, this is milk. And as long as you don't hear it is milk, all other different processes, you will get some, some information here and there. So hearing is more powerful than seeing or any other senses. Especially in spiritual life, if you want to see God, we can't see God with our blunt senses. We can see God by hearing about God. So when it comes to spiritual realization or spiritual knowledge, hearing is more powerful than seeing. We all know 5000 years back, when Krishna was there, so many people saw Krishna, the whole world practically saw Krishna. But how many of them actually realized or understood that He is the Supreme Personality of Godhead? They were just seeing Krishna, except for the five Pandavas and few other devotees. None of them knew that He is the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Only few handful of them, 5000 years back. And today, 5000 years later, we are not seeing Krishna physically. Of course, Krishna is there as a deity, but we are not seeing Krishna in His original spiritual form. We are only hearing about Krishna from the Bhagavad Gita, from Srila Prabhupada, from Bhagavatam. And so many of us are coming to this realization of Krishna as the Supreme Personality of Godhead. 5000 years back, none of them knew, although they saw, 5000 years later, so many of us are knowing without seeing that He is the Supreme Personality of Godhead. So many of us are knowing without seeing, simply by hearing. So hearing is always very, very powerful. Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati, when he would lecture, Prabhupada would be very eager to hear whatever he was saying. Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati noted that this devotee, Srila Prabhupada, is very eager to hear my messages. That was a special blessing of Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati. And that was the time when Srila Bhaktisiddhanta Saraswati actually chose Srila Prabhupada to be his special chosen disciple. We all know how Srila Prabhupada would always be waiting eagerly, when is my Guru Maharaj going to give a talk, so that I will wait and hear him. Prabhupada was very interested and very much anxious to hear him. How important hearing is for the conditioned soul, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu personally demonstrated. In that pastime of Keshav Kashmiri, when Keshav Kashmiri was challenged by Chaitanya Mahaprabhu about who is a greater scholar, myself, Keshav Kashmiri, or Chaitanya Mahaprabhu. So Keshav Kashmiri started off the competition by composing 100 verses on Mother Ganges. You all know that story. Simply, you know, instantly he composed 100 verses, complex Sanskrit shlokas, with a lot of grammatical ornamentation and things like that. And then after composing these 100 verses, he told Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, now you judge, how is my composition. So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, he said, he pointed out what? Five, you know, defects and five gunas. Dosha and guna. Dosha is the defect and guna is the other qualities. Now how did Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, Chaitanya Mahaprabhu of course is the Supreme Personality of Godhead and he knows everything. But we must remember that Chaitanya Mahaprabhu came down as devotee incarnation to show how a devotee should practice spiritual life, right? So he had taken the role of a devotee, explaining to us practically how Krishna consciousness has to be practiced. So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu pointed out, he tells Keshav Kashmiri, how did I figure out your good qualities, the good things in your composition and the bad things in your composition? How did I figure out? Chaitanya Mahaprabhu is saying, nahi padhi alankara. This alankara, alankara means the art of literary embellishment or how to decorate, you know, with nice grammar, with some metaphor, with some simile. He says, I did not go and study all these things. Nahi padhi alankara. Then how did he come to know about this? Kariyachi shravana. He simply heard about all these things from higher circles. Kariyachi shravana. Tate ei shloka dekhi bahudosha guna. So because I heard about all this grammar and this art of literal ornamentation from higher circles, that is why I am able to point out your dosha, your defects and the guna, the good qualities in your verses. You see the importance of hearing? In Bhagavatam, in another place, what is the result of anxious hearing, of careful, attentive hearing is mentioned here? Sa shraddhadhanasya vivardhamana. Shraddhadhanasya means one who is anxious to hear. One who is anxious to hear. What is the result of anxious hearing? Samastadukhaapyayam asudhatte. Samastadukhaapyayam. Samastadukha means all miseries. Simply by shraddhadhanasya, by anxious hearing, all miseries, samastadukhaapyayam. Apyayam means they will be vanquished. They will be removed. Now how much time it will take to get it removed? Lot of time. Samastadukhaapyayam asudhatte. Asu means without delay, immediately, instantly. Dhatte means one who executes this. So if we are anxiously hearing about Krishna, if we are attentively hearing about Krishna, Bhagavatam is mentioning that very quickly, immediately, asu, instantly, all the misgivings in the heart, all our difficulties, all our disturbances, all miseries, samastadukha, everything will disappear very quickly. So this is the advantage of hearing Krishna Katha very attentively. And Prabhupada, I'll just end with this one. Prabhupada is writing in one place that the eagerness to hear about God is the first qualification of a devotee to be eligible, for a devotee to be eligible to enter the kingdom of God. So goal in life is what? To go back to Godhead. And Prabhupada is saying that the eagerness to hear about God, he is not just talking about hearing about God. Yes, hearing about God is important. And by hearing only, we will develop that. But he specifically points out the eagerness to hear about God is the first qualification to become eligible to enter the kingdom of God. We will end here. Granthra Srimad Bhagavatam ki Srila Prabhupada ki Itaikor Premananda.

#### invocation, scripture

**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy

**Summary:** The speaker begins by invoking the Lord and introducing the passage from Srimad Bhagavatam that will be discussed.

OM NAMO BHAGWATE VASUDEVAYA. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 6, Chapter 9, Appearance of Demon Vitrasura, Verse No. 26 & 27. Translation and Purport by His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada.

#### incarnation, protection, shelter
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pd1f-WBVQec?t=83
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "In our fearful condition, let us take shelter of him, for we are sure that the Supreme Lord, the Supreme Soul, will give us his protection."
**Summary:** The speaker reads the translation of the verses, highlighting the Lord's various incarnations and the importance of taking shelter in Him for protection.

Translation: By His inconceivable internal potency, the Supreme Personality of Godhead expands into various transcendental bodies, as Vamana-Dema, the incarnation of strength among the demigods, Parashurama, the incarnation among saints, Narsimhadeva and Varaha, incarnation amongst animals, and Matsya and Kurma, incarnations among aquatics. He accepts various transcendental bodies among all types of living entities, and among human beings he especially appears as Lord Krishna and Lord Rama. By his causeless mercy he protects the demigods, who are always harassed by the demons. He is the supreme worshipable deity of all living entities. He is the supreme cause, represented as the male and female creative energies. All the different from this universe, he exists in his universal form, Virata Rupa. In our fearful condition, let us take shelter of him, for we are sure that the Supreme Lord, the Supreme Soul, will give us his protection.

---
#### creation, energy, Vasudeva
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pd1f-WBVQec?t=167
**Verse References:** BG 9.4, BG 9.10
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Vasudeva, Lord Shri Krishna, is the original cause of everything."
**Summary:** The speaker reads the purport, explaining that Vishnu is the original cause of creation, elaborating on the concepts of Prakriti and Purusha, and emphasizing Vasudeva as the ultimate source.

Purport. In this verse, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Vishnu, is ascertained to be the original cause of creation. Sridhar Swami, in his commentary, Bhavarth Deepika, replies to the idea that Prakriti and Purusha are the causes of the cosmic manifestation. As stated here in Param Pradhanam Purusham Vishvamanyam, he is the Supreme. He is the supreme cause, represented as the male and female creative energies. All the different from this universe, he exists in his universal form, Virata Rupa. The word Prakriti, which is used to indicate the source of generation, refers to the material energy of the Supreme Lord, and the word Purusha refers to the living entities, who are the superior energy of the Lord. Both the Prakriti and Purusha ultimately enter the Supreme Lord, as stated in the Bhagavad Gita, Prakritim Yanti Maam Ikam. Although Prakriti and Purusha superficially appear to be the causes of the material creation, both are emanations of different energies of the Supreme Lord. Therefore, the Supreme Lord is the cause of Prakriti and Purusha. He is the original cause, Sarvakarana Karanam. The Naradya Purana, Avikaropi Paramah Prakritistu Vikarini Anupravisya Govindah Prakritishchabhidhiyate. Both the Prakriti and Purusha, which are inferior and superior energies, are emanations from the Supreme Personality of Godhead. As explained in the Bhagavad Gita, Gama Vishya. The Lord enters the Prakriti and then the Prakriti, and creates different manifestations. The Prakriti is not independent or beyond his energies. Vasudeva, Lord Shri Krishna, is the original cause of everything. Therefore, the Lord says in the Bhagavad Gita, Aham Sarvasya Prabhavo Matta Sarvam Pravartate, Iti Matva Bhajante Maam, Buddha Bhava Saman Vitaha. I am the source of all spiritual and material worlds. Everything emanates from the Supreme Lord. Everything emanates from me. The wise, who perfectly know this, engage in my devotional service and worship me with all their hearts. In Srimad Bhagavatam, the Lord also says, Aham Evasam Evagre. Only I existed before the creation. Only I existed before the creation. This is confirmed in the Brahmanda Purana as follows. Smritir Avya Vadhanena, Prakrititvam Iti Sthithi, Ubhayatmaka Suthitvadha, Vasudeva Parah Puman, Prakriti Purushad Cheti, Shabder Ekobhidhiyate. To generate the universe, the Lord acts indirectly as the Purusha and directly as the Prakriti. The energies emanate from Lord Vasudeva, the all-pervasive, supreme personality of Godhead. He is known as both Prakriti and Purusha. He is known as both Prakriti and Purusha. Therefore, Vasudeva is the cause of everything. Sarvakarana Karanam. So in this chapter, chapter 9 of 6th canto, the appearance of demon Vritrasura.

---
#### story, mantra, accuracy
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pd1f-WBVQec?t=391
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "And because of this small difference of chanting it short to long, the mantra became opposite."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Vritrasura's appearance, explaining how Indra killed his priest and how Tvashta performed a yagna, emphasizing the importance of chanting Vedic mantras accurately and the consequences of mispronunciation.

So we know the story how the demon appeared. Indra, he killed his priest because he was chanting the mantras favoring the demons. So Indra becomes very afraid and kills him. And then his father becomes upset because his son is killed. So his father, Tvashta, is offering, is performing a sacrifice by which a personality will be born who will kill Indra because Indra killed his son, that is Visvarupa. So Visvarupa was the priest that Indra had engaged and he killed his own priest because he was favoring the demons. So Tvashta, his father, performs a yagna. And as we read that in the yagna this is the importance of chanting the Vedic mantras accurately. He chanted the mantra Indra Shatro Vibhar Dasva. And he was supposed to chant it short, but he chanted it long. And because of this small difference of chanting it short to long, the mantra became opposite. Tvashta, when he chanted the mantra Indra Shatro Vibhar Dasva, Indra Shatro means the Shatru of Indra, Vibhar Dasva may increase. So from the yagna a personality would be born who would be a Shatru of Indra, who would be the enemy of Indra. But because he chanted it long instead of chanting it short, the meaning became opposite.

#### fear, shelter, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pd1f-WBVQec?t=597
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So now Indra and all the devatas are seeing this personality and they have no clue from where he has appeared and how he has appeared and they all become very very afraid and now they have taken shelter of the Supreme Personality of Godhead for protection."
**Summary:** Continuing the story, the speaker describes the fearful appearance of Vritrasura and how Indra and the devatas sought shelter in the Supreme Personality of Godhead, emphasizing Krishna as the cause of all causes, including Purusha and Prakriti.

It is said that when Vritrasura appeared he was so fearful and so huge and gigantic that the demigods felt that the entire creation would be destroyed. Not just the universe, the entire creation. But Indra managed to kill him because the Yagna was performed like that. So Vritrasura has appeared. So now Indra and all the devatas are seeing this personality and they have no clue from where he has appeared and how he has appeared and they all become very very afraid and now they have taken shelter of the Supreme Personality of Godhead for protection. So this is the Mantra. So in this shloka this is the prayer of Indra to the Supreme Personality of Godhead Narayana. They are offering prayers seeking protection, seeking shelter from Lord Narayana. So here in this prayer we read this prayer how Krishna is the cause of all causes. He is the cause of Purusha. He is the cause of Prakriti. Purusha is the superior living entity, the spirit soul and the ultimate paramam Purusha is Krishna and Prakriti is this material manifestation. So all that we see in this material world is a combination of Purusha and Prakriti because we are also small Purushas. So the origin of Purusha and Prakriti is the Supreme Lord.

#### prayer, inconceivable, Adhokshaja
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pd1f-WBVQec?t=683
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "God can be understood only by Bhakti. God cannot be understood by our material logic, by our material intelligence."
**Summary:** The speaker explains how Indra begins his prayer by glorifying Lord Narayana, emphasizing that God is inconceivable and can only be understood through Bhakti, not through material logic or intelligence, and introduces the concept of Adhokshaja.

Like that the prayer starts off and then Indra is praying to the Lord. So how do you begin a prayer? You start glorifying the Supreme Lord. So Indra is glorifying the Supreme Personality of Godhead Lord Narayana. Indra says that My dear Lord, O Lord Narayana, You are inconceivable. So we try to understand God, but actually God can be understood only by Bhakti. God cannot be understood by our material logic, by our material intelligence. That's why Indra is saying You are inconceivable. And so Krishna's name is Achintya. He cannot be understood. That's why he is inconceivable. Krishna's another name to explain how he is inconceivable is Adhokshaja. Akshaja means these senses. Akshaja means the eyes. It also refers to all the senses. The five senses that we have to acquire knowledge. But Krishna's Adhokshaja means he is beyond these five senses. So he is inconceivable because we can only conceive something which is within our intellect, which we can understand through our senses, eyes, ears, nose and all those five senses. But Krishna is beyond them. That's why he is inconceivable. And that's why Krishna is called Adhokshaja. So Indra is praying You are inconceivable.

#### incarnations, cause, intelligence
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pd1f-WBVQec?t=791
**Verse References:** BG 10.8
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Real intelligence means trying to find the ultimate cause. Because if that ultimate cause does not exist, then none of these things would exist."
**Summary:** The speaker continues explaining Indra's prayer, mentioning Krishna's expansions into different transcendental bodies and emphasizing that Krishna is the cause of all causes, contrasting this with the limited intelligence of seeking only immediate causes.

All the pastimes of Krishna are also inconceivable. So many wonderful pastimes Krishna performed. These are the inconceivable pastimes of Lord. And then he also says that Indra is praying that you expand yourself into different transcendental bodies. All the list of incarnations are mentioned here. Krishna expands himself into all these bodies. And then he also says You are the cause of all causes. And Krishna himself declares in the Bhagavad Gita I am the source of all material and spiritual world. And from me everything emanates. So as we know that understanding Krishna who is the cause of all causes is the most important thing a human being should be engaged in. You are all so busy trying to understand different things happening in the material world. Why this happened, why that happened. Why this is like that, why that is like that. We are doing PhDs, research. They are considered to be the best of the scholars. And in the PhD what are we doing? We are trying to find the causes for different things. Why it is like this. Trying to explain something. But actually real intelligence means trying to find the ultimate cause. Because if that ultimate cause does not exist, then none of these things would exist.

#### analogy, engine, Krishna
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pd1f-WBVQec?t=901
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "So a train becomes meaningless without an engine. And all the meaning that is there in all the 10 bogies and people inside the train and all the things that is happening in the train is because of the engine."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a train with multiple bogies to illustrate the difference between immediate and ultimate causes, comparing the engine to Krishna as the cause of all causes.

So we are finding this material creation so wonderful, so unique, so inconceivable. Even this tiny planet earth has got so many inconceivable wonderful things. And we are so marveled and bewildered by these wonderful things. Imagine the person who created it. How powerful and how intelligent he will be. So actually just trying to understand the immediate cause is not very intelligent. Trying to understand the ultimate cause is real intelligence. Just like a simple example. In a train which is moving, it has got 10 bogies. If a person sitting in bogie number 9 is trying to understand why bogie number 10, if he is trying to understand why my bogie is moving so wonderfully and I am proceeding towards my destination and if he tries to find the immediate cause, he will know it is because bogie 9 is pulling me. And like that if someone is sitting in bogie 9 and he is trying to find out why am I moving so fast and how wonderfully I am moving, it is because bogie 8 is pulling him. But actually these are all immediate causes. The ultimate cause for the existence of the train and for the very, for the purpose of the train's existence is the engine. And beyond the engine there is nothing else. The engine is the cause of all causes. So a train becomes meaningless without an engine. And all the meaning that is there in all the 10 bogies and people inside the train and all the things that is happening in the train is because of the engine. Engine is the main invention, is the main find, is the main thing to understand.

#### fear, nature, foreign place
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pd1f-WBVQec?t=1115
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "The place that we are living in is not natural to us. It's a foreign place. So in a foreign place, in a place which is not natural to our existence, fear is certain."
**Summary:** The speaker explains that everyone in the material world experiences fear because it is not their natural place, using the example of animals and insects to illustrate this point.

The reason because everyone is afraid in this material world. Even an animal is afraid. A small insect is afraid. A small ant is afraid. We see many times a bird is pecking at some grains. It pecks one grain and it looks left and right. Again pecks at one more grain. Again it is looking left and right, afraid that somebody will come, kill him, afraid. Once Prabhupada was giving a talk in Vrindavan. In Vrindavan there are a lot of monkeys. So while he was talking one monkey entered his room. Actually not a monkey, it was a baby monkey. The mother monkey and the baby monkey were there. And the windows bars are some size and the baby is very small. So through those two bars. And the moment the baby entered, baby hasn't got so much of intelligence. The mother becomes very afraid. And the mother is shouting, screaming, making sound, trying to shake the bars. And all the devotees are watching. And then they somehow try to push that baby monkey outside the window and then the mother took it and went away. See a small insignificant being to Indra. Everyone is afraid in this material world. And the reason as Bhagavatam gives us, tells us is because the place that we are living in is not natural to us. It's a foreign place. So in a foreign place, in a place which is not natural to our existence, fear is certain.

#### analogy, ship, spiritual world
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pd1f-WBVQec?t=1227
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Newcomers, General public
**Key Quote:** "We are spirit souls. A spirit soul is supposed to stay in the spiritual world serving Krishna. And if we are not there, no matter how rich we are, how intelligent we are, how capable we are, how good a job we have, the very fact that we are in this material world, we will be afraid."
**Summary:** The speaker uses the analogy of a ship in the ocean to further illustrate that the material world is not our natural position, emphasizing that spirit souls belong in the spiritual world serving Krishna, and that being in the material world leads to fear.

Just like Prabhupada gives an example in the sea, on the ship. Ship is a very safe place, right in an ocean. You are comfortably seated. But many times there are storms. There are fogs. When there is storm the ocean becomes very turbulent. And then everyone becomes afraid. And when there is fog again the ship stops and the ship is sounding because it's afraid that another ship will come and they will collide. In an ocean, although you are in a ship, you will always be afraid because the ocean is not your natural position. When you are on land you will not be so afraid because land in your house is your natural position. So in the same way we are not in our natural position, whether on this planet earth or in Indraloka or even in the highest material planet, Brahmaloka. We are spirit souls. A spirit soul is supposed to stay in the spiritual world serving Krishna. And if we are not there, no matter how rich we are, how intelligent we are, how capable we are, how good a job we have, the very fact that we are in this material world, we will be afraid.

#### spiritual world, shelter, fearless
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pd1f-WBVQec?t=1313
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Material world is full of fear and anxiety for everyone. So how do we create a fearless situation in the material world?"
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the need to plan to return to the spiritual world to be truly fearless, citing Prahlad Maharaj as an example, and explaining that taking strong shelter of the Supreme Lord can create fearlessness even in the material world.

So we must always be making a plan that how can I, if I want to be really fearless, I have to be in the spiritual world. I have to take shelter of the supreme personality of God. Then no matter what we are, in whatever position we are, automatically fearlessness. The best example is Prahlad Maharaj, just a small boy, 5 years old, but is completely fearless. He hasn't got some big education or anything like that, but he is fearless because he was engaged in the devotional service of the Lord. So many things he went through. He is completely fearless. It is so difficult. Imagine a small boy of 5 years. He is put into a pit of poisonous snakes, fearless. He is being pierced. He is being stamped by an elephant, fearless. He is being pierced by trident, fearless. He has even drunk poison. Imagine. So only by being a devout. And on the other hand if you see his father, he conquered Indra. He has all the wealth, all the power, but full of fear. So in the material world fearful situations are common. It is a very. But all that we need to know, a soul, that's why the spiritual world is Vaikuntha. Vaikuntha means a place where there is no fear, there is no anxiety. Material world is full of fear and anxiety for everyone. So how do we create a fearless situation in the material world? This is our unnatural position, right? Material world is unnatural, like ocean is unnatural. But now I want to be fearless in this unnatural position, in this unnatural situation. How can I do that? Because the Supreme Lord, so we can do that by taking strong shelter of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. God is, even though we are in an unnatural position, if we take strong shelter of His lotus feet, we can become fearless even in this material existence which is full of fear.

Ashoka means, there is no Shoka, there is no lamentation. There is no sorrow, Ashoka. And Abhaya, it is fearless, it is a place where there is no fear. And Amrita, Adhaar, and they are the basis for Amrita. Or like, not only it is fearless, you will get nectar at His lotus feet. And so what have I, what will I do? Taha te ekho no vishrama labhiya, labhiya. Now I will take shelter, I will take rest at your lotus feet. Chhaadi nu bhavera bhaiya. And in this way, this bhava bhaiya, the fear of this material existence, bhava, I will be able to give up. So Ashoka Abhaya, Amrita Adhaar, tomara charana dhvaya. Taha te ekho no vishrama labhiya, chhaadi nu bhavera bhaiya. Taha te ekho no vishrama labhiya, chhaadi nu bhavera bhaiya.

#### chanting, fear, holy name
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/pd1f-WBVQec?t=1603
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Yet, all fear is vanquished as soon as there is the sound of the Lord, represented by his holy name."
**Summary:** The speaker reads quotes from Prabhupada emphasizing that the sound of the Lord is identical with the Lord, that material existence is full of fear, and that all fear is vanquished as soon as there is the sound of the Lord, represented by his holy name.

It is one and the same. Krishna is present in this world in the form of his holy name. So I will read a few quotes of Prabhupada. The sound of the Lord is identical with the Lord, as we have tried to explain. The material existence of our present status is full of fear. Out of the four problems of material existence, namely the food problem, the shelter problem, the fear problem and the mating problem, the fear problem gives us more trouble than the others. Eating, sleeping, mating, defending, the four miseries of the material world. Defending or fearing is giving us more problem, gives us more trouble than others. We are always fearful due to our ignorance of the next problem. Another reason of fear, I don't know what is going to happen tomorrow. The whole material existence is full of problems and thus the fear problem is always prominent. This is due to our association with the illusory energy of the Lord, known as Maya or external energy. Yet, all fear is vanquished as soon as there is the sound of the Lord, represented by his holy name, as it was sounded by Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu in the following 16 words. Hare Krishna, Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare, Hare Rama, Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare. We can take advantage of these sounds, of these sounds and be free from the threatening problems of material existence. So just by chanting this mantra, Prabhupada is writing, fear is vanished as soon as there is sound. So actually if you become afraid, just chant and hear. Immediately you will feel a sense of fearlessness, if you are hearing. It doesn't take long. It's not that you have to do a long practice to become fearless. Many, many, lot of chanting you have to do. Prabhupada is writing, all fear, not one or two fear, all fear is vanished. How long it takes to vanish? As soon as there is sound of the Lord. As soon as there is sound, all fear vanished. Just look at the meanings of these words. How significant it is. All fear vanished immediately. As soon as there is sound. So that's why if devotees focus on hearing the sound, vibrating it very clearly, distinctly and hearing, instantly you will become fearless.

### Process of Performing Intensive Devotional Service | Sri Vasudeva Keshav Dasa | SB 6.9.28-30
**Speaker:** HG Vasudev Keshav Dasa

#### prayer, appearance of the Lord, devotion
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jIrWNoaM6Po?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "O King, when all the demigods saw the Supreme Lord in that posture, smiling with eyes like the petals of the lotus, they were overwhelmed with happiness and immediately fell down like rods offering dandavats."
**Summary:** The speaker reads verses 28-30 from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 6, Chapter 9, describing the appearance of Lord Hari before the demigods and their subsequent reaction of offering prayers and obeisances.

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, Canto 6, Chapter 9, Text No. 28, 29 and 30. Shri Shukha Uvacha, Iti Tesham Maharaja, Iti Tesham Maharaja, Suranam Upatishthatam, Suranam Upatishthatam, Pratichyam Dishya Bhudabhihi, Shankachakra Gadadharah. Translation by His Divine Grace, A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Srila Prabhupada. Shri Shukadev Goswami said, My dear King, when all the demigods offered Him their prayers, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Lord Hari, carrying His weapons, the conch shell club, the conch shell desk and club, appeared first within their hearts and then before them. This is no purport, so I'll read the next verse by myself. Atma Tulyai Shodasha Bhir Vina Sri Vatsa Kaustubha Pariyo Paasitam Unniya Nidra Shadadamburu Hekshanam Drishtvatam Avanosarva Ekshanah Lada Viklavaha Dandavat Patita Rajan Chaner Uthaya Tushtuvahu. Translation: Surrounding and serving the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Narayana, were sixteen personal attendants, decorated with ornaments and appearing exactly like Him, but without the mark of Sri Vatsa and the Kaustubha jewel. O King, when all the demigods saw the Supreme Lord in that posture, smiling with eyes like the petals of the lotus, they were overwhelmed with happiness and immediately fell down like rods offering dandavats. Then they slowly rose and pleased the Lord by offering Him prayers. Purport: In Vaikuntha Loka, the Supreme Personality of Godhead has four hands and decorations like the Sri Vatsa mark on His chest, and the gem known as Kaustubha. These are special indications of the Supreme Personality of Godhead. The Lord's personal attendants and other devotees in Vaikuntha have the same features except for the Sri Vatsa mark and the Kaustubha gem.

---
#### Vritrasura, demigods, appearance of the Lord
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jIrWNoaM6Po?t=184
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "So when this demon Vritrasura appeared, he was very very powerful, very fearful, very vast, very ghastly. It was like appearing as if the whole universe, the whole creation will come to an end."
**Summary:** The speaker summarizes the events leading to the appearance of Lord Hari, including Indra's killing of Vishwarupa, Tvashta's creation of Vritrasura, and the demigods' prayers for protection.

So in this chapter, Appearance of Demon Vritrasura, in just a few verses before, we saw how the demon Vritrasura appeared and under what conditions he appeared. Indra and all the devatas have lost their kingdom. So they are praying. They first, we know the whole story, that how they got Vishwarupa as their priest to help them and win back their kingdom. And then Vishwarupa was sikandhu. He was secretly offering the ahutis in the sacrificial fire. He was offering oblations to please the devatas, to please the Supreme Lord on behalf of the devatas and also on behalf of the demons. So Indra becomes angry and in a fit of anger, he beheads Vishwarupa, kills him. And then Vishwarupa's father, Tvashta, becomes very angry. And then Tvashta starts a sacrifice where he intended to produce, to generate a personality who will be the demon of Indra and will kill Indra. But the reverse effect happens because he chanted the mantra long instead of chanting the mantra short. So instead of, because of that mistake, the personality arose a fearful demon who would not, who was supposed to be the enemy of Indra, but because of the mantra being chanted, the mantra was chanted wrong. Indra becomes his enemy. And so Indra, we will see later in the next chapter how Indra will kill him. So when this demon Vritrasura appeared, he was very very powerful, very fearful, very vast, very ghastly. It was like appearing as if the whole universe, the whole creation will come to an end. And so all the devatas are, they have no other option now but to seek shelter at the lotus feet of the Supreme Personality of God. are offering prayers to the Supreme Personality of Godhead. So they have just finished offering their prayers and at this point of time, a wonderful thing has happened. The Supreme Personality of God personally appears. It's no ordinary thing. The Lord has appeared in His full form. Actually this chapter is called Appearance of Demon Vritrasura and there is also the Appearance of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Hari and Narayan.

#### appearance of the Lord, devotees, Vaikuntha
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/jIrWNoaM6Po?t=379
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "He appeared first within their hearts and then He appeared before them. This was the process of appearance."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the Lord's appearance, noting that He first appears in the hearts of devotees before manifesting externally, drawing parallels with Krishna's appearance to Devaki and Dhruva Maharaja's experience. He also describes the Lord's entourage and the distinguishing features between the Lord and His attendants in Vaikuntha.

So these two verses are describing how the Lord has appeared. It says here, Abhud Aavihi. Abhud means He became manifested. He became visible. Aavihi means He appeared. And how did He appear? He appeared first within their hearts and then He appeared before them. This was the process of appearance. We have a very similar thing we see in Lord Krishna's appearance at the prison house of Kamsa in Mathura to Mother Devaki. Krishna also appeared in the same way. First He appeared in the heart of Vasudev. Then from the heart of Vasudev He gets transferred. He gets transferred to the heart of Devaki and then He appears. So something very similar we are seeing here also. The Lord first appeared in the heart and then the Lord manifested Himself outside. And something similar we saw in that in Dhruva's also, when Dhruva was chanting the mantra Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya. Actually the Lord appears first in His heart as a Paramatma. Dhruva could clearly see. And then suddenly His meditation on the Vishnu Murthi broke. He was seeing the Lord in His heart and then it broke and He was wondering where did the Lord disappear? And then He opened His eyes and He saw the Lord was right in front of Him standing in His beautiful form of Vishnu. So this Lord first appearing in the heart and then appearing, manifesting in front of the devotees is a very wonderful Leela of the Lord. And the Lord appears like this to satisfy His devotees when His devotees are rendering pure devotional service. It's not an ordinary thing for the Lord to appear. It's an extraordinary thing. And here, how the Lord has appeared. He has not just appeared alone like Prabhupada says, when the King goes, the King doesn't go alone. The King goes with His whole entourage. And so, here also the Lord has appeared with 16 more personal attendants. And there is only one difference between the Lord's appearance and the appearance of these 16 attendants. That is, because in the Vaikuntha planet everybody has features of Lord Vishnu, the four-handed form. But to distinguish between the Lord's associates, the Lord's devotees in the Vaikuntha and the Lord Himself, there has to be some demarcation that He is the Supreme Lord. Because everybody there appears with four hands, with a helmet and with everything. The only differentiating factor is that the Lord has two features which none of His associates have in Vaikuntha. And what are those two features? He has the Srivatsa mark on His chest. Srivatsa is a tuft of, a curl of white hair on His chest. And He has the beautiful, valuable, magnificent Kaustubha gem, the jewel, which is suspended on His neck and which is hanging on His chest. So, when He appeared, surrounding and serving the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Narayana, were 16 personal attendants decorated with ornaments and appearing exactly like Him, but without the mark of Srivatsa and Kaustubha jewel.

Dandavat Patithaha. Patithaha means they fell down and they offered dandavats to the Lord. So they offered prayers, the Lord was pleased and satisfied, Lord appeared and soon after that they offered the dandavats and again they start offering, glorifying the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Narayana with wonderful prayers. There are many places where Prabhupada says but that chanting has to be like the Lord appearance is no extraordinary, it's an extraordinary event. So similarly and the Lord here appeared just by offering prayers. Prabhupada writes in this human form of life, our goal should be to see the supreme personality of Godhead face to face. ### Supreme Lord's Assured Protection for Surrendered Souls | Sri Vasudev Keshav Dasa
**Speaker:** HG Vasudev Keshav Dasa

#### surrender, protection, divine intervention
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/As0zMfjdw4s?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "The Pandavas' life is a wonderful life of surrender and dependence on the Lord."
**Summary:** The speaker illustrates the Lord's protection of surrendered souls through examples from the Pandavas' lives, highlighting Krishna's interventions in various dangerous situations.

The Pandavas' life is a wonderful life of surrender and dependence on the Lord. Bhima was once fed a poisoned cake and Krishna protected him. Bhima was made to fight a man-eater, Hidimba, a demon, but Krishna protected them. They were put into a wax house and the wax house was put into fire, but Krishna protected them. They tried to disrobe Draupadi, Krishna protected them by supplying unlimited amounts of cloth. Look at their perseverance of the Pandavas. Repeatedly, they were put into these sufferings. In the Kurukshetra war also, Krishna protected Arjuna. And then, when everything was over, they won the battle. The son of Dronacharya, he shot the Brahmastra in the womb of Uttara and still Krishna protected him.

#### faith, chanting, divine name, protection

Whatever difficulties are arising in our life, it is all due to a plan of the Lord and protection of the Lord will also happen as per His plan. So now, how Krishna will protect me? Now Krishna is not there, the name is there. That name will protect me. So, we must take this name very seriously, very sincerely, chant very attentively and then Krishna's protection is guaranteed.

#### disciplic succession, guru, lineage
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qP267sUyrOY?t=78
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Ganga Mata Goswamini was initiated in the line of Gauranga Shakti, Srila Gadadhar Pandit Goswami."
**Summary:** The speaker explains Ganga Mata Goswamini's position in the disciplic succession, tracing her lineage back to Gauranga Shakti, Srila Gadadhar Pandit Goswami, through Sri Haridas Pandit Goswami, Ananta Acharya, and Gadadhar Pandit.

Goswamini, she didn't marry and so that's how she got the title Goswamini. So, how does she come in our disciplic society? Sachi Devi's succession and which, where is her position, so it is like this. Ganga Mata Goswamini was initiated in the line of Gauranga Shakti, Srila Gadadhar Pandit Goswami. So, she comes in Gadadhar Pandit Goswami lineage and her guru's name is Sri Haridas Pandit Goswami. And this is a reference from Chaitanya Charitamrita. Krishnadas Kaviraj Goswami has described this Haridas' character in Chaitanya Charitamrita. She has described that, Ravana, Charitamrita. So that is the guru of Sachi Mata Devi. She is the queen. Sachi Mata Devi is the queen. The chief servitor of the Govinda temple was Sri Haridas Pandit here. His qualities and fames are known all over the world. He was gentle, tolerant, peaceful, magnanimous, grave, sweet in his words and very sober in his endeavors. He was respectful to everyone and worked for the benefit of all. Diplomacy, envy and jealousy were unknown to his heart. The 50 general qualities of Lord Krishna were all present in his body. We know that 50 qualities. So all were present in his body. Ananta Acharya, a disciple of Gadadhar Pandit, was always absorbed in love of Godhead, magnanimous and advanced in all respect. He was a reservoir of all good qualities. No one can estimate how great he was. Pandit Haridas was his beloved disciple. So you have Gadadhar Pandit, Gadadhar Pandit's disciple Ananta Acharya, Ananta Acharya's disciple Pandit Haridas and Pandit Haridas' disciple was Ganga Mata Sachi Devi.

#### devotion, renunciation, pilgrimage
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qP267sUyrOY?t=199
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "She refused to accept any mortal as her husband."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the story of Ganga Mata Sachi Devi, who was a princess but renounced family life and marriage to dedicate herself to Lord Krishna, eventually embarking on a pilgrimage.

So something more about her that was from Sisi. So who she is and what are her pastimes. She is the only daughter of King of Puthiya, Princess Ganga Mata Sachi Devi. She appeared here in this place and she became an exalted devotee. And if you have been to Jagannath Puri, you know that Sarvabhauma Bhattacharya's house. And so it is after her that the house of Sarvabhauma Bhattacharya became famous as Ganga Mata Mata in Puri. She was living in that house. So that house became famous as this as Ganga Mata Mata in Puri Dham. Princess Sachi Devi was the daughter of King Naresh Narayanan. From her early childhood, Sachi Devi was indifferent to family life and was very devoted to Lord Krishna. Her parents saw this devotion in her and wanted to see her married as soon as possible. But Sachi told them that she refused to accept any marriage. She refused to accept any mortal as her husband. When she announced her determination to remain unmarried in this way, her parents could do nothing to change her mind. When her mother died, Sachi Devi left home and set out on a pilgrimage, which led her first to Puri and then to Vrindavan.

**Summary:** The speaker describes Sachi Devi's initiation by Sri Haridas Pandit Goswami, her devotional practices in Vrindavan and Radha Kund, and her subsequent journey to Jagannathpuri to restore Sarvabhauma Bhattacharya's house, following her guru's instructions.

Sachi Devi Dasi received initiation from Sri Haridas Pandit Goswami, the great worshipper of Govindaji in Puri. She began performing her buses and bhajan in Vrindavan. Later, Sachi Devi continued her Sובas at Radha Kund. Sachi Devi composed all of her Slavas. After several years of such practice at Radha Kund, when Sachi had become very advanced in her devotional life, the Guru sent her to Purushottam Kshetra. She went to the temple and to Sarvabhauma Bhattacharya's house and she went there to revive and restore that place which had fallen into despair. Obeying her Guru's instruction, Sachi Devi went to Jagannathpuri and took the Kshetra Sanyasa vow.

#### divine intervention, devotion, recognition
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/qP267sUyrOY?t=405
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Lord Jagannath, seeing this serious mistake, appeared in the dream to both the King and the head Pujari, ordering them to immediately release Sachi Devi from the jail and not only to release her, but to take Diksha from her."
**Summary:** The speaker continues the story, describing how Sachi Devi was found at the lotus feet of Lord Jagannath, mistakenly imprisoned, and then released after Lord Jagannath appeared in dreams to the King and head Pujari, ordering them to take Diksha from her.

Shri Ganga Mata Ki, Jai, Shri Sachi Devi Ki, Jai. Jai. Entering Ganga Devi's waters, she floated to the lotus feet of Jagannath Swamy in Jagannathpuri. And then early in the morning, when the Pujaris unlocked the door of Jagannath Temple and opened the gates, they discovered her lying at the lotus feet of Lord Jagannath. So all the Pujaris thought that she's a thief and somehow she has come inside the Jagannath Temple, that's what they thought. And so they locked her up and put her in the jail, in the prison. But Lord Jagannath, seeing this serious mistake, appeared in the dream to both the King and the head Pujari, ordering them to immediately release Sachi Devi from the jail and not only to release her, but to take Diksha from her. In the year 1721 AD in Sri Jagannathpuri, at the age of around 120 years, Ganga Mata Goswamini entered the eternal pastimes of Lord Shri Krishna and Jagannatha.

### The Urgency of Bhakti in a Godless World | Sri Vasudev Keshav Dasa | Sri Gaura Mandala Parikrama
**Speaker:** HG Vasudev Keshav Dasa

#### duty, death, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3CpLo1CgMbM?t=0
**Verse References:** SB 1.19.24
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The duration of life is immaterial, but the duty of a dying man is very important."
**Summary:** The speaker introduces Maharaj Pariksit's questions about duty in all circumstances and specifically for those about to die, emphasizing the importance of devotional service to Lord Krishna as the ultimate answer.

Reading from Srimad Bhagavatam, first canto, 19th chapter, 24th verse, appearance of Shukadev Goswami. Translation, O trustworthy brahmanas, I now ask you about my immediate duty. Please, after proper deliberation, tell me of the unalloyed duty of everyone in all circumstances and specifically of those who are just about to die. Purport, in this verse, the king has placed two questions before the learned sages. The first question is, what is the duty of everyone in all circumstances? And the second question is, what is the specific duty of one who is to die very shortly? Out of the two, the question relating to the dying man is most important, because everyone is a dying man, either very shortly or after 100 years. The duration of life is immaterial, but the duty of a dying man is very important. Maharaj Pariksit placed these two questions before Shukadev Goswami also on his arrival. And practically the whole of Srimad Bhagavatam, beginning from the second canto up to the last 12th canto, deals with these two questions. The conclusion arrived at thereof is that, devotional service of Lord Srikrishna, as it is confirmed by Lord himself in the last phases of the Bhagavad Gita, is the last word in relation to everyone's permanent duty in life. Maharaj Pariksit was already aware of this fact, but he wanted the great sages assembled there to unanimously give their verdict on his conviction, so that he might be able to go on with his confirmed duty without controversy.

---
#### self-realization, surrender, duty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3CpLo1CgMbM?t=153
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "The first class method demands that one give up all other methods and surrender unto the lotus feet of Lord and thus be saved from all sins and their reactions."
**Summary:** The speaker elaborates on the importance of Parikshit Maharaj's questions, highlighting the unique circumstance of his impending death and emphasizing the significance of surrendering to the Lord as the highest duty.

He has specifically mentioned the word Shuddha or perfectly correct. For transcendental realization or self-realization, many processes are recommended by various classes of philosophers. Some of them are first class methods and some of them are second or third class methods. The first class method demands that one give up all other methods and surrender unto the lotus feet of Lord and thus be saved from all sins and their reactions. So in this verse, Parikshit Maharaj is asking something very important to all the sages who have assembled there in the forest of Naimisharanya. He has just been cursed by the son of the Rishi, Sringi, that boy. And the curse is that he is supposed to die in seven days. He receives a death notice, something very unique, Prabhupada writes. And he is asking two very, very important questions. Prabhupada writes that the quality of question itself is so wonderful. Just the question itself is so wonderful. What are the two questions he is asking? Number one, what is... What is the ultimate duty, the unalloyed duty of everyone in all circumstances? Not just what is my duty, his duty. Everyone's duty, unalloyed duty, the highest duty in all circumstances. It's like everything is covered in this. And then the second question that he is asking is, and the duty of someone who is about to die immediately. These are the two questions and Prabhupada writes in the purport, the entire Srimad Bhagavatam is based out of these two questions. Right from the second canto, this is the 19th chapter of the first canto. The first canto is ending now. And now the second canto to 12th canto is all based on these two questions. So Prabhupada writes,

---
#### death, impermanence, duty
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3CpLo1CgMbM?t=278
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Every moment we must remind ourselves that we have a very important duty, and I must complete that duty before I die."
**Summary:** The speaker emphasizes the universality of death and illustrates its unexpected nature with the story of a neighbor who died suddenly, highlighting the urgency of fulfilling one's duty.

Who is not a dying man? Everyone is a dying man. We cannot say, generally people think dying means old age. But no, we cannot be so sure when death will come. Death may come any moment. There is no guarantee. And in that way, Prabhupada says, everyone is a dying man. There was one of my neighbors, we stayed in Calcutta for about 20 years. And one Mr. Mittal Ji, we shared the same floor. We were next door neighbors. We used to spend time there. They used to spend time there. I knew them very well. So just not long time ago, but very recently I got a news about him that he passed away. It was very shocking how he passed away. He was a very hale and healthy man, very nice person, very gentle. And he had built a new house and he was supposed to be doing Griha Pravesha. There was so much of excitement and happiness in the entire family. He has three children and the pujas were supposed to begin. And he was supposed to go, the Pandit Ji told him that can you please go and bring some puja items for the puja. Something was missing or something like that. So he went to bring some puja items from the market and then family members are waiting for him to come back. And he is not returning. And then after a while it was a matter of concern what happened. And then somehow they got a call after some time that he is, who is this and he is your father and he is on the road and please come. He looks like something has happened. Please come and they were all shocked and they rushed to the place from where the call came. And what had happened actually, he was carrying the puja items or something like that. He was walking and he was going and one vehicle came and hit him. It was not a very big hit, it was a small hit. And he fell down, the way he fell down something on his brain got hurt. And he was rushed to the hospital. And the doctor says 24 hours is very critical. We cannot say anything like that. But as Krishna had desired after 24 hours he passed away. This news came as a big shock. Imagine the excitement, griha pravesha, new house, new apartment. And this is what happened and nobody had any idea about it. So every moment we must remind ourselves that we have a very important duty.

#### devotional service, Krishna consciousness, prayer
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3CpLo1CgMbM?t=432
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** analogy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers, Newcomers
**Key Quote:** "Unless and until you become perfectly Krishna conscious, we have not actually done our duty."
**Summary:** The speaker shares another near-death experience, emphasizing the importance of devotional service and Krishna consciousness as the ultimate duty, and the need for sincere prayer.

And I must complete that duty before I die. Just yesterday it happened. We were all travelling to Haridas Thakur's birthplace and we were in the first vehicle. Ten of us were there. And just we were driving, the driver was driving, it was at a medium speed. And just about 15-20 meters ahead of us one big tree branch just fell on the road. It was like first time live we saw it. It got captured on the video. And it was a fraction of 3-4 seconds. If we were 3-4 seconds ahead. It was a huge one. It occupied the entire expanse of the road. It just fell. It was some old tree or something like that. And see how Krishna saved us. So this verse is actually talking about how seriously we must take to devotional service. What is the unalloyed duty of everyone in all circumstances. The question itself is so serious. I must become Krishna conscious very soon, very quickly. Because unless and until you become perfectly Krishna conscious, we have not actually done our duty. We chant that verse, Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu doya koro more. Toma bina ke dayalu jagata samsare. And what is the second verse? Shri Krishna Chaitanya Prabhu doya koro more. And what is the second line? Patita pavan hetu tavey avataar. Mosama patita Prabhu na paiy beyaar. So we sing this every day. And then we say, we offer a prayer to Krishna. That Krishna, I am the most fallen. And since I am the most fallen, my claim is first on being delivered. Please claim me. I am the most fitting candidate for being delivered. And then your name as Patita. Your name as Patita Pavana will be truly glorified. We pray like that. Prabhupada in one place writes something more than this. Yes, we can pray. Definitely we should pray that I am the most fallen so my claim is first. But then in what should be a mood and what should be a consciousness of praying and a practice of devotional service.
