#### chanting, holy name, devotional service
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/3CpLo1CgMbM?t=1010
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** story
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "Except chanting, a person has no other duty. In this holy, in this human form of life."
**Summary:** The speaker narrates the pastime of Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Lakshahira, emphasizing the importance of chanting one lakh holy names daily and highlighting the significance of intensive devotional service.

So Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, He would, devotees would invite Chaitanya Mahaprabhu for the, for lunch. That was a, that was a system. That 12 lakh, the noon time lunch, Mahaprabhu would take in the house of devotees. And all the devotees, He was very eager to host Chaitanya Mahaprabhu to, because that was a devotional service and they wanted to do that. And Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, He realized that he is receiving too many invitations. So every day you can accept only one invitation. But there were so many devotees of Lord Chaitanya that every day he was receiving 3 to 4 invitations. So Mahaprabhu had to devise a method by which, you know, it is reduced to just one invitation. So he says from today I am going to accept invitations from only those devotees who are Lakshishwar. Lakshishwar. Lakshishwar means, it is something synonymous to, in Calcutta, Lakpati. Lakpati. It was a very well known term. This is about 30 years back. Lakpati means, now a days it is all crorepati. Lakpati is gone. Millionaire. So, Lakpati means, you possess a lakh of rupees. You can imagine what a Laksh, Lakpati would be during Chaitanya Mahaprabhu's time. But there were, Hiranya and Gordhan Majundar were there like that. So, So, Mahaprabhu says, from today I am going to accept invitations only from these people. So everybody thought, oh my god, Lakpati. None of them were Lakpati. They were all very simple devotees. Kola, Vicha, Shridhara. You know what he was. So they thought that, okay, from today, then we can't host Mahaprabhu because that is the condition that he has placed. And then Mahaprabhu said something. He was actually basically trying to impress upon, stress upon, intensive devotional service. He said, no, Lakshishwar means, Ishwar means a lord of one lakh. Not one lakh rupees. You should be possessing, not one lakh rupees, you should be possessing one lakh holy names every day. So. And this is what Gaudiya Math was actually. They were chanting one lakh holy names every day. 64 rounds. And actually if you see, Srila Prabhupada also in the initial days, He told, you chant 64 rounds. And that is why our rule is, 16 rounds minimum. That word minimum is there. So all these things, when you read and hear, it is actually stressing on this word, intensive practice of devotional service. And this is our duty, only duty for everyone in all circumstances. In one place Srila Prabhupada is saying, directly is pointing out, except chanting, a person has no other duty. Except chanting, there is no other duty in this holy, in this human form of life. So basically all these things, you know, it is stressing. So at least 16 rounds. Yes finally we have 16 rounds with us. And we should stick to 16 rounds and engage ourselves in devotional service. Because that is what finally Prabhupada gave us. But these 16 rounds

And he doesn't write this to one disciple. In one year, He writes about 16 letters. In all the 16 letters, this line is repeating. Please chant 16 good rounds. ### True Meaning of Tapasya | Sri Vasudev Keshav Dasa | ISKCON Bangalore Sanga
**Speaker:** HG Vasudev Keshav Dasa

#### austerity, devotional service, Krishna consciousness
**Timestamp:** https://youtu.be/LRvhg77DyQ4?t=0
**Verse References:** —
**Content Type:** philosophy
**Audience:** Full-time devotees, Congregation / volunteers
**Key Quote:** "If you are doing worship of Krishna, that is the highest Tapasya."
**Summary:** HG Vasudev Keshav Dasa explains that true austerity is centered around devotional service to Krishna, not material gain, and that worshiping Krishna is the highest form of Tapasya.

Vrishabhadeva said to his sons that Tapo Divyam Putraka Yena Satvam Shuddhe, heart will become cleansed by practicing austerities. And we have some very simple austerities to practice: rising early in the morning, chanting 16 rounds, taking morning bath. All our austerities are actually centered around devotional service. The goal of austerity is service to Krishna, not to attain some power or some material opulence. Tapasa, you are doing Tapasya. If you are doing worship of Krishna, that is the highest Tapasya. There is no need of any other higher Tapasya. And if you are not engaged in devotional service, then all of you will be in trouble. Any other so-called Tapasya we may be doing, like sometimes some people say, "I am doing this Vrata, I am doing that Vrata, I am doing Somvar Vrata or I am..." All that is good, but it is only at a material plane. We follow Ekadashi to please Krishna. That's why if you see, all the Tapasyas and Krishna consciousness in our life is all aimed towards furthering our devotional service.

---
